observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our
recommendations that
the Local Chief
Executive instruct the
(a) Inventory Team to
(i) undertake complete
physical inventory of all
property, plant and
equipment of the
municipality; and
(ii) prepare the
corresponding Report
on the Physical Count
of Property, Plant and
Equipment and submit
the same to the Office
of the Auditor within
the prescribed period;
(b) OIC – General
Services Officer to
maintain complete
property records; and
(c) Municipal
Accountant to allocate
depreciation expenses
yearly to all Property,
Plant and Equipment
(PPE), except land,
until fully depreciated.
Furthermore, we
recommended that the
Municipal Accountant
and the OIC-General
Services Officer
(i) undertake periodic
reconciliation of records
of all properties to
establish correctness of
the balances of PPE
accounts; and (ii)
consider the guidelines
on one-time cleansing of
PPE accounts to render
a reliable PPE
information.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Team
that will conduct the
physical count.
The Municipality has
already established a
new Inventory
Committee that will be
responsible for the
conduct of physical
count of all PPE and
the preparation of the
corresponding report
to be submitted to the
Office of the Auditor.
The GSO will maintain
complete property
records.
observations.reason:
The Inventory
Committee still failed
to submit Report on
the Physical Count of
PPE.
Property records were
incomplete.
Non-preparation of
RPCPPE, thus
corresponding
reconciliation could
not be done.
The municipality did
not conduct one-time
cleansing of PPE
accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
the Local Chief
Executive direct the
(a) Municipal Planning
and Development
Officer to
(i) avail the technical
assistance of
Department of Human
Settlements and Urban
Development (DHSUD)
in updating its
Comprehensive Land
Use Plan (CLUP) to
come up with a more
effective and specific
development goals; and
(ii) ensure the
correlation of the plans
between CLUP,
Comprehensive
Development Plan
(CDP), Local
Development
Investment Program
(LDIP) and Annual
Investment Plan (AIP)
for a more focused
implementation;
(b) Local Development
Council to
(i) diversify fund
allocation to ensure
that other critical
sectors, including social
development,
infrastructure
development,
environmental
management, and
economic development,
receive adequate
funding to promote
comprehensive and
sustainable growth
within the Municipality;
and (ii) specifically
describe the projects for
inclusion in the AIP, and
always secure approval
or authorization of the
Sangguniang Bayan
before incurring
expenditures and/or
entering into contract in
the implementation of
projects described in
generic terms;
(c) Municipal Budget
Officer to monitor the
fund utilization to avoid
spending beyond the
approved budget for
each PPA;
(d) Municipal
Accountant to
(i) strengthen her
review function on the
requisite for PPAs to
support the claims; and
(ii) observe the timely
submission of
Disbursement Vouchers
to the Audit Team
particularly the
transaction documents
amounting to
₱5,370,005.82; and
(e) Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to
(i) explain/justify why
the default mode of
procurement which is
public bidding was not
observed in the
procurement of hauling
services on solid wastes;
and (ii) observe the
rules and regulations on
procurement process
and procedures to
ensure that the most
advantageous prices
will be obtained for the
government.
observations.managementAction:
The Local
Development Council
ensures that only
eligible PPAs are
financed by the 20%
Development Fund.
The Municipal Planning
and Development
Office will avail the
technical assistance of

update. The BAC to
observe procurement
rules and Municipal
Accountant to
strengthen review
functions on
documentation.
observations.reason:
Some PPAs were still
not included in CDP.
Procurement of hauling
services was not
conducted through
public bidding.
There were still delays
in submission of
accounts and
transactions in CY
2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator:

Identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF; and

Stop the inclusion of PPAs in generic terms in the AIP for all projects of the LGU to ensure full implementation thereof within the timetable indicated in the AIP.

We also reiterated our CY 2022 audit recommendations with modification that the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator: 

Conduct a detailed engineering of all projects prior to its inclusion in the AIP which includes the preparation of the Program of Work with the Detailed Estimates and plans/specifications;

Prepare the Program of Work with the Detailed Estimates and plans/specifications for the unexpended balance of the CY 2022 Construction of various barangay infrastructure project amounting to ₱256,000.00 to ensure its full implementation; and

Submit to the Bids and Awards Committee the Program of Work with the Detailed Estimates, plans/specifications and Purchase Request within the timetable indicated in the AIP, taking into consideration the period within which the procurement process and project implementation will be completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC Chairman and its members, the TWG, the Secretariat, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer should ensure that the Index-Based Pricing Scheme is adopted, including the specific procedures required under the Appendix 22 of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.

More specifically, we recommended to:

Indicate in the ITB among others the indicative volume to be procured for bidding purposes only.

Indicate in the Supplier’s bid price whether a discount was given and/or a delivery cost was included.

Include in the perfected contract provision that an index-pricing scheme was applied, the manner of payment, the time of delivery, the required volume for each delivery and other specific procedures prescribed in theabovementioned Guidelines.

Ensure that the amount payable to the Supplier should be equivalent to the unit/volume actually delivered in a specific period of time, with a consideration to the weekly price adjustments, the discounts given and the delivery cost.

Require the Supplier to provide a detailed price adjustment in each delivery, together with the certification under oath that the price adjustments were the reported and duly psoted at DOE’s website for products.

Ensure that the LGU’s end-user or acceptance team should maintain records for the unit/volume of diesel actually delivered and the remaining allowable unit/volume to be ordered, including the accounting of its equivalent amount or total cost.

observations.reason: RCDs were still not prepared on a timely basis, as the MTO waits for other collections for consolidation.

observations.recommendation: Implement close and strict supervision of all the accountable officers to ensure that rules and regulations in handling collections and accountable forms shall be strictly complied with and to forestall the possible risk of loss or misuse.
observations.reason: RCDs were still not prepared on a timely basis, as the MTO waits for other collections for consolidation.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to undertake collaborative efforts to immediately reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records, following the procedures enumerated under Items 6.3.1 and 6.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
observations.managementAction: To conduct reconciliation of RCPPE with the accounting records following the procedures of COA Circular No. 202-006
observations.reason: The MTO and the MACCO have yet to conduct the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: Avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts under COA Circular 2020-006 for the disposition of non-existing/missing/unserviceable PPE items to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability
observations.managementAction: To avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts under CO-202-006.
observations.reason: The MTO and the MACCO have yet to perform the reconciliation and disposition procedures under COA Circular No. 2020-006.

observations.recommendation: Return to the 20% DF the amount erroneously charged to the said fund.
observations.reason: The amount is not yet returned to the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Engineering Office to investigate and render a report stating the reasons therefor, for the unfinished project, and immediately mobilize resources to accelerate the project’s completion, so that the same could be fully utilized within the year, and the purpose would be delivered to and the benefits that could be derived therefrom are felt by the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office in coordination with the Municipal Mayor’s Office formulate possible innovations and strategies to restructure the project amid uncertainty, using the resources available, or provide additional budget for the completion of the project to optimally utilize the chocolate processing center.
observations.reason: The project remains incomplete as the supplemental budget was approved only in the 4th quarter of CY 2024 and there was consequent failure of bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Engineering Office render a report stating the reasons for the unidentified/unlocated project and submit the necessary supporting documents to substantiate distribution of materials to the barangays. Otherwise, the said charges shall be disallowed in audit, and all approving officers will be held liable for the amount disbursed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office maintain acceptance documents to adhere to any regulatory obligations tied to the distribution of materials.
observations.reason: The MEO, MTO, and MACCO and all other requisitioning offices do not observe the use of the Requisition and Issue Slip in the issuance of supplies, including those intended for construction projects.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursement vouchers covering the payment for materials and wages are duly supported with complete and relevant supporting documents before approving thereof. Reference to the Projects Program of Works or inquiry with the implementing office must be undertaken to identify the exact purpose of the procured materials, whether for distribution to the barangays or the prosecution of the project by the MEO.
observations.managementAction: Double check whether disbursement vouchers covering the payment for materials and wages are duly supported with complete and relevant supporting documents before approving thereof.
observations.reason: The MEO, MTO, and MACCO and all other requisitioning offices do not observe the use of the Requisition and Issue Slip in the issuance of supplies, including those intended for construction projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the MHRMO and MACCO ensure that all payrolls be duly supported with complete documentary requirements as enumerated under COA Circular 2012-001. If necessary, conduct re-orientation with those assigned in preparing payrolls to capacitate them of the said requirements.
observations.managementAction: Conduct re-orientation with representatives from different offices to capacitate them on the documentary requirements as enumerated under COA Circular 2012-001 & other laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Some Daily Time Records, with no time entries, were paid and approved despite lacking necessary documents such as certificates of appearance, among others.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action 

The granting of honoraria to members of committee/councils should be limited only to those specifically authorized by law, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Limit the granting of honorarium only to those specifically authorized by law, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Some Daily Time Records, with no time entries, were paid and approved despite lacking necessary documents such as certificates of appearance, among others.

observations.recommendation: The accomplishment reports should be specific and based on the actual performance of the individual claiming, i.e., number of received complaints/reports, no. of anti-illegal gambling operations conducted, or no. of fact-finding investigations conducted, or no. of apprehended violators during the month claimed.
observations.managementAction: To require specific data in attached accomplishment reports.
observations.reason: Some Daily Time Records, with no time entries, were paid and approved despite lacking necessary documents such as certificates of appearance, among others.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MTO furnish the MACCO with the duly certified list of Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and not by barangay only as its basis in the recording.
observations.managementAction: To furnish the MACCO with the duly certified list of Real Property TaxRealPropertyTaxReceivables/SpecialEducationTaxReceivablesbasedonTax Receivables.
observations.reason: MTO has not submitted to MACCO the certified list of RPT Receivables/ SET Receivables based on the RPT Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MACCO coordinate with the MTO on its latest report of delinquent properties, excluding those RPUs under the Republic of the Philippines, and prepare the necessary entries to correctly set the receivables and deferred income accounts.
observations.managementAction: To submit latest report of delinquent properties excluding those RPUs under the Republic of the Philippines.
observations.reason: MTO personnel is still working out to exclude in the list of delinquencies the RPUs under the Republic of the Philippines to come up with the latest report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant review the entries made pertaining to RPT/SET advance payments. Prepare reconciliation of the abstract of collections as received from the MTO and as generated from the ECPAC to establish the correct amount of advance collections to be adjusted. Furnish the audit team with copies of the JEV.
observations.managementAction: Request software provider for adjustments in the program.
observations.reason: Request was already sent to the software provider. Still waiting for feedback.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the procuring officer record in the stock card the inventories received based on the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) and submit to the Municipal Accountant (MACCO) copies of the AIR, Purchase Order (PO) and delivery receipts. Issue RIS upon issuance of inventories which shall be summarized monthly in the RSMI. The RSMI, along with the original RIS, shall be submitted to the Accounting Unit within five days after the end of the month.
observations.managementAction: MEO to submit to MACCO the list of issued/used construction materials to update the accounting records.
observations.reason: The MTO and MACCO do not observe the use of RIS in the issuance of supplies to the end-users. Thus, no RSMI was prepared to summarize the RIS.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials to be reported in the RPCI to avoid the occurrence of recording inventory when these were all consumed or used in the project or activity.
observations.reason: The physical inventory-taking covered only the Office Supplies Inventory; no count was taken for the other types of inventories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible means to enforce settlement/liquidation for the long overdue receivables; if there is a possibility of non-collection, provide an allowance for impairment in accordance with IPSAS 21; and if no chance of being collected, request for authority to write-off from the Commission on Audit and submit the required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. Municipal Accountant affirmed that as of the current year, no impairment loss was still recorded but he committed that he will provide the needed report as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions, reconcile accounts, and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. The reconciliation is ongoing.
observations.reason: No

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that is composed of two or more employees including the GSO who will conduct a physical count of all PPEs which shall be the basis for preparing the RPCPPE; the GSO to maintain PPE Cards, and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain PPE Ledgers Cards and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements; and the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory Committee to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was constituted and conducted physical counts; reconciliation and required records were initiated.
observations.reason: The LGU is still unable to assign eligible and competent personnel in the conduct of the inventory count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee take the full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at the station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU and create and require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan, to be approved by the Local Chief Executive and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period, and to comply with the prescribed unique property number indicated in the property stickers used for inventory tagging.
observations.managementAction: No complete Physical Inventory Count of all PPEs yet. The Inventory Committee will prepare the Physical Inventory Plan.
observations.reason: No complete Physical Inventory Count of all PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO retrieve the records of the properties of such Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and attach Property Tags to the old and newly acquired PPEs before these are distributed to the intended end-users to establish control on the use of the government property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. Non procurement of stickers/tags and non-completion of physical inventory count.
observations.reason: Non procurement of stickers/tags and non-completion of physical inventory count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant initiate the conduct of verification, analysis, and validation of the dormant accounts and follow the procedures and guidelines in the preparation of documents for the filing of a request for write-off, where applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that he will comply with the requirements for write-off for those eligible accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant will comply with the requirements for write-off for those eligible accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer immediately restitute the remaining shortage of ₱971,422.88, without prejudice to appropriate legal action for failure to produce the missing funds.
observations.managementAction: The withheld salaries and other benefits were still not enough to cover the unpaid net emoluments shortage. The Accountable Officer is processing the refund and the balance shall be pursued according to existing rules.
observations.reason: The withheld salaries and other benefits were still not enough to cover the unpaid net emoluments shortage.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive being the head of the agency, direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof and to perform the same regularly, pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

However, we recommended the following to Management for deficiencies noted in the review of the submitted BRS:
The Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in identifying the details, nature and supporting documents of the identified reconciling items; and
Henceforth, regularly prepare the BRS for each bank account to immediately identify the discrepancies between the agency books and bank account and effect adjustments for reconciling items noted in order to present a reliable and accurate balance of Cash in Bank account to instill confidence among financial statements users in relying on the accuracy of the amount presented.

We again reiterated our recommendations that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Review the journal entries made from CYs 2021 to 2023 that caused the sudden increase of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and effect the necessary adjustments; and
Examine the DBP Bank Account No. 000-50092-541-4 to identify the cash balance that pertain to the unutilized portion of various fund transfers from National Agencies and transfer the same to the LBP bank account maintained by the LGU for Trust Fund for proper accounting.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Make the necessary adjusting journal entries as summarized in the table below; and
Strictly comply with the proper classification of accounts in compliance with COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended the following to Management:
The Municipal Accountant to record the procured supplies and materials as inventories upon purchase and the corresponding expense accounts must be taken up only upon issuance/s thereof;
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), duly supported with the original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) on weekly or monthly basis. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant should adjust the inventory account for whatever consumption on the basis of the submitted SSMI; and
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) and Stock Cards, respectively, for each commodity/stock.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management exert efforts to locate the necessary documents of the land acquired by the Municipality through purchase or donation in order for the Accounting Office to properly value or measure the land account for fair presentation in the financial statements. Henceforth, general ledger and subsidiary ledgers for land account should be prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant trace the cost and period or date of acquisition of all the PPEs where the provision for depreciation were not provided and compute for the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007 and Section 4 (o) of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I to establish proper valuation of PPE accounts.

We recommended that:
Management direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries in their books of accounts to present fairly the balances of the PPE and other affected accounts, as follows:
To recognize reclassification of Office Equipment to Semi-Expendable Property:
Issuance of the Semi-Expendable Office Equipment to end-user
Closing of previously recognized Accumulated Depreciation:
We also recommended that the Supply Officer prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registries, and reports, as required in the Circular, to strengthen controls over that agency’s semi-expendable properties.

To address the continuing issue of incomplete subsidiary ledgers, we reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:
Locate all available documents regarding the abovementioned accounts in order to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers; and
Exhaust all possible remedies to reconcile the balances of those payable accounts in which SL is maintained. Prepare necessary adjusting entries, if any.
Furthermore, we recommended that Management institutionalize the regular updating and maintenance of both the general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers. Timely recording of transactions and periodic reconciliation between the two sets of records should be integrated into routine accounting practices. This will help prevent the recurrence of discrepancies, enhance the accuracy of financial reporting, and strengthen internal control over financial data.

We reiterate the following recommendations to the Management:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are adjusted on timely basis; and
Perform constant review of transactions to deter errors and omissions that affects the financial statements accounts.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare adjustments to correct the journal entries made on the transfer of completed project to general fund from trust fund to restate the previous balance of government equity and to properly close the Due to NGA/LGUs account; and
Thereafter, determine the composition of prior year’s transactions credited to Government Equity account under the Trust Fund and prepare and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the accounting error in the recording of government financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the corrections in the Notes to Financial Statements (FS) for the period-ended December 31, 2024 as detailed and presented in the Revisions/Remarks column above in order to enhance the understandability and reliability of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers to determine, at any given time, the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the Financial Statements. Also, submit the required records as provided under Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular 2010-001.

We commended the Management for its consistent adherence to Section 287 of RA 7160 and DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 and implementing majority of their prioritized PPAs under the 20% Development Fund for the benefits of its constituents.
However, we recommended the management to ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% DF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PPAs that were still unimplemented positive efforts should be made to fast track the completion of the said PPAs.

We recommended the following to management:
To use the template/format specified in the Annex A (LDRRMFIP);
Ensure that the LDRRMC include in their plan the utilization of the unexpended LDRRM Fund balances from prior years, and specify the list of PPAs to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in its LDRRMFIP before utilization of the same pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Submit justification for the disbursements charged against the TL DRRM to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended the following to management:
Conduct personnel audit, review, assessment and evaluation of existing manpower complement to prepare an updated organization structure/pattern;
Remove excessive and unnecessary JOs who are performing redundant functions with other workers; and
Formulate uniform policy and procedures in engaging and managing outsourced workers taking into consideration the issuances of Civil Service Commission as to the appropriate forms and type of contracts and classification of workers.

We recommended the following to management:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof;
The Municipal Treasurer designated as Petty Cash Custodian return the unutilized petty cash as at year-end and to issue the corresponding official receipt as required under the regulation; and
The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the reclassification of accounts of persons no longer connected with the LGU, which have been outstanding for years and demand settlement thereof, in order to reflect the correct balance of the Petty Cash Account.

We recommended that the Management require the recipients of financial assistance under the Socio-Civic Projects Fund to immediately submit the required supporting documents set under the Memorandum of Agreement specifically the request/personal letter addressed to the President through the LGU to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended the following to Management:
The Disbursing Officers prepare the report of disbursements to support liquidations of Advances for Payroll;
The Municipal Accountant record the liquidations in the Journal Entry Vouchers prior to recording in the cash disbursements journal; and
The Accounting Office ensure that liquidation reports were properly filled out and acknowledged by the Municipal Accountant to increase the degree of confidence in the validity, propriety, accuracy of the reports submitted.

We recommended the following to Management:
The Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission of the noted unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers with complete documents supporting the financial transactions to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension; and
Comply with the deadline set for the submission of accounts which is on the 10th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the Local School Board:
Present the SEF Budget by program, activity and project (P/A/P), by allotment class and per school using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1;
Submit immediately the Local School Board Resolution that authorizes the utilization of SEF for CY 2024 if there’s any; and
In the absence of LSB Resolution, provide justification why the disbursements should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue adhering with the
pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 to
ensure reliability and accuracy of presentation in the financial statements
of the Cash-in-Bank Local Currency-Current Account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) continue to set-up RPT/SET Receivables
based on Certified List of Taxpayers provided by Municipal Treasurer’s
Office; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the prior
years’ set-up of RPT/SET Receivables merely based on estimates; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) regularly update the Real Property Tax
Account Register (RPTAR) to properly identify and notify the
delinquent tax payers; (ii) introduce other collection strategy that would
significantly help increase the revenue from RPT/SET Receivables of the
Municipality; and (iii) institute applicable remedies, administrative and/
or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes as provided in
Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) intensify collection
measures or institute appropriate remedies for the settlement or
liquidation of past due Receivable accounts amounting to ₱1,286,835.14;
and (b) initiate the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-
008 for the application of request for write-off of the ₱3,600.00 dormant
account of one deceased former employee of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from granting additional
cash advances to the Special Disbursing Officer unless the previous cash
advance given to her is first settled or properly accounted for. Moreover,
we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consider increasing the
amount of Fidelity Bond of the Special Disbursing Officer whose cash
advances exceeded the maximum cash accountabilities as required under
Annex C of the Treasury Circular No. 002-2019 or to designate additional
disbursing officer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize in the books of accounts all
purchases of supplies and materials under inventory accounts, except
those purchased through petty cash fund regardless of whether or not
they are consumed within the accounting period in order to prepare an
accurate and correct information in the financial statements; and (ii)
recognize in the books of accounts the inventory expenses using the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the
Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) as basis; and (b) OIC-GSO to (i) conduct
physical count of inventories and submit the required RPCI to the
Auditor’s Office not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for
the first and second semesters, respectively, as mandated under Section
124 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I for LGUs; and (ii) prepare SSMI
supported by RIS and submit the same to Municipal Accountant for
preparation of JEV to record appropriate expense accounts.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the (a) Inventory Committee to (i) undertake complete
physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the
municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit the same to the
Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and (b) OIC – General
Services Office (GSO) to maintain complete property records.
Furthermore, we reiterated that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-
GSO (i) undertake periodic reconciliation of records of all properties to
establish correctness of the balances of PPE accounts; and (ii) consider
the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable
PPE information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to continue the compliance with the guidelines
outlined in Section 50 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to
ensure the fair presentation of the Construction-in-Progress account
balances in the Financial Statements; and (b) Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Engineer to continue proper coordination on timely
determination of the completion of infrastructure projects to
subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/ transfer to the
appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the
financial statements.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) continue in
monitoring the status of funds received by maintaining individual
subsidiary ledgers for all accounts and properly returning all unexpended
balances to the respective Source Agencies; (b) verify/reconcile the
account balances without supporting records and make adjusting entries,
as necessary; and (c) coordinate with the implementing offices on the
completion of the required liquidation/terminal report and to return to the
Source Agencies or remit to the National Treasury the balance of funds
transferred pursuant to item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 to settle the
long outstanding and non-moving accounts that can be used by the
government for other developmental purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a)
Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to continue updating
and reconciling the leave credits of employees of the Municipality; and (b)
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Budget
Officer to take coordinated actions to increase revenue collections and
allocate sufficient budgetary provisions to facilitate the recognition of
expense and liability accounts for employees’ benefits.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to observe full compliance with the disclosure requirements
for Prior Period Errors and Adjustments in the Notes to Financial
Statements as provided under Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to regularly conduct
examination on the books of accounts of business establishments by
assigning staff/personnel who are equipped with the necessary knowledge
and training in the examination of the books of accounts to determine the
truthfulness of the declared gross sales/receipts used in the computation of
business taxes paid and to collect the correct taxes as provided under
Section 171 of the RA 7160; (b) Business Permit and Licensing Officer
(BPLO) to fully enforce the submission of Sworn Statement of Gross
Receipts/Sales or copy of Certified Income Tax Return from taxpayers to
verify or assess the correct tax base to be used, as provided by Section
2C.04 (d) and (e) of the Revenue Code; and (c) BPLO and Municipal
Treasurer to cause recovery of the under collection of ₱43,080.65 arising
from undeclared gross receipts/Sales of the sampled business
establishments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Municipal
Planning and Development Officer to (i) avail the technical assistance of
Department of Human Settlements and Urban Development (DHSUD) in
updating its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) to come up with a
more effective and specific development goals; (ii) ensure the correlation
of the plans between CLUP, Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP),
Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) and Annual Investment
Plan (AIP) for a more focused implementation process; and (iii)
conduct a cost-benefit analysis comparing the rental and purchase
options for Heavy Equipment to make an informed decision that would
give the best benefit for the Municipality; (b) Local Development Council
to (i) diversify fund allocation to ensure that other critical sectors,
including social development, infrastructure development, environmental
management, and economic development, receive adequate funding to
promote comprehensive and sustainable growth within the Municipality;
and (ii) specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the AIP, and
always secure approval or authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan before
incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation
of projects described in generic terms; (c) Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to (i) explain/justify why the default mode of procurement which is
public bidding was not observed in the procurement of hauling services on
solid wastes; and (ii) observe the rules and regulations on procurement
process and procedures to ensure that the most advantageous prices will
be obtained for the government; and (d) Municipal Accountant to
strengthen review functions on the required documentations of transaction
to support the claims for payment before signing box A of Disbursement
Voucher.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to (i) implement
control measures on the submission of Daily Time Records (DTRs) and
Accomplishment Reports (ARs) of Job Order (JO) Personnel; and (ii)
validate the correctness of information of DTRs and ARs submitted by
JO before forwarding the same to Accounting Office; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) prepare the payrolls based on the actual services
rendered by employees as shown on the approved Daily Time Records
(DTRs) duly signed by both the employee and his/her supervisor, and
prudently check the accuracy of wages for each claimant; and (ii) ensure
completeness of supporting documents to substantiate the claims.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council (LDRRMC) headed by the Local Chief Executive continue to
adhere to the guidelines on the allocation, utilization and reporting of
calamity/disaster related programs/projects/activities pursuant to
Republic Act No. 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Act for LDRRMF and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC –
Municipal Budget Officer to (i) meticulously verify the existence of
appropriation prior to incurrence of obligation/expenditure for each
PPA; and (ii) desist from the practice of authorizing expenditure without
appropriation nor charging thereof to inappropriate PPAs; and (b)
Municipal Accountant to submit the DVs and other transaction
documents on a timely manner. Moreover, we recommended that the
Local School Board prepare the necessary request for
realignment/reprogram of funds and submit to Municipal Budget Office
to properly align the Special Education Fund expenditures to the
approved appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal
Point System (GFPS) through the GAD Focal Person to (i) ensure full
utilization of GAD funds by enhancing planning processes, particularly
in cost estimation and resource allocation; (ii) create a multi-year GAD
Agenda as basis of preparing the annual GAD Plan and Budget; (iii)
carefully plan the corresponding budget allocations of GAD PPAs by
preparing project breakdown of expenses before the approval of the plan
and budget; and (iv) conduct a review after three years and an end-term
evaluation to reflect on the status of accomplishment, variances, and
overall assessment of the implementation of the GAD agenda based on
the goals and desired results/outcomes.

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to fully implement the lawful order
of this Commission on the withholding of salaries and any amount due to
the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in
settlement of their respective accountabilities to avoid sanctions pursuant
to Rule XIII of the Revised Rules of Procedures of COA - Enforcement
and Monitoring of Decision.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) admonish their
duties and functions on the submission of financial transactions and
related reports as sanctioned by COA; (ii) submit immediately all
unsubmitted transactions and reports for CY 2024; and (iii) henceforth,
observe the timeliness of reportorial requirements of the Commission
particularly on the submission of financial transactions which is within
the first 10 days of the ensuing month; and (b) Municipal Accountant to
conduct proper review and ensure completeness of supporting documents
before signing box A of Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate full compliance
on the publicity and notification of the Municipality’s Projects, Programs,
and Activities (PPAs) by: (a) submitting to the Audit Team a list of all on-
going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the
year; (b) notifying the Audit Team within ten days after the award of
infrastructure projects that the appropriate project signboards and/or
public notices are already posted in the project sites; and (c) instructing the
Municipal Engineer to (i) provide proof of installation of signboards on the
three completed projects, otherwise cause the refund of the cost of
tarpaulin as indicated in the program of works; (ii) closely monitor the
installation of signboards/tarpaulins by the contractor/supplier and
recommend the immediate rectification had there been incomplete details
or erroneous inscription; and (iv) exercise close supervision and
monitoring on the implementation of PPAs to enable the preparation and
submission of the Monthly Monitoring Reports on projects’ status in a
timely manner.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman and BAC Secretary to prepare and
submit to the GPPB the Procurement Monitoring Report in printed and
electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each
semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare complete and accurate statement of account for
remittance of taxes/premiums/loans repayments deducted from
suppliers/contractors/ employees to the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and
PhilHealth to facilitate the intact and full remittance thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the immediate
settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of
those employees determined to be liable and strict adherence to the
accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing and
documentation of transactions to avoid further suspension or
disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary records for each road network, detailing roads and their components, to ensure accurate accounting of the Road Network account and facilitate reconciliation with physical count reports.
We also recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO):
Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components, along with a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Prepare and submit a comprehensive Report of Physical Count of Local Road Networks to the Municipal Accountant, using the format prescribed COA Circular 2015-008. This report will facilitate reconciliation and resolution of any discrepancies.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with complete descriptions and cost segregations of road components for all road projects to enhance record accuracy and financial reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Draw an adjusting journal entry to correct the misclassification of accounts by transferring the unexpended LDRRMF balance from the Trust Liability account to the Trust Liability – DRRM account in Trust Fund books;
Ensure that all expenses related to the STF for DRRM are properly closed to the Trust Liability - DRRM account at year-end, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRM Funds of LGUs as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and record all municipal transactions using the appropriate accounts as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs to ensure the faithful and correct presentation of financial statements, thereby providing reliable data to the users thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the Trust Fund book to record the unrecorded Debit/Credit Memos, deposits, disbursements and erroneous entry, totaling ₱604,127.42,
prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for SEF and all Trust Fund accounts and submit them to the Audit Team;
verify and reconcile the unaccounted discrepancies of ₱921,157.36 in the SEF and Trust Fund accounts, respectively, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries; and
prepare the required adjusting entry to reinstate the cash balance corresponding to the stale checks amounting to ₱429,007.28 and re-establish the associated payable account.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer obtain copies of the bank statements of the six bank accounts with a zero balance for the period January 1, 2023 to March 31, 2025, and submit them to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team within 15 days from receipt of this memorandum.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all the transactions of the municipality using the appropriate accounts provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs so that transactions are faithfully and correctly presented in the financial statements which will provide reliable data to the users thereof.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee:
Adopt a more realistic approach for revenue estimation by using historical data, trend analysis, and conservative assumptions;
Convene at least monthly to discuss and monitor the attainment of targeted income against actual receipts. Based on this review, adjust the cash disbursement program for the remaining months as necessary to prevent cash overdrafts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately reimburse the General Fund from the Special Trust Fund for the expenses totaling ₱2,771,997.80 that were incorrectly charged. This will restore the General Fund’s cash balance to its correct amount.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Department Heads, practice extreme measures to conserve the minimal resources of the LGU. This includes limiting the approval of disbursements to only the most basic, urgent and necessary expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate transfer of the LGSF – Support and Assistance Fund to the trust fund account of the LGU.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to record the transfer of this fund from the general fund books to the trust fund books.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are specific and implementation-ready to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
We also reiterated our CY 2023 audit recommendations with modifications, that the Municipal Engineer:
Conduct a detailed engineering of all projects before their inclusion in the AIP, which includes the preparation of the Program of Work with Detailed Estimates and plans/specifications;
Prepare the Program of Work with the Detailed Estimates and plans/specifications for the unexpended balances of the PPAs stated in generic terms (Table 18) to ensure their full implementation; and
Submit the Program of Works with the Detailed Estimates, plans/specifications and Purchase Request to the Bids and Awards Committee within the timeline specified in the AIP. Consider the time required for the procurement process and project implementation to ensure completion of all the projects within the allocated period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking DVs within 5 days from receipt of this memorandum, in order for the audit team to confirm the validity of the recorded transactions and utilization of the 20% Development Fund. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance for non-compliance thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal DRRM Council, and the Municipal DRRM Officer:
stop the utilization of LDRRM Fund for non-disaster risk reduction and management expenses; and
initiate the immediate return of the ₱2,000,000.00 borrowed from the Special Trust Fund to cover the fund deficiency of the General Fund. This action is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking DV within 5 days from receipt of this memorandum, in order for the audit team to confirm the validity of the recorded transaction and the utilization of the 5% DRRM fund. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance for non-compliance thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, GAD Executive Committee, GAD Technical Working Group and GAD Focal Person strictly adhere to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended, to ensure that all programs, projects, and activities are implemented, thereby achieving the intended objectives of the attributed funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Immediately compute the correct tax liability for each affected employee and official, encompassing the previously excluded CNA and Anniversary Bonus. The corresponding under withheld amount should be reflected in a payroll adjustment and remitted to the BIR as soon as possible, along with any applicable penalties or interest; and
Ensure accurate differentiation between taxable and non-taxable income in accordance with BIR regulations and regularly review payroll tax computation to avoid penalties and interest due to under-withholding.
We also recommended that the management, thru the Municipal Accountant, allow regular training for relevant staff on current tax laws and BIR pronouncements to ensure ongoing compliance and prevent future under withholding.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the immediate refund of the ₱22,600.45 interest paid due to the delayed remittance of PhilHealth contributions.
We also recommended that the Accounting Unit:
Conduct a detailed review of the LGU employees’ deductions against the remittances made to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG Fund for premium contributions and loan amortization to reconcile the identified over-remittances totaling ₱170,658.82; and
Regularly review the schedule of premium remittance and loan repayments as deducted from personnel payroll to prevent under/over remittance of premium contributions and loan amortizations.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant meticulously review the payroll deductions recorded in the Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth, and Due to Pag-IBIG subsidiary ledgers. These records should be reconciled with the actual amounts payable to each agency before processing payment to ensure accurate and complete remittance of all contributions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reconcile the amounts of recorded PPE accounts and the lapsing schedule;

Provide an allowance for depreciation on the identified depreciable asset and/or request for the authority from COA to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability pursuant to existing COA rules and regulations; and

Maintain PPE Ledger Cards for all types of PPE accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct one-time cleansing of PPE Accounts in coordination with the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant, to come up with a reliable PPE balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to close the Accumulated Depreciation – Buildings amounting to P332,771.30 to Government Equity account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officials to do the following courses of action:

a)	The Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified listing of taxpayers and their respective tax due and furnish a copy to the Municipal Account; and

b) The Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to continuously monitor the implementation of PPAs funded by the NTF-ELCAC, and ensure completion thereof according to approved target date of completion.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the accomplishment of the contractor and submit the approved time extension, if any;

Municipal Accountant to impose upon the contractor the liquidated damages if the approved Time Extensions together with the required supporting documents, as verified by the Municipal Engineer, will not be submitted immediately; and

MPDC to ensure that all development projects funded under 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk reduction and management Programs, Projects, and Activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the chairman of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Council provide storage facility for the relief goods and other DRR equipment to ensure that the evacuation center is at all times ready to accommodate evacuees during disaster; and, direct the MSWDO to monitor the storage of relief goods to avoid spoilage and waste of government resources.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator and MPDC to:

Take appropriate measures so that the transport terminal be of use for its intended purpose as soon as possible; and,

Henceforth, ensure that programs/projects of the LGU are well-planned so as not to waste government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following: 

The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements thru e-Payments are based on a duly approved DVs/Payrolls; and

The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Daily Report of e-Payments from the Agency Account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to intensify collection to recover government funds and to take appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books; and

If warranted, request for the authority to write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a)	GSO, through the disposal committee, to immediately conduct disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386;

GSO to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of properly accomplished IIRUP or the Report of Waste Materials duly acted upon together with a copy of the Official Receipt, if disposal is for value received; and

Municipal Accountant to derecognize from the book of accounts all disposed unserviceable properties and recognize gain/loss on disposal, otherwise reclassify the pertinent PPE assets to Other Assets account pending the disposal to reflect the true value of its PPE accounts.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with each other to effect the immediate return of the unexpended/excess balances of the same to the source agencies concerned after the implementation of each project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Assign personnel in the slaughterhouse who shall be responsible for its operations;

Provide for the sewerage, water system and electrification of the slaughterhouse; and

Ensure immediate operation of the project for the efficient and effective utilization of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from approving transactions for the grant of allowances to Municipal Officials and Employees without any law passed by the Congress authorizing such allowance/benefits.

We further recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members ensure that all ordinances shall not contravene any existing law passed by the National Government.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:

Require the Municipal Vice-mayor, as the Presiding Officer of the SB to facilitate the immediate issuance of a local revenue ordinance for the adoption of the revised rates on fees and charges of the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of certificate of titles in favor of the municipality.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified Persons Liable in the Notice suspension or the Notice of Disallowance to settle their audit suspension or disallowance.

Require the identified persons responsible/liable in the Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance to comply/settle the NS or ND.

Require the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the Notices to the Persons Responsible/Liable for their information on their unsettled suspension/ disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned payees to refund the amount received as Calamity Assistance and 14th Month Pay totaling P2,640,947.00 for the said payments have no legal basis. Henceforth, strictly observe pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and RA 6758 to avoid disallowance of disbursements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the implementation of the audit recommendations towards improved financial operations and governance. Henceforth, require all concerned to strictly observe existing laws, accounting and auditing rules and regulations to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned payees to refund the allowance/honoraria received without legal basis. Henceforth, strictly observe COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 30, 2013, Items 5.0 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and Section 101 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the LGU officials concerned be required to submit the documentary requirements for payment of honoraria and overtime services specified in Items 5.7 and 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the concerned to regular officials and employees, and job order/contractual personnel be required to refund the amount received for additional year-end benefits which are bereft of legal basis. Henceforth, stop the grant of such benefits in the absence of any legal or statutory basis.

1.7 	Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the Cash – Local Treasury subsidiary ledger and cashbook on a monthly basis, giving priority to the (P50.26) variance which remained unreconciled as of December 31, 2024;
Municipal Accountant record in the books the net book reconciling items amounting to P51,466.09; and
Municipal Treasurer record in his cashbook the net reconciling items amounting to (P4,222.11).

2.15 	Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer:
prepare immediately the Report of Collections and Deposits after receipt of the collections from the Deputized Barangay Treasurers, and deposit the same with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day; and
calculate the cashbook daily balance and reconcile the same with the cash on hand to determine any undeposited collection in a timely manner.

2.15 	Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer:
prepare immediately the Report of Collections and Deposits after receipt of the collections from the Deputized Barangay Treasurers, and deposit the same with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day; and
calculate the cashbook daily balance and reconcile the same with the cash on hand to determine any undeposited collection in a timely manner.

4.19 	Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant:
complete the “one-time cleansing” of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) by following the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs under Section 7.0 and the derecognition procedures for such properties under Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
follow the reconciliation procedures under Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish comparable and verifiable information between the accounting records and the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, and proceed with the disposition and derecognition procedures under Sections 7.0 and 8.0; and
submit to COA the Request for Relief from Property Accountability of those non-existing/missing properties, if any, and the Request for Derecognition of those non-existing/missing PPEs, which accountability/responsibility could not be pinpointed after investigation.

5.10 	Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation, and the Management agreed that the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant ensure that all payrolls for Job Order personnel are duly supported with complete documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular 2012-001, with particular emphasis on accomplishment reports and certificates of appearance.

6.9 	Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer collect, on or before April 30, 2025, from Barangays Dakudao, Datu Balong, Libuton, and Pinamuno the amount of ₱500,000.00, which was erroneously transferred as a “subsidy” without Sanggunian authorization, through the simultaneous exchange of the CY 2025 subsidy check and the barangay repayment check; otherwise, the amount shall be disallowed in audit.; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer diligently check the documents supporting the disbursement voucher and check, prior to making their respective certifications.

7.8 	Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Treasurer submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the necessary supporting documents, to the Audit Team to facilitate the request for inspection by COA Regional Office No. XI;
Inventory and Disposal Committee undertake the immediate disposal procedures of unserviceable properties within CY 2025 to avoid possible loss due to further deterioration and devaluation of said properties; and
Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Vouchers for the dropping of the affected properties from the books upon receipt of the approved IIRUP following the disposal made of the unserviceable properties.

8.12 Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and issue the thirteen (13) units of equipment to the concerned offices through the accomplishment of the Property Acknowledgement Receipt, taking into consideration their existing conditions prior to issuance.

9.11. Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator make an assessment of the unoccupied housing units and develop an action plan to fill them, taking into consideration the prioritization and allocation criteria stipulated in the MOA; and
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the development and promulgation of clear and comprehensive guidelines for housing management, including defining a reasonable timeframe for beneficiaries to occupy their allotted housing units.

10.9 	Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer ensure the completion and operational readiness of the Cacao Processing Facility and Multi-Purpose Building for RIC in CY 2025, to enable the intended beneficiaries to fully utilize the facility and maximize its benefits, and to fulfill the Local Chief Executive’s responsibility of ensuring efficiency, economy, and effectiveness in the Municipality’s operations.

11.32 	Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
MDRRMO exercise due diligence and prudence in utilizing the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund by avoiding expenditures on leisure-related activities;
Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the travel policies outlined in Executive Order No. 77; and
MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant submit, on or before April 30, 2025, the DILG authorization on the benchmarking activity conducted in Tagbilaran City, Bohol and Lapu-Lapu City, Cebu.

1.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to derecognize the tangible items that no longer meet the capitalization threshold recorded as PPE to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in accordance with the above COA Circulars.

2.8. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the adjusting entries in the books of the accounts to record the completed projects with their corresponding accumulated depreciation to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.
3.9. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the adjusting entries to correct the misclassified asset accounts with its corresponding accumulated depreciation.

4.15. We recommended that the:
4.15.1 LDRRM Officer, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Treasurer determine and recognize in the books, the actual amount of the unexpended balances of the QRF and Mitigation – MOOE at the end of each year upon closing of the accounts;
4.15.2 Municipal Treasurer transfers the determined amount from the LDRRMF-General Fund to the Special Trust Fund at year-end or at the earliest possible time in the following year; and
4.15.3 Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to record the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2021 to 2024 totaling ₱4,689,729.70 and record the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in future years in the pertinent calendar year of incurrence.
5.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the adjusting entries to correct the double recording of the procured dump truck and the under-provision of depreciation expenses.

6.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions, reconcile accounts, and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

7.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the balances recorded under Accounts Payable to their appropriate accounts as defined in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 or the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the actual liabilities of the Municipality.
8.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record semi-expendable properties in the proper accounts to avoid the presentation of misleading information in the Financial Statements.

9.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant undertake necessary procedures to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, the accounts payable in the amount of ₱1,156,947.64, which has been outstanding for more than three years and have no claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by perfected contracts.

10.8. We recommended that the:
10.8.1 SIWSMDO prepare and forward a complete statement of water meter reading from each consumer, and submit the same to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording in the books of accounts; and
10.8.2 Municipal Accountant record Waterworks System Fees and Accounts Receivable using the accrual basis, prepare a subsidiary ledger, and reconcile monthly with the MTO records.

11.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to use the appropriate accounts in recording similar transactions and make the adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

12.9. We recommended that the:
12.9.1. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the differences between their respective records on a periodic basis to arrive at the correct real property tax and special education tax collectible balances; and
12.9.2. Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry to fairly present the actual tax collectible on the Statement of Financial Position based on the certified list of tax delinquencies prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

13.10. We recommended that the:
13.10.1. Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the recognition of prior period income in the current year, and request information/data/report from the MT for year-end undeposited collections which will only be forwarded the following year; and
13.10.2. Municipal Treasurer report all collections for the year even including those due for deposit in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant discontinue the practice of derecognizing the Due to NGA account without the submission of liquidation reports or Fund Utilization Reports (FURs) to the Audit Team and Source Agency.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Engineer to submit the copies of the requests for time extensions of the contractors of the noted ELCAC projects and the approvals thereof to the Audit Team within five days from receipt of this memorandum;

the Municipal Engineer to strictly adhere to the project duration stipulated per contract and exercise diligence in the inspection of completed projects immediately after the reported date of completion to determine whether projects were completed on time, taking considerations on Variation Orders or Time Extension, if any, and make sure that all materials and prescribed procedures conform with the Program of Work, Detailed Estimates and Plan; and the Municipal Accountant to impose Liquidated Damages, equal to one-tenth (1/10) of one percent (1%) of the cost of the unfinished portion of the project for each day of delay.

Also, to protect the interest of the government and to prevent further loss and wastage of government funds and resources, we recommended that the following measures be undertaken for future projects of similar in nature:

Strictly monitor implementation of all projects, whether contracts for infrastructure, goods or consulting services, in accordance with the approved plans and specifications within the time frame specified in the contract agreement; and 

Identify contracts which require appropriate management actions. If so, impose liquidated damages and/or administrative sanctions particularly termination of contracts and recommend blacklisting of contractors for failure to comply with their obligations in accordance with the provisions of Sections 68 and 69, and Appendix 17 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

submit the lacking JEVs within 5 days from receipt of the memorandum, to confirm the validity of the recorded transactions. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance for non-compliance thereof; and

instruct the personnel in charge in the submission of the JEVs to cross check all approved JEVs based on the Check Disbursement Journals and ensure that all DVs in the Journals are transmitted to the Audit Team monthly, so that all recorded transactions in the books of the municipality have sufficient basis.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Record the cost of various items for LGU Offices, spare parts for motor vehicles, and food items for PNP personnel and other activities to the appropriate asset and expense accounts, in accordance with The Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units, rather than recording the entire amount to the Food Supplies Expense account; and

Henceforth, record all the transactions of the municipality using the proper accounts provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs so that transactions are faithfully and correctly presented in the financial statements to provide the users thereof of reliable data/information.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatements of the CIP – Buildings and Other Structures, Accounts Payable and Miscellaneous Income accounts; and

henceforth, disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide the users thereof of relevant and reliable information about the LGU’s operation.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

record the unissued relief goods and medical supplies to the Welfare goods food for distribution and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies accounts, respectively; and

stop the practice of recording immediately to expense accounts the inventories received by the municipality. Henceforth, record subsequent procurements of inventories as an asset under the appropriate inventory account and recognize the related expenses based on the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, supported by duly approved RIS, and Distribution List.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal Development Council, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator: 

submit a written justification of the LGU’s action of allowing the incurrence of ineligible expenditures charged against the 20% Development Fund;

Stop utilizing the 20% Development Fund for expenditures that are expressly not allowed in Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1; and

identify and implement as priority projects only those which are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those which are essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: A similar observation is contained in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal DRMM Officer:

Stop the distribution of relief goods to non-disaster victims and strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121 to avoid committing prohibited acts in the implementation, distribution, and utilization of resources out of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund; 

Determine the quantity of goods to be procured based on the number of victims and their basic needs; and

Include as one of the conditions in the procurement of relief goods, the expiration dates at least six months from the delivery date to avoid spoilage or wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: None
None
