observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations to the Management:

Secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan through a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation or Memorandum of Agreement to transfer PPEs used by other agencies;

The OIC – Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize from the books of the agency the PPE items that will be transferred to other agencies and to adjust the erroneous entries made on PPE and depreciation accounts; and



Ensure the full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” and undertake all procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements. It is understood that in the implementation of the One-time cleansing, the OIC - Municipal Accountant must record immediately and appropriately all the identified unrecorded PPE items; and facilitate reconciliation of PPE records with the RPCPPE and records maintained by the GSO to determine unrecorded properties and other differences for further validation and subsequent correction in records maintained. Thereafter, submit a report on the results of the reconciliation made for review and verification.

Finally, we recommended requiring all the department heads to furnish immediately to the Accounting Offices document/s relative to any receipt of PPE items from other government or private offices for proper and immediate recording thereof.
observations.reason:
The preparation of the Deed of Donation is still on going. Hence, no adjusting entries were made yet to derecognize the PPE items.
However, the appropriate adjusting entries for depreciation were already made.

The implementation of One-Time Cleansing of PPEs is still on-going. The   assigned personnel have already completed their training on the One-Time Cleansing of PPE conducted by the COA Regional Office and committed to implement the same in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

Submit a written justification for the said deficiency; and 

Ensure that all accountable officers (AOs) are bonded based on the correct cash/property accountability of each AO.

We recommended that the Management direct the offices concerned to ensure that in the succeeding similar transactions, proper coordination with the barangay officials and proper monitoring of project implementation will be made to avoid the delays in utilization of funds received by the LGU. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should also ensure that complete and proper documentation will be observed so that the transaction’s propriety will be established. 

We also recommended that the management submit a written justification for the said deficiencies.

We recommended and the Management agreed to hasten the implementation of the three development projects mentioned. Direct the officials concerned to review all the on-going and to be undertaken projects by the Municipality and come up with concrete action to address the deficiencies noted. It is highly enjoined that all offices concerned will have a holistic and documented plan on how to efficiently implement the agency’s various projects and activities taking into consideration the availability of its personnel.

Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) closely monitor and evaluate the status of the implementation of projects.

We recommended that the Management direct the Accounting and Treasury Departments to expedite the reconciliation process and promptly remit the outstanding tax amount to the BIR as required by relevant rules and regulations. It is important to ensure that future taxes withheld are remitted on time to avoid any penalties imposed by the law.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain accurate subsidiary records of individual suppliers, contractors, and employees. These records will support the Due to BIR account and enable regular monitoring and reconciliation of balances.

We recommended that the Management direct:

The Payroll-in-charge to correct the current practice of withholding taxes. Also, it is enjoined that she undergoes thorough training and stays updated on current regulations to accurately compute applicable withholding taxes. 

The Accounting Department to establish a monitoring system to check whether or not correct and appropriate withholding of taxes from employees and observance of the BIR regulations is observed.

We recommended that the Management direct the persons responsible to fast-track the reconciliation to zero out the balance in the said inter-agency payables. Amounts needed to be remitted/refunded shall be immediately remitted to the respective agencies or refunded to the identified employees/persons, whichever is applicable. 

We further recommended that the agency officials concerned require employees to submit Pag-ibig and Philhealth Numbers first before withholding any mandatory contributions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

GFPS officials and other offices concerned to formulate more responsive PPAs to address GAD issues and concerns of the Municipality. The cost of PPAs that partly address gender issues will be included in the GPB by way of attribution through the use of the HGDG tool; and 

Municipal Planning Office, GAD Focal person and other offices concerned to continue updating, completing and maintaining a more functional GAD Database which shall aid in gender analysis for the identification of gender issues and the cause/causes, to be addressed by the agency; the agency’s GAD database.

We recommended the following to the Management:

Avoid giving additional cash advances to officers and employees who have not yet settled their previous cash advances. Please strictly follow the guidelines provided under Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid recurring noncompliance.

Take appropriate action against accountable officers who have long outstanding cash advances, especially those who are still connected with the agency. Encourage them to immediately settle their accounts according to the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 97-002.

Implement an efficient and effective control procedure in the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances. Make sure that the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, are followed.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identifies items that qualify for write-off and expedite the request, supported with the required documents as outlined in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit the paid DVs on a daily or weekly basis, whichever is appropriate, to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely processing thereof and submission to the COA Office.	

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the mentioned lacking DVs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office continuously coordinate with its software provider to resolve the inconsistencies found in various PPE accounts and ensure that the amounts presented in the financial statements accurately reflect the balances in its supporting schedules.
We recommended Management to ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the timely implementation of the projects;

We recommended that Management evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of the accounts with negative balances, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances. Further, ensure accuracy in the recording of transactions to their appropriate SL accounts in order to avoid payment of unnecessary penalties and surcharges to various government agencies.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of accounts with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances.
Liquidate the cash advance for local travel within 30 days after return to his official station.

Require the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee who will conduct physical inventory of all its Property, Plant and Equipment together with the General Services Office (MGSO)/ Municipal Treasurer on a specific time table;

The Head of the Agency should cause the issuance of demand letters, thru the initiative of the Municipal Accountant.  If after two (2) notices these accountable officers employees still fail to liquidate, the matter should be brought to the attention of the legal officer for proper disposition, copy furnished the Commission on Audit.

The Municipal Mayor, being primarily responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the municipality, and being the approving officer, should enforce the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

Immediately issue corresponding official receipts (AF 51) and prepare the RCD for the receipt of checks pertaining to withheld taxes and record such collection in the cashbook. The OR and RCD shall be the basis of the supporting documents to be attached by the Municipal Accountant upon recording the transactions.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books all valid book reconciling items noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2023, and furnish this office copies of corresponding JEVs recording the adjustments made to the affected accounts. Received bank transfers undergoing verification can be temporarily recorded under Deferred Credits/Unearned Income accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Development Council, and the Local Finance Committee to:

observations.managementAction: Management prepares periodic reports on the implementation status of infrastructure projects and other PPAs under the 20% DF, highlighting issues affecting non-implementation to aid timely decision-making.

observations.managementAction: Management prepares periodic reports on the implementation status of infrastructure projects and other PPAs under the 20% DF, highlighting issues affecting non-implementation to aid timely decision-making.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipality stop charging fuel expenses to the LDRRMF unless the purpose/s support disaster risk management activities as provided under NDRRMCDBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other related issuances to ensure that the objectives set forth in the allocation of the LDRRMF are achieved thru proper usage thereof.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order was issued by the LCE on the strict charging of fuel which must be limited to the activities related to the four thematic areas of the disaster risk reduction and management. Such Order was strictly observed as recommended in the previous audit observations.

observations.recommendation: Moreover, ensure that all trips are supported by trip tickets with all necessary information specified and properly filled up, together with other supporting documents to ensure that the activity is chargeable against the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: All travels and trips are provided with trip tickets and other necessary documents as attachment to every travel made.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of processing payment of fuel expenses incurred for unauthorized travel, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Payment of fuel expenses are strictly for MDRRMO related activities only and not for any other purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to properly deduct withholding taxes on additional compensation received by its officers and employees pursuant to our tax laws. Collect from the recipients of the said allowances the corresponding tax due to the national government.
observations.managementAction: Facilitate for the refund of the amounts intended for taxes and remit the same to the BIR on/before the deadline for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office set-up the related impairment loss on long outstanding Loans Receivable – Others based on the data provided by the PESO.
observations.managementAction: Set-up the related impairment loss on long outstanding Loans Receivable – Others based on the data provided by the PESO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended in that: (a) the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Engineer to properly investigate and exhaust the available remedy to reconcile the unsupported and prior years’ balances of the CIP account. (b) Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of their classified completed projects from the time of completion until December 31, 2023, and subsequently ensure the reclassification of all completed projects in the CIP account to their appropriate asset accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already made and the Construction in Progress accounts were already classified and accounts were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Assessor, verify their historical data or records that could shed light on the acquisition costs of the properties and, if available, make an assessment so that the necessary adjusting entries shall be made to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and 17.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Assessor, already reconciled their historical records and adjusting entries were already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present the current portion and non-current portion of its Loans Payable in the financial statements, and disclose pertinent information in the Notes to the Financial Statements, to provide its users fair presentation and reliable assessment as far as liquidity and solvency are concerned.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already made and the financial statements, and disclose pertinent information in the Notes to the Financial Statements, to provide its users fair presentation and reliable assessment as far as liquidity and solvency are concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant determine the actual amount of the unexpended balances of the QRF and Mitigation-MOOE and make the necessary adjusting entries on the affected accounts; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer transfer the determined amount from the LDRRMF-General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: (a) The LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant coordinated and reconciled the balances and (b) the Municipal Treasurer ensured that there will be an actual transfer before implementing the PPAs under the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the net cash shortage of the erring official in the books of accounts as receivable pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 dated July 9, 2013, which prescribed the rules and regulations on shortages and draw adjusting journal entries of the affected accounts to reflect the correct balances reported in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the remaining shortage as receivable in the books of accounts in accordance with COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 dated July 9, 2013. The necessary adjusting journal entries of the affected accounts were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the discrepancy and regularly conduct monthly and quarterly reconciliations of their records. Moreover, due diligence should be taken by the concerned offices in the recording and the preparation of reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer update regularly his cashbooks by recording all disbursements daily and reconciling the balance with the accounting records before the end of the month, and both the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant make corrections and adjustments should there be a difference between these two records and adjust the unrecorded cash in bank balances to reflect/establish correct balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant made the necessary corrections and reconciled the records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) The Municipal Accounting Office recognize first as inventory all the procured supplies of the LGU and only be recorded as expense upon issuance; (b) maintain SLC for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items and the GSO to consolidate issuances supported with RIS on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the NGAS Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the personnel-in-charge of the preparation and submission of the required supporting documents for the cash advance for Confidential Funds submit these attachments as support to the DV for the grant of the cash advance for Confidential Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) the Municipal Budget Office properly monitors the SAAOB of the 20% DF, locate and establish the actual unaccounted balance, and prepared a separate plan; (b) upon establishment of the unaccounted balance, realign/reprogram the said amount for other viable developmental projects of the Municipality with the approval of the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to be adopted by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Office prepared the SAAOB of the continuing 20% appropriation for the period and located the unaccounted balance and submitted a copy to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer monitor the allotments and obligations of each fund, do not charge expenses without the allotted budget, and regularly monitor the spending against a specific appropriation to avoid the incurrence of expenses above the budget. Also, ensure that any augmentations and realignments of appropriation are fully supported with proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer monitored the allotments and obligations and ensured that expenditures were within approved budgets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer not release goods for stockpiling to ineligible beneficiaries and remind them that such items will only be released during emergency cases or already stored for a long time but before their expiration dates, supported with proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer released goods only to eligible beneficiaries and ensured proper documentation for storage and releases.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil, and lubricants are duly supported with filled-up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation of the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption; and (b) direct the concerned personnel to prepare and submit monthly the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis, verification and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: The GSO prepared monthly reports such as Monthly Reports of Fuel and Oil Consumption and Monthly Reports of Official Travel, and driver’s trip tickets were submitted.

observations.recommendation:

(ii) direct the current Municipal Accountant to ensure that only lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during a fiscal year shall be recognized or taken up in the accounts of that year. 

The LCE should also investigate the recurrence of infractions, thereafter, avail any legal remedy thereto appertaining, and subject the erring officers to disciplinary actions, if warranted.

observations.recommendation:

(a) obtain the required authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to justify the incurrence of expenditure amounting to ₱5,278,631.55; and 

(ii) henceforth, secure the approval/authority of the SB before implementing any projects that is not included in the appropriation ordinance; 

(b) instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Council to 

(i) meticulously prepare the Annual Investment Program and only include developmental projects that could practically or feasibly be carried out within the budget period; and 

(ii) ensure that only eligible programs/ projects/activities are to be financed by the 20% Development Fund; 

(c) require the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures against the current appropriation; 

(d) instruct the Bids and Award Committee to ensure compliance with the RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR); and 

(d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to 

(i) conduct proper review of supporting documents of Payrolls/Disbursement Vouchers to ensure completeness of the transactions; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare complete and accurate statement of account for remittance of taxes/premiums/loans repayments deducted from suppliers/ contractors/employees to BIR, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to facilitate the intact remittance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was compliant with the rules and regulations regarding the withholding of the taxes, premiums and loan repayments during CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and strict adherence to the accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned officials to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: No refund has been made to settle suspensions/ disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management take full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleaning of PPE account balances of the LGU.

Further, require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the inventory-taking so that the purpose of COA Circular 2020-006 will be achieved.

We recommend that the municipal officials exercise fiscal restraint in the disbursement of funds and stop the practice of utilizing the trust fund for current operating expenditures and utilize it for the purpose for which it was created or funds was received.

Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to limit the disbursement of funds to valid obligations only as appropriated in the Approved Annual Budget to avoid the incurrence of any cash overdraft at year end.

Furthermore, adhere to the provisions of Section 337 of RA 7160, otherwise all those found responsible in the incurrence of the cash overdraft will be held personally liable pursuant to Section 158 of GAAM Volume I.

In view thereof, we recommend that management initiate the process of closing the account with FCB and transfer the remaining funds to the authorized government depository banks maintained by the municipality in compliance with DOF Circular No. 01-2017 to avoid penalties due to dormancy.

We recommend that moving forward, the Municipal Accountant maintain and submit subsidiary ledgers that show the complete details and breakdown of the account balances reflected in the financial statements. This also facilitates the financial audit review conducted by the Commission on Audit.

We recommend that management submit justification of the non-creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommend that the management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human Resource Management Office and the Internal Audit Services to conduct personnel audit, review, assessment and evaluation of the existing manpower complement to prepare an updated organizational structure/pattern, remove excessive and unnecessary JOs/COS workers who are performing redundant functions with other JO/COS workers and regular plantilla personnel and other activities not related nor aligned with the mandates of the municipality and their assigned department/offices.

We recommend that management submit justification of the non-creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommend that the management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.

We recommend that the Municipal officials exercise fiscal restraint in the disbursement of funds and stop the practice of utilizing the trust fund for current operating expenditures and utilize it for the purpose for which it was created or funds was receive.

Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to limit the disbursement of funds to valid obligations only as appropriated in the Approved Annual Budget to avoid the incurrence of any cash overdraft at year end.

Furthermore, strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 337 of RA 7160, otherwise all those found responsible in the incurrence of the cash overdraft will be held personally liable pursuant to Section 158 of GAAM Volume I.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation letters to different government agencies and private persons identified as debtors and/or demand payment thereof.

Henceforth, request write off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 and Section 36 of PD 1445.

We further recommend for a regular review of all account balances so that dormant accounts can immediately be identified and acted upon.

We recommend that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies, remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project and/or effect any adjustments, if warranted, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency and can be used for other projects of the LGU.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the registration and transfer of title of the land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the municipality in compliance with Section 39 of PD No.1445.

Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor have to reconcile their records of the land owned by the LGU.

We recommend that management shift to a computerized accounting system and install the e-NGAS software to ensure correct accounting records and financial statements.

We also recommend that in the meantime, the Municipal Accountant have to carefully review the accounting records the accounting staff prepared.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the BAC or a committee responsible for the accreditation of NGOs/POs in recovering the related supporting documents to these fund transfers. See to it that the documentary requirements for release of funds to NGOs/POs as specified in COA Circular 2012-001 be complied before transfer is made.

We recommend the municipality and all concerned personnel to observe and comply the requirements and procedures as established in the GAM Volume I and COA Circular 2012-003.

We recommend that strict adherence to the rules and procedures concerning the processing, releasing, and recording of cash advances be observed by the Treasurer and Accountant.

Further, we recommend the municipality to submit an explanation on the above-mentioned deficiencies/ irregularities to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.

In view thereof, we recommend that the remaining funds shall not be released and shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for the other priority programs of the government.

We recommend:

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts.

Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.

Adopt and always observe austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.

Exercise fully the taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to 150 of R. A. 7160 in order to earn more revenue necessary to augment the funding for the operation of the municipality.  Otherwise, implement austerity measures to reduce operating expenses and utilize internal revenue fund for developmental projects that would be beneficial to the constituents.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested for an additional staff to handle the inventory of PPEs and maintain the RPLC. Verifications from the donors of the donated equipment are still to be conducted. Verification of the submitted documents from the MGSO disclosed that the cost information for some donated equipment are still to be conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Head of the Human Resources Management Office:
Limit the hiring of JOs only to the most necessary and only to complement the existing workforce of the LGU; Issue written guidelines on the standard procedures to be followed in the hiring of JOs to ensure professional and impartial selection process; Revise the format of JO contracts to indicate specific tasks and other necessary provisions; Ascertain that the rates indicated in the payroll is the same as the rate shown in the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant:
Submit documents such as RSMI with RIS and/or Distribution Sheets to substantiate the issuances/consumption/utilization of the Accountable Forms, Plate and Stickers, Food Supplies Inventory, Fuel Oil and Lubricant and Agricultural and Marine Supplies; Maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for every stock number and reconcile these with the Stock Cards of the GSO and the RPCI of the Inventory Committee. We also recommended that the MGSO: Prepare and submit the SSMI duly supported with RIS weekly to the Accounting Office for recording of issuances of inventory items; We further recommended that the Municipal Health Officer shall prepare a report on the issuances of Drugs and Medicines in the RHU per Fund Source and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the officers and employees concerned to:
Prepare a Conditional Contract to Sell with each Project Beneficiary or Lot Buyers with the authority of the Sangguniang Bayan, to protect the interest of the Municipality; Require the Municipal Treasurer to collect monthly the amortization from each Lot Buyers and remit the accumulated proceeds to NHA immediately in the succeeding month as required in the MOA; Will execute a Housing Lot Loan Agreement between the Lot Buyers or Project Beneficiaries with the authority of the Sangguniang Bayan, to clearly outline the parties’ obligations, rights and duties.
observations.managementAction: Paid the amount of P2.7M from the payments of the beneficiaries. The NHA approved the request for term extension to another 5 years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MGSO ensure: all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality should bear government red plates and should be marked “For Official Use Only”; prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Monthly Report of Official Travel.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied. For security reason some vehicles don’t bear the red plate. Monthly Reports of Official Travel were not prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC, Municipal Accountant and MGSO ensure that the Index-Based Pricing Scheme is adopted, including the specific procedures required under the Appendix 22 of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184. More specifically, we recommend to:
Indicate in the ITB among others the indicative volume to be procured for bidding purposes only; Indicate in the Supplier’s bid price whether a discount was given and/or a delivery cost was included; Include in the perfected contract provision that an index-pricing scheme was applied, the manner of payment, the time of delivery, the required volume for each delivery and other specific procedures prescribed in the abovementioned Guidelines; Ensure that the amount payable to the Supplier should be equivalent to the unit/volume actually delivered in a specific period of time, with a consideration to the weekly price adjustments, the discounts given and the delivery cost; Require the Supplier to provide a detailed price adjustment in each delivery, together with the certification under oath that the price adjustments were the reported and duly posted at DOE’s website for products; Ensure that the LGU’s end-user or acceptance team should maintain records for the unit/volume of diesel actually delivered and the remaining allowable unit/volume to be ordered, including the accounting of its equivalent amount or total cost.

We recommended that the erring accountable officers be directed to carry out full turn-over of unremitted collections and their consequent intact deposit by the Municipal Treasurer to the agency’s depository banks.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office continuously coordinate with its software provider to resolve the inconsistencies found in various PPE accounts and ensure that the amounts presented in the financial statements accurately reflect the balances in its supporting schedules.
observations.reason: Resolving the inconsistencies in various PPE accounts in coordination with the software  provider is still on going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

Direct the GSO and the MAO must establish an effective and practical system that would ensure the swift and regular reconciliation of records of both offices about the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment;

Conduct a complete physical count of all properties; and

3. Direct the GSO to correctly provide all the necessary data required in the RPCPPE for proper identification of all PPE items.
observations.reason: PPE records of GSO and MAO are not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:

Direct Municipal Accountant to look into the details of the unspent balances about fund transfers taken up as liability in the General Fund and Trust Fund books and facilitate the return to the source agency or remittance to the National Treasury or transfer to the General Fund of the Agency, whichever is appropriate, and make to the necessary adjustments in the books immediately;

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with and assist the Municipal Accountant in verifying whether the projects/programs associated with the funds received were already implemented and to secure the necessary documents to facilitate the recording of adjustments in the books; and

Ensure proper coordination between the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office in the future project/program implementation toward the funds which may be received from NGAs.

We recommended that management:

Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents in terms of PPAs to be implemented under 20% LGDF.
observations.reason: Three development projects under 20% were not completed during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of the accounts with negative balances, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances. Further, ensure accuracy in the recording of transactions to their appropriate SL accounts in order to avoid payment of unnecessary penalties and surcharges to various government agencies.
observations.reason: Some subsidiary ledgers of the accounts have still negative balance.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.
observations.reason: Restated in the Part II of this year’s Annual Audit Report. Refer to Audit finding no. 1 (3).

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough evaluation/ reconciliation of the journal entries made to determine the causes of the discrepancy. Likewise, effect the necessary adjustments to clear the reciprocal accounts of possible erroneous entries and/or omissions made.
observations.reason: As at December 31, 2023, the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds have a difference of P1,290,440.38 and there was no disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements relative thereto.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the return of the unutilized balances to various source agencies and constantly monitor and review the composition of the account to avoid dormancy of the balances.
observations.reason:
Restated in the Part II of this year’s Annual Audit Report.
Refer to Audit finding no. 4 (1).

observations.recommendation:

Include those which are to be implemented by the Municipality, whether infrastructure projects or other form of PPAs so as to facilitate prompt monitoring and validation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The report submitted included only infrastructure projects implemented or will be implemented by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of accounts with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances.
observations.reason: Some subsidiary ledgers of the accounts have still negative balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:

Enforce collection of the liquidated damages from the supplier if no document to prove the extensions of delivery dates

Submit written justification/explanation on the following:

deficiencies noted on the documents related to extension of deliveries;

why the Municipality continuously granted extensions?

despite the BAC resolution’s recommendation on the imposition of liquidated damages, why were there no effort exerted to collect such?
observations.reason: The present management is not aware of the transaction and have no copy of the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert/enforce efforts to collect these collectibles so that whatever collection can be used for the operation of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should determine the collectivity of these accounts so that proper adjustments could be made in order not to overstate the receivable balances as at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Partial liquidations were already made by the recipients and constant follow-ups are still to be done to facilitate the processing of liquidation

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following:

Regularly send confirmation/ demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures; and

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts.

This LGU is reminded further of the consequences provided for in section 9.0 of the same COA Circular 2012-004, as follows:

“9.1 Failure of an accountable officer to liquidate his outstanding cash advance on or before January 31, 2013 shall constitute cause for the filing of malversation charge under Article 217, failure to liquidate cash advance under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of PD No. 1445.

9.2 The suspension of salaries of erring accountable shall be ordered by the Auditor concerned to the proper agency official through the head of the agency.

9.3 Appropriate administrative proceedings shall likewise be instituted.”
observations.reason:
Restated in the Part II of this year’s Annual Audit Report.
Refer to Audit finding no. 12 (c).

observations.recommendation: Fast track the settlement of accounts disallowed within six (6) months from receipt of the Notice of Disallowance (ND) by the persons liable in accordance with Section 10.4 of said circular. Adhere to Section 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Management continue to deduct the corresponding amount from the salaries of concerned employees. However, there are some which cannot be collected since they are either no longer connected with the agency or are already dead.

observations.recommendation: Liquidate the cash advance for local travel within 30 days after return to his official station.
observations.reason: Submitted report on unliquidated cash advances showed long outstanding cash advances for travels granted in prior and current years.

observations.recommendation:
The Head of the Agency should cause the issuance of demand letters, thru the initiative of the Municipal Accountant.  If after two (2) notices these accountable officers employees still fail to liquidate, the matter should be brought to the attention of the legal officer for proper disposition, copy furnished the Commission on Audit.

The Municipal Mayor, being primarily responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the municipality, and being the approving officer, should enforce the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

Failure of the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for withholding of their salaries and the institution of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.3 of the said circular and Section 128 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: During CY 2023, the Accounting Office was able to prepare demand letters, but the same were not delivered/provided to the AOs due to lack of manpower assigned for the task including the delivery.

observations.recommendation: Require the agency head to register the land in the name of the municipality as provided in Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to ensure that the property including its improvements will be safeguarded from squatters and unlawful occupants as the case maybe.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries and prepare the Invoice Receipts (IR) Gen. Form No. 30-A to support the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance of Completed Projects to be received by the recipient schools and barangays. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher/s (JEV/s) for the dropping of the affected PPE accounts from the books with the following attachments: (1) Authority from SP; (2) JEV of the beneficiaries taking up the PPEs received by them; (3) Invoice Receipt (IR) and (4) Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance of Completed Projects duly received by the recipients.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Development Council to review all the projects undertaken and to be undertaken, and come up with a concrete action to address the deficiencies noted.

We also enjoined the offices concerned to address the root cause of the delay/non-implementation- the MEO if it pertains to the preparation of POW and other technical matters, to fast track its preparation, and the Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) if it pertains to procurement matter.

Likewise, we recommended that the MPDO closely monitor and evaluate the status of the implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: Appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF project complexity, project site relocation and/ or initial budget need to supplemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Part II of this report (refer to observation number 10).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the procuring officer record in the stock card the inventories received based on the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) and submit to the Municipal Accountant (MACCO) copies of the AIR, Purchase Order (PO) and delivery receipts. Issue RIS upon issuance of inventories which shall be summarized monthly in the RSMI. The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials to be reported in the RPCI to avoid the occurrence of recording inventory when these were all consumed or used in the project or activity.

Maintenance of records and submission to the MACCO not updated/ regularly undertaken due to limited personnel of the MTO.




Count of inventories not undertaken due to limited personnel. Previously assigned personnel in charged with count reassigned to other office.

observations.recommendation:
1. Cause the application of appropriate sanctions against erring borrowers, if necessary.

2. We also recommended that the MACCO evaluates the status of the outstanding receivables and to request for write-off of accounts whose balances are inactive or non-moving for ten years or more and its settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained in accordance with existing auditing rules and regulations.

Cleaning up of receivables account will be done starting September 1, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office properly and periodically monitor all the records of the LGU to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reported balances in the Financial Statements and make any necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and reconcile prior years’ completed projects not included/booked in the PPE accounts, the Municipal Engineer is to conduct inspection/validate the status of identified completed projects booked as CIP with no details/schedules/unknown status so that the proper adjustments may be made to the CIP accounts, the Municipal Engineer is to issue a Certificate of Completion on the validated completed projects to be used as the basis by the Municipal Accountant in the reclassification of the CIP accounts to the proper PPE account; and the Municipal Accountant is to take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of their classified completed projects from the time of completion until December 31, 2022, and henceforth ensure the reclassification of all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate asset accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is already in the process of reconciling the CIP accounts and they agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1. Page Nos. 31 to 34.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to review and assess all the completed projects of the LGU for CY 2022, take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of their classified completed projects from the time of completion until December 31, 2022, and prepare and record adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of all the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is already in the process of reconciling the CIP accounts and once properly identified it will be reclassified to their appropriate PPE accounts and agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1. Page Nos. 31 to 34.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant presents the current and non-current portion of its Loans Payable in the face of the LGU’s financial statements and disclose pertinent information in the Notes to the Financial Statements, to provide its users fair presentation and reliable assessment as far as liquidity and solvency are concerned.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
