observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: creates an Inventory Committee that is composed of two or more    employees including the GSO who will conduct physical count of all PPEs which shall be the basis in preparing the RPCPPE, The GSO maintains PPE Cards, and the Accounting Office prepares and maintains PPE Ledgers Cards and ensures that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements, that the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory Committee completes the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive already revived the composition of the Inventory Committee of the LGU and agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 17. Page Nos. 61 to 63.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management directs the Municipal Accountant to monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions and to reconcile accounts and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3. Page Nos. 37 to 38.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management directs the Municipal Accountant to submit all Bank Reconciliation Statements, duly reconciled with the cashbooks of the Treasury Office, together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period to effect the timely review and verification of the reported Cash in Bank balances.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 9. Page Nos. 47 to 49.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exhausts all possible means to enforce settlement/liquidation for the long overdue receivables; if there is a possibility of non-collection, provide an allowance for impairment in accordance with the IPSAS 21; and if no chance of being collected, request for authority to write-off from the Commission on Audit and submit the required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4. Page Nos. 37 to 39.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant addresses the issue of lacking personnel complement to the Mayor and the Accounting Office the immediate preparation and submission of all required Financial Reports for CY 2022 to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the preparation and submission of it thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appointed a new Municipal Accountant and agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 22. Page Nos. 70 to 71.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountable Officer to immediately restitute the missing funds, without prejudice to appropriate legal action for failure to produce the missing funds.
observations.managementAction:
The previous Acting Municipal Treasurer has still unpaid net shortage of ₱971,422.88 as of December 31, 2023. However, his salary was already withheld, and as of March 2024, an amount of ₱43,548.48 was withheld and be part of the restitution of his accountabilities with his approval.

The management agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 23. Page Nos. 72 to 74.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following directs the Municipal GSO-Designate to facilitate the maintenance of stock cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same and prepare Requisition and Issue Slip for issuance and the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to fairly present its inventory accounts in the financial statement, instructs the Municipal Accountant to observe proper adjustment of inventory accounts, prepare supplies ledger card per stock number and make an internal control policy on the maintenance of stock cards of the MDRRMO, MTO and MHO to establish authority on the control of inventory and prepare and submit the distribution lists to the Municipal Accounting Office monthly for recognition of expense; and reviews and revitalizes the composition of the Inventory Committee and fuel them up to religiously conduct the required annual physical count of unused/undistributed supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Already conducted an annual inventory count on supplies and materials but still not maintaining stock and ledger cards.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 11. Page Nos. 51 to 52.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (a) require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled-up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption and (b) directs the concerned personnel to prepare and submit to the Auditor, on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis, verification and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was still unable to prepare the necessary reports on payments of fuel expenses but committed that they would implement the recommendation and will impose, a no-trip ticket, no-travel policy.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 20. Page Nos. 67 to 69.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issues of demand letters to (i) notify the concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was not received, withhold from their salaries considering that they have been notified and given more adequate time to liquidate;                                (ii) employees who already resigned and transferred to immediately cause the liquidation and settlement of the unliquidated balances; request the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances of the deceased employees; reclassify “Other Receivable” account to Advances to Officers and Employees account in order to present fairly the total balance of unsettled cash advances; ensure the completeness of prescribed supporting documents for the granting of cash advances in processing claims for payment thereof; not grant additional cash advance to concerned officials and employees unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and strictly comply with the provisions of the aforementioned Circulars to avoid penalty stated thereon.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to the concerned employees and officials but no follow-up was done by the Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 21. Page Nos. 69 to 70.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting and Municipal Agriculture Office fast-track in obtaining the necessary supporting documents to support the transfer of ownership of the donated livestock and to drop the amounts of the same and of the seedlings in the books of account.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed to adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 8. Page Nos. 45 to 46.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers to determine, at any given time, the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the Financial Statements. Also, submit the required records as provided under Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular 2010-001.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to reconcile the Cash in bank account for fair presentation of the said account in the Financial Statements; and
Also, prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement account and submit the same to COA for audit purposes and maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account for easy preparation of the monthly BRS.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive, being the head of the agency, to direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof and to perform the same regularly, pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 74 of PD 1445, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management exert efforts to locate the necessary documents of the Land aqcuired by the Municipality through purchase or donation in order for the Accounting Office to poperly value or measure the Land account for fair presentation in the Financial Statements. Henceforts, general ledger and subsidiary ledgers for Land account should be prepared and mainted by the Accountibg Office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant trace the cost and period or date of acquisition of all the PPEs where the provision for depreciation were not provided and compute for the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007 and Section 4 (o) of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I to establish proper valuation of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Review the journal entries made from 2021 to 2023 that causes the sudden increase of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds account and effect the necessary adjustments; and
b. Examine the DBP Bank Account No. 000-50092-541-4 to idenfy the cash balance that pertains to the unutilized portion of various fund transfer from national agencies and transfer the same to the LBP bank account maintained by the LGU for Trust Fund for proper accounting.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts or effect the necessary adjustments. Being reciprocal accounts, it should end up with the same balances.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare adjusting entry to correct the entry made on the transfer of completed project to General Fund from Trust Fund to restate the previous balance of government equity and to properly close the Due to NGA/LGUs account; and
Thereafter, determine the composition of prior year’s transactions credited to Government Equity account under the Trust Fund and prepare and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the accounting error in the recording of government financial transactions.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to timely submit all disbursement vouchers to Accounting Office monthly;
Process all transactions/disbursement vouchers before the year-end as not to delay the closing of the books of accounts to be able to give the Municipal Accountant ample time to prepare the Financial Statements; and
Henceforth, submit the year-end Financial Statements, trial balances and other financial reports on or before February 14 of the succeeding year as prescribed under Section 41(2) of PD 1445.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
Explain and/or justify the incurrence of cash deficit/overdraft in the Trust - Proper account;
Refrain from using trust fund money for purposes other than those for which the trust was created or the funds received pursuant to Section 305 (e) of RA 7160; and
Ensure that trust obligations have sufficient cash back-up.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate his cash advances in compliance to Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Further, we recommended that the Accounting Office exert efforts to locate the required documents to make the necessary adjustments for the unrecorded liquidations of the previous Municipal Treasurers. Also, provide the subsidiary ledgers of the Advances for Payroll account for easy determination of the unliquidated balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to perform the annual physical inventory, reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

Further, we recommended that the Accounting Office review the reason for the credit or negative balance of the CIP-Building and Other Structure account. Thereafter, prepare adjusting or correcting entries, if possible.

We recommended that the Management to facilitate the titling of the aforementioned land assets to be able to provide proof of ownership and so the interest of the Municipality on the properties will be secured.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Locate all available documents regarding the abovementioned accounts in order to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers; and
Exhaust all possible remedies to reconcile the balances of those payable accounts in which SL is maintained. Prepare necessary adjusting entries, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a subsidiary ledger for the Trust Liability-DRRM account to support the balances and adopt the sample disclosure on LDRRMF to be presented in the Notes to the Financial Statement in compliance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to identify the cause or reason for the deficit or cash overdraft. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant should carry out the necessary actions to ensure that Trust Liabilities-DRMMF have a sufficient cash back-up.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain complete general and/or subsidiary ledger for the 20% Economic Development Fund in the general fund and the Loan to DBP for the construction of public market.
Also, prepare the corresponding financial reports, of all the special accounts, to form part of the schedules of the general fund trial balance as required under the abovementioned guidelines. This will serve as a tool in informing management on the condition as well as the status of operation of the special account.

We encouraged Management to continue their efforts in implementing their prioritized PPAs under the 20% Development Fund for the benefits of its constituents.

However, we also recommended that management ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% DF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PPAs that were still unimplemented positive efforts should be made to fast track the completion of the said PPAs.
We recommended the Management to use the template/format specified in the Annex A (LDRRMFIP) and B (Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk reduction and Management Fund) of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately the remaining balance of unutilized PDAF balances of P10,786,334.08 to the National Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision.
We recommended that the Municipality comply with Item 5.1.3.1 of the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 on the amount to be appropriated for Confidential and Intelligence Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to perform their responsibility of consolidating the PPMPs submitted by the end-users to APP and/or Supplemental APP in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that:
The Supply Officer to submit copies of Purchase Orders to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from issuance or execution to ensure timely and objective review of transactions; and
The official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items that within twenty-four (24) hours of acceptance, he shall notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries pursuant to COA Circular 96-010. In case of partial delivery of any item in the P.O., the copy to be submitted shall indicate which items have been delivered, and which have not.
We recommended that the Management cause the submission of the afore-mentioned unsubmitted accounts on or before April 15, 2024, otherwise a Notice of Suspension will be issued.

We recommended the following:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the required supporting documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the disbursements of BAC honoraria made in the year 2023 for further evaluation;
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant must ascertain the completeness of the documents required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 prior to processing of payment of BAC honoraria; and
As a withholding agent, Management shall ensure that taxes be withheld on payment of BAC honoraria and promptly remit to the Bureau of Internal Revenue said taxes withheld within the prescribed period to avoid penalties, surcharges and interests which if in any case of late remittance, it shall be personally borne by the defaulting LGU officials concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Submits sufficient and relevant documents to support his reimbursements to avoid disallowance in audit. Henceforth, observes  the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements;
Claims for meals and snacks while on official business should be limited to the amount provided under Executive Order No. 77 s. 2019; and
Encourages municipal officials and employees to observe prudence in the utilization of the limited funds of the municipality.

1.18 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) continue adhere with the
pertinent provisions of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and COA Circular
No. 96-011 to ensure reliability of presentation in the financial statements of
the Cash-in-Bank Local Currency-Current Account; and (ii) maintain
Subsidiary Ledgers for each programs, projects and activities (PPAs) under
the Trust Fund to monitor its cash balances at year-end.

2.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) above-
named Accountable Officers to settle their outstanding accounts; (b) Municipal
Accountant to intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedy
that would require Accountable Officers to settle or liquidate their past due
accounts; and (c) if conditions and circumstances on writing off the accounts
are met, direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the procedures for the
application of the request for write-off, under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

3.24 We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the (a) Inventory Team to (i) undertake complete physical
inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and
(ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor
within the prescribed period; (b) OIC – General Services Officer to
maintain complete property records; and (c) Municipal Accountant to
allocate depreciation expenses yearly to all Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE), except land, until fully depreciated. Furthermore, we
recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General
Services Officer (i) undertake periodic reconciliation of records of all the
properties to establish correctness of the balances of PPE accounts; and
(ii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to
render a reliable PPE information.

4.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct thorough review of accounting and
related records to determine the composition of the forwarded balances of
the Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts to establish the particular
completed project/s which remained recognized under the CIP accounts
and effect the necessary corrections/adjustment; and (ii) record the
depreciation expenses of the completed projects from the time of
completion; and (b) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer
to make proper coordination on timely determination of the completion of
infrastructure projects to subsequently facilitate the necessary
recording/ transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation
of balances in the financial statements.

5.23 We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) continue monitor the
status of funds received by maintaining individual subsidiary ledgers for
all accounts and properly return all unexpended balances to the
respective Source Agencies; (b) verify/reconcile the account balances
without supporting records and make adjusting entries, if necessary; and
(c) coordinate with the implementing offices on the completion of the
required liquidation/terminal report and to return to the source agencies
or remit to the National Treasury the balance of funds transferred
pursuant to item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 to settle the long
outstanding and non-moving accounts that can be used by the government
for other developmental purposes.

7.31 We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council (LDRRMC) headed by the Local Chief Executive require the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare reliable and accurate utilization
report on Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
(LDRRMF); (ii) render a justification showing proof that the
accumulated amount in the Trust Liabilities – DRRM was a result of
unrealized estimated income from regular sources to warrant the book
adjustments already made; and (iii) effect the necessary adjusting entries
for the transfer of unspent LDRRMF; and (b) Municipal Accountant,
OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Officer establish effective coordination mechanisms to
ensure the sharing of relevant information particularly on the budget
appropriation and utilization of LDRMMF to facilitate the complete and
accurate reporting thereof.

8.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) conduct proper review of transactions and ensure
completeness of information and supporting documents before signing on
box A of Disbursement Vouchers; (ii) submit immediately the transaction
documents pertaining to the procurement of supplementary textbooks
amounting to ₱2,496,500.00; and (iii) henceforth, observe the timeliness of
reportorial requirements of the Commission on the submission of
transactions.

9.26 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: (a)
Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDO) to conduct a
cost-benefit analysis comparing the rental and purchase options for
Heavy Equipment to make an informed decision that would give the best
benefit for the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of charging prior
year’s expenses against current year’s appropriation and that proper
matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed
pursuant to Section 119 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 and Sections 305
and 350 of Republic Act No. 7160. Moreover, we recommended that the
Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO),
MPDO and Municipal Budget Officer ensure the optimal implementation
of Solid Waste Management’s programs, projects and activities through
a more realistic project planning and budgeting.

10.14. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) ICO-
Municipal Treasurer to regularly conduct examination on the books of
accounts of business establishments by assigning staff/personnel who are
equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the examination
of books of accounts to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross
sales/receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid and to collect
the correct taxes as provided under Section 171 of the RA 7160; and (b)
Business Permit and Licensing Officer to fully enforce the submission of
Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales or copy of Certified Income Tax
Return from taxpayers to verify or assess the correct tax base to be used,
as provided by Section 2C.04 (d) and (e) of the Revenue Code.

11.19. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal
Point System (GFPS) through the GAD Focal Person to (i) inform the
concerned DILG Office, in writing, of changes introduced in the DILG-
endorsed GPB and to prepare and submit revised GAD Accomplishment
Report whenever necessary; (ii) create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis
of preparing the annual GAD Plan and Budget; (iii) carefully plan the
corresponding budget allocations of GAD PPAs by preparing project
breakdown of expenses before the approval of the plan and budget; and
(iv) conduct a review after three years and an end-term evaluation to
reflect on the status of accomplishment, variances, and overall assessment
of the implementation of the GAD agenda based on the goals and desired
results/outcomes.

12.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the ICO-
Municipal Treasurer to fully implement the lawful order of this
Commission on the withholding of salaries and any amount due to the
officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement
of their respective accountabilities to avoid sanctions pursuant to Rule
XIII, Enforcement and Monitoring of Decision.

13.6. We reiterated and amended our previous recommendations that the
Local Chief Executive require the (a) Head of the BAC Secretariat to
furnish the Audit Team with advance- certified copies of Contracts and
Purchase Orders together with its supporting documents within the
prescribed period; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that certified
copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders were received by COA before
certifying the completeness of supporting documents and signing in box
A of the Disbursement Vouchers.

14.30 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare complete and accurate statement of account for
remittance of taxes/premiums/loans repayments deducted from
suppliers/contractors/ employees to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth
to facilitate the intact remittance thereof.

15.4 We recommended and Management agreed to require the immediate
settlement of disallowances, otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries
of those employees determined to be liable and strict adherence to the
accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing and
documentation of transactions to avoid further suspension or
disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the actual RPT and SET Receivables using the amounts per Tax Delinquency Listing from the Municipal Treasurer as of December 31, 2023, and set-up at the beginning of the succeeding year the amount based on the Assessment Rolls provided by the Municipal Assessor, in the absence of RPTAR.
We further recommended that the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant exert concrete efforts to reconcile the records on real property taxes in order to establish the actual RPT/SET receivables accruing to the municipality.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee’s Property Card be regularly updated and reconciled with the Accounting Department’s PPE Ledger Card to ensure that all reflected PPEs in the financial statements are properly substantiated by existing assets duly accounted for.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the erroneous capitalization of transactions to its appropriate expense account.
We further recommended that management capitalize only costs incurred in accordance with the criteria set forth in Item 30 of IPSAS 17 to prevent overstatement of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Budget Officer submit written justification on the incurrence of cash deficit/overdraft.
We also recommended that management establish ways to improve the cash position of the municipality, such as, but not limited to, efficient revenue collection and adoption of effective austerity measures, in order to eliminate the cash deficit in the succeeding year.

We recommended that management issue a demand letter or show cause order to the contractor on its failure to turn-over a completed project and to submit the necessary final billing documents for the Construction of Flat Bed Dryer.
We further recommended that management include a stipulation in the succeeding bidding documents and/or contract regarding the period for the submission and processing of a contractor’s progress and final billing requests in order to minimize similar instances in the future.

We recommended that Management immediately return the remaining balances of the completed projects unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine all dormant accounts and follow the guidelines and procedures in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 to clean the books of accounts.  Further, prepare the necessary journal entries as a result thereof.

We recommended that management designate/hire a competent IT personnel responsible for maintaining the system that can troubleshoot the ETRACS when technical issues arise.
We also recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to capture and record the receipt and issuances of all accountable forms in the ETRACS in order to maximize the use of the system to improve the efficiency of its operations.

We recommended that the management require the concerned accountable officer to apply for a fidelity bond with the Bureau of Treasury. Otherwise, the accountable forms and its collection be handled by a bonded collecting clerk in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
We also recommended that the management adheres with the mandatory bonding requirement and prohibit delegating tasks which entails the custody, possession or control of public funds to collecting officers and employees who are not properly bonded.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive submit a written justification on the delayed liquidation of the cash advance for confidential fund, and immediately settle the account through refund or submission of liquidation documents to ICFAU and furnishing the Municipal Accountant and COA of the duly received transmittal letter. Otherwise, the Municipal Accountant immediately withhold the payment of the salaries or any amount due as measures to enforce settlement of the outstanding cash advance.
We also recommended and management agreed to submit a request for write-off of dormant cash advances after exhausting all remedies for their settlement by following the prescribed guidelines and procedures outlined in the COA Circular No. 2023-008 on the proper disposition of dormant accounts.

We recommended that management transition from manual accounting to a computerized accounting system by allocating funds to obtain the necessary server and workstations requirements for the implementation of the e-NGAS and e-Budget.
We also recommended that management avail the eNGAS & eBudget System Training by sending letter of intent to COA Accounting Systems Development and Other Services Office (ASDOSO) – Government Accountancy Sector (GAS) via email at coa.asdosotahds@gmail.com, together with duly filled-out Technical Requirements Checklist; and subsequently, apply/roll-out the e-NGAS and e-budget in the municipality.

We recommended that management submit a written justification for the inappropriate charging against 20% Development Fund-Waterworks of the unrelated expenditures.
We further recommended that management utilize the specific appropriation for expenditures which are related to the priority development projects and to refrain charging expenditure items prohibited under Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

1.11 We reiterated our audit recommendations in CY 2022 and prior years with
modification, that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Maintain a complete and updated PPE and Real Property Ledger
Cards to ensure that all PPEs are properly accounted for and to easily
facilitate the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with accounting records;
b. Trace back the transactions in the PPE balance labelled as
unreconciled items from prior years to determine its nature and prepare
the appropriate adjustments in the books of the Municipality;
c. Reclassify the incorrectly recorded PPEs to their proper account;
d. Reconcile the accounting records with the RPCPPE wherein
differences noted should be verified and the corrections be made, where
necessary; and
e. Determine the cost of the various donated equipment and
recognize them as PPE in the books.

1.12 We also reiterated our previous recommendations, that the Municipal General
Services Officer:
a. Assist the Municipal Accountant in determining the cost of the donated
equipment and submit the related documents to the Municipal Accountant
upon receipt, for proper recording; and
b. Ensure that Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) was prepared
and accomplished completely when the physical count is concluded.

2.13 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant discontinue the practice of
derecognizing the Due to NGA account without the submission of liquidation
reports or Fund Utilization Reports (FURs) to the Audit Team and Source
Agency.

2.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Engineer to submit the copies of the requests for time
extensions of the contractors of the noted ELCAC projects and the
approvals thereof to the Audit Team;
b. the Municipal Engineer to strictly adhere to the project duration
stipulated per contract and exercise diligence in the inspection of
completed projects immediately after the reported date of completion
to determine whether projects were completed on time, taking
considerations on Variation Orders or Time Extension, if any, and
make sure that all materials and prescribed procedures conform with
the Program of Work, Detailed Estimates and Plan; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to impose Liquidated Damages, equal to
one-tenth (1/10) of one (1%) of the cost of the unfinished portion of
the project for each day of delay.

2.15 Also, to protect the interest of the government and to prevent further loss and
wastage of government funds and resources, we recommended that the
following measures be undertaken for future projects of similar in nature:
a. Strictly monitor implementation of all projects, whether contracts for
infrastructure, goods or consulting services, in accordance with the
approved plans and specifications within the time frame specified in the
contract agreement; and
b. Identify contracts which require appropriate management actions. If so,
impose liquidated damages and/or administrative sanctions particularly
termination of contracts and recommend blacklisting of contractors for
failure to comply with their obligations in accordance with the provisions
of Sections 68 and 69, and Appendix 17 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. submit the lacking DVs to confirm the validity of the recorded
transactions. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of
Suspension and/or Disallowance for non-compliance thereof; and
b. instruct the personnel in charge in the submission of the JEVs to cross
check all approved JEVs based on the Check Disbursement Journals
and ensure that all DVs in the Journals are transmitted to the Audit
Team monthly, so that all recorded transactions in the books of the
municipality have sufficient basis.

4.8 We recommended that Municipal Treasurer:
a. Obtain the Bank Statements of all the depository accounts of the LGU,
especially from the Trust Fund accounts, and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the Monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements;
b. Conduct the transfer funds from Trust Fund to the General Fund for
the total amount of taxes erroneously debited from the General Fund
to be supported with a list of taxes withheld from Trust Fund projects;
c. Henceforth, ensure that funds for the payment of taxes withheld and
other charges from other funds be transferred first to the General Fund
prior to the utilization of the fund to avoid incurrence of negative Cash
in Bank balances in the book of accounts; and
d. Strictly monitor the remittances of RPT dues to LGUs, both province
and barangays, and reconcile first with the records of the Municipal
69
Accountant, if necessary, to avoid incurrence of over/under remittances
of tax dues

4.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare the latest Bank Reconciliation Statement of all depository
accounts of the LGU, especially those under the Trust Fund account,
and submit the same to the Audit Team for auditing purposes;
b. Draw the adjusting entries to record the valid reconciling items and to
restore the stale checks amounting ₱1,100,322.35 and ₱204,805.88,
respectively; and
c. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in monitoring the
remittances of taxes due and other charges to avoid incurrence of
over/under remittances and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if
necessary, to avoid reflecting the negative balances of the Cash in Bank
account in the books of accounts.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Record the cost of various items for LGU Offices, spare parts for motor
vehicles, and food items for PNP personnel and other activities to the
appropriate asset and expense accounts, in accordance with The
Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units, rather than
recording the entire amount to the Food Supplies Expense account; and
b. Henceforth, record all the transactions of the municipality using the
proper accounts provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs
so that transactions are faithfully and correctly presented in the
financial statements to provide the users thereof of reliable
72
data/information.

6.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatements
of the CIP – Buildings and Other Structures, Accounts Payable and
Miscellaneous Income accounts; and
b. henceforth, disclose relevant information in the Notes to Financial
Statements to provide the users thereof of relevant and reliable
information about the LGU’s operation.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. record the unissued relief goods and medical supplies to the Welfare
goods food for distribution and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies
accounts, respectively; and
b. stop the practice of recording immediately to expense accounts the
inventories received by the municipality. Henceforth, record
subsequent procurements of inventories as an asset under the
appropriate inventory account and recognize the related expenses
based on the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued,
supported by duly approved RIS, and Distribution List.

8.14 We recommended that the Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator:
a. Identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for
implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimal utilization
of the 20% DF; and
b. Stop the inclusion of PPAs in generic terms in the AIP for all projects
of the LGU to ensure full implementation thereof within the timetable
indicated in the AIP.

8.15 We also reiterated our CY 2022 audit recommendations with modification that
the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator:
a. Conduct a detailed engineering of all projects prior to its inclusion in
the AIP which includes the preparation of the Program of Work with
the Detailed Estimates and plans/specifications;
b. Prepare the Program of Work with the Detailed Estimates and
plans/specifications for the unexpended balance of the CY 2022
Construction of various barangay infrastructure project amounting to
₱256,000.00 to ensure its full implementation; and
82
c. Submit to the Bids and Awards Committee the Program of Work with
the Detailed Estimates, plans/specifications and Purchase Request
within the timetable indicated in the AIP, taking into consideration the
period within which the procurement process and project
implementation will be completed.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking DVs in
order for the audit team to confirm the validity of the recorded transactions
and utilization of the 20% Development Fund. This is without prejudice to the
issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance for non-compliance
thereof.

9.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer coordinate
with the Municipal Accountant and make the necessary adjustments in the
RAAOb to determine the correct total unexpended balance of the 20%
Development as of yearend.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal
Development Council, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator:
a) submit a written justification of the LGU’s action of allowing the
incurrence of ineligible expenditures charged against the 20%
Development Fund;
b) Stop utilizing the 20% Development Fund for expenditures that are
expressly not allowed in Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No.
83
2020-1; and
c) identify and implement as priority projects only those which are
necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local
governance, and those which are essential to the promotion of the
general welfare of the people.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal DRMM Officer:
a. Stop the distribution of relief goods to non-disaster victims and strictly
comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121 to avoid committing
prohibited acts in the implementation, distribution, and utilization of
resources out of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM)
Fund;
b. Determine the quantity of goods to be procured based on the number
of victims and their basic needs; and
c. Include as one of the conditions in the procurement of relief goods, the
expiration dates at least six months from the delivery date to avoid
spoilage or wastage of government funds.

12.8 We recommended that the Collecting Officers remit all collections intact on a
daily basis to the Municipal Treasurer as mandated in Section 26 of COA
Circular No. 92-382.

12.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Demand the remittance of all collections of the Collecting Officers on a
daily basis and refrain from issuing additional accountable forms to the
Collecting Officer with collections pending for remittance; and
b. Deposit all collections of the Municipality intact to the authorized
depository bank to avoid handling of cash for a prolonged period of
time and possible misuse and loss thereof.

13.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and government officials and
employees concerned:
a) Submit the lacking supporting documents noted in Appendix D.8 to
avoid suspension in audit; and
b) Refund the excess amount of Daily Travel Expense claimed for their
respective travels totaling ₱395,204.02 (Appendix D.9); and
c) Henceforth, only approve travels to the most necessary and essential to
the effective performance of an official or employee’s mandates and
functions.

13.9 We also recommended management to stop granting travel allowances beyond
what is allowed under Executive Order No. 77, to avoid incurrence of irregular
expenditures and disallowances in audit.

14.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Ensure thru the Property/Supply Officer to accomplish the pre-repair
inspection report after the conduct of pre-repair inspection of
properties subjected for repairs. Likewise, when the subject properties
95
were already repaired, conduct immediately post-repair inspection and
submit a report thereon;
b. Direct the Property/Supply Officer to attach the Certificate of
Acceptance and Report of Waste Material duly approved by the LCE
after preliminary inspection/evaluation has been made;
c. Direct the GSO to ensure that the damaged/replaced, unserviceable
supplies and property were kept in a safe stockroom located within the
premises of the Municipality waiting for disposal thereof and disposed
of in the manner prescribed by existing rules and regulations; and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all claims for repairs
are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government
funds are expended for proper and valid expenses as required under
existing regulations.

1.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to intensify collection to recover
government funds and to take appropriate remedies to clear the amount
in the books; and
b. If warranted, request for the authority to write-off of the accounts
following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated
December 19, 2016

2.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) GSO, through the disposal committee, to immediately conduct disposal of
all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA
Circular No. 92-386;
b) GSO to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of properly
accomplished IIRUP or the Report of Waste Materials duly acted upon
together with a copy of the Official Receipt, if disposal is for value
received; and
c) Municipal Accountant to derecognize from the book of accounts all
disposed unserviceable properties and recognize gain/loss on disposal.
Otherwise reclassify the pertinent PPE assets to Other Assets account
pending the disposal to reflect the true value of its PPE accounts.

3.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory
Committee, in coordination with the General Services Officer and
Municipal Accountant, to conduct one-time cleansing of PPE Accounts to
come up with a reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence,
condition and accountability.

4.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Reconcile the amounts of PPE accounts recorded in the FS and lapsing
schedule;
b. Coordinate with the Inventory Committee to identify and investigate the
whereabouts, condition and accountability of these assets; and
c. Provide an allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets identified
and/or request for the authority from COA to derecognize non-
existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability
pursuant to existing COA rules and regulations.

5.13. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Direct the department heads of the implementing offices of funds
received from various national government agencies for the timely
implementation of projects; and
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
coordinate with each other to effect the immediate return of the
unexpended/excess balances of the same to the source agencies
concerned after the implementation of each project.

6.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare and finalize the plans and
drawings for the Completion of Multi-Purpose Building;
b. Advise the MPDO to identify other priority development projects in
order to utilize the fund for the acquisition of service vehicle of
₱1,000,000.00;
c. Instruct the MIASO to follow up with the DENR on the approval of the
project site for the Construction of Public Market; and
d. Henceforth, ensure that the development projects to be funded out of
the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation ready.

7.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to optimize the use of its
DRRM funds taking into consideration all the thematic areas as mandated
by Republic Act No. 10121.

8.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) Municipal Engineer to investigate the causes of negative slippage of all
infrastructure projects to be submitted to the Municipal Mayor for
appropriate action and adopt the guidelines issued by the DPWH in
addressing negative slippage; and
b) Municipal Accountant to require the contractor to payback the
liquidated damages due to negative slippage if the required duly
approved Time Extensions together with the required supporting
documents, verified by the Municipal Engineer, will not be submitted
immediately.

9.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Assign personnel in the slaughterhouse who shall be responsible for its
operations;
b. Provide for the sewerage, water system and electrification of the
slaughterhouse; and
c. Ensure immediate operation of the project for the efficient and effective
utilization of government funds.

10.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from approving
transactions for the grant of allowances to Municipal Officials and
Employees without any law passed by the Congress authorizing such
allowance/benefits.

10.8. We further recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor and the
Sangguniang Bayan Members ensure that all ordinances shall not
contravene any existing law passed by the National Government.
We reiterated the following recommendations to the Management:

We recommended the following to the Management:
1. Submit a written justification for the said deficiency; and
2. Ensure that all accountable officers (AOs) are bonded based on the correct cash/property accountability of each AO.

We recommended that the Management direct the offices concerned to ensure that in the succeeding similar transactions, proper coordination with the barangay officials and proper monitoring of project implementation will be made to avoid the delays in the utilization of funds received by the LGU. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should also ensure that complete and proper documentation will be observed so that the transaction’s propriety will be established.
We also recommended that the management submit a written justification for the said deficiencies.

We reiterated our recommendations that the management:
1. Direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to look into the details of the balances pertaining to fund transfers taken up as liability in the General Fund and Trust Fund books and take appropriate action such as submission of liquidation report to the source agency if needed and facilitate the return of unspent balances to the source agency or remit to the Bureau of the Treasury or transfer to the General Fund of the Agency, whichever is applicable/appropriate, and immediately make the necessary adjustments in the books;
2. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with and assist the OIC - Municipal Accountant in verifying whether the projects/programs associated with the funds received were already implemented and to secure the necessary documents in order to facilitate the liquidation to source agency, if needed, and in the recording of adjustments in the books; and
3. Ensure that there is proper coordination between the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office to avoid recurring the noted observation in any future undertakings the agency may accept, especially fund transfers from NGAs.

We recommended and the Management agreed to hasten the implementation of the three development projects mentioned. Direct the official concerned to review all the on-going and to be undertaken projects by the Municipality and come up with concrete action to address the deficiencies noted. It is highly enjoined that all offices concerned will have a holistic and documented plan on how to efficiently implement the agency’s various projects and activities taking into consideration the availability of its personnel. Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) closely monitor and evaluate the status of the implementation of projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the MDRRMO shall:
A. Ensure that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are serially numbered and accomplished properly and completely. Require also the driver to prepare and submit a Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption and a Monthly Report of Official Travel which summarize all the Driver’s Trip Tickets during the month (Appendices G and F of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles).
B. Ensure that the quantity of fuel consumed is monitored through the preparation and proper maintenance of the gasoline control card and conduct reconciliation between the gasoline control card and the storage tank periodically, pursuant to Section D(5) of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.
C. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to resolve the discrepancies between records maintained by the Accounting Office and the MDRRMO and to determine the accurate quantity of fuel remains in the storage facility.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all supporting documents are complete and appropriate before making certification of the DV.

We recommended that the management direct the following personnel:
1. The HRMO to:
A. Prepare a contract instead of an office order to serve as a basis for the hiring of personnel on a Job Order Basis and
B. Furnish a copy of the contract, Accomplishment Reports and DTRs of the JOs the personnel/ office who is processing the payroll for payment of their salary; and
2. The Municipal Accountant to:
A. Submit the required supporting documents, and
B. Make sure that all supporting documents for all disbursements made are complete to preclude the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the Management direct the Accounting and Treasury Departments to expedite the reconciliation process and promptly remit the outstanding tax amount to the BIR as required by the relevant rules and regulations. It is important to ensure that future taxes withheld are remitted on time to avoid any penalties imposed by the law.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain accurate subsidiary records of individual suppliers, contractors and employees. These records will support the Due to BIR account and enable regular monitoring and reconciliation of balances.

We recommended that the Management direct:
1. The Payroll-in-charge to correct the current practice of withholding taxes. Also, it is enjoined that she undergoes thorough training and stays updated on current regulations to accurately compute applicable withholding taxes.
2. The Accounting Department to establish a monitoring system to check whether or not correct and appropriate withholding of taxes from employees and observance of the BIR regulations is observed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office direct the persons responsible to fast-track the reconciliation to zero out the balance in the said inter-agency payables. Amounts needed to be remitted/refunded shall be immediately remitted to the respective agencies or refunded to the identified employees/persons, whichever is applicable.
We further recommended that the agency officials concerned require employees to submit Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth Numbers first before withholding any mandatory contributions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. GFPS officials and other offices concerned to formulate more responsive PPAs to address GAD issues and concerns of the Municipality. The cost of PPAs that partly address the gender issues will be included in the GPB by way of attribution through the use of the HGDG tool; and
b. Municipal Planning Office, GAD Focal person and other offices concerned to continue updating, completing, and maintaining a more functional GAD Database which shall aid in gender analysis for the identification of gender issues and the cause/causes, to be addressed by the agency; the agency’s GAD database.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a. Avoid giving additional cash advances to officers and employees who have not yet settled their previous cash advances. Please strictly follow the guidelines provided under Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid recurring noncompliance.
b. Take appropriate action against accountable officers who have long outstanding cash advances, especially those who are still connected with the agency. Encourage them to immediately settle their accounts according to the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 97-002.
c. Implement an efficient and effective control procedure in the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances. Make sure that the guidelines set out in COA Circular No. 97-002 are followed.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identifies items that qualify for write-off and expedite the request, supported with the required documents as outlined in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit the paid DV on a daily or weekly basis, whichever is appropriate, to the Accounting Office to facilitate timely processing thereof and submission to the COA Office. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the mentioned lacking DVs.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a. Refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers who have not settled their previous cash advances in compliance with Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445, and to those personnel not connected or employed in the LGU.
b. Enforce appropriate action against accountable officers with long-overdue outstanding cash advances to immediately liquidate or settle their accounts. For cash advances that are identified and qualified to be written off, expedite the request supported with the required documents as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
c. Implement an efficient and effective control procedure in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in conformity with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that management: 
1. Adequately plan and thoroughly study the feasibility of each project before proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded under the 20% LGDF; 
2. Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the timely implementation and completion of the mentioned projects; and 
3. Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents from the PPAs to be implemented under 20% LGDF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office continuously coordinate with its software provider to resolve the inconsistencies found in various PPE accounts and ensure that the amounts presented in the financial statements accurately reflect the balances in its supporting schedules.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the GSO and the MAO must establish an effective and practical system that would ensure the swift and regular reconciliation of records of both offices about the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment;
Conduct a complete physical count of all properties; and
3. Direct the GSO to correctly provide all the necessary data required in the RPCPPE for proper identification of all PPE items.

We recommended Management to:
Direct Municipal Accountant to look into the details of the unspent balances about fund transfers taken up as liability in the General Fund and Trust Fund books and facilitate the return to the source agency or remittance to the National Treasury or transfer to the General Fund of the Agency, whichever is appropriate, and make to the necessary adjustments in the books immediately;
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with and assist the Municipal Accountant in verifying whether the projects/programs associated with the funds received were already implemented and to secure the necessary documents to facilitate the recording of adjustments in the books; and
Ensure proper coordination between the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office in the future project/program implementation toward the funds which may be received from NGAs.

We recommended Management to:
Require the immediate submission of documents from recipient barangays for the proposed projects to be implemented;
Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the timely implementation of the projects; and 
Reprogram idle funds from unimplemented projects to finance other priority projects.

We recommended that management:
Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents in terms of PPAs to be implemented under 20% LGDF.

We recommended that Management evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of the accounts with negative balances, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances. Further, ensure accuracy in the recording of transactions to their appropriate SL accounts in order to avoid payment of unnecessary penalties and surcharges to various government agencies.

In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:
Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough evaluation/ reconciliation of the journal entries made to determine the causes of the discrepancy. Likewise, effect the necessary adjustments to clear the reciprocal accounts of possible erroneous entries and/or omissions made.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the return of the unutilized balances to various source agencies and constantly monitor and review the composition of the account to avoid dormancy of the balances.

We recommended the following:
Include those which are to be implemented by the Municipality, whether infrastructure projects or other form of PPAs so as to facilitate prompt monitoring and validation thereof.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of accounts with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances.

We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:
Enforce collection of the liquidated damages from the supplier if no document to prove the extensions of delivery dates
Submit written justification/explanation on the following:
deficiencies noted on the documents related to extension of deliveries;
why the Municipality continuously granted extensions?
despite the BAC resolution’s recommendation on the imposition of liquidated damages, why were there no effort exerted to collect such?

We recommended that Management exert/enforce efforts to collect these collectibles so that whatever collection can be used for the operation of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should determine the collectivity of these accounts so that proper adjustments could be made in order not to overstate the receivable balances as at year-end.

We have recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following:
Regularly send confirmation/ demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures; and
Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts.

Fast track the settlement of accounts disallowed within six (6) months from receipt of the Notice of Disallowance (ND) by the persons liable in accordance with Section 10.4 of said circular. Adhere to Section 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
Liquidate the cash advance for local travel within 30 days after return to his official station.

The Head of the Agency should cause the issuance of demand letters, thru the initiative of the Municipal Accountant. If after two (2) notices these accountable officers/employees still fail to liquidate, the matter should be brought to the attention of the legal officer for proper disposition, copy furnished the Commission on Audit.
The Municipal Mayor, being primarily responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the municipality, and being the approving officer, should enforce the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
Failure of the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for withholding of their salaries and the institution of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.3 of the said circular and Section 128 of PD 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
Reconcile their records to undertake proper adjustments on the variance noted between the cashbook and ledger balances.
Immediately issue corresponding official receipts (AF 51) and prepare the RCD for the receipt of checks pertaining to withheld taxes and record such collection in the cashbook. The OR and RCD shall be the basis of the supporting documents to be attached by the Municipal Accountant upon recording the transactions.
Prepare RCDs immediately upon receipt of collections from the barangay, and deposit the same within the prescribed period; and
Implement close and strict supervision of all the accountable officers to ensure that rules and regulations in handling collections and accountable forms shall be strictly complied with and to forestall the possible risk of loss or misuse.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books all valid book reconciling items noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2023, and furnish this office copies of corresponding JEVs recording the adjustments made to the affected accounts. Received bank transfers undergoing verification can be temporarily recorded under Deferred Credits/Unearned Income accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct in writing the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and send demand letters to concerned party/ies to enforce the collection of dormant accounts.  Cause the application of appropriate sanctions against erring borrowers, if necessary.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the status of the outstanding receivables and request for write-off or derecognition from the books of the dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to investigate and exhaust the available remedy to reconcile the unsupported prior years’ balance of the CIP account. Moreover, requires the Municipal Accountant to take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of their classified completed projects from the time of completion until December 31, 2023, and subsequently ensure the reclassification of all completed projects in the CIP account to their appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Assessor, verify their historical data or records that could shed light on the acquisition costs of the properties and, if available, make an assessment so that the necessary adjusting entries shall be made to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant presents the current and non-current portion of its Loans Payable in the face of the LGU’s financial statements, and disclose pertinent information in the Notes to the Financial Statements, to provide its users fair presentation and reliable assessment as far as liquidity and solvency are concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible means to enforce settlement/liquidation for the long overdue receivables; if there is a possibility of non-collection, provide an allowance for impairment in accordance with the IPSAS 21; and if no chance of being collected, request for authority to write-off from the Commission on Audit and submit the required supporting documents.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant determine the actual amount of the unexpended balances of the QRF and Mitigation -MOOE for CYs 2021 and 2022 and make necessary adjusting entries on the affected accounts. Likewise requires the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the determined amount from the LDRRMF-General Fund to the Special Trust Fund. Moreover, the LDRRM Officer ensures that there will be an actual transfer before implementing the PPAs under the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the net cash shortage of the erring official in the books of accounts as receivable pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 dated July 9, 2013, which prescribed the rules and regulations on accounting shortages and draw adjusting journal entries of the affected accounts to reflect the correct balances reported in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer reconcile the discrepancy noted and regularly conduct monthly and quarterly reconciliations of their records. Moreover, due diligence should be taken by the concerned offices in the recording and the preparation of reports.

We recommended that the Accounting and Municipal Agriculture Office fast-track in obtaining the necessary supporting documents to drop the amounts of the same and of the seedlings in the books of account.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer update regularly his cashbooks by recording all disbursements daily and reconciling the balance with the accounting records at the end of the month, and both the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant make necessary corrections should there be a difference between these two records and adjust the unrecorded cash in bank balances to reflect/establish correct balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions, reconcile accounts, and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of accounts all purchases of supplies, whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, as Inventory while its issuances are recorded as expenses, with RIS and SSMI as support documents. Further, a supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items shall be maintained to record the acquisition, receipt, and issuance pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; and the GSO to consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS, on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the same Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all unreleased checks at the end of the year are reported and properly treated in accordance with the Manual on NGAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adhered with the provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to 

(i) set-up RPT/SET receivable based on Certified List of Taxpayers provided by Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and 

(ii) prepare and recognize the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted; and 

(b) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the RPT/SET collection of the Municipality and institute remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991; and 

(ii) timely prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, particulars of the property, location, amount due and collectible for the applicable year and other pertinent information and submit the same to the Accounting Unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET tax receivable in the books of accounts of the Municipality at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant set-up RPT/SET receivable based on Certified List of Taxpayers provided by Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive in his capacity as Chairman of the GFPS 

(i) create a centralized GAD database that would consolidate the information gathered and compiled by all offices to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy making; 

(ii) prepare and comply on the timely submission to the Audit Team of the GAD Accomplishment Report; and 

(iii) employ the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular program/ project to GAD. Henceforth, inform the DILG in writing for any changes in the GPB before the implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to reconstitute GFPS, prepared sex-disaggregated and GAD related information, and submit GAD accomplishment report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) reassess its existing organizational structure to upgrade the plantilla of personnel requirement for possible budgeting, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job-order personnel; 

(b) instruct the Municipal Human Resources Management Officer (MHRMO) to observe prudence and diligence on determining the necessity and propriety of hiring of job orders; and 

(c) direct the Municipal Accountant to address the noted deficiencies before allowing payments thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendations.
The Municipality was able to issue contracts to old/new tenants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to consider the immediate adoption a system of payroll payment through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) of the authorized government banks in order to protect government funds and minimize operating costs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality now adopt a system of payroll payment through Automated Teller Machine (ATM).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the 

(a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) ensure that payments for procurements are made directly to suppliers/contractors except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently paid by check; and 

(ii) set-up Petty Cash Fund duly supported with estimate of expenses which shall be used for petty operating expenses; and 

(b) all officials and employees to discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursement unless the item/service is petty in nature and necessary for emergency cases; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant 

(i) conduct thorough review and scrutiny of transactions before signing the box A of the Disbursement Voucher; and 

(ii) coordinate with the previous Municipal Officials to facilitate the submission of lacking documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer set-up petty cash fund for petty operating expenses.
observations.reason:
There were transactions during CY 2023 with incomplete documentation.

Lacking documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to require all departments/offices to submit their respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for the implementation of different projects/programs/ activities; and 

(b) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to 

(i) prepare the Annual Procurement Plan based on PPMP and adopt the required format as prescribed by GPBB; 

(ii) observe prudence in accomplishing the APP that should contain relevant details including the procurement strategy to be employed and scheduling of procurement activities; and 

(iii) avoid the splitting of contracts and employ appropriate mode of procurement based on the approved APP.
observations.managementAction: Department/Offices submitted their respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP).
observations.reason:
Prescribed format of Annual Procurement Plan was not prepared.
