We recommended Management to:


     1. Direct Municipal Accountant to look into the details of the unspent
        balances about fund transfers taken up as liability in the  General
        Fund and Trust Fund books and facilitate the return to  the  source
        agency or remittance to the National Treasury or  transfer  to  the
        General Fund of the Agency, whichever is appropriate, and  make  to
        the necessary adjustments in the books immediately;


     2. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to  coordinate
        with and assist the Municipal Accountant in verifying  whether  the
        projects/programs associated with the funds received  were  already
        implemented and to secure the necessary documents to facilitate the
        recording of adjustments in the books; and


     3. Ensure proper coordination between the Municipal Accounting  Office
        and the Municipal Planning and Development  Office  in  the  future
        project/program  implementation  toward  the  funds  which  may  be
        received from NGAs.

Recommendations:

We recommended the Municipal Development Council to:


     1. Prepare the required Revised AIP if there are changes in  the  PPAs
        of the original AIP and only prepare SIPs if there are new PPAs  to
        be implemented which are to be funded by new fund sources; and


     2. Properly document in its Minutes of Meetings, the presentations and
        deliberations on the exclusion and inclusion of PPAs for  inclusion
        in the Revised AIP/Supplemental Investment Programs.

Recommendations:

We recommended Management to:


     1. Require  the  immediate  submission  of  documents  from  recipient
        barangays for the proposed projects to be implemented;
     2. Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the  timely
        implementation of the projects; and


     3. Reprogram idle funds from unimplemented projects to  finance  other
        priority projects.

Recommendations:

We recommended Management to:


     1. Thoroughly plan and ensure proper monitoring of  the  projects  and
        activities  to  be  implemented  under  the  LDRRMF  such  as   the
        preparation of a monitoring report that would check the  status  of
        each project/activity;


     2. Ensure proper coordination between implementing  offices  and  full
        utilization of the appropriated amounts  for  the  benefit  of  the
        constituents in disaster preparedness and mitigation; and

     3. Reprogram  the  excess  unused  appropriation  for  other  disaster
        preparedness and mitigation activities for inclusion in the revised
        Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund  Investment  Plan
        (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2022.

Recommendations:

We recommended Management to:


      1.  Enforce  the  immediate  liquidation  and/or  settlement  of   all
         outstanding cash advances;


      2.  Stop  the  practice  of  granting  additional  cash  advances   to
         officials/employees who have previous unliquidated cash advances as
         well as granting multiple  cash  advances  to  the  same  employees
         regardless of the purpose;


      3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to expedite  the  request  for  the
         write-off of long outstanding cash  advances  that  are  considered
         dormant following the guidelines and  procedures  provided  in  COA
         Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and


      4. Strictly adhere to the guidelines outlined in COA Circular No.  97-
         002 dated February 10, 1997, in the  granting  and  liquidation  of
         cash advances.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries and prepare the Invoice Receipts (IR) Gen. Form No. 30-A to support the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance of Completed Projects to be received by the recipient schools and barangays.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher/s (JEV/s) for the dropping of the affected PPE accounts from the books with the following attachments: (1) Authority from SP; (2) JEV of the beneficiaries taking up the PPEs received by them; (3) Invoice Receipt (IR) and (4) Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance of Completed Projects duly received by the recipients.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant exercise professional judgment on recording projects as either expense or asset taking into consideration the nature of the financial transaction.  Draw appropriate adjusting entry/ies to record the above transactions under its appropriate expense accounts and attach the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in the reply to this AOM.
We also recommended to the Municipal Engineer that the IPPOW in projects about road maintenance taken from the 20% DF must state the actual cost component/s to be incurred.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant correct the entries made in the Notes to Financial Statement and observed the disclosure requirements in accordance with IPSAS 30 for the ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:
Exert effort to locate the supporting documents to prove the validity of the claims and to enforce payment thereof; and
Retain copies of supporting documents for all payables recorded.

1.11    We recommended that the Management instructs:


        a. The Municipal Treasurer to  prepare  a  Schedule  of  Unreleased
           Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit  the  said
           schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and


        b. The Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting  journal  entries  in
           the books of accounts every year-end for  the  total  amount  of
           unreleased  checks,  to  fairly  present  the  accounts  in  the
           financial statements.

      Management Comment

      1.12  Municipal Accountant assured that the noted observation will  be
        acted upon in the ensuing year and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  then
        agreed to prepare the statement of unreleased checks every  end  of
        the year.

2.14    We recommended that the Management:


        1.  Directs  the  Municipal  Accountant   to   prepare   the   Bank
           Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all the funds/bank  accounts
           maintained by the Municipality with  the  authorized  depository
           banks  every  month  or  upon  the  availability  of  the   bank
           statements.


        2. Obliges the Municipal  Accountant  and  Municipal  Treasurer  to
           conduct  regular  reconciliation  of  their  records  at   least
           quarterly and adjust the verified reconciling items to avoid its
           accumulation in order to reflect correct  and  reliable  account
           balances, and:


        3. Requires the Accounting  and  Treasury  Offices  to  assess  and
           review the nature of the uncashed checks whether these are stale
           or cancelled checks and prepare the necessary adjusting  entries
           in the books of accounts.
    Management Comment


    2.13    The Municipal Accountant committed  that  he  will  address  the
        timely preparation of the bank reconciliation as based on the audit
        recommendations, and  agreed  to  the  Team  to  have  a  quarterly
        reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer.

3.7      We  recommended  that  the  Management  directs  the  Municipal
        Accountant to  submit  all  Bank  Reconciliation  Statements,  duly
        reconciled with the cashbooks of the Treasury Office, together with
        the supporting documents within the prescribed period to effect the
        timely review  and  verification  of  the  reported  Cash  in  Bank
        balances.

4.10    We recommended the following:


        a.  Directs  the  Municipal   GSO-Designate   to   facilitate   the
           maintenance of stock cards  for  supplies  to  account  for  the
           receipt and disposition of the same and prepare Requisition  and
           Issue Slip for issuance and the Report of Supplies and Materials
           Issued monthly and submit the same to the  Municipal  Accountant
           for recording purposes to fairly present its inventory  accounts
           in the financial statement;


        b. Instructs the Municipal Accountant to observe proper  adjustment
           of inventory accounts, prepare supplies ledger  card  per  stock
           number and make an internal control policy on the maintenance of
           stock cards of the MDRRMO, MTO and MHO to establish authority on
           the control of inventory and prepare and submit the distribution
           lists to the Municipal Accounting Office monthly for recognition
           of expense; and


        c.  Reviews  and  revitalizes  the  composition  of  the  Inventory
           Committee and fuel them up to religiously conduct  the  required
           annual  physical  count  of  unused/undistributed  supplies  and
           materials.


    Management Comment


    4.11     The  Municipal  Accountant  expressed  that  they  have   never
        performed physical inventory count of properties and equipment  and
        a member of the Inventory Committee added that they should meet and
        plan on their next steps with regard to the counting  of  PPEs  and
        inventories. But, nevertheless,  they  committed  strict  adherence
        with the audit recommendations. Further, the  designated  GSO  also
        committed to prepare the required forms in the maintenance  of  the
        Municipality’s inventory.

5.7     We  recommended  that  the  Management  requires  the  Municipal
        Accountant to:


        1. Review and assess all the completed projects of the LGU  at  the
           time of his assumption;


        2.  Take  up  the  corresponding  depreciation  expenses  of  their
           classified completed projects from the time of completion  until
           December 31, 2021, and;


        3. Prepare and record adjusting  entries  to  reflect  the  correct
           amount of all the affected accounts.


    Management Comment


    5.8     The Municipal Accountant accepted  the  noted  deficiencies  and
        committed to prepare adjusting entries and  record  the  unrecorded
        completed projects of CYs 2020-2021 and  assured  strict  adherence
        with the audit recommendations.

6.9     We recommended the following:


        a. That the Municipal Accountant and  other  concerned  offices  to
           coordinate and complete the  inventory  and  accounting  of  the
           damaged PPEs due to the typhoon;


        b. The accountable officer of the damaged PPEs to submit Notice  of
           Loss and apply for a relief from accountability to COA; and


        c. The Accounting Office  to  record  the  loss  of  properties  in
           accordance with the GAM for LGUs.


    Management Comment


    6.10     The  Municipal  Engineer  agreed  that  they  will  revise  the
        assessment and submit  the  same  to  the  COA.  Additionally,  the
        Municipal  Accountant  also   agreed   to   implement   the   audit
        recommendations.

7.11    We recommended the following:


        a. That the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory
           Committee to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with  the
           Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs;


        b.  That  the  General  Services  Officer’s  Office  to  cause  the
           immediate disposal of unserviceable items and the Municipal
           Accounting Office to record the dropping from the books  of  the
           items disposed; and


        c. The Accounting Office to prepare and maintain PPE Ledgers  Cards
           and ensure that reconciliation of their records is  done  before
           preparation of the annual financial statements.




Management Comment


    7.12    The Municipal Accountant admitted that they have never performed
        physical inventory count of  properties  and  equipment,  then  one
        member of the Inventory Committee added that they should  meet  and
        plan on their next steps with regard to the counting  of  PPEs  and
        inventories. But, nevertheless,  they  committed  strict  adherence
        with the audit recommendations.

8.9      We recommended the following that:


        1. The Municipal Accountant coordinates with the Municipal Engineer
           and  reconcile  of   prior   years’   completed   projects   not
           included/booked in the PPE accounts;


        2. The Municipal Engineer conducts  inspection/validate  status  of
           identified  completed   projects   booked   as   CIP   with   no
           details/schedules/unknown status so that the proper  adjustments
           may be made to the CIP accounts;


        3. The Municipal Engineer issues Certificate of Completion  on  the
           validated  completed  projects  to  be  used  as  basis  by  the
           Municipal Accountant in the reclassification of the CIP accounts
           to the proper PPE account; and


        4. The Municipal Accountant takes up the corresponding depreciation
           expenses of their classified completed projects from the time of
           completion until  December  31,  2021,  and  henceforth  ensures
           reclassification of all completed projects in the CIP account to
           the appropriate asset accounts.


    Management Comment


    8.10    The Municipal Accountant emphasized that he needs the assistance
        of the Municipal Engineer in tracing and reconciling the  completed
        projects which were still included in  the  CIP  accounts  balance.
        Moreover, he also committed that religious monitoring of  the  said
        balance and strict adherence of the audit recommendations  will  be
        acted upon in the ensuing year. The Municipal Engineer also  agreed
        and committed to comply with the recommendations.

9.9     We recommended that the  Accounting  and  Municipal  Agriculture
        Office fast-tracks in obtaining the necessary supporting  documents
        to support the transfer of ownership of the donated  livestock  and
        to drop the amounts of the same and of the seedlings in  the  books
        of account.


    Management Comment


    9.10    The Municipal Accountant clarified that the said  accounts  were
        forwarded balance from his predecessor and he had no  knowledge  as
        to the status and its supporting documents but he  will  seek  help
        from the Municipal Agriculture  Office,  who  has  the  first  hand
        information as to the status of the above accounts.

10.8 We recommended that the Accounting Office properly and  religiously
        monitors all the records of the LGU  to  ensure  the  accuracy  and
        reliability of the reported balances in  the  Financial  Statements
        and make any necessary  adjusting  journal  entry  to  reflect  the
        correct amount of the affected accounts.

        For the current year’s erroneous entry, we suggest  the  correcting
           entry below:


        Dr. Due to National Agencies                 ₱ 10,510,669.00
            Due to LGUs                            1,618,250.24
            Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable      525,422.16
                             Cr.       Prior       Period       Adjustments
      ₱ 12,654,341.40

      Management Comment

      10.9  The  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  and  committed  that  proper
        monitoring of records will be implemented and adjustments  will  be
        made in the ensuing year. Likewise, he committed  strict  adherence
        to the audit recommendations.

11.9    We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  presents  the
        current and non-current portion of its Loans Payable in the face of
        the LGU’s financial statements to provide users  of  the  financial
        statements’ fair presentation as far as liquidity and  solvency  is
        concerned.


    Management Comment


    11.10  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  with  the  audit  observation  and
        committed strict adherence to the recommendations.

12.9     We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  maintains  a
        subsidiary ledger of each account and perform constant  review  and
        appraisal of transactions to deter  errors  and  omissions  and  to
        reconcile  accounts  and  effect  adjustments  to  reflect  correct
        balances of Due from Other Funds and Due  to  Other  Funds  in the
        Financial Statements.

    Management Comment

    12.10  The  Municipal  Accountant  explained  that  due  to   voluminous
        transactions,  he  was  not  able  to  give  time  to  prepare  the
        subsidiary ledgers of the reciprocal accounts of  Due  to  and  Due
        from Other Funds. However, in the ensuing years,  he  committed  to
        religiously prepare the SLs of the said accounts.

13.11 We recommended that the Management:


        1. Requires the  Office  in-charged  to  accrue  the  earned  leave
           benefits of the unpaid retired employees and  elected  officials
           and that are expected to retire the following year;


        2. Obliges the HRMO to monitor the accumulated leave credits of the
           Master List of the LGU especially the employees who are expected
           to retire  within  the  year  and  to  furnish  a  copy  to  the
           Accounting Office;


        3. After ensuring the reliability of the accumulated  leave  credit
           balances of employees as of  December  31,  2021,  requires  the
           Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to record the
           Accrued  Terminal   Leave   Benefits   due   to   the   retiring
           employees/officials in the ensuing year; and


              Terminal Leave Benefits (current year)          xxx
                 Leave Benefit Payable                   xxx

        4. Direct the MBO to annually appropriate for the accrued  terminal
           leave benefits earned by retired employees and officials even on
           a staggered basis depending on the availability of funds.


    Management Comment

    13.12 The Municipal Accountant remarked that it  was  not  practiced  to
        record the same since they usually wait of  the  appropriation  for
        the  purpose,  however,  he  understood  the  recommendations   and
        committed to record the terminal leave  benefits  accruing  to  the
        retired officials or employees in the ensuing year.

14.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant makes  the  necessary
        adjusting entries to report the correct  balance  of  the  affected
        accounts. Further, follow  strictly  the  recording  and  reporting
        guidelines for LDRMMF as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002  dated
        September 12, 2012. Presented below are the Audit Team’s  suggested
        accounting entries:


        A. Unrecorded transferred unexpended balance of ₱ 666,952.82


        Dr. Prior Period Adjustment          ₱ 666,952.82
                     Cr.  Trust  Liabilities-DRRMF                        ₱
           666,952.82


        B. For the transactions not closed to  its  appropriate  asset  and
           expense accounts


        |GF Books                   |TF Books                   |
|Year-end Accounting Entries                            |
|NONE                       |TL-DRRMF                   |
|                           |99,750.00                  |
|                           |    PPA                    |
|                           |99,750.00                  |
|Transfer of asset to the General Fund                  |
|Information & Comm. Tech.  |TL_DRRMF                   |
|Equip   122,931.60         |163,628.03                 |
|Communication Equipment    |   Information & Comm.     |
|40,696.43                  |Tech. Equip 122,931.60     |
|    Gov’t Equity           |  Communication Equipment  |
|163,628.03                 |40,696.43                  |

    Management Comment

    14.12 The Municipal Accountant assured that  adjustments  will  be  made
        with the affected accounts to have strict adherence  to  the  audit
        recommendations.

15.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


        1.  Makes necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the errors
           made to  ensure  that  transactions  are  properly  recorded  to
           reflect in  the  financial  statements  the  actual  results  of
           operation for every calendar year; and


        2. Reviews thoroughly that only actual and valid expenses shall  be
           taken up in the books using the  appropriate  expenses  accounts
           and be recorded in the period to which these expenses relate.


    Management Comment

    15.9    The Municipal Accountant confirmed that he already adjusted  the
        expenses and will submit a copy of the JEVs to the COA, however, he
        also emphasized that he needs    an  accounting  assistant  who  is
        competent enough to assist the massive transactions to be monitored
        in  the  Office.  Nevertheless,  he  committed  to  implement   the
        recommendations.

16.8 We recommended the Accounting Office  to  establish  and  properly
        maintain Subsidiary  Ledgers  (SL)  for  those  accounts  that  are
        composed   of   several    specific    accounts/category/accounting
        responsibilities, and make sure  that  beginning  balances  carried
        forward in a particular year in the SL should be the same with the
        ending balances in the previous year.

    Management Comment

    16.9    The Municipal Accountant revealed that they are only waiting for
        the installation of the Accounting System  in  order  for  them  to
        implement  the  audit  recommendations   in   the   ensuing   year.
        Nevertheless,   he   committed   to   implement   all   the   audit
        recommendations.

17.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly applies  the
        proper accounting principles in keeping and recording  of  accounts
        as provided in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and  other
        regulations in relation to the Trust Fund transactions, in order to
        present fairly the correct balances in the Financial Statements.



    Management Comment

    17.13   Municipal Accountant agreed and committed  strict  adherence  to
        the audit recommendations.

18.7    We recommended that charges of 20% Development Fund shall be for
        projects which contribute to social and  economic  development  and
        environmental management in accordance with pertinent provisions of
        DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated  November  4,  2020  and  purchase  of
        administrative office furniture and fixtures, or appliances  should
        be appropriately charged against the General Fund.


    Management Comment

    18.8    The Municipal Engineer confirmed the observation  and  that  the
        items enumerated above were  inappropriately  charged  to  the  20%
        Development  Fund.  However,  they   assured   to   implement   the
        recommendations in the ensuing years.

19.10 We recommended that the Management:


        1. Carefully plans the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to ensure  that
           the development projects identified  are  feasible  and  can  be
           implemented within the year with the proposed budget allocation
           and;


        2. Ensures that  20%  Development  Fund  and  it’s  PPAs  be  fully
           utilized and monitored in order to  immediately  address  issues
           that will be  encountered  in  the  project  implementation  and
           achieve the optimum desirable socio-economic  and  environmental
           development.

      Management Comment

      19.11 The Municipal Budget Officer commented that  the  projects  were
        reprogrammed to rehabilitate the Municipal Building  which  damaged
        caused by typhoon. The Municipal Engineer seconded and  added  that
        they used it for the roofing of the building and other projects  in
        response to the typhoon. Moreover, they  agreed  and  committed  to
        implement the audit recommendations.

20.7 We recommended that the Management refrains from using  the  LDRRMF
        for payment of administrative costs and  strictly  utilize  the  5%
        LDRRMF only for disaster risk management activities as provided  in
        the above guidelines so as to ensure that the objectives set  forth
        in the allocation of  the  fund  are  achieved  thru  proper  usage
        thereof.

      Management Comment:

      20.8  The MDRRM Officer assured that in the ensuing  years  they  will
        charge administrative expenditures to the General Fund and that the
        Plan should  also  be  carefully  formulated.  They  confirmed  and
        committed to implement the audit recommendations.

21.8    We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, MDRRM Officer  and
        Budget Officer stops charging of expenses in excess of the allotted
        budget, and regularly  monitor  the  spending  against  a  specific
        appropriation in order to avoid incurrence of expenses in excess of
        the budget.


Management Comment:

      21.9  The MBO commented that they were not able  to  properly  monitor
        the fund, and that the excess was used to buy seeds for the farmers
        as their response after  the  Typhoon.  Likewise,  they  agreed  to
        implement the audit recommendations.

22.9 We recommended that the Management  ensures  that  augmentation  of
        savings is always  supported  by  an  appropriation  ordinance  and
        strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160 to ensure  the  validity
        and legality of the newly programmed PPAs and its implementation.

Management Comment:

      22.10 The Municipal Budget Officer commented that  they  thought  that
        only the Resolution was required. However, they  will  comply  with
        the recommendation starting the ensuing year.

23.7    We recommended that  Management  requires  the  MDRRM  and  MSWD
        Officers for immediate submission of Relief Distribution Sheets  of
        all the related transactions above  as  proof  that  all  available
        relief  goods  were  actually  and  fully  received   by   intended
        beneficiaries and strictly comply  to  attach  all  the  supporting
        documents in the ensuing transactions.

Management Comment:

      23.8  The MSWDO commented that  RDS  were  already  submitted  to  the
        Municipal Accounting Office, however, it was the Accounting  Office
        who failed to forward the same to the COA. The Municipal Accountant
        commented to furnish the audit team a copy of the RDS.

24.7     We recommended that Management updates the list  of  properties
        that need to be insured and  allocate  funding  for  the  insurance
        coverage of at the least newly constructed building  and  purchased
        motor vehicle/s to protect the same against loss or damage which in
        this case by acts of nature, then the LGU can have at least recover
        the insurable amount and minimized the risks.

25.8    We recommended  that  the  Management  directs  the  responsible
        official/s  of  the  Municipality  to  submit  the  Memorandum   of
        Agreements (MOAs) of the above fund transfers. Henceforth,  in  the
        ensuing years that all  MOAs  covering  fund  transfers  should  be
        submitted to the Audit Team within  the  prescribed  period  for  a
        timely and thorough audit.

Management Comment:

      25.9  The Municipal Budget Officer commented that  they  will  try  to
        ask the Provincial Government to provide MOAs of the fund transfers
        and they agreed and assured to implement the audit recommendations.

26.9    We recommended that the Management: (a) requires  the  Municipal
        Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline,  oil
        and  lubricants  are  duly  supported  with  completely   filled-up
        driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on  the  propriety  and
        reasonableness of fuel consumption and (b)  directs  the  concerned
        personnel to prepare and submit to the Auditor, on a monthly basis,
        the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the  Monthly  Report  of
        Fuel  Consumption  for  proper  analysis,  verification   and   the
        necessary audit action on fuel consumption.


Management Comment:

      26.10 The Municipal Accountant  commented  that  the  only  supporting
        documents they  have  are  the  billings  from  the  supplier,  and
        confirmed that no Driver’s Trip Tickets and  monthly  reports  were
        maintained. The Municipal Engineer also  added  that  the  BFP  did
        allow them to stock oils and fuel that’s why  they  cannot  buy  in
        bulk. However, they  agreed  to  comply  and  implement  the  audit
        recommendations starting 2022.

27.10 We recommended  that  the  Management  immediately  designates  an
        employee who shall be responsible  person  to  facilitate  the  LTO
        renewal of registration and insurance of all motor  vehicles  owned
        by the Municipality. More so, apply and/or renew  the  registration
        of all serviceable motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO  and
        contracts of insurance with the GSIS within the prescribed time  to
        avoid additional expenses in terms  of  penalties  and  payment  of
        damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.

Management Comment:

       27.11  The  Management  assured  that  they  will  designate  certain
        personnel to process  the  renewal  of  the  LTO  registration  and
        insurance of all vehicles of the LGU.

28.8    We recommended the Accounting  Office  to  cause  the  immediate
        preparation and submission of all the  required  Financial  Reports
        for CY 2021 and strictly comply with the rules and  regulations  on
        the preparation and submission of it thereafter.

      Management Comment

      28.9  The Municipal Accountant assured to  submit  the  reports  since
        they  already  have  prepared  them  and  agreed  to   consistently
        implement the recommendations.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation letters to different government agencies and private persons identified as debtors and/or demand payment thereof. Henceforth, request write off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 and Section 36 of PD 1445. We further recommend for a regular review of all account balances so that dormant accounts can immediately be identified and acted upon.

We recommend that management require the accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate their cash advances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Issue demand letter on unliquidated cash advances and remind grantees on their responsibilities in the liquidation of cash advances within the required period.

Likewise, institute measures to enforce settlement of outstanding cash advances through deduction from the salary of the official or employee who fails to comply with the requirement on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the registration and transfer of title of the land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the municipality in compliance with Section 39 of PD No.1445. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor have to reconcile their records of the land owned by the LGU.

We recommend that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies, remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project and/or effect any adjustments, if warranted, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency and can be used for other projects of the LGU.

We recommend that the management require the Local Finance Committee and the  Municipal Budget Officer to prepare a budget based on a reasonable estimate of income and monitor and control that disbursements does not exceed the released allotment. Likewise, minimize the expenses of the LGU to at least break even by limiting total disbursements to fifty percent (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue accruing to the fund in addition to the actual collections, provided that no cash overdraft shall be incurred, pursuant to Section 158 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I, in order not to incur net loss of current income.

We recommend to management to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 309 of Republic Act 7160 and Section 4 (3) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 on the utilization of Trust Fund. Henceforth, stop the practice of paying various expenditures which are not related to the purpose of the trust was created or the funds was received as required under existing regulations. Likewise, provide an explanation on using trust fund for expenditures other than the intended purpose.

We recommend that management avoid the procurement of services for tasks that are already under the duty of a regular employee. We also recommend that management ensure the appropriateness of expenditures, keeping in mind that they are necessary and not redundant.

We recommend that management submit to the audit team a justification for charging against the 20% DF the above-mentioned transaction together with the lacking supporting documents, including the supplemental budget and supplemental investment program covering the same. Likewise, we also recommend that management submit an explanation for the certification of the disbursement voucher despite the note stating that there are no supporting documents. Further, submit the basis for the selection of the 2,400 people listed as recipients.

We recommend that the Municipal officials exercise fiscal restraint in the disbursement of funds and stop the practice of utilizing the trust fund for current operating expenditures and utilize it for the purpose for which it was created or funds was receive. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to limit the disbursement of funds to valid obligations only as appropriated in the Approved Annual Budget to avoid the incurrence of any cash overdraft at year end. Furthermore, strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 337 of RA 7160, otherwise all those found responsible in the incurrence of the cash overdraft will be held personally liable pursuant to Section 158 of GAAM Volume I.

We recommend that management require the Property Officer to conduct inspections before and after the repair of properties, prepare the corresponding reports and support the payment with Pre and Post Repair Inspection Report. Also, furnish copies of the reports to this Commission within five days from the date of evaluation/inspection.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant and  Municipal Budget Officer to submit the pre-closing trial balance supported with SAAOB on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.

We recommend that management submit justification of the non-creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office. Further, we recommend that the management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.

We recommend that management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that management shift to a computerized accounting system and install the e-NGAS software to ensure correct accounting records and financial statements. We also recommend that in the meantime, the Municipal Accountant have to carefully review the accounting records the accounting staff prepared.

1.22 We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to obtain
the Bank Statement of the eleven (11) depository accounts under Trust
Fund and submit these to the Municipal Accountant. Henceforth, obtain
monthly a snapshot of bank account transactions of all depository accounts of
the LGU while bank statements from the depository banks are not yet
received.

1.23 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement of the eleven depository
accounts referred to in paragraph 1.9 and submit these to the Audit Team
for review;
b. restore the unreleased checks referred to in paragraph 1.18 if these are still
unclaimed as of May 31, 2022;
c. inform the depository bank in writing that the amount debited to the
account for CY 2021 was more than the semi-annual amortization as per
loan agreement and reconcile payments made with the bank records so
proper adjustments can be made and the actual balance of the bank
account can be corrected and conduct actual reconciliation of the records
with the bank to determine which record needs to be adjusted and to affect
the necessary corrections to avoid inaccurate and misleading information;
and
d. prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all depository
accounts regularly and submit the same to the Audit Team for review.

1.24 We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate
with the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no cash overdraft is incurred at
any given time of the year. Monthly monitoring of collections and
disbursements should be conducted to ensure availability of funds for all
disbursements to avoid difficulty in settling obligations and ensure stability of
fiscal resources.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
e. require Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to record the
recoupment of the advance payment to contractors as discussed in
paragraph 2.4 to put the account Advances to Contractors in order;
f. require Circle Z Ventures and Development Corporation to post and
submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit, a bank guarantee or a surety
bond callable upon demand equivalent to the its outstanding balance issued
by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance
Commission;
g. require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to
determine and locate the documents for the unfinished project, prepare
Statement of Work Accomplished and compare the total amount with the
amount billed to the contractor and refund the unrecouped advance
payment; and
h. strictly adhere with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184
relative to advance payment to contractors.

3.22 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to:
i. maintain a complete and up-dated Property, Plant and Equipment
Ledger Card and Real Property Ledger Card, so that all PPEs are
properly accounted for;
j. prepare adjusting entries to recognize donated equipment from DOLE and
DOH;
k. derecognize items below the capitalization threshold of P15,000.00
and recognize the same as prior period adjustment.
l. prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the incorrectly recorded PPE
to its proper account.
m. Reconcile the PPELC and RPLC with the RPCPPE
n. Prepare the necessary entries based on the result of the reconciliation to
reflect the correct balances of the accounts.
2. MGSO to:
o. submit the documents on the donated properties to the Municipal
Accountant for properly recording.
p. ensure updated RPCPPE with complete information and unit value.
q. issue a Property Acknowledgment Receipt for donated equipment and
Inventory Custodian Slip for items below the capitalization threshold in
lieu of the previously issued Property Acknowledgment Receipt.
3. Officials and employees concerned to strictly implement the guidelines
and procedures required in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.

4.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant:
s. to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for inventories as
provided in Section 114 of the Manual for NGAS for LGUs;
t. to submit documents such as RSMI with RIS and/or Distribution Sheets to
substantiate the issuances/consumption/utilization of the Accountable Forms,
Plate and Stickers, Food Supplies Inventory, Fuel Oil and Lubricant and
Agricultural and Marine Supplies;
u. to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for every stock number and reconcile
these with the Stock Cards of the GSO and the RPCI of the Inventory
Committee;

4.17 We also recommended that the MGSO:
v. issue supplies from stock room based on properly accomplished and duly
approved RIS;
w. prepare and submit the SSMI duly supported with RIS weekly to the
Accounting Office for recording of issuances of inventory items;
x. maintain and update regularly Stock Cards for each type of supplies per
stock number reconciled with the RPCI at the end of the year;
y. conduct regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant.

4.18 We further recommended that the Municipal Health Officer shall prepare a
report on the issuances of Drugs and Medicines in the RHU per Fund Source
and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.

5.12 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
z. Municipal Agriculturist to determine the fair market value of the
biological assets of the Municipality at every reporting date, furnish the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO)
with documents and updated reports relative to additions, distribution,
reproduction and death of the breeding stocks;
aa. MGSO to maintain WOABSPC and validate the correctness of the
submitted report from the OMA;
bb. Municipal Accountant to record the changes in the carrying amount of
breeding stocks, maintain WOABSLC and disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statement the reconciliation of changes in the carrying amount
of biological assets between the beginning and the end of the current
period; and
cc. Strict compliance with the provisions of IPSAS 27 and MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

6.9 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
dd. Stop the practice of retaining collections, instead, deposit all collections to
the AGDB at least once a week;
ee. Stop using collections for operating expenses of the Municipality, instead,
charge petty non-recurring expenses to the Petty Cash Fund (PCF).

6.10 We also recommended to increase the PCF to an amount sufficient to cover
the Municipality’s non-recurring expenses for one month and improve its
operation by requiring the Petty Cash Custodian to replenish it upon reaching
50% utilization.

6.11 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to furnish his office copy of the Report of Collections and Deposits
every week.

8.12 We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
officers and employees concerned to:
ff. Remit to the NHA the available amount of P2,054,269.14 (net of bank’s
required maintaining balance) with an accompanying request for
condonation of any accumulated penalties and surcharges;
gg. Prepare a Conditional Contract to Sell with each Project Beneficiary or
Lot Buyers with the authority of the Sangguniang Bayan, to protect the
interest of the Municipality;
hh. Require the Municipal Treasurer to collect monthly the amortization from
each Lot Buyers and remit the proceeds to NHA immediately in the
succeeding month as required in the MOA.
ii. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit explanation on the non-
payment of the loan despite the availability of funds.

8.13 We further recommended that the Municipality will execute a Housing Lot
Loan Agreement between the Lot Buyers or Project Beneficiaries with the
authority of the Sangguniang Bayan, to clearly outline the parties’ obligations,
rights and duties.

9.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
jj. require the Municipal Accountant to submit justification why payment
was made to these claims despite the deficient and lacking supporting
documents; and
kk. direct the BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to submit immediately
the lacking contract documents within five (5) days from receipt of this
AOM. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

9.9. Moving forward, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee shall
exert their best effort that all supporting documents to the contracts are
completely filled out and compliant with the requirements of Section 3.1.2 of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and Section 17.1 of the
RIRR of RA 9184 and submit these to the Audit Team within five (5) days
after perfection of the contract.

10.5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and other officials and employees concerned to submit to the
Audit Team the lacking appropriate documents presented in Table 1 within 5
days from receipt of this Memo. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a
Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

10.6. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit explanation for
the Audit Team’s evaluation why disbursement vouchers were approved for
payment despite the lacking documents. Henceforth, ensure that all
disbursements should be supported with complete documentations in
accordance with the provisions in PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

11.7 We recommended that the MPDC should ensure inclusion of Occupational
Safety and Health as separate pay item in every infrastructure project
implemented in compliance with DPWH D.O. No. 56 s. 2005 dated April 20,
2005.

11.8 We also recommended that the compliance on Construction Safety and Health
Program shall be required on all winning bidders and monitor the
implementation of LGU projects that the workers are adequately protected
against occupational risks.

12.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
ll. submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents and a written explanation
why the disbursement vouchers were approved despite the deficiency; and
mm. henceforth, review the completeness of the supporting documents of the
disbursement vouchers before forwarding these to the Municipal Mayor
for approval.

12.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer review the completeness
of the signatures on the disbursement voucher before a check is prepared and
signed and demand from the suppliers the issuance of properly dated official
receipts or sales invoice as acknowledgment of the payment to avoid
suspension in audit.

12.13 These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice(s) of
Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

13.6 We recommended that the MGSO ensure:
nn. all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality should bear government red
plates and should be marked “For Official Use Only”;
oo. the official use of motor vehicles should be supported with properly filled-
out Driver’s Trip Ticket;
pp. prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Monthly Report of
Official Travel.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and General Services Officer to reconcile the discrepancies
between the PPE balances per accounting and property records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:

a. The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all properties
owned by the municipality and prepare and submit the RPCPPE not
later than January 31 of each year; and,
b. The Appraisal and Disposal Committee to take immediate action to
facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable items in line
with PD 1445 and PPSAS 17; and comply with the preparation of IIRUP
for the identified unserviceable items, thereafter, proceed to the disposal
processes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to
observe strict compliance with COA Circular 2012-002 especially the
inclusion of the list of identified projects and activities to be charged to the
unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the annual LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that claims for RATA are valid, proper and
supported with complete documentation.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as a result of our audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Administrator and Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials and
employees to study and consider the shifting to automated payroll system
over manual system to tighten internal control over payroll funds and
minimize operating costs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that liquidation of cash advances for payroll be
supported with approved DTRs, Certificates of Service, or accomplishment
reports.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the certification attesting to the truthfulness of the
contents of the report in Item b of DBM and DOH Joint Circular Nos. 1
and 2, both dated November 25, 2020, to confirm compliance with the
circular.

We recommended the following courses of action:

a) The Municipal Engineer to immediately prepare the revised plans and
designs of the Construction of Multi-Purpose Building;
b) The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), in
coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to ensure that at the
planning stage, every reasonable effort is exerted to determine and
address any issue that may affect the implementation of a project so as
not to cause the delay or non-implementation of the same, otherwise the
budget should be realigned to other priority development projects in
the LGU; and
c) The MPDC and Municipal Engineer to immediately implement the
Construction of the Multi-Purpose Building and Access Road to
Cemetery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to
facilitate the implementation of the identified PPAs funded under
LDRRMF in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the
municipality for the occurrence of threats and calamities.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to take the following courses of
action:

a. Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to lay down the issues upon which
the implementation of the Innovative Aquasilviculture Livelihood
Project becomes difficult or impossible; and
b. Make representations with the head of the Department of Labor and
Employment – Regional Office in pursuing the immediate and proper
implementation of the project, if still feasible, or in the return of the
fund to the source agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a) District Supervisor to optimize the utilization of Special Education
Fund Budget pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1,
series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017, DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, series of 2020 (Addendum No. 1) and DepEd-DBM-
DILG Joint Circular No. 2, series of 2020 (Addendum No. 2); and
b) GSO – Designate and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking
Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) and/or Requisition and Issue
Slip (RIS).

We recommended that management:

Regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with the subsidiary ledgers to ensure accuracy of the cash accounts; and

Prepare and timely submit bank reconciliation statements for all accounts maintained by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to conduct the reconciliation of cashbooks with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately submit the financial reports and strictly observe the timely submission of such reports within the prescribed period pursuant to the abovementioned guideline.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory account all purchases of supplies and materials upon acceptance of the delivered items and only recognize the expense upon consumption or distribution;

Direct the General Services Officer to ensure that all issuances are supported with Requisition and Issue Slip which shall be summarized in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued and shall be submitted the same to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the corresponding expense; and

Observe proper recording of transactions as prescribed under Volume III of Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units in order to provide a fair financial statement presentation.

We recommended that management:

Ensure strict compliance with the pertinent provisions of law, rules, and regulations relative to the utilization of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities;

Revise the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/ Project/Activity Implementation and provide the lacking information; and

Send written notices to House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other offices, once the revised Report has been posted on the website.

We recommended that management:

Submit the lacking documents pertaining to the procurement transactions;

Submit an explanation on the lacking supporting documents of the procurement transactions; and

Strictly follow the guidelines spelled out in the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and use the appropriate alternative method of procurement in order to preclude the issuance of audit suspension/ disallowance.

We recommended that management should secure the necessary funding authority of the budgeted PPAs in a form of an appropriation ordinance and ensure that such an authority is secured before any subsequent disbursements/ utilization of the funds are made.

Also, we recommended strict adherence to the provisions of the law in the disbursements/ utilization of government funds to avoid suspension/ disallowance in the audit.

We recommended that management should secure the necessary funding authority of the budgeted PPAs and ensure that such authority is secured before any subsequent disbursements/ utilization of the funds are made.

observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply with the audit recommendation
observations.reason: The amount of P3,030,535.45 was unutilized which pertained to the implementation of Local Economic Development Projects for 16 Barangays (The balance of P2,030,535.45 allocated for Brgys. Iba and Maputi) and Purchase of Lot for Resettlement Site for Victims of Storm Surge Coastal Flooding in Brgy. Baon. (P1,000,000) Reiterated as Finding No. 7 in this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant shall record in the appropriate Inventory account all purchases of supplies, materials and relief goods upon acceptance of the delivered items and only recognize an expense upon consumption or distribution; and

Ensure that all issuances are supported with RIS which shall be summarized in the RSMI and shall submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the corresponding expense.

We recommended that management submit immediately the lacking documents and ensure compliance with the prompt submission of financial transaction documents. Otherwise, the penal provision under Section 122 of PD 1445 shall be applied.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the analysis of the balance of the Government Equity account under the Trust Fund and to determine and effect the appropriate reclassification or adjusting entries.

We recommended that management:

Adequately plan and study the feasibility of each project before proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by the 20% LGDF;

Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the speedy implementation of the projects; and 

Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents in terms of PPAs to be implemented under 20% LGDF.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply with the audit recommendation
observations.reason: The amount of P3,030,535.45 was unutilized which pertained to the implementation of Local Economic Development Projects for 16 Barangays (The balance of P2,030,535.45 allocated for Brgys. Iba and Maputi) and Purchase of Lot for Resettlement Site for Victims of Storm Surge Coastal Flooding in Brgy. Baon. (P1,000,000) Reiterated as Finding No. 7 in this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Provide all the information deemed necessary in the evaluation of the projects to be presented in details upon submission of the report in order to establish reliability of the accomplishment embodied therein; and

Include those which are to be Implemented by the Municipality, whether infrastructure projects or other form of PPAs so as to facilitate prompt monitoring and validation thereof.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by management. The report submitted included only those infrastructure projects to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: 

MHO and all offices of the municipality who are recipients of goods/supplies from any government offices or private individuals/ entities to ensure that at all times a copy of the Invoice Receipt for Property, Property Transfer Receipt, RIS, Deed of Donation, or any document evidencing the goods/supplies received by their office shall be given to the MACCO; and 

The MACCO to record the said receipt in appropriate income and inventory accounts as soon as a copy of the document evidencing the receipt of goods/supplies is received, and likewise draw necessary entry to expense the same upon submission of the end-users utilization report.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and its concerned key officials stop approving advance payment of COVID-19 related expenses, without observing the pertinent provisions of MO No. 28.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman to post information of Procurement Projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act and refer to GPPB Circular and GPPB Advisories cited in this AOM for reference and guidance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to follow the format of APP with the guide provided in the GPPB website and shall see to it that the  projects/activities/programs code/identification in the APP will be the same code/identification forwarded to the PMR so that these are easily linked. 

Also, we recommended that the Procurement Strategy (page 47/120) of GPM Volume 1 be referred to in the preparation PPMP/APP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with its concerned department heads ensure that granting of allowances and other personnel benefits conform with existing law, rules and regulations authorizing the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the head of the LDRRMC: 

1. Formulate the LDRRMF-IP prior to the passing of the Annual and Supplemental Budgets; 

2. Direct the MBO and the Local Finance Committee to ensure that no lump sum appropriation should be provided in the approved Annual and Supplemental Budgets; and 

3. Secure prior approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for every contract to be executed or entered in instances that it is charged/sourced from the lump sum appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the MSWDO to:

Submit justification on the: 355 beneficiaries who presented IDs that do not belong to the primary and secondary IDs required;  17 beneficiaries who have no IDs; 1,498 beneficiaries who have no specific certification or ID; 1,151 beneficiaries who have no barangay certification per format required in the implementing guidelines;  23 beneficiaries who have no specimen signature in the documents supporting the payroll; 75  beneficiaries whose specimen signatures were different from their signatures  in the payroll and in the documents supporting the same and lastly; and  13 beneficiaries’ encoded name using the SAC form which are different from the beneficiaries’ name in the payroll. 

Require the 56 out 53 beneficiaries with UMID to secure certification from their respective barangays that they are in need of such assistance at the time when the quarantine implementation was strictly enforced, or to submit certification from their respective employers/DOLE that:  they are “no work no pay” during the ECQ periods; and they did not receive any cash subsidy under the DOLE amelioration program for private employees. 

Conduct barangay-to-barangay campaigns to encourage those under the vulnerable sectors (i.e. PWD, Solo-Parents, Senior Citizens) to register and secure appropriate ID from the MSWDO for them to be able to conveniently access government’s services accorded to them; 

Maintain an updated database of the municipality’s residents through better coordination with Puroks and Barangays to properly identify possible beneficiaries of social amelioration programs of the government and to expedite the delivery of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure from the Sangguniang Bayan a resolution confirming its authority to accept donations from various sources for COVID-19 related purposes to rectify the absence of such during the actual receipt.

We recommended that Management instructs all department heads and/ or concerned offices to fully comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 and designate a focal person to consolidate the reports submitted by each department and to thoroughly review the reports before submission to COA in hard and electronic copies.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is designated as focal office that will receive and consolidate all the PPA quarterly report from other offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government of San Isidro immediately establish the MDRRMO and hire qualified personnel as required under RA 10121 and JMC Circular No. 2014-1.  The creation of LDRRMO in every LGU is one of the basic elements in disaster preparedness.  It is primarily responsible for carrying out or implementing various disaster risk reduction and management plans/programs of the municipality to ensure the safety and protection of the LGU when disaster/calamity occurs.

We recommended that:

The MACCO and the MEO reconcile their records to determine the actual status of the said projects;

The MEO provide the MACCO with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents to serve as the basis for the transfer of these projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; and

Based on the adjusted data, the MACCO computes the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.
The MACCO and Municipal Treasurer immediately facilitate the return of the unutilized balance to the source agency or deposit the amount to the National Treasury.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoins the Municipal Engineer to fast track the completion of all projects so that desired benefits are immediately enjoyed by the people.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the HOPE require the BAC and other participants on the procurement of goods to strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 dated October 28, 2016 and other issuances related to procurement. Otherwise, any procurement not in accordance with the procurement law shall be disallowed in audit. Likewise, we recommended that the supporting documents attach to the claim be thoroughly checked before approval for payment is made.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking reports and henceforth, observe the submission within the prescribed period of the Report of Collections and Deposits, DVs, LRs, JEVs and general journal transactions to the Audit Team.

observations.reason: Not yet reverted because they are still contemplating to continue the project by administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1.	Stop the practice of assigning job order personnel to collect revenues;
	
Utilize the existing bonded collectors to collect revenues under the strict   supervision of the Municipal Treasurer; and

If warranted by funds, consider the regularization of the job order personnel so as they can perform collection functions.
observations.reason: Only field collectors remain under job-order status.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that a copy of the report of inspection or its equivalent shall be submitted to the Head of the Auditing Unit within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered and, in the case of unserviceable and disposable property/assets, immediately after inspection thereof by management.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipality assign the inspection of the procured items to personnel not under the control or supervision of the supply/property officer of the Municipality who is entrusted to accept the items delivered.
observations.reason: Management still failed to submit the AIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management immediately use the amount deposited in the HYSA to pay off portion of the principal amount of the loan to economically manage its resources.

To resolve this observation, we recommend the management to create a committee to work on the titling of the acquired lots and to undertake measures necessary to hasten the documentation for the absolute ownership of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issues of demand letters to (i) notify the concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was not received, withhold from their salaries considering that they have been notified and given more adequate time to liquidate; (ii) employees who already resigned and transferred to immediately cause the liquidation and settlement of the unliquidated balances; request the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances of the deceased employees; reclassify “Other Receivable” account to Advances to Officers and Employees account in order to present fairly the total balance of unsettled cash advances; ensure the completeness of prescribed supporting documents for the granting of cash advances in processing of claims for payment thereof; not grant additional cash advance to concerned officials and employees unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and strictly comply with the provisions of the aforementioned Circulars to avoid penalty stated thereon.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to the concerned employees and officials but no follow-up was done by the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provides a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects. For existing road networks, the Municipal Assessor assists the Municipal Engineer in the assessment of the fair value of the road lot component of local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government; the Municipal Accountant recognizes the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices still conducting actual valuation of the components of road networks and report was still pending for submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MTO exerts more effort in the collection of delinquent taxes, especially those due for over 10 years, otherwise, apply the provision of Section 259 of the RA 7160 regarding penalty for failure to issue and execute warrant of levy.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal treasurer presented already its collection strategy with the additional inputs from the Local Finance committee

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the concerned offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The local revenue code of the LGU was already updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly implement actions to strengthen its collection and monitoring procedures; and direct Local Finance Committee to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and realistic projection of revenues and conduct an analysis of identified revenue accounts with decreasing trend in collection.
observations.managementAction: The Annual Budget of the LGU was properly plan and executed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the TUPAD LGU Coordinator and the MSWD Office notify the concerned beneficiary and take necessary actions and also for other concerned personnel to thoroughly review the qualified beneficiaries of the any assistance from the government to avoid any errors, duplications and other matters.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office obtains and maintains an updated record of all indigent and in-need individuals per barangay to fast track the distribution of the assistance and improves the system of distribution for future similar occurrences. Also, establishes adequate guidelines on coordination mechanisms so as not to incur the same deficiencies in the future.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Officer updates themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and ensures the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office installs a scheme to facilitate the timely remittance of withheld amount to different government institutions and should strictly comply with law so as not to deprive the officials and employees of further benefits and to avoid the burden of any surcharges and interests not due to them.
observations.managementAction: Withheld remittances for CY 2021 were remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant scrutinizes carefully the disbursement claims of the leagues of local elective officials for membership dues and contribution and determine if the rates/amounts claimed are strictly in accordance with their respective constitution and by-laws; the Municipal Treasurer not to release payments to leagues without the government prescribed O.R. or any commitment letter from the league for issuance thereof; and requires leagues annual accomplish reports, as a prerequisite for the renewal of membership fees in the ensuing years, to ascertain that government funds have been utilized for lawful and exclusively for public purposes.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management shall strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also for the procurement of goods. Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with the at least 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount, otherwise any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period be charged to concerned approving officials.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is already compliant in withholding warranty security in their goods procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stops the payment of honorarium to the Comelec Official and strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth by the Department of Budget and Management implementing the certain provisions of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: No further payments were made to the COMELEC Official.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS appropriately identifies PPAs that are gender-responsive and gender-sensitive for inclusion to its GAD Plan and stop disturbing GAD Funds to PPAs that are not related to gender and development.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System properly plans and regularly monitors the implementation of GAD PPAs and maximize the utilization of the GAD budget to achieve the objective of the government regarding the institutionalization of GAD in the community.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. require officers and employees to submit the payrolls with the approved DTRs and its mandated format by CSC, application for leave and other pertinent supporting documents as required in Sections 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; b. demand the HRMO to recompute/recalculate the balances of employees’ leave credits to reflect the correct balances on their leave cards and; c. direct concerned personnel who prepares the payroll to exclude those employees, who failed to submit their DTRs, in the payroll to adopt the “NO DTR, NO SALARY” policy and to ensure the prompt submission of their DTRs.
observations.managementAction: DTRs were properly supported with appropriate supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Formulate measures that will protect its personnel against delayed payment of compensation that could cause demoralization, hence personnel’s productivity will not be optimized. And also, the declared policy of the State should be observed and adhered into in order to help address the needs of the people, particularly the job orders rendering their services in this Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Wages of the Job Orders were paid within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accounting Office to stop the granting of fixed rate of ₱150.00 and ₱1,500.00 for Laundry and Subsistence Allowance, respectively, and follow strictly the rules and regulations on the grant of compensation-related Magna Carta benefits to PHWs based on their Daily Time Records and/or actual number of workdays rendered.
observations.managementAction: Payment of Laundry and Subsistence Allowance to PHW were already based on the actual number of hours of their DTRs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish a strong internal control in the implementation of weekly deposit of collections to the depository banks to avoid possible loss, misuse and misappropriation of government funds. And ensure that the deposited collections are intact and complete.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office already established guidelines in the implementation of weekly remittance and deposit of collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the MENRO-Designate to persistently follow-up the NSWMC regarding the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality and demand the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to support the MENRO-Designate in the implementation of the solid waste programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year Solid Waste Management of the LGU was already approved by the National Solid Waste Management Council.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We have reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor:

1.9.1 Require the Municipal Accountant – Designate and Treasurer to
submit the remaining DVs and all other reports/documents enumerated
in item 1.6 as soon as possible;
1.9.2 Determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to comply
with the required submission dates of accounts and reports;
1.9.3 Hire a qualified Municipal Accountant or assign additional personnel
in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate prompt submission
of accounts and reports;
1.9.4 Ensure that the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Acting
Municipal Treasurer exert all necessary efforts to render the agency’s
accounts within the prescribed period; and
1.9.5 Consider imposing sanctions to officers and employees due to failure to
render reports and accounts as provided for in Section 122 of PD 1445.

2.10 We have reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant - Designate henceforth
maintain, on a timely basis, the required registries, accomplish the required
obligation requests as well as the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and
Obligations.

3.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate review
the journal entries made for the above transactions and consider the
following proposed adjusting entry:
Account Name Debit Credit
Advances to Officers and Employees 550,000.00
Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account 10,142.54
Due to BIR 101.15
Due to PhilHealth 51,908.10
Due to NGAs 6,875.00
Due to LGUs 127,621.30
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable 0.01
Other Payables 32,285.38
Prior Period Adjustments/Government Equity 341,351.60

3.5 We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
coordinate with the LGU’s depository bank in order to identify the nature
of the credit/debit memos reflected in the bank statements and make the
necessary adjustments in the books.

3.6 We have also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant –
Designate:

3.6.1 Analyze transactions carefully prior to recording in the book of
accounts;
3.6.2 Prepare monthly reconciliation statements, not only with the records
