per bank, but also with the records per cashbook of the Municipal
Treasurer, and ensure that any reconciling items are entered in the
books of accounts and cashbook, whichever is applicable; and
3.6.3 Submit the said reconciliation statements to this office within the
prescribed period.

3.7 Finally, we recommend Management for the submission of the above noted
unsubmitted check disbursement vouchers/ liquidation vouchers as
discussed in Item 3.2.3.

4.6 We have recommended that Management retrace all transactions prior to
November 1, 2018 which might have affected the Cash – Local Treasury
and Advances balances in the Financial Statements as well as in the
Cashbooks to determine the causes of the noted discrepancies.

4.7 We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider
the proposed accounting entry, as follows, to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts:
Account Name Debit Credit
Prior Period Adjustments/Government Equity 60,920.93
Cash – Local Treasury 11,079.07
Other Payables 12,000.00
Petty Cash Fund 60,000.00

5.15 We have recommended that Management undertake the following actions:
5.15.1 Require concerned officials to submit the required liquidation
documents through the issuance of demand letters;
5.15.2 Upon submission of the required documents by concerned officials,
the Municipal Accountant – Designate should properly review the
documents and take up the necessary journal entries in the books of
accounts; and
5.15.3 If appropriate, necessitate the refund of unliquidated cash advances.

5.16 We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider
the proposed accounting entry as follows to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts.
Account Name Debit Credit
Advances to Special Disbursing Officer 7,300.00
Prior Period Adjustments/Government Equity 700,500.00
Advances for Operating Expenses 707,800.00

5.17 We have further recommended that, henceforth, Management limit the
grant of cash advances only to specific legal purposes and the Municipal
Accountant – Designate to strictly implement the policy that no additional
cash advances granted to officials and employees in the event that they fail
to submit all the liquidations on time.

5.18 Finally, we have recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant
- Designate see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with
complete documentation and in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and
regulations in order to establish the validity and propriety of the
transactions and disbursements. Processing and payment of claims that are
not properly documented shall be discontinued.

6.5 We have recommended that the Accounting Unit:
6.5.1 Prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers and follow the rules on
accounting for Receivables and Liabilities as prescribed in the
Manual on NGAS for LGUs;
6.5.2 Identify items for reconciliation, adjustment or those for further
action pertaining to long outstanding receivables and liabilities; and
6.5.3 Review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and
consider the proposed accounting entry, as follows to reflect the
correct balances of the accounts.

For item 6.3.5 (under 20% Development Fund)

Due from Other Funds 229,210.39

established on January 1 the following year)

Due to Special Accounts 229,210.39
Eliminating Entry for Consolidated Financial Statements to be re-
established on January 1 the following year)

Due to Special Accounts 229,210.39
Government Equity 229,210.39

For item 6.3.6 (under Trust Fund)
Account Name Debit Credit
Due to NGAs 124,893.14
Due to Other Funds 20,000.00
Other Payables 144,893.14

7.9 We have recommended that the:
7.9.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and semi-annual inventory taking, and prepare and submit
the RPCI to the COA Office for verification;
7.9.2 Management invite through letter a representative of the
Commission on Audit to witness the inventory taking;
7.9.3 The Accounting Office and the Property Unit maintain and update
Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Cards, respectively, for each
inventory item to promote effective monitoring and controls over
Inventory; and
7.9.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian reconcile
with the RPCI and verify variances for appropriate action and
subsequent adjustments as necessary.

7.10 We have also recommended that Management submit: (1) the original
copies of the DVs totaling ₱13,696,660.75 together with their supporting
documents; and (2) supplemental listings for those not duly signed by
recipients/intended beneficiaries in the amount of ₱402,037.14.

8.8 We have recommended that the:
8.8.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and yearly inventory taking, and prepare and submit the
RPCPPE to COA Office for verification and reconciliation with the
complete Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC)
and the Property Cards maintained by the Accounting Office and
Property Unit, respectively;
8.8.2 Management invite through writing a representative from the
Commission on Audit to witness the inventory taking;
8.8.3 The Accounting Office and Property Unit maintain and update
PPELC, Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) and
Property Cards, respectively, for each inventory item to promote
effective monitoring and controls over PPE;
8.8.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian reconcile
with the RPCPPE and verify variances for appropriate action and
subsequent adjustments as necessary; and
8.8.5 The Municipal Accountant trace back those PPE noted to not have
been depreciated, compute for the supposed depreciation to date and
prepare adjusting as necessary.

9.9 We have reiterated our recommendations that the:
9.9.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and yearly physical count of local road networks, and prepare
and submit the RPCLRN to COA Office for verification and
reconciliation with the complete and updated LRNLC and the
LRNPC to be maintained by the Accounting Office and General
Service Office, respectively;
9.9.2 Management invite through letter a representative of the
Commission on Audit to witness the physical count;
9.9.3 The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects; and
9.9.4 The General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory and
Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card
for all roads and its components; and for
9.9.5 The Municipal Accountant-Designate trace the recorded amounts of
roads, highways and bridges, which were closed to government
equity in the prior years in its books of accounts, keep and maintain
Registry of Public Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary records
for roads and road components for every road network and provide
for the depreciation for the local road network.

10.7 We have recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that the
correct computations are made in the withholdings/setup of GSIS
contributions in the preparation of the payroll, and properly remit the same
within the reglementary period. The Municipal Accountant - Designate
properly account for the withholdings and remittances.

10.8 We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider
the proposed accounting entries as follows to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts.
Accounts Debit Credit
Due from Officers and Employees 750.60
Due to GSIS 26,034.25
Prior Period Adjustment/ Government Equity 7,445.29
Due to Officers and Employees 19,339.56

10.9 Furthermore, we have reiterated our recommendation that Management
submit a copy of the MOA signed with the GSIS. Management should
revisit the said MOA and determine whether overpayment was made to the
GSIS.

11.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate review
the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider the
proposed accounting entries as follows to reflect the correct balances of the
accounts.
Accounts Debit Credit
Due from Officers and Employees 12,047.28
Prior Period Adjustment/ Government Equity 12,047.29
Due to PhilHealth 24,094.57

11.8 We have further reiterated our recommendations that Management revisit
the payrolls for those with noted differences in CY 2019 and CY 2018 and
undertake the following actions:
11.8.1 Recalculate the PhilHealth Contributions to include salary
adjustments, take up the necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts and carry out the corresponding action in the
underpayment and overpayment of salaries due to errors in the
preparation of the payrolls and the interest/penalties to be incurred
for delayed remittances for the under-withholdings be borne by the
erring officials and employees; and
11.8.2 Demand the erring officials and employees for the penalties incurred
the amount of ₱6,516.47 on the delayed remittances in CY 2018.

11.9 Henceforth, ensure that the correct computations are made in the
preparation of the payroll and that the appropriate journal entries are
made for the same.

12.4 We have recommended that Management require concerned officials to
promptly submit copies of government contracts and their supporting
documents within the prescribed period to enable the Audit Team timely
review of transactions and early detection and correction or errors, if any.
We recommended that Management submit the year-end Financial Statements, trial balances and other financial reports within the deadline set by the provisions of Section 41(2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers to determine at any given time the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the Financial Statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary and general ledgers to facilitate preparation of BRS, and for submission to COA for audit purposes.

The Municipal Accountant commented that the Liquidation Reports for cash advances for payrolls  were only submitted after the cut-off period of December 31, 2021 and closing of book of accounts, hence the liquidations were not recorded and reflected in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit all the documents or returns the unused cash to fully liquidate his cash advances at the end of the year to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly on the liquidation of cash advances and require the Municipal Accountant to provide the subsidiary ledgers of the Advances for Payroll account for easy determination of the unliquidated balances.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office and Supplies Custodian in ensuring that:
all purchases are recorded to its corresponding inventory classification and the Perpetual Inventory Method is being observed;
the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports are prepared, maintained and submitted for the recognition of the expense portion of supplies inventories; and
conduct periodic physical count of inventory on hand to pave way for any necessary adjustment in the books and/or inventory records for proper valuation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to perform the annual physical inventory, reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to effect the prompt transfer of the Property Plant and Equipment account from the Trust Fund Book to General Fund books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the period or date of acquisition of the PPEs mentioned above and compute for the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007 and Section 4 (o) of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I to establish proper valuation of PPE accounts.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant:
To stop the practice of charging Construction in Progress in the payment of mobilization fee and in deducting retention money. Follow the prescribed accounting entry to conform with the generally accepted accounting principles; and
To draw adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should also derecognized the PPE account for the semi-expendable assets and medical supplies and to recognize the corresponding Inventory and/or expense account for proper presetation in Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm the existence of the biological assets and thereafter prepare an adjusting entry, if any, to derecognize the recorded balance of the biological assets.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate all available documents regarding the abovementioned accounts in order to prepare the necessary general and subsidiary ledgers to support the balances presented in the financial statements.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the misclassified accounts.

The Municipal Accountant commented that they maintain subsidiary ledgers for the trust fund concerning infrastructure projects. However, there are trust funds that they were only aware when withdrawal was made or when the documents were submitted to their Office, hence, the needed subsidiary ledgers were not prepared immediately.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a subsidiary ledger for the Trust Liability-DRRM account to support the balances and adopt the sample disclosure on LDRRMF to be presented in the Notes to the Financial Statement in compliance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts or effect the necessary adjustments. Being reciprocal accounts, it should end up with the same balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the municipal officials concerned in the procurement process of the Municipality to strictly comply with the requirements of the aforementioned COA Circular for the improvement of the agency’s internal control system and procedures related to procurement.

We therefore recommended that the agency revisit its procurement process in order to protect the integrity of the process.
Also, we reiterated our recommendation that management require the BAC to observe Section 20.1 and 20.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and with all the applicable guidelines and regulations related to procurement in order to promote transparency and accountability.
Moreover, we reiterated our recommendation that management justify the failure to conduct the required pre-procurement conference in the procurement of infrastructure projects and goods with an Approved Budget exceeding the threshold provided by the law.
Finally, we reiterated our recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the processing of payment to contractor without the BAC conducting the required pre-procurement conference.

We recommended that management ensure that COA Project Billboards are properly installed and conform to the prescribed guidelines of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to inform the public on matters of public concern and to fully adopt the full disclosure policy of all transactions governing public interest.
We also recommended that management inform the audit team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
Lastly, justify/explain the non-installation of COA Project Billboard in the Construction of Public Market despite setting aside an amount for the same.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require:
the BAC to post in its PhilGEPS and Agency Website the Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and Approved Contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects through public bidding to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement;
the BAC to explain the failure to post in PhilGEPS the Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and Approved Contracts despite being a requirement of RA 9184; and
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the printout copy of posted Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS were included among the supporting documents for the payment of the claim for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through public bidding.

We recommended that the agency submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
Also, in the future, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant not process transactions that lacked the required documentary requirements. First and foremost,  by signing Box A of the Disbursement Voucher he is certifying the completeness of the supporting documents.

We recommended that the BAC members perform their assigned responsibilities as set forth under the provisions of RA 9184.Henceforth, BAC members should strictly comply with the procedural and documentary requirements of RA 9184 regardless of the mode of procurements used by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management submit explanation why the the Municipality entered into a contract with MV Office Supplies and Trading.  and why the above procurements were made in piecemeal or in series although the same could have been conducted in a single procurement activity thru public bidding.

We recommended that all procurement of the Municipality either through public bidding or alternative method, specially in the sending of RFQ, should be done by the BAC and not by the staff of requesting departmen/office.

We recommended that management:
Explain/justify the charging of unrelated expenditures to the Engineering Overhead and why should it not be disallowed in audit;
In the future, charge only engineering and administrative overhead expenditures incidental to the project; and
Carefully review, evaluate, analyze the unexpended balance of the Engineering Overhead to ensure that if the projects were already completed, the accumulated amount could be considered as savings and should be reverted to the Source Agency.
We recommended that management provide the specific details and location of the projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund to be provided on the Annual Budget.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development fund and see to it that the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure. Also, facilitate the immediate implementation of the PPAs so that the desired benefits be delivered to the constituent at the earliest time.

We recommended that Management, thru the BAC and the concerned officials and employees to strictly conform to the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR in all stages of the procurement of infrastructure projects, submit all the necessary documents for the perfected contracts, bidding documents and supporting documents, and comply with the timely completion of the entire procurement process to preclude any issues that may arise upon implementation of the projects.

We recommended the submission of the required reports from the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer on the sources and utilization of DRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management explain/justify the non-remittance of the unexpended PDAF balance despite repetitive demands.
Also, we reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately the remaining balance of unutilized PDAF balances of P10,786,334.08 to the National Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be more vigilant in the performance of his duties.
Also, we recommended that the proper disbursement process/flow be faithfully adhered to. Shortcuts in the preparation of disbursement vouchers and the payment of checks should not be tolerated.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular in the Project Implementation  of the Local Government Support Fund – Other Financial Assistance, for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that management justify and explain the non-remittance of the abovementioned unutilized balances.
Further, we reiterated our recommendation that management religiously comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 particularly on the handling of unutilized fund transfers from national and local government agencies for implemented programs/projects/activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to submit to the audit team the necessary supporting documents, such as those enumerated above, to prove the entitlement of the officials/employees to the SRA under AO No. 36.
Submit a brief justification/explanation for each official/ employee as to how said official/ employee has complied with such requirement as PHW as defined under AO No. 36 dated November 16, 2020 and reiterated under DBM – DOH Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020. The justification/explanation for each official/employee may be shown in tabular form as to how he/she qualified for the claimed allowance.

We therefore recommended that management be vigilant in the management of funds entrusted to them. Comply to the provision of the Local Government Code particularly in the keeping of separate books and depository accounts.
We also recommended that management draw adjusting journal entries to recognize the increase in the “Asset” and “Government Equity” account as a result of the utilization of the funds received.

We recommended that management prepare and submit the LGU’s GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Reports within the prescribed period as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended that the agency submit the required supporting documents/DV for the abovementioned expenditures to avoid suspension in audit of the said disbursement. Also, in the future, in order to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims of payment unless all the required supporting documents are present and attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended the management to utilize the GAD fund based on its objective as stated on its GAD Plan and Budget. Also charge the financial assistance to general fund if the intended beneficiaries were not those mentioned in its GAD objective.  And lastly, the LGU should disburse to a more concrete or specific project, programs or services that would address gender issues and refrain from charging various expenses because these would deplete the budget for the intended activities.

We recommended that the Management see to it that the amount allocated are disbursed for strengthening and implementing the programs of LCPC. Moreover, prioritize programs for the children in conflict with the law and those who are at risk.
Also, we recommended the management to come up with Local Development Plan containing the strategic plan of the LCPC to specify specific projects, programs, and activities consistent with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 addressing the issues concerning the children who are in conflict with the law and those who are at risk.

We recommended that management to prioritize in utilizing the LCPC fund in accordance with the objective of its programs, plans or activity. In addition, process only DVs with complete documentation to ensure that all payments made are adequately supported and valid expenses of the Municipality.

We recommended that approving authorities ensure that disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are completely accomplished and strictly comply with the procedural and documentary requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that management:
Submit the lacking documents as enumerated above to support the first progress billing of the Construction of Public Market to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit. Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to not process payments that lacked the required documentary requirements; and
Explain/justify the payment of items of work not yet performed by the Contractor. After verification, adjust the payment due to the Contractor in subsequent billings.

We recommended that management:
Submit the documentary requirements to support the grant of cash advance for BAC Honoraria;
Cause the refund of the excessive honoraria of the TWG Chairman and BAC Secretariat amounting to P1,094.00; and
Comply to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, COA Circular 
No. 2012-001 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A particularly in the granting of honorarium to the BAC.

We recommended that only the OIC-Municipal Treasurer should perform the disbursing function. Also, stop the practice of transferring cash advances to other accountable officers specially to coterminous employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
Refund the amount of P14,200.00 immediately
enforce the direct payment to suppliers/creditors through check for all procurement of good pursuant to above provision of. Limit the amount of expenses subject for reimbursements.
Furthermore, the Municipality should stop from resorting to reimbursement for the procurement of goods necessary for the implementation of planned PPAs and strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management explain the necessity of providing daily meals to the PGI and LGU employees while performing their regular works/jobs. Further, the Municipal Mayor, as head of the local government unit, safeguard the municipality’s funds and resources by exercising prudence in utilization thereof.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the lacking documents for each payment of TLB as enumerated above for audit purposes otherwise Notice of Suspension will be issued
Cause the payment/refund of the outstanding financial obligations of the retired/deceased employees, immediately
We recommended that the Management cause the submission of the afore-mentioned unsubmitted accounts immediately, otherwise a Notice of Suspension will be issued.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to prepare a complete Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectibles during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET receivables in the succeeding year; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the ICO-Municipal Treasurer. Further, we recommended that the LCE require the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to update and properly filled out the RPTAR particularly the data on the tax dues and the balance of unpaid taxes; (b) Treasurer and Accountant to regularly conduct reconciliation of their records; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustment in the books to recognize the income and share of the Province and Barangays.

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) the Inventory Team to (i) undertake a complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of PPE (RCPPE); (b) the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards; (ii) apply depreciation accounting yearly to all depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment items; and (iii) exert effort to locate the previous records of assets not subjected to depreciation; (c) the General Services Officer (GSO) to (i) maintain property cards for PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of properties; and (ii) issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to establish the accountability for properties; and (d) the GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the conduct of physical count to establish the correctness of balances of PPE accounts. Furthermore, we recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in order to render reliable PPE information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough review of transactions affecting the accounts, determine the cause of the abnormal balances, and effect the necessary adjusting entries to report the accounts in their normal balances.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (i) instruct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from accommodating prior year’s expenditures against the current year appropriations; and (ii) direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that only lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during a fiscal year shall be recognized or taken up in the accounts of that year. The LCE should also investigate the recurrence of infractions, thereafter, avail any legal remedy thereto appertaining, and subject the erring officers to disciplinary actions, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Council to justify the absence of SB’s approval/authority on implementing PPAs amounting to ₱13,533,026.10; (b) the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures against the current appropriation in the absence of legislative intervention; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct proper review of supporting documents of payrolls/disbursement vouchers to ensure completeness of the transactions; and (ii) submit the identified lacking documentary requirements and untransmitted DVs and henceforth, strategize prudently to avoid delays in the submission of transaction documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to: (i) justify the absence of budgetary realignment ordinance duly approved by the Local Sanggunian in implementing PPAs amounting to ₱2,860,000.00 of which ₱1,299,457.00 was used in the implementation of PPAs that are exclusions in the approved AIP; and (ii) cause the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor and DILG-LGOO as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002; (b) ICO-Municipal Treasurer (i) remit the excess NDRRMF to the Bureau of Treasury; and (ii) submit proof/evidence that the accumulated amount in the Trust Liabilities – DRRM was a result of unrealized income from regular sources; (c) OIC-Municipal Budget Officer refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures against the current appropriation; and (d) OIC-Municipal Accountant (i) comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts; and (ii) immediately submit the identified lacking documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in his capacity as Chairman of the GFPS (i) issue Executive Order to complete the composition of the GFPS including its ExeCOM and TWG relevant for gender mainstreaming; (ii) instruct the GFPS to create a centralized GAD database that would consolidate the information gathered and compiled by all offices to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy making; and (iii) employ the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD. Henceforth, inform the DILG in writing for any changes in the GPB before the implementation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Local School Board to (i) carefully plan the appropriation of funds to address the needs for the operation and maintenance of public schools; (ii) issue a supplemental budget in case the actual expenditure exceeds the approved budget provided there is available funds; and (iii) ensure that all SEF disbursements are supported with complete documentation and the execution of PPAs are in accordance with the guidelines and parameters prescribed by the JC; (b) members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) determine the appropriate mode of procurement for every government transaction; (ii) ensure that purchases beyond the threshold on shopping or small value procurement be done through public bidding; and (iii) limit the use of alternative modes of procurement in only highly exceptional cases as provided in Republic Act 9184; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all transactions documents including the disbursement vouchers, and their supporting documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct (a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Officer (MDDRMO) to strictly follow the guidelines in the utilization of the Local Government Support Fund as provided under the corresponding Local Budget Circular issued by the Department of Budget and Management (DBM); and (b) Local Finance Committee to prepare the required reports and observe prudence on the disclosure policy prescribed by the guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) members of the Bids and Awards Committee to (i) justify the use of Emergency Cases as Alternative Mode of Procurement; and (ii) limit the use of alternative modes of procurement to only highly exceptional cases as provided in Republic Act No. 9184; (b) Municipal Development and Planning Officer to (i) submit documentations relative to the distribution of relief goods; and (ii) ensure the completeness of documents on funds utilization and liquidation on future similar relief operations to ascertain that government resources are properly utilized; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) coordinate with BAC and MPDO to facilitate the submission of lacking requirements; (ii) ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation in compliance with COA rules and regulations; and (iii) properly record the purchases of inventories in the appropriate inventory accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) ensure that payments for procurements are made directly  to suppliers/contractors except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently paid by check; and (ii) set-up Petty Cash Fund duly supported with estimate of expenses which shall be used for petty operating expenses; and (b) all officials and employees to discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursement unless the item/service is petty in nature and necessary for emergency cases.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to conduct cost-benefit analysis on the reproduction activities and consider the procurement of photocopying machines; (b) the responsible officers to submit explanation for the  deficiencies above noted; (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that the claims are properly and completely supported with documentary requirements before making the payment; and (d) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) review the validity of OR issued by the supplier before releasing the check payments; and (ii) require the supplier to replace the unauthorized ORs for the subject transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct (a) the Sangguniang Bayan Secretary and the BAC to submit explanation for or justify the deficiencies noted on conflicting dates and unreasonable cost of publication per column centimeters; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that the claims are properly and completely supported by documentary requirements affirming his certification on Disbursement Voucher and that the corresponding taxes are properly withheld from government payments to supplier/contractor; and (ii) submit immediately the lacking documentary requirements above-stated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to provide a legal basis for the payment of OT services to the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer, otherwise, cause the refund of the amount paid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) convene all responsible heads of offices and admonish them of their duties and functions on the submission of the accounts, financial records and reports as sanctioned by COA; (ii) instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately submit all paid disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents; and (b) henceforth, observe the timeliness of reportorial requirements of the Commission; and (iii) direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit swiftly the  periodic trial balances and financial statements to the office of the Auditor for the months of January to December 2021 and the Year-end Financial Statements for CY 2021.  The LCE shall cause the suspension of payment or withholding of the salaries of the erring officers until they shall have complied with the complete submission of the above transaction documents and financial reports.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Heads of Municipal General Services Office (GSO), Municipal Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) and Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit regularly the advance copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders together with its supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to require all departments/offices to submit their respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for the implementation of different projects/programs/activities; and (b) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to (i) prepare the Annual Procurement Plan based on PPMP and adopt the required format as prescribed by GPBB; (ii) observe prudence in accomplishing the APP that should contain relevant details including the procurement strategy to be employed and scheduling of procurement activities; and (iii) avoid the splitting of contracts and employ appropriate mode of procurement based on the approved APP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Municipal Administrator and Municipal Health Officer to (i) observe the instructions of the Department of Health Order No. 163 s.2002 in requesting and procuring drugs and medicines and ensure that only those drugs fall within the context of Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) shall be requested and procured; and (ii) use generic name in all transactions related to purchasing, prescribing, dispensing and administering of drugs and medicines; (b) Bids and Awards Committee to (i) submit justification on the use of alternative mode of procurement of medicines above cited; and (ii) observe the guidelines and procedures prescribed in Republic Act No. 9184 in all procurement of the Municipality; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the transactions; and (ii) record the purchase of drugs/medicines using the appropriate inventory account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) review the claim as to validity, propriety and legality before approving the transaction; (b) ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents before approving the Disbursement Voucher as prescribed in Section 4, Paragraph 6 of PD No. 1445; (c) ensure that supporting documents are properly and completely filled-up; and (d) submit immediately the lacking supporting documents pertaining to the transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (a) submit an explanation for the non-withholding of the required taxes totaling ₱148,638.44 from the payments made; (b) apply the correct computation and withholding of taxes on payments of non-VAT registered suppliers to avoid over-remittance; and (c) make a recomputation for taxes of transactions previously subjected to a 3% instead of 1% Percentage Tax and file the corresponding tax refund to the BIR.
We also recommended that the management continue to administer vaccines efficiently so that local herd immunity or 70% of the target population can be attained in the soonest time possible.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant (a) to prepare complete and accurate statement of account of taxes/premiums/loans repayments deducted from suppliers/contractors/employees for remittance to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; (b) effect the adjusting entry for the accounting error in recording the remittance of withheld taxes amounting ₱293,289.07 by debiting the Due to BIR and crediting Other Payables account; and (c) update regularly the posting of transactions in the Subsidiary Ledgers to ensure correctness and accuracy of the reported balances.

We recommend that management submit a valid justification letter for not preparing the APP based on the required format. Moreover, management has to prepare the APP based on the approved format provided in the GPPB Circular 07-2015.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared and submitted the 2021 APP based on the required format.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management ensure that the required and appropriate withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the pertinent BIR regulations and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period. Likewise, the management is to submit a justification for the noted deficiency.
observations.managementAction: The management already ensured that the required and appropriate withholding taxes are deducted from the next payments made in accordance with the pertinent BIR regulations and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification for the procurement of the aforecited items above the suggested price of the DOH.  Henceforth, procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the Standard Retail Price (SRP) set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of medical supplies after these and in the future is at reasonable prices guided by the Standard Retail Price (SRP) set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management abide by the prescribed rates of Magna Carta Benefit for Public Health Workers as stipulated in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 of 2012.  Further, require the Municipal Accountant to check and refer from time to time different government rules and issuances that are applicable to disbursements to ensure that funds are used in accordance with accounting and auditing rules.
observations.managementAction: The management abides in the prescribed rates of Magna Carta Benefit for Public Health Workers as stipulated in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 of 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management reimburse the General Fund with the total amount erroneously charged against it. The Municipal Accountant is also recommended to record the reimbursement in order to reflect the correct balances of the accounts of each fund. We further recommend that the proper Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment be issued to the appropriate personnel.
observations.managementAction: The total amount erroneously charged to the General Fund was already reimbursed by Special Educational Fund through SEF Check No. 59436658, JEV No. 200-21-07-22.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide justification through written explanation or provide additional documentary requirement on this noted observation. Moreover, we recommend that they observe and be updated with new procedures, guidelines and advisories on procurement required by the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: The management is committed to the recommendation given, to be observant and be updated with new procedures, guidelines and advisories on procurement required by the GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in the financial statements and facilitate the monitoring of its liquidation.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation was noted for future transaction as reference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit justification to the deficiency noted to support the payment of catering services to LSI/Medical Teams. Furthermore, the BAC must ensure that the returned RFQ from the suppliers of the caterings services will indicate the food specifications/menu they will offer in support to the price quoted.
observations.managementAction: Transactions after and future transactions were supplied with food specification/menu in the RFQ, PR and PO.

Per December 31, 2020 inventory, those projects were completed as of December 31, 2020. It was undeniably directly recorded as PPE accounts upon payment of progress billing; we will be now refraining on doing so as per recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjusting entries based on the reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statement and to reflect the correct adjusted cash in bank balance.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made last June 1, 2021 through General Journal JEV No. 300-21-06-03 and General Journal JEV No. 100-21-06-15

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the cancellation of above mentioned checks and set up the cash and payable accounts relative to these transactions and prepare the following proposed journal entries:
	
           Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Current Account 		₱4,336.20 
    Accounts Payable		₱4,336.20
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made last March 1, 2021 through General Journal JEV No. 100-21-03-10

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made last August 2, 2021 through General Journal JEV No. 300-21-08-08 and General Journal JEV No. 100-21-08-18

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the entries made in the Government Equity account and provide adjustments to correct the misstatement of the equity and other affected accounts in the current and prior years pursuant to the applicable accounting standards.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made last June 1, 2021 through General Journal JEV No. 300-21-06-(3-5)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books and eventually reported in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer already submitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year 2021 last August 31, 2021. The Municipal Accountant already made the necessary entries using the copy of the list as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit intact all the collections for a certain period with the depository bank and not to retain any amount to prevent the misuse and/or loss of the government funds.
observations.managementAction: MTO complies with the proper procedure of depositing intact and regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to require the daily turnover of collections by the collectors and that the turnover must be the total amount collected during the day. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer closely monitor the collections and remittances of the collectors in order to prevent the risk of the collections being misused and/or lost.
observations.managementAction: MTO now requires the revenue collection clerks to submit all daily collections to the liquidating officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management the creation of an inventory committee to conduct the actual physical count of all the municipality’s PPEs as well as assess its actual condition to establish reliability of reported amount. Further, require the committee/s to prepare the inventory report and submit to the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 124 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: Inventory committee was updated per Memorandum 05-2019 and already conducted physical inventory taking.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents as noted above and to submit an explanation on the deficiencies enumerated on the remarks indicated. We recommend that the municipality observe and comply strictly with the documentary requirements as required by COA Circular 2012-001 and P.D. No. 1445 and submit them to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials/employees concerned to strictly comply on the prompt submission of copies of contracts, P.O.s and other similar contracts of understanding to the Auditor within five (5) days from its perfection pursuant to Section 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted copy of Purchase Orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Acting Municipal Accountant to adhere with the required time frame for the submission of the monthly trial balance and schedules. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend immediate liquidation/settlement of outstanding cash advances for Confidential/Intelligence Fund. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on allowing the grant of cash advances despite non-liquidation of previous cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant no longer grants cash advances relating to Confidential/Intelligence Fund with an outstanding unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to submit copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports on procured items to the Auditor’s Office within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to comply with the provision of Paragraph 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and for the Auditor to conduct timely inspection of items delivered.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports of procured items to the COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management prepare documents to transfer these PPE items to the barangays. After which we recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the overstatement of the LGU’s asset, contra-asset and equity accounts.

Further, the journal entries have to be supported with proof of transfer of ownership of the PPE items to barangays.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant already transferred these PPE items to the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the recipient barangays to liquidate the funds. Henceforth, observe COA Circular Nos.2012-001 and 2015-009 on the documentation and recording requirements on fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: The municipality is already practicing releasing subsidy to the recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require that the Trip Tickets be serially numbered and completely filled out with necessary details for the purpose of monitoring and controlling the use of government vehicles and consumption of fuel. Moreover, require the Municipal Drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle duly certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer and submit it to the auditor for verification. Further, the Agency personnel concerned have to prepare a Report of Fuel Consumption and submit it to the auditor on a monthly basis in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 to determine the reasonableness of the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses.
observations.managementAction: Already prepared as per recommendation but not on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant investigate and examine why abnormal negative balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated above occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them. Likewise, Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has to be maintained as basis in recording in the GL.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has been maintained as basis in recording in the GL.

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough verification of the dormant items and request for write-off for the particular accounts that qualify and meet the requirements of COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997. Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments to clean the books of this deficiency. We further recommend a regular review of all account balances so that dormant accounts can immediately be identified and acted upon.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already conducted a thorough verification of the dormant items and made necessary adjustments for the three out of eight accounts that were dormant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit proper listing of accounts payable together with an aging schedule and other pertinent documents to facilitate the review that these payables are due and valid for reversion.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submit proper listing of accounts and some of the other pertinent documents are already submitted to facilitate the review that these payables that are due and valid for reversion.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% Development Fund and maximize utilization of the said fund to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.
observations.managementAction: Projects funded by the CY 2019 20% Development Fund were already fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management the following:
Immediately liquidate/settle all outstanding cash advances.
Withhold the salaries of the accountable officers and employees who failed to promptly liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
The municipal accountant must comply to COA Circular 97-002 and 2012-001 and issue a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.
Refrain granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant already complied with COA Circular 97-002 and 2012-001 and issued a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books. But undeniably failed to withhold the salaries of the accountable officers and employees who failed to promptly liquidate their outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry close/derecognize the balance of the above mentioned PPE items in order to fairly present the PPE balance.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries were already prepared in order to fairly present the PPE balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create an inventory and disposal committee to act immediately in the proper identification, ascertain existence, location and disposal of these assets in order to fairly present the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We further recommend that municipal accountant restore back the PPE accounts dropped from the books and adjustment have to be made only after proper inventory and disposal of the properties.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Team was already created to clearly verify those assets listed in the damaged assessment report during the 7.2 magnitude earthquake and made an Inventory Report of Property, Plant & Equipment as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
Must initiate the move by creating the inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties of the municipality.  The corresponding equipment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts.  In the absence of available records of acquisitions in the past years, they will have no other recourse but to take hold of the disbursement vouchers to obtain the necessary data to be recorded in the equipment/property ledger cards.

Require the general services officer or municipal treasurer to submit the request for inspection with complete supporting papers so that the auditors can take proper action on the request.
observations.managementAction: COA Technical personnel already inspected the damaged properties and were already dropped from the books basing from the inventory report made by the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer investigate and examine why negative balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated above occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them. Likewise, Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has to be maintained as basis in recording in the GL.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant already investigated and examined why negative balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them.

observations.recommendation:
1.	Submit justifications/explanations why the Municipal Mayor was made the payee of the purchase of lot instead of the true  vendor.

2.	Submit justification why the same amount was booked as Accounts Payable still with the Mayor as payee. 

3.	Submit justifications/explanations why the dates in the submitted documents did not reconcile with the logical chronology of things.

4.	Submit justifications/explanations why DBP CheckNo.  43830881 dated September 23, 2014 previously issued to the Municipal Mayor and encashed on September 24, 2014  was the same check allegedly issued/received by Uri Sallacay  per undated DV No. 100-15-01-68.

5.	Submit all documents to support the purchase of the lot pursuant to Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and  other pertinent provisions to support the payment.
observations.managementAction: The management is still in the process of complying the requirements for the titling of these lands.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and  Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts.

Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.


Adopt and always observe austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already maximized the services of permanent employees by assigning additional functions not related to their actual term of reference.

observations.recommendation:
Require the municipal accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV) to reclassify cash advances  for travel and special purpose/ time-bound undertakings  recorded under the Receivables- Due from Officers and Employees (123) account to the appropriate account, Advances to Officers and Employees (148) pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002 dated January 19, 2007.  On the other hand, the cash shortage of Benjamin Abad amounting to ₱491,497.84 should be reclassified to the account Due from Officers and Employees (123).  

Send demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitor the liquidation of these cash advances.

Exert more effort to collect these receivables such as withholding payments of claims of municipal officials/employees who have long overdue accountabilities with the LGU as in the case of audit disallowances.

Follow-up with the Office of the Ombudsman the status of the case filed against the defalcating municipal treasurer.

Issue bills or collection/confirmation letters  to  agencies/persons concerned  as  proofs  that the Municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means  to  collect these receivables that have become dormant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has sent demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitored the liquidation of these cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Exercise fully the taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to 150 of R. A. 7160 in order to earn more revenue necessary to augment the funding for the operation of the municipality.  Otherwise, implement austerity measures to reduce operating expenses and utilize internal revenue fund for developmental projects that would be beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management already reduced operating expenses and implemented tax campaign and mapping but due to pandemic, the collection was not maximized.

The management already reduced operating expenses by reducing staff from market operations and intensifying collection for market fees. Municipality is still in the process of improving its market operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the municipality and all concerned personnel to observe and comply the requirements and procedures as established in the GAM Volume I and COA Circular 2012-003. We recommend that strict adherence to the rules and procedures concerning the processing, releasing, and recording of cash advances be observed by the Treasurer and Accountant. Further, we recommend the municipality to submit an explanation on the above mentioned deficiencies/irregularities to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Accountant who is now the Municipal Mayor paid the amount of ₱120,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the classification/identification of payable account to establish correct balances in the financial statements.  Comply COA Circular No. 2004-008 and COA Circular No. 94-103.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going classification/identification of payable for Due to NGAs account to establish correct balances in the financial statements by the accounting staff.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the BAC or a committee responsible for the accreditation of NGOs/POs in recovering the related supporting documents to these fund transfers. See to it that the documentary requirements for release of funds to NGOs/POs as specified in COA Circular 2012-001 be complied before transfer is made.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant submitted documents relating to the fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to trace the material variance of ₱22,662,054.80 for Cash in Bank Account and ₱23,730.11 for Cash in Local Treasury Account as of December 31, 2019; (b) require henceforth, reconciliation of entries in the cashbook and general ledger at least on a quarterly basis and promptly resolve any significant discrepancy accordingly.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management the immediate submission of the liquidation report to the Office of the Chairman, COA, Quezon City and furnish to COA Audit Team the transmittal letter of the said liquidation report duly stamped/received by the said Office as proof of compliance of the required report.
observations.managementAction: The management planned to recover and gather the vouchers and documents for submission to COA.

observations.recommendation: In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall not be released and shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for the other priority programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submitted the JEV attributing to the removal of the said amount from the Due To NGAs and Due From LGUs accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following: a. The Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method in accounting for purchases of inventories and maintain supplies ledger cards; b. The General Services Officer to maintain stock cards pursuant to COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002; and c. The inventory committee to conduct physical count of inventories for every semester and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
Entries to the supply’s ledger cards is still being reconstructed.
Stock cards are maintained.
Inventory taking was conducted and a report thereon was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Reconcile the lapsing schedule and the financial statements, and ensure that all depreciable PPEs are provided with depreciation; b. Submit the Journal Entry Vouchers and General Journal to support the entries in the General Ledger of the PPE accounts; and c. Reconcile the discrepancies in the Schedule of Allowance for Depreciation and provide for an allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets, and provide the disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for the succeeding year as required by COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction:
Lapsing schedule was submitted.
Required JEVs and GJs were submitted.
There was reconciliation of Accumulated Depreciation and Depreciation Expense.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, the lapsing schedule was submitted. The lapsing schedule still did not reconcile with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entry to correct the noted deficiencies and submit the same to the audit team for verification; and henceforth, record the purchase of equipment and relief goods under the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment and/or Inventory account in the succeeding procurement of disaster-related PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjusting entry was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entry to correct the error in recording the fund received exclusively for Vermicomposting Facility.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2021 adjusting entry was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus (Deficit) and to present the same either as part of the Statement of Cash Flows or as disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with the provisions of IPSAS No. 2 to serve as proof of correctness of the reported net income (deficit) and the net cash from operating activities.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus (Deficit) was prepared and presented as disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) are prepared for all transactions of the municipality, as this shall serve as basis for recording the transactions in the General Journal and Special Journals.
observations.managementAction: Accounting entries in the journals were supported with JEVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Department Heads of Municipal Health Office, MDRRMO, and Municipal Engineering Office, and the Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson, in coordination with BAC Secretariats and Municipal Accountant, to submit the lacking documentary requirements as provided in Annex E.2 of this report, in compliance with GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The lacking documentary requirements were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor expedite the processing of certificate of titles in favor of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Processes for the issuance of certificates of titles are being carried out.
observations.reason: Only some of the parcels of land with certificates of title.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer the following courses of actions: a. to post tarpaulin signboards at the locations of infrastructure projects as soon as the award has been made to supplier; b. to complete the posting of relevant information on tarpaulin signboards including the project duration, target completion date, project cost and source of fund.
observations.managementAction:
Every contractor was directed to post the necessary signboards at the project location.
Every signboard contained all the relevant information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Ensure that all appropriation ordinance enacted bear his attestation/approval; and b. Ensure that the leeway and flexibility afforded on the utilization of the 20% DF be optimally utilized for the benefit and welfare of the constituent.
observations.managementAction: Every appropriation ordinance enacted bear his attestation/approval. The CY 2020 PPAs were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to facilitate the implementation of the PPAs funded by the LDRRMF, and the amount granted by the Office of the President.
observations.managementAction: PPAs funded by LDRRMF were implemented. The grant from Office of the President was likewise utilized this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MENRO to utilize the fund for its specific purpose and liquidate within 10 days after the implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: This fund transfer was already utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. PPEs In-charge to complete the physical inventory of PPEs and conduct the same every year, and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the RPCPPE to be submitted every January 31 of each year; b. Municipal PPE Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card as well as Property Ledger Card maintained by the Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate, respectively; c. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies in the Schedule of Allowance for Depreciation and provide for an allowance for depreciation on all PPEs; and d. Municipal Accountant to transfer the completed project to its appropriate asset account that has long existed as CIP under SEF.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory of PPEs was conducted for CY 2021. Property Ledger Cards as well as PPELC were maintained by the Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate, respectively. The discrepancies were reconciled. The accounting entry for the transfer was completed.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE do not include all PPEs in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the General Services Officer to prepare at the end of each accounting period a Report on Local Road Network and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; b. Require the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to submit the required reports so that the Municipal Accountant can record all the road network owned by the Municipality to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the infrastructure assets in the financial statements; and c. Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the accumulated depreciation for all road networks owned by the Municipality and provide the disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for the succeeding year as required by COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The GSO Designate has prepared the required report and has maintained a road inventory and road map. The required reports were furnished to the Municipal Accountant. This allowed the recording of road networks in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Not all road networks are provided with documents as basis thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to: a. Require the Municipal Engineer to ensure timely implementation of PPAs funded by NGAs and to supervise the completion thereof; b. The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the DOH on their request for realignment of the fund received in CY 2015 and cause the return thereof if not granted. Identify if the project funded by DTI in CY 2016 have already been completed and cause the return of the unused funds. Prepare and update schedules on the utilization to support the book balance and prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the erroneous recording of fund received from PhilHealth and CFI; and c. The Municipal Treasurer determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books and issue the check in favor of the Source Agencies to return all unutilized/unused amount, including the dormant balances. In case the source agency can no longer be identified, the check shall be made in favor of the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, significant PPAs funded by various national government agencies were implemented. The CY 2015 and 2016 PPAs were implemented. Excesses of funds were returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to: a. Remind the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to stop charging wages of Job Order Employees not engaged in solid waste management against the SWM Fund; b. Instruct the MENRO, who was delegated as person-in-charge of SWMP, to implement the planned and approved programs during the year; c. Conduct seminars and trainings to garbage collectors and personnel to ensure proper handling of solid wastes; d. Procure the required protective gears for garbage collectors; and e. Implement programs to campaign for the waste segregation, reuse, recycling and composting of solid waste.
observations.managementAction: The excess cash advances were returned. The planned PPAs were implemented. Seminars and trainings to garbage collectors and personnel were conducted. Procurements were made. Programs to campaign for waste segregation, reuse and recycling were conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified Persons Liable in the Notice suspension or Notice of Disallowance to settle their audit suspension or disallowance. Further recommended the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the Notices to the Persons Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Notices were already sent to persons liable.
observations.reason: Partial settlements were made. There was still a balance in the Notices of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Enjoin the department heads to strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on prioritization, training of personnel responsible for planning and implementation of GAD PPAs, and that the committee attend trainings; and b.
