Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: Prioritization and trainings to personnel responsible for planning and implementation of GAD PPAs were conducted. A GAD code was passed and made effective.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor to fully implement the 3rd General Revision of Assessment and update every 3 years thereafter, pursuant to Section 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The 3rd General Revision of Assessment was implemented throughout the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local School Board to implement the planned and approved PPAs during the year. It is also important that in planning, the needs of all the schools in the LGU are considered in the planning.
observations.managementAction: Identified PPAs during the year were implemented. It is understood that in the planning, the needs of all the schools in the LGU were considered.
observations.reason: Other PPAs were not implemented due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to intensify collection for those accounts invested in the program and to institute appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books and if warranted, request/apply for write off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances were collected and dormant balances were written off where applicable. Request for write-off was prepared for amounts that are dormant for several years.
observations.reason: To request/apply for write off of the accounts for those that are already dormant for several years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and require to: a) Liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances by compelling them to submit liquidation papers and refund the unused cash advances, if any; b) Monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; and c) Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advance to those who have previous unliquidated balances.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation of outstanding cash advances was made. Status of cash advances was continuously monitored. No additional cash advance is granted unless outstanding cash advance is settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Follow-up the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of understanding the environmental benefits of waste reduction and proper waste disposal; b) Require the necessary training to the garbage collectors and personnel to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and in accordance with the guidelines; c) Provide all collectors and other personnel directly engaged in the collection of solid waste with personal protective equipment to protect them from the hazards of handling wastes; and d) Enforce the proper segregation of solid waste at source and information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year AAR plan was already defended and is pending approval at the national level. Trainings to the collectors were conducted. Collectors were equipped with the necessary protective equipment. Proper segregation of solid waste was observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Require the GAD Focal Person and Committee to attend trainings to heighten their knowledge on gender audit and analysis to generate gender responsive GPB; b) Recognize gender issues within the mandate of the LGU and incorporate in the GPB the causes of the gender issues; and c) Assess the impact of GAD activities and policies in relation to gender equality and monitor the GPB implementation.
observations.managementAction: The necessary training was attended by the GAD Focal Person and the committee. The issues were recognized. A GAD code was passed. Assessment was made.

observations.recommendation: Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial accounts together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, remind the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.
observations.managementAction: Submission of accounts was timely made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the LGU strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF; b) the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance from Trust Fund into General Funds be made available for other social services; c) the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the MPDC and LDRRM Officer to Review the LDRRMFIP and AIP; and d) the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the trust fund which lapsed the 5-year period is transferred to the general fund.
observations.managementAction: The required appropriation for LDRRMF of at least 5% of income from regular sources was observed. The positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC 2014-1 were filled. The necessary transfer of the unexpended balance of P1,025,131.92 to Trust Fund was complied. The review of the LDRRMF and AIP was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials and employees refund the amount received as allowance/honoraria received without legal basis. Also recommended management to rectify the deficiencies and require the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess amount of P2,000.00 per day paid to the private institution.
observations.managementAction: Settlement was made. The accountable form No. 51 be issued for all payments. The concerned were directed to refund the said payments.
observations.reason: Only partial settlement was made. Not all persons liable have fully refunded their disallowance of P10,000.00 as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the audit recommendations towards improved financial operations and governance. Henceforth, require all concerned to strictly observe existing laws, accounting and auditing rules and regulations to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Complied other audit recommendations. Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance which as of December 31, 2021 were not yet settled.
observations.reason: There were still audit findings which recommendations were issued with Notice of Suspension or Disallowance which as of December 31, 2021 were not yet settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the accountable officers concerned to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The subject cash advances were already settled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management the following:
a) The respective personnel in charge of the noted deficiencies to submit the said required supporting documents; and
b) Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing the claims, otherwise the claim should be returned to the respective employee/ personnel for compliance of the requirements.
observations.managementAction:
Management Not
that the responsible
personnel will be

been submitted
as of date.
No supporting
disbursement
vouchers with
lacking
supporting
documents and
shall exert all

in completing all
the required

thereto.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit to the Audit Team the 20% Development Fund Utilization Report and accounts in CY 2018 for audit/evaluation.
observations.managementAction:
In compliance with the above recommendation,
the unsubmitted accounts in CY 2018 for management
submitted a copy of its Report on
Fund Utilization and Status of
Program/Project Implementation
under the 20% Development
Fund on June 3, 2021. However,
the submitted report only
included current year
appropriations. Details on
continuing appropriations were not
presented.
Likewise, the remaining
balance, including savings, under
the 20% Development Fund as of
December 31, 2018 was not
determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under the provision of R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of operations of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management has already posted the vacancies for the said positions but no applications were received to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of CDP and CLUP both plans for CY 2017 onwards.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the CDP and CLUP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency prepare and submit a copy of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency for 2017, 2016 and 2015 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted copies of the APPs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget officer to submit supplemental investment plan/ NADAI or additional appropriation ordinance as justification for the utilization of CY 2016 and CY 2017 Share from Tobacco Excise Tax.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the NADAI and Supplemental Investment Plan

observations.recommendation: We recommended that for the programs/projects and activities which are MOOE in nature which remained unimplemented/unobligated, the LDC should identify other projects needed to be implemented which are capital expenditures in nature in order to properly re-align the previously noted MOOE projects.
observations.managementAction: Implemented MOOE for CY 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a) Management see to it during the Budget planning phase, all programs, project and activities proposed to be charged to the 20% Development Fund conform to Item 3.1 of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1; and
b) Henceforth, Management should familiarize themselves with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the expenditure items covered and prohibited to be paid out of the 20% Development Fund for guidance.
observations.managementAction: Implemented for CY 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the following actions be undertaken by management:
a) The Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, thru the issuance of an SB resolution, create a MDRRMO and provide the LDRRMF appropriate PS and MOOE fund relative thereto; and
b) In the event that hiring a full time LDRRMO could not be made, the Municipal Mayor should see to it that the corresponding designation to be issued entails the services of a permanent official;
c) The Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRM Designate, Budget Officer for CY 2019 and Municipal Accountant Designate to account the disbursements funded by the 5% LDRRM Fund. Submission of accomplishment report which includes all the necessary supporting documents to support said claims should be prioritized; and
d) Concerned personnel familiarized themselves with the relevant rules and regulations with regards to the planning, allocation, utilization and generation of reports for the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The management has already created the position of LDRRM Officer and as to date the LDRRMF is now properly utilized and monitored.
An LDRRM Officer was designated by management.
observations.reason: SB Resolution/Executive Order for the creation of MDRRMO is yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the noted lapses, we recommended the following:
a) The Municipal Mayor creates a GFPS Execom, TWG and Secretariat that will supervise, advocate and install a GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) as well as the required Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System; and
b) The Municipal Mayor with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate and adopt a Local GAD Code.
observations.managementAction: The management has already created its GFPS, TWG and Administrative Order/Executive Order for the creation of GFPS and as to date the GAD FPS and M&E system is yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a) The concerned officials and employees justify the noted lapses otherwise these will be disallowed in our audit;
b) Submit the itinerary of travels and certificate of appearances for the paid travelling expenses with incomplete supporting documentations; and
c) Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer review the supporting documents of the claims for travelling expenses.
observations.managementAction:
No Action
Management noted that issues and concerns of previous year transactions, specifically 2013-2015 cannot be determined by management due to non-availability of documents upon turn-over in July 2016.
observations.reason: No documents submitted for CYs 2013-2015; Implemented for CY 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer explain the erroneous charging of trust bank accounts.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be more vigilant in charging an account whenever a disbursement is made by the Municipality to conserve money, time and effort.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 San Isidro, Isabela, page 47
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer is now withdrawing disbursements from trust funds to correct bank account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer submits all the documents or returns the unused cash to fully liquidate his cash advances at the end of the year to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly on the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 San Isidro, Isabela, page 48
observations.reason:
The cash advances of the former Municipal Treasurer were all liquidated in CY 2021.

Most of the cash advances by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer for CY 2021 were liquidated.

There as till unliquidated cash advances

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:
continue issuing demand letters to concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was not received, withhold from their salaries any unliquidated cash advances, considering that they have been notified and given adequate time to liquidate;  
exert extra effort to issue demand letters to employees and officials who are no longer connected with the LGU to immediately cause the liquidation or settlement of the unliquidated balances; and,
Strictly comply with the provisions of the abovementioned Circulars.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 San Isidro, Isabela, page 50
observations.reason:
All cash advances granted on CY 2021 were already liquidated

The Unliquidated Cash Advances pertains to previous years. No action was made by the LGU to recover the previous unliquidated cash advances.

Reiterated under Finding No. 7.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the implementing department (e.g. Engineering Office) to identify, determine and reconcile those projects that were already completed and close the same to the appropriate PPE account. Subsequently, prepare entries to transfer the PPE accounts amounting to P69,702,764.40  to the General Fund book to close the Government Equity account in the Trust Fund book.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 San Isidro, Isabela, page 56
observations.reason: The Municipal Account already recognized the appropriate PPE accounts for the completed projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management formulates well-defined policies and guidelines on donation/financial assistance containing but not limited to the following:
Kind of financial assistance (medical, educational, burial, etc.)
Approved amount per specific case, including the frequency of grants to each individual
Criteria in granting the donation and the documentary requirements for each particular case.
Medical, Educational and Burial Assistance should be referred to MSWDO for eligibility evaluation and the Case Study Report prepared should form part of the supporting documents for the claim.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 San Isidro, Isabela, page 61
observations.reason: The requests for financial assistance are now referred to the MSWDO. However, Management still failed to formulate well-defined policies and guidelines on donation/financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the afore-mentioned provisions of LBC 103 and Executive Order No. 77 s. 2019.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 San Isidro, Isabela, page 64
observations.reason: No disbursement for meals and snacks made for regular meetings and official travel for CY 2021

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Vice Mayor and BAC Chairperson to submit explanation why the above transactions should not be disallowed in audit and why the above procurements were made in piecemeal or in series although the same could have been conducted in a single procurement activity thru public bidding.

Further, we recommended to Management that:
 all procurement of the Municipality either through public bidding or alternative method, specially in the sending of RFQ, should be done by the BAC and not by the staff of requesting departmen/office.
 all procurements of the Municipality should be done in accordance with the approved APP, including changes thereto.
 the provision of Section 25 of  GAA FY 2020 on the allowed inventory requirement of supplies  be strictly observed and complied.
 the Municipal Accountant be instructed to make an accounting adjustment recognizing the Office Supplies Inventory account and derecognize the recorded office supplies expense account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 San Isidro, Isabela, page 66
observations.reason:
The Municipal Vice-Mayor was able to explain the circumstances that lead to the procurement of various office supplies.

The list of beneficiary of the office supplies (different schools and departments of LGUs) was submitted

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management, through the BAC Secretariat or the Municipal Accountant, furnishes the Office of the Auditor of the following:
Justification on why the payments of salaries/wages of the consultants should not be disallowed in audit;
Bidding documents, and other pertinent documents provided under RA 9184, in the procurement of the Consultancy Services; and
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter into contracts for the procurement of consultancy services.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 San Isidro, Isabela, page 71

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment;

Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties; and

Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.

We recommended the following:

Provide all the information deemed necessary in the evaluation of the projects to be presented in details upon submission of the report in order to establish reliability of the accomplishment embodied therein; and

Include those which are to be Implemented by the Municipality, whether infrastructure projects or other form of PPAs so as to facilitate prompt monitoring and validation thereof.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:

Create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE. To ensure the success of the committee, all department heads are advised to instruct their members to cooperate in the conduct of physical count;

The inventory committee shall prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation with PPE Ledger Cards and the Property Cards to be maintained by the Accounting and Property Offices, respectively;

Proper disposal of unserviceable property should be made.

The following provisions of Sec. 490 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies, materials and equipment. — Physical stock-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. In all cases, the physical inventory-taking which is required semi-annually or annually should be regarded with importance.

For effective control of government property, the COA prescribes the following regulations:

Chiefs of agencies are required to take a physical inventory of all the equipment and supplies belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, unless otherwise determined by the COA Chairman in specific cases. Such inventory shall be made as of December 31 on General Form 41 (A) (Appendix 12) and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, unless extended by the Chairman, Commission on Audit, upon prior request of the chief of agency concerned. When the exigencies of the service permit, the taking of inventory should be in the charge of a committee of two or more employees designated by the chief of agency, including the property officer or custodian, depending on the extent of property accountability in a particular agency, to be witnessed by a representative of the Agency Auditor.

Xxx”

The required annual/semi-annual inventory shall be certified correct by the committee in charge thereof, attested by the representative of the auditor concerned and approved by the chief of agency in interest.”
We recommended that the officials and employees concerned adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1 on appropriating and utilizing the 20% DF for development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor being the head of the Solid Waste Management Board direct the concerned officials/employees and offices to prioritize the SWM-PPAs in line with its ten-year period plan through inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget sufficient funds for its implementation.

We also recommended that the funded SWM-PPAs be immediately implemented.

We recommended that the municipality strictly utilize the 5% LDRRMF only for disaster risk management activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 so as to ensure that the objectives set forth in the allocation of the LDRRMF are achieved thru proper usage thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of PPE for submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards; (ii) allocate depreciation expense yearly to all Property, Plant and Equipment except Land, until fully depreciated; and (iii) make necessary adjustment for over allocation of depreciation expense; (c) oblige the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain property cards for PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of properties; and (ii) issue Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) to establish the accountability for properties; and (d) require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the conduct of physical count to establish correctness of balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make a detail review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are equal at all times.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) identify the problem concerning the failure to submit the paid Disbursement Vouchers and determine and implement the best possible measures for the prompt submission thereof; and (b) submit the required documents pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of PD No. 1445.
We also recommended the withholding of salary of persons responsible for the continued failure to submit the Disbursement Vouchers until the submission of all the required accounts/documents for audit.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly trial balances and year-end financial statements for all funds as required under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 70 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I as amended by Section 361 of Government Accounting Manual, Volume I, for LGUs.
Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive make a personal re-assessment and evaluation on the performance of the Accounting Unit and accordingly introduce necessary corrective measures pursuant to penalty clause under Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all subsequent financial reports are submitted within the reglementary period as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer, OIC-Municipal Accountant, OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and BAC Chairman to (i) explain in writing the inconsistencies of dates in the supporting documents of some transactions which resulted in the incurrence of obligations that can be considered irregular in nature; (ii) submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and comply with the required reportorial requirements of GPPB Circular 2020-001; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that all  the supporting documents are properly signed and the necessary information in various forms are properly and completely filled-up; (c) ICO-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant (i) explain why Disbursement Voucher (DV) and its corresponding check was prepared under the name of an individual who was not a party to the subject transaction; and (ii) ensure that in the preparation of DVs and checks, only the name of bona-fide suppliers/contractors shall be indicated as payees.

We recommended and the Municipal Development Council headed by the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to (i) refrain from charging expenditures which are not aligned in the fund’s intended purpose and (ii) refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures against the current appropriation; (b) Human Resources and Management Officer to review and verify the submitted DTRs and forwards the same to the Accounting Office for preparation of payrolls; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the payrolls based on the submitted Daily Time Records (DTRs) to avoid overpayment; and (d) Municipal Planning and Development Council to conduct cost-benefit analysis between rental and purchase of Heavy Equipment to arrive at a sound decision that would be most advantageous to the government.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Sangguniang Bayan, OIC-Municipal Accountant, Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) and BAC Chairman to (i) write explanation on the failure of the agency to revise the LDRRMFIP and APP to incorporate the expenses related to address the COVID-19 pandemic; (ii) submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for further review/evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and in compliance with the required reportorial requirements of GPPB Circular 2020-001; (b) Human Resources and Management Officer to review and verify the submitted DTRs and forwards the same to the Accounting Office for preparation of payrolls; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the payrolls based on the submitted Daily Time Records (DTRs) to avoid overpayment (ii) comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts in the Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; (ii) ensure that all  the disbursements are supported with complete documents before making the payment; and (iii) ensure that all  the supporting documents are properly signed and the necessary information in various forms are properly and completely filled-up.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to (i) refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures against the current year appropriations; (ii) obligate/recognize the expenditures in the year they are incurred to reflect the correctness of accounts recorded in the books; and (iii) adhere to the provisions of Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 119 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 in the use of appropriations to prevent the incurrence of irregular expenditures.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) require the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to  (i) take necessary action to prove the receipt of one beneficiary which cannot be located during the conduct of random confirmation; (ii) facilitate the certification of identification cards submitted by the beneficiaries; and (iii) ensure compliance with the guidelines on emergency program pursuant to DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 s. 2020; and  (b) MSWDO, OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) cause the submission of photos of the beneficiaries during the actual receipt of the assistance; and (ii) arrange the submitted identification cards in chronological order based on the name of beneficiaries as appearing in the payrolls.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that disbursement vouchers and check payments for fund transfers/financial assistance to Local Government Units or National Government Agencies be made payable to the official designation or position of the agency head or treasurer of the agency in compliance with Section 77 of the GAAM, Volume I; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant (i) ensure completeness of supporting documents before certifying/ signing box A of Disbursement Vouchers; (ii) cause the submission of lacking documentary requirement pertaining to release of fund totaling ₱400,00.00; and (c) require the head of schools to submit the utilization report on the funds received from the Municipality together with the other supporting documents such as pictures of completed projects/ and/or activities, etc., as proof/evidence that funds are utilized for intended purpose.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (a) render an explanation or justification on procurement of common-use supplies thru shopping instead of direct purchase from DBM-PS or thru competitive bidding; (b) adhere to Sections 10 and 54.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the general policy on competitive bidding and prohibition of splitting of contracts; and (c) resort only to alternative modes of procurement if the conditions provided under Sections 48, 52 and 53.9 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 are met.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the designated officer responsible for the receipt, distribution and inventory of donations in-kind to submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and supporting documents of the donations in-kind received from all the donors such as the acknowledgement receipt, proof of distribution and inventory report as required under Section II.1.a of COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Officer-in -charge to stop the practice of granting fuels to be used by private vehicles; (b) OIC-Municipal Budget Officer (i) meticulously check the available appropriation/budget for a particular disbursement before charging it against the fund; (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) present the expense accounts in the financial statements based on the nature of expense; (ii) ensure that all  the disbursements are supported with complete required documents before making the payment; and (iii) submit the lacking documentary requirements pertaining to the transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Officer to (a) intensify the information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with barangay officials; and (b) accelerate efforts in the enforcement of “no segregation, no collection” and “no littering” policies.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the GAD Focal Person to (a) prepare and submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report vis-a-vis Annual GAD Plan and Budget; (b) maintain a GAD database that could either be manually operated or developed through software; (c) propose a training program on the HGDG in its GPB for the incoming year and seek the assistance of a gender analysis specialist/expert in the use of and/or administration of the HGDG; and (d) deepen their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting and GAD-related tools among others.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to fully implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries and any amount due to the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities to avoid sanctions pursuant to Rule XIII, Enforcement and Monitoring of Decision.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (a) submit an explanation for the non-withholding of the required taxes for payment of utilities; and (b) ensure withholding of taxes on Government Money Payments due or payable to supplier of goods and/or services in accordance with RMC No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007 and Republic Act No. 1051 to avoid imposition of penalties equal to the total amount of tax not withheld pursuant to Section 251 of Republic Act 8424.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman, Human Resources and Management Officer and  General Services Officer/ICO-Municipal Treasurer to: (i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase/Job Orders/Contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and (ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively, on a sampling basis.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to (a) post signboards comprising relevant information of PPAs of the Municipality in either of the forms stated in Section 2.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to notify the public of the project to promote transparency and accountability; (b) stop the practice of putting billboard/poster bearing the name and image of the local official beside the required signboard in complying with the provisions of Section 2.2.6 of the same COA Circular No. 2013-004.; and (c) submit the required report such as the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year and monthly monitoring report.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare Disbursement Voucher for corresponding to the unremitted balance of Due to BIR account in compliance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012; and (ii) ensure that all taxes withheld for a particular month are remitted to avoid accumulation of unremitted balance of Due to BIR account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/loan repayments withheld to the GSIS and adhere to the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue remitting promptly all compulsory contributions/amortization withheld to the Pag-IBIG within the reglementary period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to strictly observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of premium contributions to PhilHealth.

We recommended that Management submit the year-end Financial Statements, trial balances and other financial reports on or before February 14 of the succeeding year as prescribed under Section 41(2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers to determine; at any given time, the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the Financial Statements. Also, submit the required records as provided under Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular 2010-001.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a.) prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same to COA for audit purposes; and b.) maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account for easy preparation of the monthly BRS.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof and to perform the same regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume 1, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management explain and/or justify the incurrence of cash deficit/overdraft in the Trust - Proper account.
Also, we recommended that management refrain from using trust fund money for purposes other than those for which the trust was created or the funds received pursuant to Section 305 (e) of RA 7160.
Lastly, we recommend that management ensure that trust liability obligations have sufficient cash back-up.
The Management commented that the overdraft was due to inadvertent error on the use of checks by the Treasury Department. However, the said error has been corrected trough the replenishment of the used fund from the correct account that the checks should have been withdrawn.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer explain the erroneous charging of trust bank accounts.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be more vigilant in charging an account whenever a disbursement is made by the Municipality to conserve money, time and effort.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer submits all the documents or returns the unused cash to fully liquidate his cash advances at the end of the year to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:
continue issuing demand letters to concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was not received, withhold  from their salaries any unliquidated cash advances, considering that they have been notified and given adequate time to liquidate;
exert extra effort to issue demand letters to employees and officials who are no longer connected with the LGU to immediately cause the liquidation or settlement of the unliquidated balances; and,
Strictly comply with the provisions of the abovementioned Circulars.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office record all purchases to its corresponding inventory classification and to adjust the account for whatever consumption on the basis of Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI), duly supported with the original copy of Requisition Issue Slip (RIS), which should be prepared and submitted five days after the end of the month to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that Management:
Require the driver/employee concerned to subject the motor vehicles/heavy equipment to perform pre-repair inspection before requisition for repair and post-repair inspection by designated LGU inspector to ascertain whether the desired repairs have been actually carried out.
Prepare Waste Material Report after every report and to turn-over to designated Property Officer all waste material even such materials are considered scrap items.
Require the designated property officer to maintain Equipment Ledger Cards indicating the history of repair for each motor vehicle/heavy equipment owned by the Municipality.
Require the Municipal Accountant to only process payments for repairs with complete supporting documents to ensure that government funds are for proper and valid claims.
The Management commented that most of the time repairs done on Heavy Equipment were on emergency basis. Oftentimes the driver/operator is the one who performed the repair especially when they are on field. However, the Municipality committed to comply with the audit observations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Vice Mayor and BAC Chairperson to submit explanation why the above transactions should not be disallowed in audit and why the above procurements were made in piecemeal or in series although the same could have been conducted in a single procurement activity thru public bidding.
Further, we recommended to Management that:
all procurement of the Municipality either through public bidding or alternative method, specially in the sending of RFQ, should be done by the BAC and not by the staff of requesting departmen/office.
all procurements of the Municipality should be done in accordance with the approved APP, including changes thereto.
the provision of Section 25 of  GAA FY 2020 on the allowed inventory requirement of supplies  be strictly observed and complied.
the Municipal Accountant be instructed to make an accounting adjustment recognizing the Office Supplies Inventory account and derecognize the recorded office supplies expense account.

We recommended that Managment require the MSWDO to provide justification, with complete supporting documents, on the aforementioned noted deficiencies on the payout of SAP especially the inclusion of the Job Order employees and Consultant as beneficiaries.
We also recommended that the Management require the above-mentioned JO employees and Consultant to refund the SAP received amounting to P5,500.00 each because they are not included as target beneficiaries of ESP, hence were not eligible to receive SAP from LGU, as provided under Item VI-B of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020.
The Mangement commented that the the basis of the Municipality in providing SAP to JO Employees are as follows:
Their salary of P300.00 daily is below the minimum regional wage P345.00 to P370.00 daily;
The JO employees’ salary depend on the days they work or thir attendance
They are exposed to hazards but were not provided  with hazard pay due to LGU’s limited budget
During pandemic, they work for more than 8 hours a day but has no overtime pay.
On the other hand, the basis for giving SAP to hired consultant were because she is a single mother with 3 schooling kids and is exposed to hazards during the pandemic and is not provided with overtime pay and hazard pay.
In addition, the Management commented that most of the names that appeared in payroll but not in theMasterlist was due to erroneous ttping/entry in the payroll of the names
The Audit Team stand firm that the the JO Employees and the Consultants should not be included as beneficiaries of SAP. The aforementioned DSWD Memorandum Circular cleary enumerated those eligible and non-eligibel beneficiaries of the SAP. Included in the non-eligible are those personnel contracted by the LGU such  JO employees and Consultants. Further, the said DSWD Memorandum did not give qualifications that will make JO employees and Consultant to be eligible of the SAP. The said DSWD Memorandum infact straighly stated that personnel contracted by LGUs are not eligible of SAP.
Further, the basis provided by the LGU in granting the JO employees of SAP were true to all JO employees of the LGU, thus it does not justify why only nine (9) of the fifty-one (51) JO employees were granted SAP by the LGU.

We recommended that management:
Adopt/install internal control on the transactions of the Municipality to safeguard its asset from wastage or loss.
Should have an updated report/data on the number of household in the Municipality in for a more comprehensive plan in the procurement and/or distribution of relief goods.
Require the Municipal Treasurer or Designated Supply Officer to accept all procured goods/property of the Municipality. In addition, the Municipality should create an Inspection Committee tasked to inspect all procured goods of the Municipality; and
Henceforth, report on the distribution of relief goods should specify the number/quantity and kind of relief goods distributed to make a comparison between the procured and distributed goods with the goods received by the beneficiary/recipient.

1.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should provide depreciation
for the Road Network account, specifically for those completed and
transferred from Trust Fund to General Fund.

entry to restore the amount in the books. Henceforth, he should monitor the
status of outstanding checks and prepare the appropriate journal entries to
restore to Cash in Bank account the equivalent amount of stale checks.

3.11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the lacking
procurement documents and justification why payments were made despite
the lacking documents.

that in all procurements under the Bayanihan Act, the bidder/supplier are
required to execute an Omnibus Sworn Statement with the stipulations
provided in Appendix 1 of GPPB Circular 01-2020; and

payment is made to avoid audit suspensions

4.11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Engineer
and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator should submit the
lacking documents justifying the approval of the variation order.

5.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should comply with the
requirement to deduct 10% retention money from the payments of progress

6.7. We recommended that BAC Chairman and its members, the TWG, the
Secretariat, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services
Officer should ensure that the Index-Based Pricing Scheme is adopted,
including the specific procedures required under the Appendix 22 of 2016
RIRR of RA No. 9184. More specifically, we recommend to:
g. indicate in the ITB among others the indicative volume to be procured for
bidding purposes only.
h. indicate in the Supplier’s bid price whether a discount was given and/or a
delivery cost was included.
i. include in he perfected contract provision that an index-pricing scheme
was applied, the manner of payment, the time of delivery, the required
volume for each delivery and other specific procedures prescribed in the
above-mentioned Guidelines.
j. ensure that the amount payable to the Supplier should be equivalent to the
unit/volume actually delivered in a specific period of time, with a
consideration to the weekly price adjustments, the discounts given and the
delivery cost.
k. require the Supplier to provide a detailed price adjustment in each
delivery, together with the certification under oath that the price
adjustments were the reported and duly posted at DOE’s website for
products.
l. ensure that the LGU’s end-user or acceptance team should maintain
records for the unit/volume of diesel actually delivered and the remaining
allowable unit/volume to be ordered, including the accounting of its
equivalent amount or total cost.

7.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General
Services Officer should require the drivers to properly accomplish the driver’s
trip ticket.
7.15. We also recommended the submission of the required supporting documents
on all payments for fuel consumption of government vehicles to minimize any
wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures. Otherwise, a notice of
suspension or disallowances shall be issued, if warranted.

8.5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the lacking DVs and supporting documents and an
explanation why the same could not be submitted within the reglementary
period.
8.6. Thereafter, ensure that submission of financial reports, DVs and attachments
be made within the prescribe period.

9.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should submit a justification
why: (a) the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF were not included
in the LDRRMIP, (b) the unexpended of prior years’ LDRRMF were not
transferred to the Special Trust Fund account, and (c) why the utilization of
prior year’s LDRRMF was made even if these expenditures were not included
in the LDRRMFIP.
9.10. We also recommended that henceforth, the Local Chief Executive should:
a) cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to
the Special Trust Fund account, and
b) require the LDRRM Council the inclusion in the LDRRMFIP, under
separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged/to be charged
under the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years and submit a copy
of the Revised LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team

10.10. We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer should prepare
a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances on all in-
kind donations, for the information of the donors and other stakeholders.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant to adopt perpetual inventory method in
accounting for purchases of inventories and maintain supplies ledger
cards to monitor the issuances of the inventories;
b. The General Services Officer to maintain stock cards pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002; and
c. The inventory committee to conduct physical count of inventories for
every semester and submit the Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) within the prescribed period.

2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Reconcile the lapsing schedule and the financial statements, and ensure
that all depreciable PPEs are provided with depreciation;
b. Submit the Journal Entry Vouchers and General Journal to support
the entries in the General Ledger of the PPE accounts; and
c. Reconcile the Accumulated Depreciation with the Depreciation Expense
for CY 2020.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Prepare adjusting entries to correct the noted deficiencies and submit
the same to the audit team for verification; and
b. Henceforth, record the purchase of equipment and relief goods under
the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment and/or Inventory
account in the succeeding procurement of disaster-related PPAs.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to make adjusting entry to correct the error in recording the
fund received exclusively for Vermicomposting Facility.

5.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from
Operating Activities to Surplus (Deficit) and to present the same either as
part of the Statement of Cash Flows or as disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements in compliance with the provisions of IPSAS No. 2 to
serve as proof of correctness of the reported net income (deficit) and the
net cash from operating activities.

6.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) are
prepared for all transactions of the municipality, as this shall serve as basis
for recording the transactions in the General Journal and Special Journals.

7.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Department
Heads of Municipal Health Office, MDRRMO, and Municipal Engineering
Office, and the Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson, in coordination
with BAC Secretariats and Municipal Accountant, to submit the lacking
documentary requirements as provided in Annex E.2 of this report, in
compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB
Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020.

8.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor
to expedite the processing of certificate of titles in favor of the municipality.

9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer the following courses of actions:
a. to post tarpaulin signboards at the locations of infrastructure projects
as soon as the award has been made to supplier; and
b. to complete the posting of relevant information on tarpaulin signboards
including the project duration, target completion date, project cost and
source of fund.

10.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Ensure that all appropriation ordinance enacted and implemented bear
his attestation/approval; and
b. Ensure that the leeway and flexibility afforded on the utilization of the
20% DF be optimally utilized for the benefit and welfare of the
constituent.

11.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to facilitate the implementation of
PPAs funded by the LDRRMF, and the amount granted by the Office of
the President.

Veterinarian, Sanggunian Bayan, and other concerned officials to
immediately comply with all the requirements set by the DBM for Local
Economic Enterprises to utilize the municipal abattoir, holding pen and its
access road for the benefit of the constituents and additional income for the
LGU.

the fund for its specific purpose and liquidate within 10 days after the

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the entries made in the Government Equity account and provide adjustments to correct the misstatement of the equity and other affected accounts in the current and prior years pursuant to the applicable accounting standards.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjusting entries based on the reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statement and to reflect the correct adjusted cash in bank balance.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books and eventually reported in the Financial Statements.

We recommend that the management reimburse the General Fund with the total amount erroneously charged against it, and instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the reimbursement in order to reflect the correct balances of the accounts of each fund. We further recommend that the proper Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment be issued to the recipient or end user of said equipment.

We recommend that management require that the Driver’s Trip Tickets be serially numbered and completely filled out with necessary details for the purpose of monitoring and controlling the use of government vehicles and consumption of fuel. Moreover, require the Municipal Drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle duly certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer and submit it to the auditor for verification. Further, the Agency personnel concerned have to prepare a Report of Fuel Consumption and submit it to the auditor on a monthly basis in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 to determine the reasonableness of the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses.

We recommend that management abide by the prescribed rates of Magna Carta Benefit for Public Health Workers as stipulated in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 of 2012.  Further, require the Municipal Accountant to check and refer from time to time different government rules and issuances that are applicable to disbursements to ensure that funds are used in accordance with accounting and auditing rules.

We recommend that the management ensure that the required and appropriate withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the pertinent BIR regulations and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period. Likewise, the management has to submit a justification for the noted deficiency.

We recommend that management submit a valid justification letter for not preparing the APP based on the required format. Moreover, management has to prepare the APP based on the approved format provided in the GPPB Circular 07-2015.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in the financial statements and facilitate the monitoring of its liquidation.

We recommend that management submit justification on the deficiency noted in the payment of catering services for LSI/Medical Teams. Furthermore, the BAC has to ensure that the returned RFQ from the suppliers of the catering services will indicate the food specifications/menu in support to the price quoted.

We recommend that management provide a written explanation or provide additional documentary requirement on this noted observation. Moreover, we recommend management and the BAC to observe and be updated with new procedures, guidelines and advisories on procurement required by the GPPB.

We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification for the procurement of the aforecited items above the suggested price of the DOH.  Henceforth, procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the Standard Retail Price (SRP) set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.

Directs the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all the funds/bank accounts maintained by the Municipality with the authorized depository banks every month duly reconciled with the cashbooks of the Treasury Office and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

1.	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

The Accounting and Municipal Agrarian Office shall fast-track in obtaining the necessary supporting documents to support the transfer of ownership of the donated livestock and to drop the amounts of the same and of the seedlings in the books of account.

The Accounting Office to establish and properly maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) for those accounts that are composed of several specific accounts/category/accounting responsibility; and make sure that beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous year.

Instruct the MTO to exert more effort in the collection of delinquent taxes, especially those due for over 10 years, otherwise, apply the provision of Section 259 of the RA 7160 regarding penalty for failure to issue and execute warrant of levy.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

1.	Strictly implement actions to strengthen its collection and monitoring procedures; and

Requires the TUPAD LGU Coordinator and the MSWD Office to notify the concerned beneficiary and take necessary actions and also for other concerned personnel to thoroughly review the qualified beneficiaries of the any assistance from the government to avoid any errors, duplications and other matters.

The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to obtain and maintain an updated records of all indigent and in-need individuals per barangay to fast track the distribution of the assistance and improve the system of distribution for future similar occurrences. Also, establish adequate guidelines on coordination mechanisms so as not to incur the same deficiencies in the future.

a. Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and

The Accounting Office install a scheme to facilitate the timely remittance of withheld amount to different government institutions and should strictly comply with law so as not to deprive the officials and employees of further benefits and to avoid the burden of any surcharges and interests not due to them.

(a) Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled-up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption; and

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize carefully the disbursement claims of the leagues of local elective officials for membership dues and contribution and determine if the rates/amounts claimed are strictly in accordance with their respective constitution and by-laws.

Strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also for the procurement of goods. Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with the at least 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount, otherwise any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period be charged to concerned approving officials.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the payment of honorarium to the Comelec Official and strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth by the Department of Budget and Management implementing the certain provisions of RA 7160.

Instruct the GFPS to appropriately identify PPAs that are gender-responsive and gender-sensitive for inclusion to its GAD Plan and stop disturbing GAD Funds to PPAs that are not related to gender and development.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the procuring officer record in the stock card the inventories received based on the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) and submit to the Municipal Accountant (MACCO) copies of the AIR, Purchase Order (PO) and delivery receipts. Issue RIS upon issuance of inventories which shall be summarized monthly in the RSMI. The RSMI together with the supporting RIS shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials to be reported in the RPCI to avoid the occurrence of recording inventory when these were all consumed or used in the project or activity.
We further recommended that the MACCO make the necessary adjusting entry/ies to correct the overstatement and henceforth ensure that inventory balances as of reporting date represents undistributed/unconsumed supplies/materials.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
MHO and all offices of the municipality who are recipients of goods/supplies from any government offices or private individuals/ entities to ensure that at all times a copy of the Invoice Receipt for Property, Property Transfer Receipt, RIS, Deed of Donation, or any document evidencing the goods/supplies received by their office shall be given to the MACCO; and
The MACCO to record the said receipt in appropriate income and inventory accounts as soon as a copy of the document evidencing the receipt of goods/supplies is received, and likewise draw necessary entry to expense the same upon submission of the end-users utilization report.

We recommended that the MACCO conscientiously review the entries made by the accounting personnel so that proper accounts are used upon recording the liquidation of petty cash fund.

observe the afore-mentioned rules and regulations on the hiring of consultants.
