observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency enforce strict compliance with the preparation and submission of monthly DTRs as basis in updating the leave ledger cards of all officers and employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XVII, Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292.
observations.managementAction: DTRs were prepared and submitted by the employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant (a) prepare and maintain a CIPLC on a per project basis; (b) ensure that in recording transactions, the proper accounts are used; and (c) effect the necessary adjusting entry for the transfer of the cost of the completed projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared and maintained a CIPLC on a per project basis; ensure that in recording transactions, the proper accounts are used; and effect the necessary adjusting entry for the transfer of the cost of the completed projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately reclassify the foregoing accounts into their proper accounts following the Revised Chart of Accounts as provided in COA Circular 2015-009 in consonance with the International Public Sector Accounting Standards to fairly present the municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Misclassified PPEs were already classified into their proper accounts following the Revised Chart of Accounts as provided in COA Circular 2015-009 in consonance with the International Public Sector Accounting Standards to fairly present the municipality’s financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer these accounts from the Trust Fund to the General Fund and to provide the depreciation for each asset account in order to fairly present the PPE and the equity balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already transferred these accounts from the Trust Fund to the General Fund and provided depreciation for each asset account in order to fairly present the PPE and equity balances

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry in order to fairly present the PPE and the Depreciation Expense balances.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries were already prepared in order to fairly present the PPE and the Depreciation Expense balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry in order to fairly present the PPE and the Repairs and Maintenance Expense balances.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries were already prepared to fairly present the PPE and the Repairs and Maintenance Expense balances

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable assets booked under the Property, Plant and Equipment in order to present accurate balances of the assets in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit an explanation of the non-depreciation of these assets.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation for all the depreciable assets booked under the Property, Plant and Equipment were already provided in order to present accurate balances of the assets in the financial statements. Non-depreciation of these assets was made since there is a difficulty on tracing the supporting documents of those properties as it was acquired wherein the current Municipal Accountant is not yet installed in the Municipality. Fortunately, the Dept. of Labor and Employment gave the LGU enough manpower to help on tracing those documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary reclassification entry to comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and use the correct account which is the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable.
observations.managementAction: Already reclassified to comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and use the correct account which is the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the preparation of journal entry vouchers as the source documents in recording in the General Ledger and consequently in preparing the financial statements. We also recommend that the vouchers be reviewed to check the correctness of the entries before its approval and recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant clarified that the error was only in the JEV but was properly recorded under Due to GSIS account per FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect adjustments to the Due from NGOs/POs Account relating to the grants to the individuals.

Further, we require the Municipal Accountant to verify the qualifications of the organizations granted assistance by recovering the documents supporting the transfers and coordinating with the BAC which is tasked to evaluate and accredit the NGOs/POs under COA Circular No. 2007-001.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments to the Due from NGOs/POs Account relating to the grants were already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected Cash in Bank and Advances to Officers to Employees Accounts. In case of lost check, we recommend that the payee submit a Sworn Affidavit of Loss to support the cancellation of check and removal of his account from the outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Already adjusted upon payee’s submission of Sworn Affidavit of Loss.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate in recovering the Disbursement Vouchers pertaining to the said transactions in order to effect the necessary adjusting entries in Cash in Bank account balance, otherwise, said disbursements have to be disallowed in audit.

The Municipal Treasurer must strictly adhere to the above-mentioned policies and not to release/issue check without a duly approved disbursement voucher because doing so renders the transaction irregular in nature and without basis. 

The Municipal Mayor must not approve the payment and sign on the check without the supporting documents necessary for its validity.
observations.managementAction: Unrecorded checks were recorded as its DVs together with its supporting documents were recovered

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that  intended purpose  is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18,  Republic Act 10121.
observations.managementAction: LDRRM Funds were properly utilized by the municipality in CY 2020 due to COVID Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal LDRRM Officer to provide explanation for the non-compliance with the required amount to be appropriated for the LDRRM Fund. Further, we require that in the succeeding budget periods, the municipality faithfully comply with the required amounts of appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Management properly appropriated the LDRRM fund for the year.

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the preparation of the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) as required by NGAS Vol. II to properly control and monitor the utilization of the accountable forms. The Municipal Treasurer must ensure that the accountable forms are properly accounted for in order to prevent any misuse or loss of said forms. Further, we recommend that the log book be maintained as a backup record to support the RAAF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office has already prepared their RAAF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of project completion. Likewise, corresponding liquidation report for the aforementioned project is requested for submission and for verification. We also recommend that liquidated damages be imposed and deducted from the total amount payable to the suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Liquidating damages were imposed and deducted from the total amount payable to the suppliers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱5,000 take home pay per month for them to meet their needs and maintain self-respect and dignity as employees of the government, in pursuance of Section 48 of the 2018 GAA.  

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and regulate/control loan availment by not signing certifications for application of loans should an employee net pay fall below ₱5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant and HRMO already monitor the payroll deductions and regulate/control the loan application.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Inventory Committee to establish existence of the breeding stocks by conducting physical inventory taking. Require the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAO) to maintain individual record of items to match/reconcile with the report of the inventory committee and the LGUs books.  Likewise, the Accounting Unit maintain ledger cards for Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Inventory was made by the Inventory Committee and Inventory report was produced.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre-disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020 said programs/ projects and activities was not charged anymore to 70% for pre-disaster preparedness fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that: 

The Municipal Treasurer must strictly adhere to the above-mentioned policies and not to release/issue check without a duly approved disbursement voucher because doing so renders the transaction irregular in nature and without basis. 

The Municipal Mayor must not approve the payment and sign on the check without the supporting documents necessary for its validity.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply strictly with the proper procedure regarding the disbursement of government funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply to RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.
observations.managementAction: Motor Vehicles are insured.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant investigate and examine why abnormal negative credit balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated above occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them. Likewise, Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has to be maintained as basis in recording in the GL.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has been maintained as basis in recording in the GL.

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough verification of the dormant items and request for write-off for the particular accounts that qualify and meet the requirements of COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997. Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments to clean the books of this deficiency. We further recommend a regular review of all account balances so that dormant accounts can immediately be identified and acted upon.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already conducted a thorough verification of the dormant items and made necessary adjustments for the three out of eight accounts that were dormant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit proper listing of accounts payable together with an aging schedule and other pertinent documents to facilitate the review that these payables are due and valid for reversion.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submit proper listing of accounts and some of the other pertinent documents are already submitted to facilitate the review that these payables that are due and valid for reversion.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% Development Fund and maximize utilization of the said fund to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of the 20% Development Fund of CY 2019 were already started. Remaining unutilized fund pertains to the fund for the on-going construction/ implementation of projects.

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit intact all the collections for a certain period with the depository bank and not to retain any amount to prevent the misuse and/or loss of the government funds.
observations.managementAction: The MTO collections are deposited but not complete as to full collected amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to require the daily turnover of collections by the collectors and that the turnover must be the total amount collected during the day. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer closely monitor the collections and remittances of the collectors in order to prevent the risk of the collections being misused and/or lost.
observations.managementAction: The practice of not remitting collections immediately to the Municipal Treasurer is still being done.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management the creation of an inventory committee to conduct the actual physical count of all the municipality’s PPEs as well as assess its actual condition to establish reliability of reported amount. Further, require the committee/s to prepare the inventory report and submit to the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 124 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: Inventory committee was updated per Memorandum 05-2019 and already conducted physical inventory taking.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry close/derecognize the balance of the above mentioned PPE items in order to fairly present the PPE balance.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries were already prepared in order to fairly present the PPE balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create an inventory and disposal committee to act immediately in the proper identification, ascertain existence, location and disposal of these assets in order to fairly present the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We further recommend that municipal accountant restore back the PPE accounts dropped from the books and adjustment have to be made only after proper inventory and disposal of the properties.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Team was already created to clearly verify those assets listed in the damaged assessment report during the 7.2 magnitude earthquake and made an Inventory Report of Property, Plant & Equipment as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
Must initiate the move by creating the inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties of the municipality.  The corresponding equipment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts.  In the absence of available records of acquisitions in the past years, they will have no other recourse but to take hold of the disbursement vouchers to obtain the necessary data to be recorded in the equipment/property ledger cards.

Require the general services officer or municipal treasurer to submit the request for inspection with complete supporting papers so that the auditors can take proper action on the request.
observations.managementAction: COA Technical personnel already inspected the damaged properties and were already dropped from the books basing from the inventory report made by the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer investigate and examine why negative balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated above occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them. Likewise, Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has to be maintained as basis in recording in the GL.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant already investigated and examined why negative balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents as noted above and to submit an explanation on the deficiencies enumerated on the remarks indicated. We recommend that the municipality observe and comply strictly with the documentary requirements as required by COA Circular 2012-001 and P.D. No. 1445 and submit them to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Acting Municipal Accountant to adhere with the required time frame for the submission of the monthly trial balance and schedules. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted incomplete documents without following the required time frame for their submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials/employees concerned to strictly comply on the prompt submission of copies of contracts, P.O.s and other similar contracts of understanding to the Auditor within five (5) days from its perfection pursuant to Section 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted copy of Purchase Orders.

observations.recommendation:
1.	Submit justifications/explanations why the Municipal Mayor was made the payee of the purchase of lot instead of the true  vendor.

2.	Submit justification why the same amount was booked as Accounts Payable still with the Mayor as payee. 

3.	Submit justifications/explanations why the dates in the submitted documents did not reconcile with the logical chronology of things.

4.	Submit justifications/explanations why DBP CheckNo.  43830881 dated September 23, 2014 previously issued to the Municipal Mayor and encashed on September 24, 2014  was the same check allegedly issued/received by Uri Sallacay  per undated DV No. 100-15-01-68.

5.	Submit all documents to support the purchase of the lot pursuant to Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and  other pertinent provisions to support the payment.
observations.managementAction: The management is still in the process of complying the requirements for the titling of these lands.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and  Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts.

Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.


Adopt and always observe austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already maximized the services of permanent employees by assigning additional functions not related to their actual term of reference.

observations.recommendation:
Require the municipal accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV) to reclassify cash advances  for travel and special purpose/ time-bound undertakings  recorded under the Receivables- Due from Officers and Employees (123) account to the appropriate account, Advances to Officers and Employees (148) pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002 dated January 19, 2007.  On the other hand, the cash shortage of Benjamin Abad amounting to ₱491,497.84 should be reclassified to the account Due from Officers and Employees (123).  

Send demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitor the liquidation of these cash advances.

Exert more effort to collect these receivables such as withholding payments of claims of municipal officials/employees who have long overdue accountabilities with the LGU as in the case of audit disallowances.

Follow-up with the Office of the Ombudsman the status of the case filed against the defalcating municipal treasurer.

Issue bills or collection/confirmation letters  to  agencies/persons concerned  as  proofs  that the Municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means  to collect these receivables that have become dormant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has sent demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitored the liquidation of these cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend immediate liquidation/settlement of outstanding cash advances for Confidential/Intelligence Fund. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on allowing the grant of cash advances despite non-liquidation of previous cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant no longer grants cash advances relating to Confidential/Intelligence Fund with an outstanding unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to submit copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports on procured items to the Auditor’s Office within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to comply with the provision of Paragraph 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and for the Auditor to conduct timely inspection of items delivered.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports of procured items to the COA but beyond the required deadline.

observations.recommendation: Exercise fully the taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to 150 of R. A. 7160 in order to earn more revenue necessary to augment the funding for the operation of the municipality.  Otherwise, implement austerity measures to reduce operating expenses and utilize internal revenue fund for developmental projects that would be beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management already reduced operating expenses but to earn more revenue, the Municipality failed to maximize the exercise of its taxing power due to limited manpower in the treasurer’s office.
The management already reduced operating expenses by reducing staff from market operations and intensifying collection for market fees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the municipality and all concerned personnel to observe and comply the requirements and procedures as established in the GAM Volume I and COA Circular 2012-003. We recommend that strict adherence to the rules and procedures concerning the processing, releasing, and recording of cash advances be observed by the Treasurer and Accountant. Further, we recommend the municipality to submit an explanation on the above mentioned deficiencies/irregularities to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Accountant who is now the Municipal Mayor paid the amount of ₱120,000.00

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the classification/identification of payable account to establish correct balances in the financial statements.  Comply COA Circular No. 2004-008 and COA Circular No. 94-103.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going classification/identification of payable for Due to NGAs account to establish correct balances in the financial statements by the accounting staff.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management prepare documents to transfer these PPE items to the barangays. After which we recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the overstatement of the LGU’s asset, contra-asset and equity accounts.

Further, the journal entries have to be supported with proof of transfer of ownership of the PPE items to barangays.
observations.managementAction: The management is still on its process of preparing and securing documents to transfer these PPE items to the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the BAC or a committee responsible for the accreditation of NGOs/POs in recovering the related supporting documents to these fund transfers. See to it that the documentary requirements for release of funds to NGOs/POs as specified in COA Circular 2012-001 be complied before transfer is made.
observations.managementAction: No action

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to trace the material variance of ₱22,662,054.80 for Cash in Bank Account and ₱23,730.11 for Cash in Local Treasury Account as of December 31, 2019; (b) require henceforth, reconciliation of entries in the cashbook and general ledger at least on a quarterly basis and promptly resolve any significant discrepancy accordingly.
observations.managementAction: No action

observations.recommendation: We recommend management the immediate submission of the liquidation report to the Office of the Chairman, COA, Quezon City and furnish to COA Audit Team the transmittal letter of the said liquidation report duly stamped/received by the said Office as proof of compliance of the required report.
observations.managementAction: The management planned to recover and gather the vouchers and documents for submission to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the recipient barangays to liquidate the funds. Henceforth, observe COA Circular Nos.2012-001 and 2015-009 on the documentation and recording requirements on fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall not be released and shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for the other priority programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submitted the JEV attributing to the removal of the said amount from the Due To NGAs and Due From LGUs accounts.

observations.recommendation: Directs the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all the funds/bank accounts maintained by the Municipality with the authorized depository banks every month duly reconciled with the cashbooks of the Treasury Office and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

1.	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and

2.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

1.	Adhere strictly with laws and provisions concerning proper usage of accounts for every transactions and events; and

2.	Use the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account for the recording of cash advances for confidential and intelligence funds from the Municipality.

The Accounting and Municipal Agrarian Office shall fast-track in obtaining the necessary supporting documents to support the transfer of ownership of the donated livestock and to drop the amounts of the same and of the seedlings in the books of account.

The following remedial measures to ensure the proper recording and monitoring of earned leave benefits:

1. 	Require the HRMO to monitor the accumulated leave credits of the Master List of the LGU and to furnish a copy to the Accounting Office;

2. 	After ensuring the reliability of the accumulated leave credit balances of employees as of December 31, 2020, require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to record the Accrued Terminal Leave Benefits due to the employees; and

Terminal Leave Benefits (current year)		xxx
Government Equity (prior years)		xxx
Leave Benefit Payable			xxx

3. 	Direct the MBO to annually appropriate for the accrued terminal leave benefits earned by each employee even on a staggered basis depending on the availability of funds.

The Accounting Office to establish and properly maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) for those accounts that are composed of several specific accounts/category/accounting responsibility; and make sure that beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous year.

Instruct the MTO to exert more effort in the collection of delinquent taxes, especially those due for over 10 years, otherwise, apply the provision of Section 259 of the RA 7160 regarding penalty for failure to issue and execute warrant of levy.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

1. 	Strictly implement actions to strengthen its collection and monitoring procedures; and

2. 	Direct Local Finance Committee to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and realistic projection of revenues and conduct an analysis of identified revenue accounts with decreasing trend in collection.

Requires the TUPAD LGU Coordinator and the MSWD Office to notify the concerned beneficiary and take necessary actions and also for other concerned personnel to thoroughly review the qualified beneficiaries of the any assistance from the government to avoid any errors, duplications and other matters.

The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to obtain and maintain an updated records of all indigent and in-need individuals per barangay to fast track the distribution of the assistance and improve the system of distribution for future similar occurrences. Also, establish adequate guidelines on coordination mechanisms so as not to incur the same deficiencies in the future.

a. 	Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and

b. 	Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

The Accounting Office install a scheme to facilitate the timely remittance of withheld amount to different government institutions and should strictly comply with law so as not to deprive the officials and employees of further benefits and to avoid the burden of any surcharges and interests not due to them.

(a)	Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled-up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption; and
(b)	Direct the concerned personnel to prepare and submit to the Auditor, on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis, verification and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize carefully the disbursement claims of the leagues of local elective officials for membership dues and contribution and determine if the rates/amounts claimed are strictly in accordance with their respective constitution and by-laws. 

b) 	Instruct the Municipal Treasurer not to release payments to leagues without the government prescribed O.R.; and

c) 	Require leagues annual accomplish reports, as a prerequisite for the renewal of membership fees in the ensuing years, to ascertain that government funds have been utilized for lawful and exclusively for public purposes.

Strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also for the procurement of goods. Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with the at least 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount, otherwise any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period be charged to concerned approving officials.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the payment of honorarium to the Comelec Official and strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth by the Department of Budget and Management implementing the certain provisions of RA 7160.

Instruct the GFPS to appropriately identify PPAs that are gender-responsive and gender-sensitive for inclusion to its GAD Plan and stop disturbing GAD Funds to PPAs that are not related to gender and development.

Direct the GAD Focal Point System to properly plan and regularly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs and maximize the utilization of the GAD budget to achieve the objective of the government regarding the institutionalization of GAD in the community.

We recommended directing the Municipal Accountant to properly classify the current and non-current portions of the Loans Payable-Domestic with its appropriate amount in the presentation of the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: The management already stopped the granting of cash advances to those personnel with outstanding cash advances. Also, the request for write-off will be submitted as soon as the needed supporting documents are already available.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Stop the practice of transferring cash advances drawn to other personnel; and

Consider designating personnel of the other office/department as a disbursing officer of the cash advances for special purpose/s.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the analysis of the balance of the Government Equity account under the Trust Fund and to determine and effect the appropriate reclassification or adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The management will review and make adjustments to correct the account.
observations.reason: The management had already started reviewing the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly monitor the implementation of the projects by establishing a system for monitoring the projects so that prompt action can be made to address the issues/constraints causing the delays in the implementation of the projects.

We recommended that management:

Adequately plan and study the feasibility of each project before proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by the 20% LGDF;

Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the speedy implementation of the projects; and 

Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents in terms of PPAs to be implemented under 20% LGDF.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
Only four (4) projects remained unimplemented:

Construction of Sanitary Landfill
Land Acquisition for Slaughterhouse
Greening with Landscaping Project – Sangguniang Bayan Ground
Construction of Multi-purpose Building for Civil Society Organizations – Continuing Appropriations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Enforce strict control procedures in the disbursement of the fund to ensure that it is utilized only for its intended purpose to preclude the issuance of audit suspensions/disallowances; and

Adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of law, rules, and regulations relative to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund, so that constituents could directly enjoy the benefits for which the fund was primarily established.

We recommended that the management thoroughly plan and ensure proper monitoring of the projects and activities to be implemented under the LDRRMF such as the preparation of a monitoring report that would check the status of each project/activity. Also, ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents in terms of disaster preparedness and mitigation. Further, we also recommend that the excess appropriation be reprogrammed for other disaster preparedness and mitigation activities for inclusion in the revised Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2020.

We recommended that management:

Strictly enforce the "No Segregation, No Collection" Policy on wastes to be disposed of;

Prioritize the purchase of additional equipment for garbage collection and operation of disposal facility; and 

Expedite the processing of the necessary procedures and provide adequate funding for the establishment and utilization of the SLF.
We recommended that management exhaust all measures to ensure that the vacant stalls will be leased to prospective vendors in order to generate more revenues for the LGU.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:

Issue a uniform and clear-cut policy on the hiring of Job Order workers to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto;

Reduce/limit the hiring of job order workers and fill-up vacant positions; and

Evaluate and determine the appropriate personnel complement of each office.

We recommended that Management evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of the accounts with negative balances, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances. Further, ensure accuracy in the recording of transactions to their appropriate SL accounts in order to avoid payment of unnecessary penalties and surcharges to various government agencies.
observations.managementAction: The management will verify the accounts and will effect necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following:

Regularly send confirmation/demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures;

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts.

In compliance with COA Circular 2012-004, furnish this Audit Team with the list of accountable officers with unliquidated and outstanding cash advances, together with copy of written notices duly acknowledged by the concerned accountable officers.  Submit also copies of disbursement vouchers (DVs) and related support documents of those with outstanding balances, especially long overdue accounts.

This LGU is reminded further of the consequences provided for in section 9.0 of the same COA Circular 2012-004, as follows:

“9.1 Failure of an accountable officer to liquidate his outstanding cash advance on or before January 31, 2013 shall constitute cause for the filing of malversation charge under Article 217, failure to liquidate cash advance under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of PD No. 1445.

9.2  The suspension of salaries of erring accountable shall be ordered by the Auditor concerned to the proper agency official through the head of the agency.

9.3 Appropriate administrative proceedings shall likewise be instituted.”

The concerned Municipal Officers should submit to COA a written explanation for non-compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386, the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government.
We recommended that the MACCO appropriately classifies the Loans Payable-Domestic into current and non-current payables in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor requires the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to trace all mortalities of dispersed animals since the inception of the project until CY 2019 and to substantiate all claims of loss through death of said animals with complete documentary attachments upon filing of a request for relief from accountability for the proper disposition thereof pursuant to Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the MACCO to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and send demand letters to concerned party/ies to enforce collection of dormant accounts.  Cause the application of appropriate sanctions against erring borrowers, if necessary.

We also recommended that the  MACCO evaluates the status of the outstanding receivables and to request for write-off of accounts whose balances are inactive or non-moving for ten years or more and its settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained in accordance with existing auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended full compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, otherwise, withholding of salaries of accountable officers and or officials/employees concerned who fail to effect prompt liquidation in accordance with existing rules and regulations shall be made. 

Dissemination of existing rules and regulations on cash advances to all officials and employees was likewise enjoined.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Treasurer to make representations with the bank to discontinue the auto-debit arrangement on loan payments and to regularly secure copies of billing statements to serve as basis in the preparation of disbursement vouchers and obligation requests for payment thereof.

We recommended that Management instructs all department heads and/ or concerned offices to fully comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 and designate a focal person to consolidate the reports submitted by each department and to thoroughly review the reports before submission to COA in hard and electronic copies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government of San Isidro immediately establish the MDRRMO and hire qualified personnel as required under RA 10121 and JMC Circular No. 2014-1.  The creation of LDRRMO in every LGU is one of the basic elements in disaster preparedness.  It is primarily responsible for carrying out or implementing various disaster risk reduction and management plans/programs of the municipality to ensure the safety and protection of the LGU when disaster/calamity occurs.
We recommended that the MACCO complies with the procedures on the recording of transactions for fund transfers prescribed in the NGAS and in conformity with applicable PPSAS.

We recommended that:

The MACCO and the MEO reconcile their records to determine the actual status of the said projects;

The MEO provide the MACCO with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents to serve as the basis for the transfer of these projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; and

Based on the adjusted data, the MACCO computes the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.

The MACCO and Municipal Treasurer immediately facilitate the return of the unutilized balance to the source agency or deposit the amount to the National Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the MEO to install the billboards, or monitor the installation thereof, following the required specifications of tarpaulins/billboards for infrastructure projects as stated under COA Circular No. 2013-004. For on-going projects, submit proof of compliance therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the BAC Members and its Secretariat to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken are in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 to safeguard the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the local finance committee to revisit the provisions on the imposition of the market rates and fees for possible modification, if warranted, and submit its proposals to the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriate action. Otherwise, exert additional efforts to operationalize the public market.

We recommended that the LDRRMO ensures that the RDS be properly filled-up and a separate record to monitor the goods by kind should be prepared to serve as a guide in determining actual inventory levels. Likewise, install appropriate measures in safeguarding its inventories to avoid the risks of inventory infestation, pilferage, spoilage and expiration of goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Accounting Office to properly deduct withholding tax on professional fees, talent fees and similar payments to professionals and other recipients not otherwise subject to withholding tax on compensation as required under subsection (A) of Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to trace all mortalities of dispersal animals from inception of the project until CY 2018 and to substantiate all claims of loss through death of said animals with complete documentary attachments upon filing of a request for relief from accountability for the proper disposition thereof pursuant to Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to demand the collection of the P471,556.17 Loans Receivable-Others from its borrowers and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like. Management should undertake reasonable efforts to collect this debt owing from the borrowers lest the intention of the program benefits will be defeated.

We recommended that the Municipality source the payment of honoraria to its BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat from those expressly enumerated under DBM BC 2007-3, otherwise, the irregular payments thereof shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the MOAs pertaining to all BuB and other NGA-funded projects of the Municipality for the years 2015 and above and should exert all reasonable efforts and diligence in procuring said contracts/ MOAs in a timely manner from the source agency.

The Local School Board should ensure that expenditures charged against the SEF include only those among the priority list of expenses to be charged against SEF, thus guaranteeing the welfare of the school children and that SEF are being utilized for the purpose for which it was created; and

We recommended that management intensify its efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of preserving our natural resources by proper disposal of wastes.

2. We also recommended that management comply with all the applicable rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and the environment by utilizing the 2.9 hectares lot that the municipality purchased last 2016.

(iii) file/institute the necessary administrative and/or criminal proceeding against the Accountable Officer pursuant to Articles 171 and 217 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: The management terminated the accountable officer.
observations.reason: The Accountable Officer still failed to return the missing funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWDO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to 

(a) strictly adhere to the fundamental principles in the disbursement of funds prescribed under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; 

(b) observe full compliance with the requirements consistent with the guidelines of the DSWD in the implementation of Cash for Work for Climate Change  Adaption and Mitigation Program; and 

(c) facilitate the immediate submission of lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor such as the cash for work vouchers of the respective claimants/beneficiaries, logbook certified by the assigned person-in-charge/Team Assigned Worker, Certificate of Indigency and Residency duly certified by the respective Punong Barangay and validated accomplishment reports pertaining to the program.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: The lacking documents were still unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the 

(a) Municipal Administrator to 

(i) submit documents or any proof that will show where the 1,028 pieces of tarpaulin were used; and 

(ii) submit written explanation why the purpose and information to be printed in the tarpaulin were not indicated in the Purchase Request; and 

(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of information and supporting documents before certifying or signing box A of the Disbursement.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason:
The purpose for which the tarpaulins were used was still not provided.

Reiterated as part of Observation No. 9.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to 

(a) only include projects under the 20% Development Fund which are in conformity with the guidelines in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and 

(b) refrain from charging expenditures that are not aligned in the fund’s intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason:
Various ineligible expenditures were still charged to the 20% Development Fund.

Reiterated in Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management revised its Local Revenue Code to generate more revenues in order to strengthen the cash position of the Municipality and to afford for more development projects for its constituents.

observations.reason: The Local Revenue Code was still unrevised.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to conduct examination of books, accounts and pertinent records of registered businesses operating within its jurisdiction to ensure that correct amount of business tax is collected; 

(b) BPLO 
(i) to be prudent in assessing the declared gross sales to compute for the correct amount of tax due; and 

(ii) require submission of sworn statement of gross receipts or sales and copy of Certified Income Tax Return as required in the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality; and 

(c) BPLO and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to cause recovery of the under collections of ₱77,715.00 arising from undeclared gross receipts/sales of the six (6) sampled business establishments.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Examination of books of accounts was still not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board ensure compliance with the limitations provided in Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160; RA No. 5447; DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 2017 and its implementing regulations.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason:
Ineligible expenses were still charged against the fund.

Reiterated in Observation No. 6.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive procure all common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM.

Likewise, the Municipal Government should continuously submit yearly to the DBM, its Annual Procurement Plan for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment to assist the Procurement Service in strengthening its service in delivery and expanding its product list.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason: Common-use supplies were still purchased from outside sources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local DRRM Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012–002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere with the provision of Section 5.5 of JMC 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA regarding submission of Accomplishment Report not later than end of January of the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason:
The Audit Team was not furnished with copy of GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2019.

Reiterated in Observation No. 22.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with provision of Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996,  requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection and Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries to validate if the same conform with the quality and quantity asked for.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason: Copies of contracts, purchase/job orders and notice of deliveries were still not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: No action taken  by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and record OBR for all procurements based on the Purchase Request and prior to issuance of PO. If the amount paid differs from that stated in the OBR, the difference should form part of the unobligated allotment and the amount of recorded obligations should be adjusted in the RAAOs accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed to comply with the recommenda-tions
observations.reason: OBR are still prepared after the pre-audit of DVs by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management the immediate submission of the liquidation report to the Office of the Chairman, COA, Quezon City and furnish to COA Audit Team the transmittal letter of the said liquidation report duly stamped/received by the said Office as proof of compliance of the required report.
observations.managementAction: The management planned to recover and gather the vouchers and documents for submission to COA.
observations.reason: These accounts are still unliquidated per verification with the Report on Cash Advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the municipality and all concerned personnel to observe and comply the requirements and procedures as established in the GAM Volume I and COA Circular 2012-003. We recommend that strict adherence to the rules and procedures concerning the processing, releasing, and recording of cash advances be observed by the Treasurer and Accountant. Further, we recommend the municipality to submit an explanation on the above mentioned deficiencies/irregularities to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the recipient barangays to liquidate the funds. Henceforth, observe COA Circular Nos.2012-001 and 2015-009 on the documentation and recording requirements on fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre-disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained.

observations.reason: Review of the budget and utilization report revealed that the non-related expenses were still incurred.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that: 

The Municipal Treasurer must strictly adhere to the above-mentioned policies and not to release/issue check without a duly approved disbursement voucher because doing so renders the transaction irregular in nature and without basis. 

The Municipal Mayor must not approve the payment and sign on the check without the supporting documents necessary for its validity.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to comply strictly with the proper procedure regarding the disbursement of government funds.
observations.reason:
Review of the Report of Checks Issued revealed that some checks were still issued ahead of the preparation of the corresponding Disbursement Vouchers (DVs).

Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all his collections with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) daily or on the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the classification/identification of payable account to establish correct balances in the financial statements.  Comply COA Circular No. 2004-008 and COA Circular No. 94-103.

observations.reason: No detailed supporting schedules were submitted to the audit team to verify the balances and the corresponding projects or purposes for the funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) intensify collection actions to recover the government funds invested in the program and to institute appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books; and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 directed the Municipal Accountant to take appropriate actions.
observations.reason: The accountant is still on the process of gathering data that would constitute for the eventual approval by the Commission of the request to write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough evaluation/reconciliation of the journal entries made to determine the causes of the discrepancy. observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the return of the unutilized balances to various source agencies and constantly monitor and review the composition of the account to avoid dormancy of the balances.
observations.managementAction: The management is still on the process of tracing the remaining amount to be returned to the various NGAs.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Include those which are to be Implemented by the Municipality, whether infrastructure projects or other form of PPAs so as to facilitate prompt monitoring and validation thereof.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by management.
This was not prioritized by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of accounts with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances.

The inventory report submitted was only as of June 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to trace all mortalities of dispersal animals from inception of the project until CY 2018 and to substantiate all claims of loss through death of said animals with complete documentary attachments upon filing of a request for relief from accountability for the proper disposition thereof pursuant to Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to the audit team.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part 11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality source the payment of honoraria to its BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat from those expressly enumerated under DBM BC 2007-3, otherwise, the irregular payments thereof shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Head of Procuring Entity require the Bids and Awards Committee and other participants on the procurement of goods to strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 dated October 28, 2016 and other issuances related to procurement. Otherwise, any procurement not in accordance with the procurement law shall be disallowed in audit. Likewise, we recommended that the supporting documents attach to the claim be thoroughly checked before approval for payment is made.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation/completion of projects from prior and current years that have unobligated allotments.

Further, we recommended that the Local Development Council should optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through: (a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; (b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result and (c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality strictly utilize the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) only for disaster risk management activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 so as to ensure that the objectives set forth in the allocation of the LDRRMF are achieved thru proper usage thereof.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor implementation of awarded projects to ensure that these are completed as scheduled. Ensure that projects are inspected on their scheduled completion dates so that percentage of unfinished work is determined as basis in the computation of liquidated damages to be deducted in the final billing. And comply with the required documentary requirements in granting time extensions.

Moreover, we recommended that the BAC issue a resolution blacklisting the concerned contractor for willful or deliberate abandonment or non-performance of the project or contract by the contractor resulting to substantial breach thereof without lawful and/or just cause pursuant to GPPB Resolution No. 40-2017.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to evaluate the collectability of the Loans Receivable-Others and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office its recommendation. If warranted, request for write-off of certain accounts in accordance with the COA guidelines.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office set-up the related impairment loss on long outstanding Loans Receivable – Others based on the data provided by the PESO.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Part II
Not yet reverted because they are still contemplating to continue the project by administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to ensure compliance to Section 262 of GAAM, Section 4 (2) of PD 1445, and Section 335 of RA 7190 by stopping the practice of accommodating payroll deductions for personal loans of its employees acquired from private banks and other lending institutions.
observations.reason: With MOA entered by LGU that they must abide.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1.	Stop the practice of assigning job order personnel to collect revenues;
	
Utilize the existing bonded collectors to collect revenues under the strict   supervision of the Municipal Treasurer; and

3. If warranted by funds, consider the regularization of the job order personnel so as they can perform collection functions.
observations.reason: Lack of Funds

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should exert additional efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government within the prescribed period and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty imposition except if waived by concerned parties.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that a copy of the report of inspection or its equivalent shall be submitted to the Head of the Auditing Unit within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered and, in the case of unserviceable and disposable property/assets, immediately after inspection thereof by management.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipality assign the inspection of the procured items to personnel not under the control or supervision of the supply/property officer of the Municipality who is entrusted to accept the items delivered.
observations.reason: Management still failed to submit the AIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should provide a written explanation on what caused such delays or if there were time extensions granted and to exert extra efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty deduction.
observations.reason: Delays were still observed in the implementation of projects funded by national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee thru the BAC Secretariat comply with the requirement on the submission of perfected contracts and/or purchase orders together with all supporting documents within five (5) days after approval to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to submit the contracts/ purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof within 5 days from approval thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management immediately use the amount deposited in the HYSA to pay off portion of the principal amount of the loan to economically manage its resources.
observations.reason: Management explained that they are earning from such deposit which form part of the LGU income.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that legal processes be availed of to recover the amount of P196, 585, 74 from the supplier JVC, which amount was erroneously charged against the funds of the municipality. Also, require the personnel responsible for the omission to submit written justification why he/she may not be held personally responsible therefore.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Part II

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We have reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor:
1.9.1 Require the Municipal Accountant – Designate and Treasurer to
submit the remaining DVs and all other reports/documents enumerated
in item 1.6 as soon as possible;
1.9.2 Determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to comply
with the required submission dates of accounts and reports;
1.9.3 Hire a qualified Municipal Accountant or assign additional personnel
in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate prompt submission
of accounts and reports;
1.9.4 Ensure that the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Acting
Municipal Treasurer exert all necessary efforts to render the agency’s
accounts within the prescribed period; and
1.9.5 Consider imposing sanctions to officers and employees due to failure to
render reports and accounts as provided for in Section 122 of PD 1445.

2.10 We have reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant - Designate henceforth
maintain, on a timely basis, the required registries, accomplish the required
obligation requests as well as the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and
Obligations.

3.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate review
the journal entries made for the above transactions and consider the
following proposed adjusting entry:
Account Name Debit Credit
Advances to Officers and Employees 550,000.00
Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account 10,142.54
Due to BIR 101.15
Due to PhilHealth 51,908.10
Due to NGAs 6,875.00
Due to LGUs 127,621.30
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable 0.01
Other Payables 32,285.38
Prior Period Adjustments/Government Equity 341,351.60

3.5 We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
coordinate with the LGU’s depository bank in order to identify the nature
of the credit/debit memos reflected in the bank statements and make the
necessary adjustments in the books.

3.6 We have also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant –
Designate:
3.6.1 Analyze transactions carefully prior to recording in the book of
accounts;
3.6.2 Prepare monthly reconciliation statements, not only with the records
per bank, but also with the records per cashbook of the Municipal
Treasurer, and ensure that any reconciling items are entered in the
books of accounts and cashbook, whichever is applicable; and
3.6.3 Submit the said reconciliation statements to this office within the
prescribed period.

3.7 Finally, we recommend Management for the submission of the above noted
unsubmitted check disbursement vouchers/ liquidation vouchers as
discussed in Item 3.2.3.

4.6 We have recommended that Management retrace all transactions prior to
November 1, 2018 which might have affected the Cash – Local Treasury
and Advances balances in the Financial Statements as well as in the
Cashbooks to determine the causes of the noted discrepancies.

4.7 We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider
the proposed accounting entry, as follows, to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts:
Account Name Debit Credit
Prior Period Adjustments/Government Equity 60,920.93
Cash – Local Treasury 11,079.07
Other Payables 12,000.00
Petty Cash Fund 60,000.00

5.15 We have recommended that Management undertake the following actions:
5.15.1 Require concerned officials to submit the required liquidation
documents through the issuance of demand letters;
5.15.2 Upon submission of the required documents by concerned officials,
the Municipal Accountant – Designate should properly review the
documents and take up the necessary journal entries in the books of
accounts; and
5.15.3 If appropriate, necessitate the refund of unliquidated cash advances.

5.16 We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider
the proposed accounting entry as follows to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts.
Account Name Debit Credit
Advances to Special Disbursing Officer 7,300.00
Prior Period Adjustments/Government Equity 700,500.00
Advances for Operating Expenses 707,800.00

5.17 We have further recommended that, henceforth, Management limit the
grant of cash advances only to specific legal purposes and the Municipal
Accountant – Designate to strictly implement the policy that no additional
cash advances granted to officials and employees in the event that they fail
to submit all the liquidations on time.

5.18 Finally, we have recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant
- Designate see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with
complete documentation and in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and
regulations in order to establish the validity and propriety of the
transactions and disbursements. Processing and payment of claims that are
not properly documented shall be discontinued.

6.5 We have recommended that the Accounting Unit:
6.5.1 Prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers and follow the rules on
accounting for Receivables and Liabilities as prescribed in the
Manual on NGAS for LGUs;
6.5.2 Identify items for reconciliation, adjustment or those for further
action pertaining to long outstanding receivables and liabilities; and
6.5.3 Review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and
consider the proposed accounting entry, as follows to reflect the
correct balances of the accounts.

established on January 1 the following year)

Government Equity 229,210.39

For item 6.3.6 (under Trust Fund)

Due to NGAs 124,893.14
