7.9 We have recommended that the:
7.9.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and semi-annual inventory taking, and prepare and submit the
RPCI to the COA Office for verification;
7.9.2 Management invite through letter a representative of the
Commission on Audit to witness the inventory taking;
7.9.3 The Accounting Office and the Property Unit maintain and update
Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Cards, respectively, for each
inventory item to promote effective monitoring and controls over
Inventory; and
7.9.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian reconcile
with the RPCI and verify variances for appropriate action and
subsequent adjustments as necessary.

7.10 We have also recommended that Management submit: (1) the original
copies of the DVs totaling ₱13,696,660.75 together with their supporting
documents; and (2) supplemental listings for those not duly signed by
recipients/intended beneficiaries in the amount of ₱402,037.14.

8.8 We have recommended that the:
8.8.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and yearly inventory taking, and prepare and submit the
RPCPPE to COA Office for verification and reconciliation with the
complete Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC)
and the Property Cards maintained by the Accounting Office and
Property Unit, respectively;
8.8.2 Management invite through writing a representative from the
Commission on Audit to witness the inventory taking;
8.8.3 The Accounting Office and Property Unit maintain and update
PPELC, Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) and
Property Cards, respectively, for each inventory item to promote
effective monitoring and controls over PPE;
8.8.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian reconcile
with the RPCPPE and verify variances for appropriate action and
subsequent adjustments as necessary; and
8.8.5 The Municipal Accountant trace back those PPE noted to not have
been depreciated, compute for the supposed depreciation to date and
prepare adjusting as necessary.

9.9 We have reiterated our recommendations that the:
9.9.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and yearly physical count of local road networks, and prepare
and submit the RPCLRN to COA Office for verification and
reconciliation with the complete and updated LRNLC and the
LRNPC to be maintained by the Accounting Office and General
Service Office, respectively;
9.9.2 Management invite through letter a representative of the
Commission on Audit to witness the physical count;
9.9.3 The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects; and
9.9.4 The General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory and
Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card
for all roads and its components; and for
9.9.5 The Municipal Accountant-Designate trace the recorded amounts of
roads, highways and bridges, which were closed to government
equity in the prior years in its books of accounts, keep and maintain
Registry of Public Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary records
for roads and road components for every road network and provide
for the depreciation for the local road network.

10.7 We have recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that the
correct computations are made in the withholdings/setup of GSIS
contributions in the preparation of the payroll, and properly remit the same
within the reglementary period. The Municipal Accountant - Designate
properly account for the withholdings and remittances.

10.8 We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider
the proposed accounting entries as follows to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts.
Accounts Debit Credit
Due from Officers and Employees 750.60
Due to GSIS 26,034.25
Prior Period Adjustment/ Government Equity 7,445.29
Due to Officers and Employees 19,339.56

10.9 Furthermore, we have reiterated our recommendation that Management
submit a copy of the MOA signed with the GSIS. Management should
revisit the said MOA and determine whether overpayment was made to the
GSIS.

11.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate review
the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider the
proposed accounting entries as follows to reflect the correct balances of the
accounts.
Accounts Debit Credit
Due from Officers and Employees 12,047.28
Prior Period Adjustment/ Government Equity 12,047.29
Due to PhilHealth 24,094.57

11.8 We have further reiterated our recommendations that Management revisit
the payrolls for those with noted differences in CY 2019 and CY 2018 and
undertake the following actions:
11.8.1 Recalculate the PhilHealth Contributions to include salary
adjustments, take up the necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts and carry out the corresponding action in the
underpayment and overpayment of salaries due to errors in the
preparation of the payrolls and the interest/penalties to be incurred
for delayed remittances for the under-withholdings be borne by the
erring officials and employees; and
11.8.2 Demand the erring officials and employees for the penalties incurred
the amount of ₱6,516.47 on the delayed remittances in CY 2018.

11.9 Henceforth, ensure that the correct computations are made in the
preparation of the payroll and that the appropriate journal entries are
made for the same.

12.4 We have recommended that Management require concerned officials to
promptly submit copies of government contracts and their supporting
documents within the prescribed period to enable the Audit Team timely

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) create an Inventory Team to (i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of PPE for submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards; (c) designate General Services Officer to (i) maintain property cards for PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of properties; and (ii) issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to establish the accountability for properties; and (d) require the GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the conduct of physical count to establish correctness of balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and maintain updated subsidiary ledgers of individual taxpayer to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct periodic reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts to ascertain the accuracy of the balances of accounts appearing in the Cashbooks and Accounting Financial Records and (i) investigate differences and adjust the corresponding ledgers/cashbooks accordingly; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all funds pursuant to the abovementioned provisions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to cause the immediate submission of the required documents and reports pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of PD No. 1445.
We also recommended that the withholding of salary of persons responsible for the continued failure to submit the transaction documents/Disbursement Vouchers until the submission of all the required accounts/documents for audit.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct (a) the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to judiciously plan and ensure that the programs/projects/activities which will be charged to the 20% Development Fund are in conformity with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1; and (b) the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging expenditures which are not aligned in the fund’s intended purpose.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the Local School Board headed by the Local Chief Executive and co-chaired by the District Supervisor (a) conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the planning and budgeting practices of the Municipality; (b) prepare the Annual Budget using the prescribed format; (c) ensure that Annual Budget be covered by a LSB Resolution; and (c) submit the supplemental budget to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate verification of the transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) ensure that the existing MRF of all of the component Barangays be fully operational; (b) provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining two Barangays to establish their own MRF; (c) intensify the information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with barangay officials; (d) accelerate efforts in the enforcement of “no segregation, no collection” and “no littering” policies; and (e) issue Executive Order to reorganize the members of the Local Solid Waste Management Board (LSWMB).

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) stop the practice of using funds received other than for its intended purpose; (b) adopt austerity measures on disbursements to improve the cash position of the Municipality; (c) prioritize outstanding payables to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and Philhealth to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and for member-beneficiaries to avail of the privileges and benefits due them; and (d) encourage department heads to submit revenue generating programs to increase collection and be able to pay its outstanding obligations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) review the claim as to validity, propriety and legality before approving the transaction; and (b) instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents before signing Box A of the Disbursement Voucher as prescribed in Section 4, Paragraph 6 of PD No. 1445

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (a) render an explanation or justification on procurement thru shopping instead of competitive bidding of the subject transactions; (b) adhere to Sections 10 and 54.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the general policy on competitive bidding and prohibition of splitting of contracts; and (c) resort only to alternative modes of procurement if the conditions provided under Sections 48, 52 and 53.9 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 are met.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to fully implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries and any amount due to the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to take appropriate legal action against the former accountable officer in order to recover the missing funds of ₱1,244,486.57 pursuant to Articles 171 and 217 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed (a) instruct the (i) Local Finance Committee to conduct a thorough review on the Municipality’s resources and its budget; and (ii) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that all transactions are properly obligated prior to the actual disbursement to avoid incurrence of a deficit/net loss; and (b) adopt austerity measures on disbursements to improve the financial position of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of charging prior year’s expenses to current year’s appropriations; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that (i) all payable accounts with valid claims are recorded in the books of accounts every end of the year; and (ii) supporting documents are properly reviewed to ensure validity and legality of the claims.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Human Resources and Management Officer to review and verify the submitted DTRs and forwards the same to the Accounting Office for preparation of payrolls; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the payrolls based on the submitted Daily Time Records (DTRs) to avoid overpayment; and (ii) ensure that claims are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (a) submit a Report of Physical Count for Property, Plant and Equipment for all the assets and properties of the Municipality to the GSIS; and (b) ensure that all insurable assets and properties are insured against any insurable risk with the GIF of the GSIS in compliance with RA No. 656 otherwise known as the Property Insurance Law, as amended.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Market Supervisor to (i) ensure that all lessees have valid contracts; (ii) limit the contract period to two years; and (iii) secure authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan allowing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with the lessee; (iv) prepare a monthly monitoring report of collections to determine if the targeted income is realized or not; and (v) initiate extra efforts to enforce collection of rentals and impose penalties on delayed payments, as the case maybe; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger to monitor payment of rental of each stallholders; (c) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Official Receipts are properly and completely accomplished to include stall numbers and rental period.; and (d) Market Supervisor and ICO-Municipal Accountant coordinate, prepare and submit a report of unpaid rental fees of delinquent stall holders as of December 31, 2019, to office of the Auditor, for verification.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (i) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) submit the DTRs together with other supporting documents; and (b) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to require the Cash Disbursing Officer to (i) ensure that all paid disbursements are duly signed or supported with acceptable evidence or proof of receipt of payment in compliance with Section 3.3.1 (d)  of COA Circular 2004-006 dated September 9, 2004; (ii) pay directly to claimants and in-case of non-payment to the payees themselves, a Special Power of Attorney or other form of authority be presented to form part of the Liquidation Reports with the proper identification cards, both of the payees and the representative; and (iii) require the employees to sign in the payrolls, otherwise reverse the entry made to Advances to Officers to the subject transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to adopt a system of payroll payment through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) of the Government Banks in order to protect government funds and minimize operating costs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the Municipal Treasurer; and (b) require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to consolidate the individual RAAF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the General Services Officer to (i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and (ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively, on a sampling basis.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere with the provision of Section 5.5 of JMC 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA regarding submission of Accomplishment Report not later than end of January of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the Disbursement Vouchers pertaining to QRF together with the copy of the resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan declaring the Municipality under state of calmity; (b) MDRRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73; and (c) instruct the MDRRMO to ensure that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions set forth in Section 2.Q of COA Circular No. 92-389 by regularly stamping the “PAID” marking to disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents upon payment to prevent possible re-use of the same, and avoid misappropriation of government funds.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/loan repayments withheld to the GSIS and adhere to the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue remitting promptly all compulsory contributions/amortization withheld to the Pag-IBIG within the reglementary period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.

We recommended and management agreed that that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notice of Suspensions, to prevent maturing into disallowances.

The Municipal Mayor must not approve the payment and sign on the check without the supporting documents necessary for its validity.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to verify the discrepancy and make/record the necessary adjusting entries based on the duly verified reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statement to reflect the correct adjusted cash in bank balance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to trace the material variance of ₱22,662,054.80 for Cash in Bank Account and ₱23,730.11 for Cash in Local Treasury Account as of December 31, 2019; (b) require henceforth, reconciliation of entries in the cashbook and general ledger at least on a quarterly basis and promptly resolve any significant discrepancy accordingly.

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% Development Fund and maximize utilization of the said fund to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that  intended purpose  is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18,  Republic Act 10121.

We require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal LDRRM Officer to provide explanation for the non-compliance with the required amount to be appropriated for the LDRRM Fund. Further, we require that in the succeeding budget periods, the municipality faithfully comply with the required amounts of appropriation.

We require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit intact all the collections for a certain period with the depository bank and not to retain any amount to prevent the misuse and/or loss of the government funds.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to require the daily turnover of collections by the collectors and that the turnover must be the total amount collected during the day. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer closely monitor the collections and remittances of the collectors in order to prevent the risk of the collections being misused and/or lost.

We require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the preparation of the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) as required by NGAS Vol. II to properly control and monitor the utilization of the accountable forms. The Municipal Treasurer must ensure that the accountable forms are properly accounted for in order to prevent any misuse or loss of said forms. Further, we recommend that the log book be maintained as a backup record to support the RAAF.

We recommend that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of project completion. Likewise, corresponding liquidation report for the aforementioned project is requested for submission and for verification. We also recommend that liquidated damages be imposed and deducted from the total amount payable to the suppliers.

1.15. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare an Adjusting Entry to correct the balance of the Accumulated
Depreciation of the Road Network account and the Prior Period
Adjustments in the amount of ₱249,332.63; and
b) record depreciation of road network accounts correctly from its initial
recognition and subsequent years, including the computation of its
estimated useful life without providing for any residual value.

2.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry
to reclassify the purchased CCTV equipment amounting to ₱1,897,035.48 to
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account from Information and
Communication Technology account.

3.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) issue demand letters to the barangays concerned for immediate submission
of liquidation reports and its supporting documents;
b) Submit the lacking supporting documents on the funds transferred to Brgy
Daja Diot and the liquidation made by Brgy. Hda. Maira in the amounts
of ₱100,000.00 and ₱97,600.00, respectively; and
c) Review and record the liquidations made by other barangays totaling to
₱487,157.79, including the submission of its supporting documents.

3.15. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Revise the MOA with the barangays to include the provisions on the
specific project to be implemented, the amount to be released and the
period in which the project is to be implemented; and
b) Obtain authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the grant of financial
assistance to different barangays.

4.16. We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
a) Remit to the NHA the available amount of ₱1,703,624.60 (net of the bank’s
required maintaining balance) with an accompanying request for
condonation of any accumulated penalties;
b) Prepare a Conditional Contract to Sell with each Project Beneficiary or
Lot Buyers with the authority of the Sangguniang Bayan, to protect the
interest of the LGU; and
c) Require the Municipal Treasurer to collect monthly the amortization from
each Lot Buyers and remit the proceeds to NHA immediately in the
succeeding month as required in the MOA.

5.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant, as the designated withholding agents of the BIR, refund the
penalties paid from the government funds amounting to ₱117,297.23. We also
recommend the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the balances of Due to
BIR account in the books of accounts for all funds to avoid any penalties to be
imposed by the BIR.

6.12. We recommended that the BAC Chairperson and the BAC Secretariat:
a) submit justifications for the noted deficiencies and submit the lacking
documents within five days from receipt of this AOM, otherwise, a Notice
of Suspension will be issued for the purpose;
b) prepare the required documents when procuring through competitive
bidding or other alternative mode, to prove that each and every stage of
the procurement as prescribed in RA No. 9184 are being strictly complied
at all times; and
c) submit to the Audit Team all contracts and its complete supporting
documents within five (5) days after approval by the Municipal Mayor.

7.11. We recommended that the Chairman on Bids and Award Committed (BAC)
require the Winning Bidders or Contractors to indicate and specify the date of
receipt on the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed to determine the
timelines and deadlines of each procurement activity and the overall
procurement process and to ensure that the terms of the contract will have its
force and effect.

8.13. We recommended that the Municipal Development Council, through the
Municipal Mayor as Chairman:
a) Include only in the Annual Investment Plan the projects which can readily
be implemented by the LGU during the year, through proper scheduling
and prioritization; and
b) Re-appropriate the balances of previous year’s 20% development fund by
identifying projects for its utilization and inclusion in the AIP.

8.14. We also recommended that the BAC conduct the bidding of projects contained
in the AIP in accordance with the timetable indicated therein.

8.15. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement all the projects
awarded during the year to ensure utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

9.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Constitute a Local Finance Committee to review and propose amendments
on the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance 02-08 s. 2008 to include the
fixed rental fees of the Wet or Meat and Fish Market and the Dry Market
(Stalls No.39-54) and the usage, rental, fees and charges for the Socio-Civic
Center;
b) Recommend approval and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan the proposed
amendments on the Municipal Ordinance 02-08 s. 2008 for the issuance of
another ordinance,
c) Revise the contents of the Contract of Lease to conform with Municipal
Ordinance No. 02-08 s. 2008 and any amendments thereto; and
d) Sign a Contract of Lease with each Stallholder after authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan has been obtained to enter into such contract
agreements.

10.7. We recommended that the Property Officer prepare a separate inventory list
of all items and equipment procured from LDRRMF and maintain an updated
stock cards or property cards.

10.8. We also recommended that the Property Officer and the Municipal
Accountant reconcile the amount presented in the RPCPPE and Trial Balance
under the Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account.

11.14. We recommended that Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Solid Waste
Management Board, resubmit its MSWMP to EMB after integrating the
WACS, for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

11.15. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MPDO, identify
projects on Solid Waste Management and include in the AIP to be funded from
the 20% Development Fund. Appropriate funds from the 70% LDRRMF
Mitigation Fund only if the project or program are for disaster management
and prevention.

11.16. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, require the MENRO as
focal person of the SWM program of the LGU, to implement continuously the
programs and activities identified in its MSWP to encourage awareness on
recycling among the people of San Isidro.

12.6. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor initiate the procurement of the lot
as the new site for its dumpsite and develop a sanitary landfill as a final
garbage disposal site of the LGU in order to stop the operation of open dump
site.

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended directing the Municipal Accountant to properly classify
the current and non-current portions of the Loans Payable-Domestic with  its
appropriate amount in the presentation of the financial statements.

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended that management:


      a)  Enforce  the  immediate  liquidation  and/or  settlement  of   all
         outstanding cash advances;




      b)  Stop  the  practice  of  granting  additional  cash  advances   to
         officials/employees who have previous unliquidated cash advances as
         well as granting multiple  cash  advances  to  the  same  employees
         regardless of the purpose;



      c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to expedite  the  request  for  the
         write-off of long outstanding cash  advances  that  are  considered
         dormant in accordance with the guidelines and  procedures  provided
         in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and



      d) Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 97-
         002 dated February 10, 1997, in the  granting  and  liquidation  of
         cash advances

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended that management:

     1. Stop the practice of transferring  cash  advances  drawn  to  other
        personnel; and



     2. Consider designating personnel of the other office/department as  a
        disbursing officer of the cash advances for special purpose/s.

Recommendation/s:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the   Municipal
Accountant to prioritize the analysis  of  the  balance  of  the  Government
Equity account under  the  Trust  Fund  and  to  determine  and  effect  the
appropriate reclassification or adjusting entries.

Recommendation/s:


      We recommended that management:


     1. Enforce strict control procedures in the disbursement of  the  fund
        to ensure that it is utilized only  for  its  intended  purpose  to
        preclude the issuance of audit suspensions/disallowances; and



     2. Adhere strictly to the pertinent  provisions  of  law,  rules,  and
        regulations relative to the  utilization  of  the  20%  Development
        Fund, so that constituents could directly enjoy  the  benefits  for
        which the fund was primarily established.

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended that management:

     1. Strictly enforce the "No  Segregation,  No  Collection"  Policy  on
        wastes to be disposed of;


     2.  Prioritize  the  purchase  of  additional  equipment  for  garbage
        collection and operation of disposal facility; and



     3. Expedite the processing of the  necessary  procedures  and  provide
        adequate funding for the establishment and utilization of the SLF.

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:

     1. Issue a uniform and clear-cut policy on the  hiring  of  Job  Order
        workers to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto;

     2. Reduce/limit the hiring of job order  workers  and  fill-up  vacant
        positions; and


     3. Evaluate and determine the appropriate personnel complement of each
        office.

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended that Management evaluate  and  analyze  the  subsidiary
ledgers of the accounts with negative balances,  and  effect  the  necessary
adjusting and/or correcting entries to  arrive  at  the  accurate  balances.
Further,  ensure  accuracy  in  the  recording  of  transactions  to   their
appropriate SL accounts  to  avoid  payment  of  unnecessary  penalties  and
surcharges to various government agencies.

Recommendation/s:

       We reiterated the prior year’s  recommendation  to  reconstitute  the
municipality’s GFPS Executive Committee, TWG, and M&E Team to  conform  with
the minimum requirements  to  accelerate  gender  mainstreaming  within  the
Municipality.

Recommendation/s:


      We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  perfected   contracts,
Purchase Orders (POs), and supporting documents,  including the Memorandum
of Agreement (MOA), of projects, programs, and activities to be  implemented
by the LGU to the COA audit team in accordance with the deadline  set  forth
under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management strictly monitor the implementation  of
the projects by establishing a system for monitoring the  projects  so  that
prompt action can be made to  address  the  issues/constraints  causing  the
delays in the implementation of the projects.

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended that management:


     1. Adequately plan and study the feasibility of  each  project  before
        proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by
        the 20% LGDF;


     2. Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the  speedy
        implementation of the projects; and


     3. Ensure the full utilization of the  appropriated  amounts  for  the
        benefit of the constituents in terms  of  PPAs  to  be  implemented
        under 20% LGDF.

Recommendation/s:

      We recommended that the management thoroughly plan and  ensure  proper
monitoring of the projects  and  activities  to  be  implemented  under  the
LDRRMF such as the preparation of a monitoring report that would  check  the
status of each project/activity. Also, ensure the full  utilization  of  the
appropriated amounts for  the  benefit  of  the  constituents  in  terms  of
disaster preparedness and mitigation. Further, we also  recommend  that  the
excess appropriation be reprogrammed for  other  disaster  preparedness  and
mitigation activities for inclusion  in  the  revised  Local  Disaster  Risk
Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2020.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management exhaust all measures to ensure that the
vacant stalls will  be  leased  to  prospective  vendors  to  generate  more
revenues for the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant appropriately classifies the Loans Payable-Domestic into current and non-current payables in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor requires the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to trace all mortalities of dispersed animals since the inception of the project until CY 2019 and to substantiate all claims of loss through death of said animals with complete documentary attachments upon filing of a request for relief from accountability for the proper disposition thereof pursuant to Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and send demand letters to concerned party/ies to enforce collection of dormant accounts.  Cause the application of appropriate sanctions against erring borrowers, if necessary.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant evaluates the status of the outstanding receivables and to request for write-off of accounts whose balances are inactive or non-moving for ten years or more and its settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained in accordance with existing auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended full compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, otherwise, withholding of salaries of accountable officers and or officials/employees concerned who fail to effect prompt liquidation in accordance with existing rules and regulations shall be made.
Dissemination of existing rules and regulations on cash advances to all officials and employees was likewise enjoined.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Treasurer to make representations with the bank to discontinue the auto-debit arrangement on loan payments and to regularly secure copies of billing statements to serve as basis in the preparation of disbursement vouchers and obligation requests for payment thereof.

We recommended that Management instructs all department heads and/ or concerned offices to fully comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 and designate a focal person to consolidate the reports submitted by each department and to thoroughly review the reports before submission to COA in hard and electronic copies.

We recommended that the Municipal Government of San Isidro immediately establish the MDRRMO and hire qualified personnel as required under RA 10121 and JMC Circular No. 2014-1.  The creation of LDRRMO in every LGU is one of the basic elements in disaster preparedness.  It is primarily responsible for carrying out or implementing various disaster risk reduction and management plans/programs of the municipality to ensure the safety and protection of the LGU when disaster/calamity occurs.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant complies with the procedures on the recording of transactions for fund transfers prescribed in the NGAS and in conformity with applicable PPSAS.

We recommended that:
The MACCO and the MEO reconcile their records to determine the actual status of the said projects;
The MEO provide the MACCO with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents to serve as the basis for the transfer of these projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
Based on the adjusted data, the MACCO computes the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.
The MACCO and Municipal Treasurer immediately facilitate the return of the unutilized balance to the source agency or deposit the amount to the National Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the MEO to install the billboards, or monitor the installation thereof, following the required specifications of tarpaulins/billboards for infrastructure projects as stated under COA Circular No. 2013-004. For on-going projects, submit proof of compliance therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the BAC Members and its Secretariat to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken are in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 to safeguard the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the local finance committee to revisit the provisions on the imposition of the market rates and fees for possible modification, if warranted, and submit its proposals to the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriate action. Otherwise, exert additional efforts to operationalize the public market.

We recommended that the LDRRMO ensures that the RDS be properly filled-up and a separate record to monitor the goods by kind should be prepared to serve as a guide in determining actual inventory levels. Likewise, install appropriate measures in safeguarding its inventories to avoid the risks of inventory infestation, pilferage, spoilage and expiration of goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Accounting Office to properly deduct withholding tax on professional fees, talent fees and similar payments to professionals and other recipients not otherwise subject to withholding tax on compensation as required under subsection (A) of Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
Enjoins the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation/completion of projects from prior and current year; and
Further, we recommended that the Local Development Council should optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through: (a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; (b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result and (c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.
We recommended that the officials and employees concerned adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1 on appropriating and utilizing the 20% DF for development projects.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
Enjoins the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation/completion of projects from prior and current year; and
Further, we recommended that the Local Development Council should optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through: (a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; (b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result and (c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.

1.6     We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Office to
        establish and properly maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SL)  for  those
        accounts    that    are    composed     of     several     specific
        accounts/category/accounting responsibility;  and  make  sure  that
        beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in  the  SL
        should be the same with the ending balances in the previous year.

2.15    We recommended that management require the Municipal  Accountant
        to:


        a. Issue of demand letters to (i) notify  the  concerned  officials
           and  employees  of  their   responsibility   to   settle   their
           unliquidated cash advances.  If  settlement  was  not  received,
           withhold from their salaries considering  that  they  have  been
           notified  and  given  more  adequate  time  to  liquidate;  (ii)
           employees who already resigned and  transferred  to  immediately
           cause  the  liquidation  and  settlement  of  the   unliquidated
           balances;
        b. Request the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances  of  the
           deceased employees;
        c. Reclassify “Other Receivable” account to  Advances  to  Officers
           and Employees account in  order  to  present  fairly  the  total
           balance of unsettled cash advances;
        d. Ensure the completeness of prescribed supporting  documents  for
           the granting of cash advances in processing claims  for  payment
           thereof;
        e. Not grant additional cash advance  to  concerned  officials  and
           employees unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated;
        f. Strictly monitor  periodically  the  submission  of  liquidation
           reports to ensure timely  accounting  thereof  and  avoid  undue
           accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and
        g. Strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of  the  aforementioned
           Circulars to avoid penalty stated thereon.

3.5      We  recommended  that  Management  strictly  adhere  with   the
        requirements and provisions of existing laws concerning  Accounting
        for Supplies and Materials and:


        a. Designate a Municipal General Services Officer to facilitate the
           maintenance of stock cards  for  supplies  to  account  for  the
           receipt and disposition of the same and prepare Requisition  and
           Issue Slip; Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and  submit
           the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording  purposes  to
           fairly  present  its  inventory  accounts   in   the   financial
           statement;



        b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe  proper  adjustment
           of inventory accounts, prepare supplies ledger  card  per  stock
           number, make an internal control policy on  the  maintenance  of
           stock cards on the MDRRMO, MTO and MHO to establish authority on
           the control of inventory of the same and recognize  and  measure
           reliably the unconsumed or unused supplies and materials as part
           of the inventory account and adjust to its corresponding expense
           account; and



        c. Review and revitalize the composition of the Inventory Committee
           and fuel them up to religiously conduct the required semi-annual
           physical count of unused/undistributed supplies and materials.

4.8     We  recommended  that  the  Management  instruct  the  Municipal
        Accounting Office to strictly observe compliance with the perpetual
        inventory method  of  accounting  on  the  supplies  and  materials
        procured for construction projects by administration  by  recording
        all purchases under the account Construction  Materials  Inventory.
        Also, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer, as  the  construction
        materials custodian, to  implement  controls  on  the  receipt  and
        utilization of the same and  submit  to  the  Accounting  Office  a
        summary of the utilized construction  materials.  Likewise,  effect
        the necessary adjustment for the amount of supplies  and  materials
        which remained unutilized for the intended projects or repairs.

5.14    We recommended that Management:


        a.       designate a new Municipal General Services Officer (GSO);
        b. instruct the designated Municipal GSO to maintain property cards
           and issue acknowledgement receipt for equipment upon issuance of
           the same in addition to his tasks expressly recommended  in  the
           previous AOM for Supplies and Materials;
        c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to train one of her staff  who
           will be tasked to maintain the required PPE Ledger Card; and
        d. reactivate the Inventory Committee and assure its commitment  to
           religiously exhaust all  available  resources  and  conduct  the
           required annual physical count of plant, property and  equipment
           duly documented through incorporating in the Report on  Physical
           Count on Property, Plant and Equipment.

6.6     We recommended that Management direct the  Municipal  Accountant
        to:


        a. Adhere strictly with laws and provisions concerning proper usage
           of accounts for every transactions and events;
        b. Transfer the amount from Due to Other Funds to  Due  to  Special
           Accounts; and
        c. Use the Due to Special Accounts for the recording of funds to be
           transferred from one special account to  another  maintained  by
           the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: Management Not that the responsible personnel will be submitted as of date. No supporting disbursement documents have been submitted as of date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit to the Audit Team the 20% Development Fund Utilization Report and NADAI or additional appropriation submitted accounts in CY 2018 for audit/evaluation.
observations.managementAction: In compliance with the above recommendation, the unsubmitted accounts in CY 2018 for management submitted a copy of its Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation under the 20% Development Fund on June 3, 2021. However, the submitted report only included current year appropriations. Details on continuing appropriations were not presented. Likewise, the remaining balance, including savings, under the 20% Development Fund as of December 31, 2018 was not determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under the provision of R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of operations of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management has already posted the vacancies for the said positions but no applications were received to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of CDP and CLUP for CY 2017 onwards.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the CDP and CLUP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency prepare and submit a copy of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency for 2017, 2016 and 2015 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted copies of the APPs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget officer to submit supplemental investment plan/ NADAI or additional appropriation as justification for the utilization of CY 2016 and CY 2017 Share from Tobacco Excise Tax.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the NADAI and Supplemental Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that for the programs/ projects and activities which are MOOE in nature which remained unimplemented/ unobligated, the LDC should identify other projects needed to be implemented which are capital expenditures in nature in order to properly re-align the previously noted MOOE projects.
observations.managementAction: MOOE for CY 2019 implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a) Management see to it during the Budget planning phase, all programs, project and activities proposed to be charged to the 20% Development Fund conform to Item 3.1 of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1; and b) Henceforth, Management should familiarize themselves with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the expenditure items covered and prohibited to be paid out of the 20% Development Fund for guidance.
observations.managementAction: Implemented for CY 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the following actions be undertaken by management: a) The Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, thru the issuance of an SB resolution, create a MDRRMO and provide the LDRRMF appropriate PS and MOOE fund relative thereto; and b) In the event that hiring a full time LDRRMO could not be made, the Municipal Mayor should see to it that the corresponding designation to be issued entails the services of a permanent official; c) The Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRM Designate, Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant Designate to account the disbursements funded by the 5% LDRRM Fund. Submission of accomplishment report which includes all the necessary supporting documents to support said claims should be prioritized; and d) Concerned personnel familiarized themselves with the relevant rules and regulations with regards to the planning, allocation, utilization and generation of reports for the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: An LDRRM Officer was designated by management. An LDRRM Officer was designated and as to date the LDRRMF is now properly utilized and monitored. SB Resolution/Executive Order for the creation of MDRRMO is yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: In view of the noted lapses, we recommended the following: a) The Municipal Mayor creates a GFPS Execom, TWG and Secretariat that will supervise, advocate and install a GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) as well as the required Gender secretariat, with its Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System; and b) The Municipal Mayor with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate and adopt a Local GAD Code.
observations.managementAction: The management has already created its GFPS, TWG and Administrative secretariat, with its GAD FPS and as to date the GAD Fund is already properly utilized as to date. The GFPS and M&E system is yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement and submit the same to COA for audit purposes; and
b.	Maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account for easy preparation of the monthly BRS.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already their prepared Bank Reconciliation Statements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof and to perform the same regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume 1, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer is in the process of reconciling the Cash in Local Treasury account. However, difference in amount balances still exists.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 1 in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Collecting Officers/Municipal Treasurer should strictly observe the above mentioned provision of DOF-BLGF Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) to protect the funds of the municipality. Henceforth, collections should be diligently turned-over to the Municipal Treasurer daily.
observations.managementAction: Collections made by Revenue Collection Officers were turned-over to Municipal Treasurer at the end of the day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submits all the documents or returns the unused cash to fully liquidate his cash advances at the end of the year to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly on the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Liquidations were made, however, unliquidated balance still exist.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 5 in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is strict in granting of cash advances. Most of the current year cash advances were already liquidated. Most of the unliquidated balances were from previous year. The agency did not issue demand letters on CY 2019 and no request for write off was made.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 6 in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office record all purchases to its corresponding inventory account classification and to adjust the account for whatever consumption on the basis of Summary of Supplies and Material Issued (SSMI), duly supported with the original copy of Requisition Issue Slip (RIS), which should be prepared and submitted weekly or monthly to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: office supplies, drugs and medicine and other supplies were recorded as inventory upon purchase.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the implementing department (e.g. Engineering Office) to identify, determine and reconcile those projects that were already completed and close the same to the appropriate PPE account. Subsequently, prepare entries to transfer the PPE accounts amounting to P69,702,764.40  to the General Fund book to close the Government Equity account in the Trust Fund book.

require the Engineering Office to provide the Accounting Office with copies of a comprehensive report of completed projects and other necessary documents; and 

c. based on the adjusted data, require the Municipal Accountant to compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer is in the process of locating documents pertaining to the completed projects for adjustment purposes. However, adjusting entries were still not made by the Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 9 in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute a disposal committee to initiate the proper disposal of the above unserviceable PPE pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor decided to repair the Dump Truck

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:

Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are adjusted on timely basis.

b.	Perform constant review of transactions to deter error and omissions in recording.
observations.managementAction: There are adjustments made by the Municipal Accountant but unreconciled balances still exist.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 11 in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts or effect the necessary adjustments. Being reciprocal accounts, it should end up with the same balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is in the process of locating the reason for the differences in balances. However, adjustments were not made.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 12 in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

explain why the procurement of three (3) units of air-condition were made in piecemeal or in series although the same can be conducted in a single procurement activity thru public bidding.

b.	strictly comply with provisions set forth in the R.A. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: There are purchased made through public bidding. However, purchases through splitting of contracts still exist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all financial assistance given to barangays should be directly paid and under the name of the requesting barangay and should be supported by an AF 51 for proper accounting and auditing of the donations made.
observations.managementAction: Financial Assistance to barangays are now duly acknowledged with AF 51.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

protect and conserve the municipality’s funds and resources by exercising prudence in utilization thereof.

b.	Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-003 to avoid further disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Disallowance was issued. An appeal was made by the Municipal Registrar and Municipal Health Officer

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

We recommended that the agency submit the lacking supporting documents for the progress/final payments of the infrastructure projects in the Trust Funds to avoid suspension of the said disbursements.
Also, in the future, in order to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit,  we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims of  progress billing/final payment unless  all the required supporting documents are present and attached to the disbursement vouchers; and

b.	comply strictly to Sections 9.1.1.1 to 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated  June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Lacking documents were submitted. However, DVs for payments during CY 2019 were still submitted with lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular in the Project Implementation  of the Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities, for transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is now adhering with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular in the Project Implementation  of the Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular in the Project Reporting  of the Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Municipalities for transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is now adhering with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular in the Project Implementation  of the Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the BAC to observe Sections 20.1 and 20.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and with all the applicable guidelines and regulations related to procurement in order to promote transparency and accountability.

Finally, we recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the processing of payment to contractor without the BAC conducting the pre-procurement conference for the procurement of infrastructure projects with an Approved Budget for the Contract of more than Five Million Pesos (P5,000,000.00).
observations.managementAction: The LGU is now doing the Pre-procurement Conference on projects with ABC of P5M.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

the BAC to post in PhilGEPS the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through public bidding to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement procedures.

the BAC to explain the failure to post in PhilGEPS the Notice to Award and Notice to proceed despite being a requirement of RA 9184.

c.	the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the printout copy of posting of the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS were included among the supporting documents for the payment of the claim for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through public bidding.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 17 in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality remit the unutilized fund relating to LGSF – ADM amounting to P124,691.46 to the Bureau of the Treasury. Also, we recommended the agency to not use any unutilized fund contrary to the provisions of any related guidelines of the fund entrusted to the agency and the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P124,691.46 were already remitted to BTr.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality open a separate depository account with the Local Branch of the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) for the Supplemental Feeding and Senior Citizen Social Pension Programs as required in the Omnibus Guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program.
observations.managementAction: Separate Bank account is not necessary

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 117 in utilization and implementation of the Local Government Support Fund – Financial Assistance for transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The agency prepared the quarterly reports for CY 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to explain the one year delay on the implementation of CBMS activities as approved in the agency’s activity design of implementation.

Also, we recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-69 dated May 23, 2016, also known as the Guidelines for the Implementation of Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that management:

Submit explanation on the reason of delay/non-implementation of the above unimplemented PPAs; and 

b.	Facilitate the immediate implementation of the programmed PPAs funded from the 20% Development Fund so that the desired services and benefits of these PPA be delivered to the constituents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Most of the projects for CY 2019 were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Ensure that the utilization of 20% Development Fund must be for capital expenditure and/or in the nature of investments; and

b.	Strict adherence with the DILG and DBM joint Circular No. 2017-1 in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to be funded out of the 20% EDF to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities which are compliant with the guidelines.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to transfer the unutilized balance of LDRRMF to Trust Liability –DRRMF account under the Trust Fund account; and

b.	Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the unutilized fund to the LDRRMF bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: 2018 balance of LDRRMF was transferred to Trust Fund account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO fully implements the planned programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the DRRM Funds would be maximized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.
observations.managementAction: The LGU implemented most of its PPAs for CY 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality should disburse the GAD Fund in pursuance to its appropriation and utilize it in accordance with the DILG-endorsed GAD Plan and Budget. Moreover, the accountable officers responsible in the disposition of government funds shall be cautious in discharging their duties and responsibilities.

In addition, submit also an explanation why the said expenditures were disbursed even without the certification as to existence of appropriation by the Municipal Budget Officer and why should it not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the municipality utilize the GAD Budget in accordance with the DILG-endorsed GAD Plan and refrain from charging to the GAD Fund the aforementioned not GAD related activities in order to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to 

(a) instruct the collectors to use/issue only the Accountable Forms under their accountability; 

(b) verify whether the official receipts included in the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) submitted by the collectors are part of their accountability; and 

(c) make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors.
observations.managementAction: The management duly noted the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) require the Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) strictly enforce the daily turn-over of collections by the collectors and likewise deposit intact all collections daily or in the next banking day; and 

(ii) designate a responsible Collecting Officer and ensure close supervision to avoid occurrence of risks of loss of public funds; 

(b) direct the Municipal Treasurer and the assigned Liquidating Officer to diligently verify the Accountable Officers’ RCDs and Statement of Accountable Forms and conduct physical count and inspection of Official Receipts under their custodies; and 4

(c) designate an Accountable Officer for each type of transaction in the Treasury Department to avoid mishandling of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommenda-tions.
The Municipality submitted its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to the EMB Regional office.
observations.reason:
Various deficiencies were still noted in the municipality’s compliance with R.A.  No. 9003.

Reiterated in Observation No. 7.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MDRRM Officer to 

(a) comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121, the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, and COA Circular 2012-002; 

(b) ensure that the standards set forth in the NDRRMC Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards, Volume 2 was complied with to be considered fully disaster-prepared municipality; and 

(c) refrain from charging expenditures which are not aligned with the fund’s intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason: Due to the municipality’s non-compliance with the pertinent laws related to the DRRM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure/monitor the posting of signboards containing relevant information of the PPAs of the Municipality in either of the forms stated in Section 2.2.1 to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were given to comply with the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the 

(a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance to Section 6 of Revenue Regulation No. 10-93 to avoid sanctions as stated in Section 272 of Republic Act No. 8424 otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act; and 

(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher for taxes withheld but not yet remitted to the BIR as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: The management duly noted the recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: There are still delay in the remittance of taxes withheld.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The management duly noted the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the Pag-IBIG.
observations.managementAction: The management duly noted the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: The management duly noted the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer fully implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries of the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The management duly noted the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notice of Suspensions, to prevent maturing into disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The management duly noted the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due From Other Funds and Due To Other Funds and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant reviewed the accounts and their transactions and adjusted the same to reflect the correct balances.
observations.reason: The trial balance and ledgers reflect that the accounts are properly reconciled and adjusted to show the same amount accredited to the funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to promptly prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the Cash in Bank account of the municipality and submit the same with the bank statements, paid checks and debit/credit memos. 

It is further recommended that adjusting journal entries must be prepared on the month following the preparation of the BRS in order to correct the book balances and avoid the accumulation of substantial reconciling items.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant already prepared the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the Cash in Bank account.
observations.reason: The Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted together with the bank statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports. Furthermore, we recommend the management to have an accountant in the most immediate time in order to efficiently and effectively record transactions, correct account balances and submit reports promptly to this office.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the complete year-end FS and other schedules on the legal deadline.
observations.reason: The Financial Statements and other schedules together with the trial balances and ledgers were promptly submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and submit subsidiary ledgers that show the complete details and breakdown of the account balances reflected in the financial statements. This also facilitates the financial audit review conducted by the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant maintained and submitted the subsidiary ledgers that show the details of the account balances.
observations.reason: The subsidiary ledgers were submitted to support the account balances reflected in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

Intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, objectives, laws, regulations and the utilization of the GAD Budget.

Consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address issues concerning women’s human rights and economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Person already underwent training in formulation of GAD plan. GAD Plan and Budget was submitted to DILG for review and endorsement. A certification from the Provincial Director of DILG was received stating the compliance of the plan and budget with the provision of JMC 2013-01.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal Person in formulating the plan and budget was compliant with the recommendation per review of submitted GAD Budget  and Utilization Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the municipality to properly formulate the projects to be included in the appropriation under the 20% development fund and to ensure that the projects fall under the provisions enumerated in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1. It is to be noted that the projects must address the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The management already reviewed the projects included in the 20% DF Budget and Utilization to include only those that address socio-economic development and environmental management.
observations.reason: The review of the 20% DF Budget and Utilization Report showed that the management complied with the recommendation and omitted projects inconsistent with the objectives of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the:

Municipal Accountant certify on the completeness of the supporting documents to the payrolls as enumerated in the above-mentioned Circular, including the verified/accepted accomplishment reports.

Municipal Treasurer prepare, issue and release check based on a duly approved DV for cash advance corresponding to the net amount of payrolls.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant implemented the recommendations by certifying on the completeness of the supporting documents to the payrolls including the verified/accepted accomplishment reports.
observations.reason: The required supporting documents are already attached to the cash and check disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality submit a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products procured fall within and conformed with PNDF, Volume I, current edition, pursuant to the above mentioned EO and AO otherwise the transaction will be suspended/disallowed in audit.

Henceforth, all procurement of drugs and medicines need to be accompanied with this certification.
observations.managementAction: The municipality submitted the required certification as indicated in the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Health Officer submitted to COA the required certification which was duly signed, stating that the purchased medicines all fall within the conformity of PDNF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the:

Municipal driver/s comply with Section 361 (e) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and pertinent section of COA Circular No.75-6 in accomplishing the Driver’s Trip Ticket in order to properly monitor and control gasoline consumption.

Municipal Accountant certify on the completeness of the supporting documents to the purchase of fuel as enumerated in the above-mentioned regulations, particularly the Driver’s Trip Ticket.

Municipal Treasurer issue/release check only upon a duly approved DV with the certification from the Municipal Accountant of the completeness of its supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The required trip tickets were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: The trip tickets were already submitted to COA. Management explained that the trip tickets were prepared but were not attached to the vouchers. However, such practice is already stopped by the management. The municipal accountant is responsible for certifying the completeness of the supporting documents relating to all disbursement of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management assign immediately to another personnel the liquidating or accounting function, since these activities being not compatible.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already hired a new Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The recommendation was well taken. A new municipal accountant was hired and the previously designated officer was returned to his position as liquidating officer.
