observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, review all the transactions affecting the account Advances for Payroll, and submit an aging schedule or detailed list of cash advances granted to the disbursing officer, to facilitate review thereof. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant be more cautious in recording transactions in order to properly reflect the account balance in the financial statements, pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled the negative balances with the help of the disbursing officer.
observations.reason: The financial statements showed that the Advances for Payroll account is without a negative balance and was closed as of December 31, 2019, indicating that all advances for payroll were properly liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectable for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording.

Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the debit to RPT/SET receivable and credit to Deferred RPT/SET income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectable and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET receivables.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant has the list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectable for the year from the Municipal Treasurer and Certification from Municipal Assessor.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer has prepared the list of taxpayers and secured certification from the Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concern elected officials to demand the issuance of an Official Receipt of the Republic of the Philippines for every payment of League assistance, contributions or any other kind of disbursement related thereto.

Strict observance of the Section 515 and 510 especially pertaining to the keeping of league fund and disbursement as provided.
observations.managementAction: Leagues were required to be registered to BIR and one of the requirements for registration is the League’s official receipt. Section 505 & 510 of RA 7160 does not specifically require an official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines for every payment to Leagues. While Section 4(6) of PD 1445 states that claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation, thus supported official receipts registered to BIR completes the documentation.
observations.reason: Disbursements relating to payment to leagues were supported with BIR-registered official receipts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management the following:
Immediately liquidate/settle all outstanding cash advances.
Withhold the salaries of the accountable officers and employees who failed to promptly liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
The municipal accountant must comply to COA Circular 97-002 and 2012-001 and issue a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.
Refrain granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant complied with COA Circular 97-002 and 2012-001.
observations.reason: Review of the quarterly report on cash advances showed that the Municipal Accountant indeed imposed proper control on the granting of cash advances and did not grant additional cash advance without prior liquidation of previously granted cash advance. However, the withholding of salaries was not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management the creation of an inventory committee to conduct the actual physical count of all the municipality’s PPEs as well as assess its actual condition to establish reliability of reported amount. Further, require the committee/s to prepare the inventory report and submit to the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 124 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: Inventory committee was updated per Memorandum 05-2019 and already conducted physical inventory taking.
observations.reason: The management submitted a duly approved Physical Count of PPE Report as of December 31, 2019. However, the said report is not in accordance with the proper format (RPCPPE).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer investigate and examine why negative balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated above occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them. Likewise, Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has to be maintained as basis in recording in the GL.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant investigated and examined the transactions leading to the negative balances and effected the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason:
Review of the schedules of the FS CY 2019 reveal that there are still accounts that remain negative as of December 31, 2019.

Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents as noted above and to submit an explanation on the deficiencies enumerated on the remarks indicated. We recommend that the municipality observe and comply strictly with the documentary requirements as required by COA Circular 2012-001 and P.D. No. 1445 and submit them to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: The documents required were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱5,000 take home pay per month for them to meet their needs and maintain self-respect and dignity as employees of the government, in pursuance of Section 48 of the 2018 GAA.  

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and regulate/control loan availment by not signing certifications for application of loans should an employee net pay fall below ₱5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant and HRMO already monitor the payroll deductions and regulate/control the loan application.
observations.reason: Only two (2) employees were left with lower than the required ₱5,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Acting Municipal Accountant to adhere with the required time frame for the submission of the monthly trial balance and schedules. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted incomplete documents without following the required time frame for their submission.
observations.reason: The Management submitted the trial balances and other schedules but outside the required timeframe.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce all collectors to deposit their collections daily and intact and to monitor the issuances of all accountable forms. We also recommend compliance of Section 30 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual and to adhere strictly to existing rules and regulations on cash management to avoid the occurrence of loss or shortage.
observations.managementAction: The revenue collection clerks were required to remit their collections daily. The Municipal Treasurer was required to monitor closely the remittance of the collections and deposited in the depository bank.
observations.reason:
Remittance by collection clerks was not done in a timely manner.

This is reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials/employees concerned to strictly comply on the prompt submission of copies of contracts, P.O.s and other similar contracts of understanding to the Auditor within five (5) days from its perfection pursuant to Section 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted copy of Purchase Orders.
observations.reason: Purchase Orders were submitted with no supporting documents and outside of the required timeframe for submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create an inventory and disposal committee to act immediately in the proper identification, ascertain existence, location and disposal of these assets in order to fairly present the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We further recommend that municipal accountant restore back the PPE accounts dropped from the books and adjustment have to be made only after proper inventory and disposal of the properties.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Team was already created to clearly verify those assets listed in the damaged assessment report during the 7.2 magnitude earthquake and made an Inventory Report of Property, Plant & Equipment as of December 31, 2014.
observations.reason: The amounts of PPE dropped from the books were based on the Inventory Report of Property Plant & Equipment as of December 31, 2014. A report of PPE was submitted as of December 31, 2019, however, no indication of the said amount dropped was emphasized in the report.

observations.recommendation:
1.	Submit justifications/explanations why the Municipal Mayor was made the payee of the purchase of lot instead of the true  vendor.

2.	Submit justification why the same amount was booked as Accounts Payable still with the Mayor as payee. 

3.	Submit justifications/explanations why the dates in the submitted documents did not reconcile with the logical chronology of things.

4.	Submit justifications/explanations why DBP CheckNo.  43830881 dated September 23, 2014 previously issued to the Municipal Mayor and encashed on September 24, 2014  was the same check allegedly issued/received by Uri Sallacay  per undated DV No. 100-15-01-68.

5.	Submit all documents to support the purchase of the lot pursuant to Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and  other pertinent provisions to support the payment.
observations.managementAction: The management is still in the process of complying the requirements for the titling of these lands.
observations.reason: Some of the required documents were already submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Must initiate the move by creating the inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties of the municipality.  The corresponding equipment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts.  In the absence of available records of acquisitions in the past years, they will have no other recourse but to take hold of the disbursement vouchers to obtain the necessary data to be recorded in the equipment/property ledger cards.

Require the general services officer or municipal treasurer to submit the request for inspection with complete supporting papers so that the auditors can take proper action on the request.
observations.managementAction: COA Technical personnel already inspected the damaged properties and were already dropped from the books basing from the inventory report made by the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: Management did not submit the inventory report for the damaged properties as well as the request to drop these properties from the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and  Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts.

Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.

Adopt and always observe austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already maximized the services of permanent employees by assigning additional functions not related to their actual term of reference.
observations.reason: The municipality recorded the wages of casual employees to Salaries and Wages-Casual account and maximized the services of permanent employees.

observations.recommendation:
Require the municipal accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV) to reclassify cash advances  for travel and special purpose/ time-bound undertakings  recorded under the Receivables- Due from Officers and Employees (123) account to the appropriate account, Advances to Officers and Employees (148) pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002 dated January 19, 2007.  On the other hand, the cash shortage of Benjamin Abad amounting to ₱491,497.84 should be reclassified to the account Due from Officers and Employees (123).  

Send demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitor the liquidation of these cash advances.

Exert more effort to collect these receivables such as withholding payments of claims of municipal officials/employees who have long overdue accountabilities with the LGU as in the case of audit disallowances.

Follow-up with the Office of the Ombudsman the status of the case filed against the defalcating municipal treasurer.

Issue bills or collection/confirmation letters  to  agencies/persons concerned  as  proofs  that the Municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means  to collect these receivables that have become dormant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has sent demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitored the liquidation of these cash advances.
observations.reason: Copies of the demand letters were submitted to COA. However, they did not withhold the salaries of the officers concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend immediate liquidation/settlement of outstanding cash advances for Confidential/Intelligence Fund. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on allowing the grant of cash advances despite non-liquidation of previous cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant no longer grants cash advances relating to Confidential/Intelligence Fund with an outstanding unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: The related Confidential Fund cash advance in the Report of Cash Advances is still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant fast-tract the identification/verification of payable account to establish correct amount and to present correct balances of accounts payable in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant sent to COA a certification for the reversion of dormant accounts payable in the book for more than two years.
observations.reason: No other supporting documents were submitted to verify the actual age of the accounts. Schedule and aging of accounts payable were not submitted to COA hence, we could not verify properly whether the accounts to be reverted were properly taken up as payables and are actually qualified for reversion.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to submit copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports on procured items to the Auditor’s Office within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to comply with the provision of Paragraph 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and for the Auditor to conduct timely inspection of items delivered.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports of procured items to the COA but beyond the required deadline.
observations.reason: Management has submitted a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports of procured items to the COA but beyond the required deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Inventory Committee to establish existence of the breeding stocks by conducting physical inventory taking. Require the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAO) to maintain individual record of items to match/reconcile with the report of the inventory committee and the LGUs books.  Likewise, the Accounting Unit maintain ledger cards for Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Inventory was made by the Inventory Committee and Inventory report was produced.
observations.reason: Management submitted Inventory report, however, no ledger cards for the monitoring of breeding stocks were maintained. Moreover, this amount remains unmoving in the books.

observations.recommendation: Exercise fully the taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to 150 of R. A. 7160 in order to earn more revenue necessary to augment the funding for the operation of the municipality.  Otherwise, implement austerity measures to reduce operating expenses and utilize internal revenue fund for developmental projects that would be beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management already reduced operating expenses but to earn more revenue, the Municipality failed to maximize the exercise of its taxing power due to limited manpower in the treasurer’s office.
observations.reason: Tax revenue increased as shown in the comparative Consolidated Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2019 however its operating costs also increased by a considerable amount.

observations.managementAction: The management already reduced operating expenses by reducing staff from market operations and intensifying collection for market fees.
observations.reason: According to the municipal mayor, there is no other way of increasing market collections due to the geographic location of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to constitute the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and reconcile the results with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 constituted an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory and prepared the Property, Plant and Equipment cards.
observations.reason: There are lapses from the accounting records versus actual inventory of PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require to: a) Liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances; b) Monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; and c) Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advance to those who have unliquidated balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 and one is discontinued by compelling those who still have unliquidated balances to submit unliquidated liquidation balances.
observations.reason: There are some accountable officers were discontinued and some deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Office concerned to take up the procurement of all items in the appropriate inventory accounts and maintain an updated inventory accounts under the perpetual inventory method, and maintain an updated ledger cards to keep an eye on the inflows and outflows of an inventory.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 the Municipal Mayor to require the personnel concerned to take up the procurement of all items in the appropriate inventory accounts and to institute appropriate actions to recover the government funds invested in the program and to institute appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books.
observations.reason: There are lapses from the accounting records and the supplies ledger card is not yet done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned to strictly observe the limitations on the use of the Special Education Fund as provided for in Section 4 of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the utilization of SEF.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 submitted the School Board Plan showing the expenses eligible for funding out of SEF.
observations.reason: There are remaining appropriate implement actions. Some report on SEF utilization was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The three (3) position be filled immediately to begin the institutionalization of the organization for better operations and implementation of LDRRM; and b) Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned department heads to optimally utilize the LDRRMF in order to uphold the rights of the people to life and properties through preparing for disasters, mitigating its effect, post-disaster response and recovery measures.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 constituted the three (3) position for the LGU and the LDRRM office to begin institutionalization.
observations.reason: There are still some outstanding matters; only two (2) DRRM Staffs were funded for this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Follow-up the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 by the National Solid Waste Management Commission; b) Require the necessary training to the collectors and personnel to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and in accordance with the guidelines; c) Provide all collectors and other personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste with personal protective equipment to protect them from the hazards of handling wastes; and d) Enforce the proper segregation of solid waste at source and information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 follow-up of the Solid Waste Management Plan is being undertaken and the LGU provided reorientation and trainings to collectors and personnel.
observations.reason: The SWM Plan is still not approved. Reorientation and trainings were conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prudently monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 the Local Chief Executive directed the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prudently monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipal Government.
observations.reason: There are project monitoring reports being submitted to the LCE. However, utilization still not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Local Chief Executive to enjoin the department heads to strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on prioritization, and training of personnel responsible for planning and implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings; b) Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS; and c) Recognize gender issues within the mandate of the LGU and incorporate in the GPB the causes of the gender issues and assess the impact of GAD activities and policies in relation to gender equality and women empowerment and monitor the GPB implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 directed the GAD Focal Person to undergo trainings and adhere with recommendati ons.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal will have to obtain trainings related to GAD. Some corrective actions undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Safeguard the full utilization of the source/program purpose; b) Supervise and monitor the completion of all remaining projects without further delay for the enjoyment of benefits of the targeted beneficiaries; and c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with each other to affect the immediate return of the unexpended/excess balances of the same to the concerned source agencies after the implementation of each project.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 the Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned to comply with the recommendation and return funds.
observations.reason: There are some unexpended amounts refunded/returned but some balances remain in the Financial Statements. Some were submitted to Central Office several years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the Local Chief Executive instruct the MSWDO to cause the immediate submission of the required documents as prescribed under the rules and procedures of the DSWD Guidelines to guarantee attainment of the primary purpose of the social protection program; and b) Municipal Treasurer for the immediate liquidation of his cash advances and submit complete supporting documents of his liquidation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 submitted the required documents to conform with implementing procedures provided by the DSWD.
observations.reason: Some documents were submitted and efforts made to comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld intact to lessen the liability and accountability of the LGU, and prevent the possibility of using funds for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 the department heads complied with the recommendati on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions and bank reconciliation together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, remind the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 submitted the 2017 accounts on the average of 0-15 days after the 10th day of the succeeding month for the DVs, and an average of 15 to 30 days for the bank recon.
observations.reason: Some reports failed to be submitted on the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to: a) Reconcile the accounts and institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books; and b) Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the reason why these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action thereon; and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 requested to write off of the accounts not yet done.
observations.reason: There are still remaining balances not yet written off. Request/apply for the write-off of the accounts not yet done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the personnel concerned to: a) Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) regularly and submit to the Accountant as basis in preparing the journal entry voucher (JEV) to booked up the issuance of supplies and materials on stock; b) Make a regular updating of the supplies ledger card/stock cards/Bin Cards; advise Accounting and the Supply Sections to conduct regular reconciliation of their records; c) Conduct physical count/inventory of said items every semester as prescribed in the NGAS; and d) Prepare Subsidiary Ledgers as prescribed by Section 111 (1) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 SSMI was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the Cash in Bank-LCCA adjustment for the stale checks and submit the bank statements and list of deposits in transit and other items for adjustment by the bank; and b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit timely the monthly Bank Reconciliation statements including bank statements, paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memos as prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011; and submit the list of deposits in transit and other items for adjustment by the bank, indicated in the BRS, and the negotiated and cancelled checks pursuant to the pertinent paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to facilitate the review on the accuracy of the submitted BRS.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Recorded properly the adjusted balances of the cash accounts; submission of bank statements are being provided and the Cash in Bank-LCCA balances are being corrected.
observations.reason: Bank statements are being provided and the Cash in Bank-LCCA balances are being adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local School Board to implement the planned and approved PPAs during the year and to fully utilize the funds during the year in order to achieve its purpose, and to strictly observe the provision of Section 6 of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the preparation and submission of the quarterly and annual fund utilization reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Submitted the required reports and the LGU complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Some PPAs not yet implemented; SEF Utilization report was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant ensure that only expenditures enumerated under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund and that projects funded from the 20% DF are feasible and implementable within the period which it was proposed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 strict observance to the provision of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 was instructed and ensure that projects funded under 20% DF are feasible.
observations.reason: Some 20% DF projects were not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 5% of the revenue from the regular sources for disaster preparedness and mitigation to help achieve desirable protection and preservation of the lives and properties of the constituents of the municipality; and that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office. Also recommended transfer the unutilized balance of P1,025,131.92 to comply with the prescribed rules and regulations and transfer the balance from Trust Fund into General Funds to be made available for other social services.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 the LGU prepared the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund. DRRM Staffs were hired.
observations.reason: Some reports submitted. Only two (2) DRRM staffs were hired; unexpended balance transfer pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Custodian and Accountant and the Inventory Team to reorient on NGAS for LGUs and continue their efforts in reconciling their respective records to ascertain the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts reflected in the financial statements; prepare the inventory and inspection report of unserviceable property (IIRUP) and restore all unserviceable properties to other asset accounts; and ensure transfer of titles of purchased and donated parcels of land in the name of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 re-orientation of the Property custodian and inventory team on NGAS has been conducted and the physical inventory is being undertaken.
observations.reason: Physical inventory still on-going and difficulties in locating some purchased 5-10 years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with Section 51 and 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units and that the procurements be recorded following the perpetual inventory method.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 recorded properly the inventory following the perpetual inventory method.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance to the accounting and reporting guidelines for local road network construction and maintenance system prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 an Inventory Committee was organized and reoriented.
observations.reason: Physical inventory completed but full documentation not yet done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management reconcile the General Journal and General Ledgers, submit the supporting documents for journal entries, prepare the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and prepare schedules for depreciation and accounts payable and submit annually a complete set of supporting schedules and documents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 the accountant started to take appropriate action and submitted the supporting documents for some entries.
observations.reason: Waiting for submission of remaining supporting documents and completion of reconciliation. Some items still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit explanations and justifications why procurement and disbursements were done despite lack of funds for the project, collect liquidated damages from non-compliant contractors, refrain from payment without complete documentation and ensure authenticity of documents submitted by bidders and furnish COA with delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 the LGU has started rectifying deficiencies and submitting documents. BAC to submit justifications where necessary.
observations.reason: Some procurement documents were not yet submitted to COA; justifications being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate a 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan; justify the plan and procurement of the plastic trash containers despite being not included in the AIP and PPAs and submit the distribution list or Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment for the plastic trash containers. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 prepared a 10-year solid waste plan and further actions underway.
observations.reason: 10-year solid waste management plan prepared but not yet approved. Some procurements not included in AIP/PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) require the officials and employees to issue Accountable Form No. 51 for all their payments; b) demand refund of the excess registration fee paid to the private institution and to refrain from such practice; and c) ensure compliance with NBC rules.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 demanded accountable officers to issue Accountable Form No. 51 for all their payments and to refund excess amounts.
observations.reason: Some officials did refund but some did not; recovery still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) with a DRRMO and three (3) staffs as required in RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1, and direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF and correct the deficiencies and submit the lacking supporting documents, and transfer the balance from Trust Fund into General Fund if appropriate.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 the LGU started to hire DRRM Staffs and submitted some reports.
observations.reason: Only two (2) DRRM Staffs were hired. Some documentation and transfers pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials and employees identified to refund the amounts received as calamity assistance and 14th month pay which have no legal basis and to stop issuance of similar payments without legal authority.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 the LGU appealed to the Commission on the disallowance; petition for review filed.
observations.reason: Waiting for the decision of the appeal made by the LGU; some refunds being processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit promptly the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements together with bank statements, paid checks and original copies of the debit/credit memos.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 the LGU has submitted the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management include the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and the reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis in the Notes to the Financial Statements and present the required disclosures in accordance with PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 the accounting office submitted the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows.
observations.reason: The submitted report was erroneous and did not achieve its purpose initially; corrections made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory committee, require the conduct of annual physical count and render a report thereon. Likewise, an inventory and inspection report of unserviceable properties should be prepared prior to disposal. Meanwhile, require the Accountant to transfer all unserviceable properties to other asset accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 the physical inventory committee was organized and inventory procedures re-oriented.
observations.reason: Physical inventory is on-going; some unserviceable properties located and disposal/transfer pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the audit recommendations towards improved financial operations and governance and require all concerned to strictly observe existing laws, accounting and auditing rules and regulations to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 some implementation undertaken to improve financial operations; recommendations being followed up.
observations.reason: Some recommendations remain outstanding and being worked on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the accountable officers to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances and issue demand letters for non-compliant accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 sent demand letters to outstanding accountable officers to liquidate their advances.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers refunded; others are no longer connected with the LGU anymore.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the officials and employees to refund the allowance/honoraria received without legal basis and to strictly observe COA Circular No. 2013-003 and other pertinent rules.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 sent demand letters to the concerned payees to refund the allowance/honoraria received without legal basis.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers refunded; others not connected with the LGU anymore.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require employees to strictly comply with the provisions of EO 298 and NBC 486, refund the amount received in excess of the authorized travel rates and registration fees, and stop the practice of issuing travel authority for attendance to conventions of organizations/leagues to which the personnel is not affiliated.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 employees were ordered to refund the excess and strict compliance was reminded.
observations.reason: Refunds are not yet fully completed; recovery ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials concerned submit the documentary requirements for payment of honoraria and overtime services as specified in Items 5.7 and 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and refund amounts where not supported.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 employees were ordered to submit the documentary requirements needed and to refund where necessary.
observations.reason: Some documents submitted; some claims still unsupported awaiting documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to stop the grant of such benefits in the absence of any legal or statutory basis and that the officials and employees be required to refund the amounts received without legal basis.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 the LGU appealed to the Commission Proper for the disallowance decision.
observations.reason: Waiting for the decision of the appeal made by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare the MRSMI for Drugs and Medicines and draw a JEV to restore the amount credited under the Drugs and Medicines Inventory account without the supporting MRSMI.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 the Municipal Health Officer and Property Custodian prepared the MRSMI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management either hire or augment personnel for the Inventory Committee for the purpose of conducting the physical inventory count, and prepare the necessary report on the results thereof (RPCPPE).
observations.reason: The inventory report submitted was only as of June 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to secure the prior approval/authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan in the implementation of projects, programs, and activities which are described in the budget of the Municipality in generic terms, and to also, as much as possible, thoroughly plan and propose only PPAs for implementation that are specific and sufficiently detailed.

We recommended that management adequately plan and study the feasibility of each project before proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by the 20% Development Fund, and to ensure proper coordination among concerned offices. 

We also recommended either employing additional workforce for the Engineering Office to help implement priority development projects, or to resort to implement projects by contract in case hiring of additional workers is not feasible.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to take measures to ensure the implementation of the projects.
observations.reason: Several projects and programs from previous year had already been implemented. Reiterated as Finding No. 6 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management thoroughly plan and ensure proper coordination of the projects and activities to be implemented under the LDRRMF. 

In addition, we also recommended the preparation of a monitoring report in order to check the status of each project/activity, and also for the management to be able to quickly and carefully plan the measures to be done in case there are difficulties encountered in the implementation of the said projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to take measures to ensure the implementation of the projects.
observations.reason: Several projects and programs from previous year had already been implemented. Reiterated as Finding No. 8 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management 1) strictly enforce the "No Segregation, No Collection" Policy on wastes to be disposed; 2) prioritize the purchase of additional equipment for garbage collection and operation of RCA/SLF and; 3) expedite the processing of the necessary procedures and provide adequate funding for the establishment and utilization of the SLF.
observations.reason: Additional equipment has been purchased for garbage collection. Reiterated as Finding No. 9 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adopt the ATM Payroll System in the payment of salaries and wages of its employees.

We recommended that management thoroughly plan each PPA before proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by the GAD Fund, as well as ensure that each proposed PPA should be implemented on time. 

We also recommended to direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly monitor the status of implementation of each approved GAD PPA.

We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and to prepare corresponding financial statements with complete subsidiary records to provide users of financial statements with vital information relative to its assets, liabilities, equity, and results of operations.

In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment;

Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties; and

Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.
observations.reason: The inventory report submitted was only as of June 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances and strictly comply with provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting and liquidation of cash advances; and

Avail of the remedy of write-off prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 for dormant unliquidated cash advances which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The management already stopped the granting of cash advances to those personnel with outstanding cash advances. Also, the request for write-off will be submitted as soon as the needed supporting documents are already available.
observations.reason: No additional cash advances were granted to individuals with outstanding cash advances. Requests for write-off are still to be prepared by Management. Reiterated as Finding No. 2 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and the Municipal Accountant to submit timely the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund.

We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:

Enforce collection of the liquidated damages from the supplier if no document to prove the extensions of delivery dates

Submit written justification/explanation on the following:

deficiencies noted on the documents related to extension of deliveries;

why the Municipality continuously granted extensions?

despite the BAC resolution’s recommendation on the imposition of liquidated damages, why were there no effort exerted to collect such?
observations.managementAction: The demand letter prepared by the BAC is still for resolution.
observations.reason: Demand letter was prepared by the BAC Secretariat but was not signed by the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert/enforce efforts to collect these collectibles so that whatever collection can be used for the operation of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should determine the collectivity of these accounts so that proper adjustments could be made in order not to overstate the receivable balances as at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Constant follow-ups are made by management to facilitate the processing of liquidation of receivable accounts. Demand Letters will also be sent to the accountable officers concerned.
observations.reason: Partial liquidations were already made by the recipients and constant follow-ups are still to be done to facilitate the processing of liquidation

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following:

Regularly send confirmation/demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures;

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts.

In compliance with COA Circular 2012-004, furnish this Audit Team with the list of accountable officers with unliquidated and outstanding cash advances, together with copy of written notices duly acknowledged by the concerned accountable officers.  Submit also copies of disbursement vouchers (DVs) and related support documents of those with outstanding balances, especially long overdue accounts.

This LGU is reminded further of the consequences provided for in section 9.0 of the same COA Circular 2012-004, as follows:

“9.1 Failure of an accountable officer to liquidate his outstanding cash advance on or before January 31, 2013 shall constitute cause for the filing of malversation charge under Article 217, failure to liquidate cash advance under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of PD No. 1445.

9.2  The suspension of salaries of erring accountable shall be ordered by the Auditor concerned to the proper agency official through the head of the agency.

9.3 Appropriate administrative proceedings shall likewise be instituted.”
observations.managementAction: The management already stopped the granting of cash advances to those personnel with outstanding cash advances. Also, the request for write-off will be submitted as soon as the needed supporting documents are already available.
observations.reason: No additional cash advances were granted to individuals with outstanding cash advances. Requests for write-off are still to be prepared by Management. Reiterated as Finding No. 2 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the settlement of accounts disallowed within six (6) months from receipt of the Notice of Disallowance (ND) by the persons liable in accordance with Section 10.4 of said circular. Adhere to Section 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Management will continue to deduct the corresponding amount to concerned employees.
observations.reason: The disallowed amount is still being deducted from the salary/wage of the employee concerned who are still connected with the LGU. There are some which cannot be collected since they are either no longer connected with the government or are already dead.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:

Create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE. To ensure the success of the committee, all department heads are advised to instruct their members to cooperate in the conduct of physical count;

The inventory committee shall prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation with PPE Ledger Cards and the Property Cards to be maintained by the Accounting and Property Offices, respectively;

Proper disposal of unserviceable property should be made.

The following provisions of Sec. 490 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies, materials and equipment. — Physical stock-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. In all cases, the physical inventory-taking which is required semi-annually or annually should be regarded with importance.

For effective control of government property, the COA prescribes the following regulations:

Chiefs of agencies are required to take a physical inventory of all the equipment and supplies belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, unless otherwise determined by the COA Chairman in specific cases. Such inventory shall be made as of December 31 on General Form 41 (A) (Appendix 12) and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, unless extended by the Chairman, Commission on Audit, upon prior request of the chief of agency concerned. When the exigencies of the service permit, the taking of inventory should be in the charge of a committee of two or more employees designated by the chief of agency, including the property officer or custodian, depending on the extent of property accountability in a particular agency, to be witnessed by a representative of the Agency Auditor.

Xxx”

The required annual/semi-annual inventory shall be certified correct by the committee in charge thereof, attested by the representative of the auditor concerned and approved by the chief of agency in interest.”
observations.reason: The inventory report submitted was only as of June 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: Liquidate the cash advance for local travel within 30 days after return to his official station.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters will be sent to those accountable officers who are still connected with the LGU.
observations.reason: Several accountable officers have already submitted liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommend the following:

Create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE. To ensure the success of the committee, all department heads are advised to instruct their members to cooperate in the conduct of physical count;

The inventory committee shall prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation with PPE Ledger Cards and the Property Cards to be maintained by the Accounting and Property Offices, respectively; and

Proper disposal of unserviceable property should be made.
observations.reason: The inventory report submitted was only as of June 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following:

Regularly send confirmation/demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures; and

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts.

Furnish the Audit Team with the list of accountable officers with unliquidated and outstanding cash advances, together with copy of written notices duly acknowledged by the concerned accountable officers.  Submit also copies of disbursement vouchers (DVs) and related supporting documents of those with outstanding balances, especially long overdue accounts.
observations.managementAction: The management already stopped the granting of cash advances to those personnel with outstanding cash advances. Also, the request for write-off will be submitted as soon as the needed supporting documents are already available.
observations.reason: No additional cash advances were granted to individuals with outstanding cash advances. Requests for write-off are still to be prepared by Management. Reiterated as Finding No. 2 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
Hasten the implementation of developmental projects as embodied in the Municipal Annual Investment Plan within the time frame to accelerate socio-economic growth and environmental development in the Municipality.  

Direct the Municipal Planning Development Council or the Municipal Project Engineer to exercise their monitoring function to immediately identify and address any problem(s) that tend to hamper / slow down development.
Fast track the settlement of accounts disallowed and to assist the persons liable for the settlement. Adhere to Section 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009
observations.managementAction: The Management will continue to deduct the corresponding amount to concerned employees.
observations.reason: The disallowed amount is still being deducted from the salary/wage of the employee concerned who are still connected with the LGU. There are some which cannot be collected since they are either no longer connected with the government or are already dead.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances. Demand letters/confirmation letters should regularly be prepared by the accounting office as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officer’s record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures.
observations.managementAction: The management already stopped the granting of cash advances to those personnel with outstanding cash advances. Also, the request for write-off will be submitted as soon as the needed supporting documents are already available.
observations.reason: No additional cash advances were granted to individuals with outstanding cash advances. Requests for write-off are still to be prepared by Management. Reiterated as Finding No. 2 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
The Head of the Agency should cause the issuance of demand letters, thru the initiative of the Municipal Accountant.  If after two (2) notices these accountable officers employees still fail to liquidate, the matter should be brought to the attention of the legal officer for proper disposition, copy furnished the Commission on Audit.

The Municipal Mayor, being primarily responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the municipality, and being the approving officer, should enforce the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

Failure of the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for withholding of their salaries and the institution of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.3 of the said circular and Section 128 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management already stopped the granting of cash advances to those personnel with outstanding cash advances. Also, the request for write-off will be submitted as soon as the needed supporting documents are already available.
observations.reason: No additional cash advances were granted to individuals with outstanding cash advances. Requests for write-off are still to be prepared by Management. Reiterated as Finding No. 2 in this year's Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Require the agency head to register the land in the name of the municipality as provided in Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to ensure that the property including its improvements will be safeguarded from squatters and unlawful occupants as the case maybe.

observations.recommendation:
The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUPs) for non-functional or our-of-order properties should be prepared and submitted to COA, and for the management to recommend for inspection so that the disposal of the same may be done in accordance with prescribed procedures under Sec. 79 of PD 1445 and the NGAS Manual and existing auditing rules and regulations.

The concerned Municipal Officers should submit to COA a written explanation for non-compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386, the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to demand the collection of the P471,556.17 Loans Receivable-Others from its borrowers and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like. Management should undertake reasonable efforts to collect this debt owing from the borrowers lest the intention of the program benefits will be defeated.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the revised withholding tax table under Republic Act 10963 or TRAIN Law effective January 1, 2018.
We recommended that the responsible employees abide by the guidelines set by the DBM on the payment of honoraria to those involved in procurement activities.

We recommended that an annual transfer of excess collections over payments made for the honoraria and overtime pay of the members of the BAC should be made to be recorded as income in the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) pertaining to all BuB and other NGA-funded projects of the Municipality for the years 2015 and above and should exert all reasonable efforts and diligence in procuring said contracts/ Memorandum of Agreements in a timely manner from the source agency.
observations.reason: MOAs were only submitted when requested.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct Municipal Accountant that financial statements such as Balance Sheet, Statement of Income and Expenses and Cash Flows should be prepared and presented as part of the schedules of the General Fund Trial Balance. Compliance with Section 105 and 106 on provision of adequate information as to assets, liabilities and equity for each special account is enjoined.

We recommended the following courses of action:

The Local School Board should ensure that expenditures charged against the SEF include only those among the priority list of expenses to be charged against SEF, thus guaranteeing the welfare of the school children and that SEF are being utilized for the purpose for which it was created; and

Stop the practice of utilizing SEF to expenditures of wages and other allowances of non-teaching personnel.

We recommended that management intensify its efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of preserving our natural resources by proper disposal of wastes.

2. We also recommended that management comply with all the applicable rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and the environment by utilizing the 2.9 hectares lot that the municipality purchased last 2016.
observations.reason: The revised SWMP was only submitted to the DENR-EMB XI on 09.26.2019 and is still awaiting for deliberation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that coordination between Municipal and San Isidro Fire Station Officials shall be made to demand the release of 20% share in revenues arising from the enforcement of R.A. No. 9514.  Further, require the Fire Station to submit copies of its Monthly Report of Collections and Deposits to the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate the monitoring and accounting of the LGU’s 20% share from the Fire Code Revenues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit team assigned to the agency within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking reports and henceforth, observe the submission within the prescribed period of the Report of Collections and Deposits, Disbursement Vouchers, Liquidation Reports, Journal Entry Vouchers and general journal transactions to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the concerned NGOs/POs to liquidate the long outstanding funds transferred to them; failure to liquidate the same should disqualify the said NGOs/POs from availing additional funds from the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: The Agency is in the process of establishing GAD Database

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
(a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to payees with
unclaimed checks at least one month before a check becomes stale. If it
remains unclaimed and had become stale, to cancel the stale check in
accordance to Section 83 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.
At the of the year, he shall submit to the Accounting Office a Schedule of
Unreleased Checks;
(b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile monthly the book and bank
balances and properly apply the correct accounting treatment of
bank/book reconciling items in accordance with the generally accepted and
accounting principles; and
(c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV to record all book
reconciling items, including the restoration of stale checks and unreleased
checks, which were not yet taken up in CY 2019. Also, may we be furnished
of the journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to record the adjusting entries for
purposes of verification.

2.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) PPEs In-charge to complete the physical inventory of PPEs and conduct
the same every year, and prepare thereon the Report on the Physical Count
of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted every
January 31 of each year;
(b) Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger
Card (PPELC) and the GSO the Property Ledger Card;
(c) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted in the Schedule
of Depreciation and provide for an allowance for depreciation on all
depreciable assets, and
(d) Municipal Accountant to transfer the completed project to its appropriate
asset account that has long existed as CIP under SEF.

3.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
(a) Require the General Services Officer to prepare at the end of each
accounting period a Report on Local Road Network and maintain a Local
Road Inventory and Road Map;
(b) Require the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to submit
the required reports so that the Municipal Accountant can record all the
road network owned by the municipality to ensure that all roads are
properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the
account infrastructure assets in the financial statements; and
(c) Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the accumulated
depreciation for all the road networks owned by the Municipality and
provide the disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for the
succeeding year as required by COA Circular COA Circular No. 2015-008.

4.9. In compliance with accounting rules and regulations and in order to correctly
present the cost of the Land Transport Terminal, we recommended that the
Municipal Accountant draw a JEV to correct the erroneous entry made. The
entry is to Debit the Asset account and Credit Prior Period Adjustment.

5.6. We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to classify
liabilities as to current and non-current and present accordingly in the Statement
of Financial Position of succeeding years.

6.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) Prepare the Statement of Cash Flows in accordance to the provisions of
IPSAS 2, together with supporting schedules that provide breakdown of
computations of items classified under operating, investing and financing
activities; and
(b) Prepare a correct Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating
Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) supported with schedules showing how
reconciling items were derived/computed.

7.5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
(d) The Municipal Engineer to ensure the timely implementation of PPAs
funded by NGAs and to supervise the completion thereof. Establish close
coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office so that
the latter could monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different
PPAs of the LGU;
(e) The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the DOH on their request
for realignment of the fund received in CY 2015 and cause the return
thereof if not granted. Identify if the project funded by DTI in CY 2016
have already been completed and cause the return of the unused funds.
Prepare and update schedules on the utilization to support the book
balance and prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the erroneous recording
of fund received from PhilHealth and CFI; and
(f) The Municipal Treasurer to determine the availability of cash of all fund
transfers which are reported in the books and issue the check in favor of
the Source Agencies to return all unutilized/unused amount, including the
dormant balances. In case the source agency can no longer be identified,
the check shall be made in favor of the Bureau of Treasury.

8.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer,
Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and all others concerned to
implement the programs/projects which have not yet been started in order to
ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant
to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

9.4. The Local Chief Executive, the Municipal Accountant and the MDRMM Officer
of the LGU are recommended to undertake the following:
(d) To revert back the CY 2013 unutilized/unexpended balance of
₱981,975.00 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and be
made available for appropriation.
(e) To transfer the unexpended/unutilized/unobligated balance of
₱1,255,568.57 from the CY 2019 appropriation to Trust Liabilities
account.
(f) To program the utilization of the donated funds and implement the
approved PPAs to maximize utilization of funds to achieve the benefits of
the programs.

10.10. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
(f) Remind the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to stop
charging wages of Job Order Employees who are not directly involved in
the solid waste management of the LGU;
(g) Instruct the MENRO, who was delegated as person-in-charge of SWMP,
to implement planned and approved programs during the year;
(h) Conduct seminars and trainings to garbage collectors on the proper
handling of solid wastes;
(i) Procure the required protective gears for garbage collectors; and
(j) Implement programs to campaign for the waste segregation, reuse,
recycling and composting of solid waste.

11.14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to do the following:
(d) Direct the Human Resource Management Officer in coordination with
Program Coordinator to assess the manpower requirements of their
respective LGU programs and projects to identify the appropriate size of
workforce needed for the implementation thereof; and to provide manning
schedule for the delegation of work assignment to avoid duplication of
tasks;
(e) Direct the immediate supervisors to check and review the accomplishment
reports and daily time records of job before submission to HRMO to
ensure that entries in the DTRs are correct and Accomplishment Reports
are properly complied; and
(f) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance of the documentary
requirements enumerated under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012 in the liquidation of cash advances for wages of job order workers.

12.8. We recommended to the Municipal to:
(d) Instruct the HRMO that the Job Order employees should be paid on a
daily basis or, if applicable, in a piecework basis pursuant to Section 77 of
the Republic Act No. 7160 dated October 10, 1991 and Section 2 of Rule
XI of the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40, s. 1998 dated December 14,
1998.;
(e) Direct the HRMO to check the correctness/completeness of entries in daily
time records and accomplishment reports; and
(f) Direct the Municipal Accountant to check the correctness/completeness of
supporting documents and check the number of days worked that shall be
paid.

13.5. We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified Persons
Liable in the Notice of Suspension or the Notice of Disallowance to settle their
audit suspension or disallowance.
13.6. We recommended further for the Municipal Accountant to send a copy of the
Notices to the Persons Liable for their information on their unsettled
suspensions/disallowance.

14.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
c. Enjoin the department heads to strictly comply with the provisions of
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on
prioritization, and training of personnel responsible for planning and
implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities to ensure that
the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended
beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance
that may be provided; and
d. Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or
ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization
of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in
local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.

15.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor
to fully implement the 3rd General Revision of Assessment and Property
Classification of taxable real property units (RPUs) and update every 3 years
thereafter, pursuant to Section 219 of RA No. 7160.

16.7. We recommended for the Local School Board:
c. To implement the planned and approved PPAs during the year. It is
understood, that in the planning, the needs of all the schools in the LGU
are being considered; and
d. To fully utilize the funds during the year in order to achieve its purpose.

17.6. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or the Designated Disbursing
Officer exercise due care in the release of payments for every transaction and to
stamp “PAID” on the payrolls and supporting documents, and Disbursement
Vouchers upon release of payment so that double payment and other irregular
use of paid vouchers will be avoided.
