observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the:

 (1) Municipal Accountant to 

(a) ensure   that  all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated  under Section 4.2 of DSWD  MC  No. 02 s. 2014 to establish  the validity and propriety of claims; and  

(b)  ensure that the proper account is use in recording the payment of AICS; and 

(2) MSWDO to prepare and maintain a file of each client/beneficiary’s case evaluation to facilitate the validation of the propriety of the said transaction.
observations.managementAction: The Management had called the attention of the person in charge in the implementa-tion of AICS program of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The transactions for AICS in CY 2018 still lacked the required supporting documents.

The account MOOE is still used in recording payment of AICS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with provision of Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996,  requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection and Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries to validate if the same conform with the quality and quantity asked for.
observations.managementAction: The Management had called the attention of the person in charge to comply on the required submission of report/ documents.
observations.reason: The corresponding report/ documents for CY 2018 were still not submitted by concerned official.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procurement Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management called the attention of the person in charge to comply on the required submission of report.
observations.reason: The corresponding report for CY 2018 was still not submitted by concerned official.         Reiterated in Observation No. 20

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Management called the attention of the person in charge to comply on the required submission of report.
observations.reason:
The corresponding report for CY 2018 was still not submitted by concerned official.

Reiterated in Observation No. 14

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LGU GAD Focal Person and Municipal Accountant to submit an Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year using the format in Annex B   pursuant to paragraph  V of COA Circular No. 2012–004 and Section 10 of the Joint Circular No. 2012-1.
observations.managementAction: The Management had called the attention of the person in charge to comply on the required submission of report.
observations.reason:
The corresponding report for CY 2018 was still not submitted by concerned official.

Reiterated in Observation No. 18

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, review all the transactions affecting the account Advances for Payroll, and submit an aging schedule or detailed list of cash advances granted to the disbursing officer, to facilitate review thereof. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant be more cautious in recording transactions in order to properly reflect the account balance in the financial statements, pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled the negative balances with the help of the disbursing officer.
observations.reason: Per General Fund Statement of Financial Position as of December 31, 2018, Advances for Payroll account still has a negative balance of ₱429,459.02.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all his collections with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) daily or on the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor ordered the Municipal Treasurer to conduct meeting with all the Revenue Collection Clerks (RCCs) so that the Audit Recommendation will be addressed in a timely manner and to provide comprehensive guidance in all his RCC’s for the accomplishment of the action underway.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in 2017 and is still not complied by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management demand immediate liquidation/settlement of cash advances from concerned LGU personnel and compliance to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Further, impose withholding of salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation/settlement of outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: Accounts aged over one to more than 10 years are still included in the unliquidated cash advances. Liquidation/settlement of these accounts are still not implemented. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management immediately settle/liquidate cash advances of Intelligence/Confidential Fund pursuant to DBM-DILG-DND-GCG-COA Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 and provide COA the proof of transmittal of liquidation documents sent to ICFAU, COA, Quezon City.

Submit explanation on the delayed/late and non-compliance of the above-mentioned provisions regarding the release /liquidation of Intelligence/Confidential Fund.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: These accounts are still unliquidated per verification with the Report on Cash Advances. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend immediate liquidation/settlement of outstanding cash advances for Confidential/Intelligence Fund. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on allowing the grant of cash advances despite non-liquidation of previous cash advances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: The existing unliquidated cash advances for Confidential/Intelligence Fund reveals that the Municipal Accountant failed to monitor the issuance of cash advances without prior liquidation of previous cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant fast-tract the identification/verification of payable account to establish correct amount and to present correct balances of accounts payable in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: No schedule and aging of payables were submitted so we could not verify the account’s validity as to presented amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the classification/identification of payable account to establish correct balances in the financial statements.  Comply COA Circular No. 2004-008 and COA Circular No. 94-103.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: No supporting schedules were submitted to the audit team to verify the balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply to RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.
observations.managementAction: Only some of the insurable agency’s assets were already insured due to limited funds.
observations.reason: Per Statement of Financial Performance, no Insurance Expense was recorded for the year ended December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to submit copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports on procured items to the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to comply with the provision of Paragraph 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and for the Auditor to conduct timely inspection of items delivered.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: No copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports were submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned employees to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports pursuant to Section 100 & 107 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were required to coordinate so that all the respective vouchers, official receipt and financial report will be submitted on time.
observations.reason: Required documents were not submitted on time, causing delay in audit. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the unreconciled difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management make an immediate review and conduct tracing of the differences between the two accounts once periodic financial statements are generated so as to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: Per Consolidated Financial Statements, there is still an unreconciled difference between the reciprocal accounts. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to establish existence of the breeding stocks by conducting physical inventory taking. Require the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAO) to maintain individual record of items to match/reconcile with the report of the inventory committee and the LGUs books.  Likewise, the Accounting Unit maintain ledger cards for Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: The agency set up an inventory committee to conduct physical inventory.
observations.reason:
Management failed to conduct physical inventory despite having an inventory committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Inventory Committee to establish existence of the breeding stocks by conducting physical inventory taking. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the bank statements, list of all bank and book reconciling items, paid and cancelled checks in order to facilitate verification of the submitted BRS, to validate the accuracy of the recorded Cash in Bank balances. In addition, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant explain the variance between the Cash in Bank balances per Trial Balances and per submitted BRS. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, observe Paragraph 3.5 of COA Circular No.96-011 dated October 2, 1996, to render reliability on the reported Cash in Bank balances per submitted financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will submit Bank Reconciliation Statement together with the Bank Statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate liquidation of cash advances through demand letters signed by him or authorized representative.  Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code states in unequivocal terms that the failure of a public officer to have duly forthcoming any public funds or property with which he is chargeable upon demand by any duly authorized officer shall be prima facie evidence that he has put such missing funds or property to personal use.

We also recommend that the concerned officials adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002 especially the withholding of salaries of those who refuse to settle their accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The management had stopped the granting of additional cash advances for travel for employees with existing unliquidated advances.
observations.reason: This practice is still being done by management, thus accumulating a significant unliquidated cash advance balances.

observations.recommendation: In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall not be released and shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for the other priority programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management is still questioning the bank if the fund’s purpose was actually PDAF or not.
observations.reason: The unutilized amount still remains in the books.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectable for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording.

Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the debit to RPT/SET receivable and credit to Deferred RPT/SET income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectable and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET receivables.

observations.recommendation: Exercise fully the taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to 150 of R. A. 7160 in order to earn more revenue necessary to augment the funding for the operation of the municipality.  Otherwise, implement austerity measures to reduce operating expenses and utilize internal revenue fund for developmental projects that would be beneficial to the constituents.

observations.reason: Tax revenue decreased as shown in the comparative Consolidated Statement of Financial Performance for 2018 and 2017.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concern elected officials to demand the issuance of an Official Receipt of the Republic of the Philippines for every payment of League assistance, contributions or any other kind of disbursement related thereto.

Strict observance of the Section 515 and 510 especially pertaining to the keeping of league fund and disbursement as provided.
observations.managementAction: Every disbursements made were supported with Official Receipts.
observations.reason: Provided receipts were not the Official Receipt of the Republic of the Philippines.

observations.managementAction: The management already reduced operating expenses by reducing staff from market operations and intensifying collection for market fees.
observations.reason: According to the municipal mayor, there is no other way of increasing market collections due to the geographic location of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the requisites on the hiring of a legal consultant and shall undertake the following: a) Secure written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General as well as the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit before the hiring of legal consultant.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books;
Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the reason why these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action thereon; and

observations.reason: Request/apply for the write-off of the accounts not yet done.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment;

Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties; and

Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.
observations.managementAction: As reiterated in this year's Annual Audit Report, the management is amenable to the recommendation provided by the Audit Team to hire or augment personnel to take charge of the physical inventory count and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower since only one (1) personnel was tasked to conduct the physical count and prepare the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:

• Create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE. To ensure the success of the committee, all department heads are advised to instruct their members to cooperate in the conduct of physical count;

• The inventory committee shall prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation with PPE Ledger Cards and the Property Cards to be maintained by the Accounting and Property Offices, respectively;
• Proper disposal of unserviceable property should be made.

The following provisions of Sec. 490 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies, materials and equipment. — Physical stock-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. In all cases, the physical inventory-taking which is required semi-annually or annually should be regarded with importance.

For effective control of government property, the COA prescribes the following regulations:

a. Chiefs of agencies are required to take a physical inventory of all the equipment and supplies belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, unless otherwise determined by the COA Chairman in specific cases. Such inventory shall be made as of December 31 on General Form 41 (A) (Appendix 12) and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, unless extended by the Chairman, Commission on Audit, upon prior request of the chief of agency concerned. When the exigencies of the service permit, the taking of inventory should be in the charge of a committee of two or more employees designated by the chief of agency, including the property officer or custodian, depending on the extent of property accountability in a particular agency, to be witnessed by a representative of the Agency Auditor.

Xxx”
b. The required annual/semi-annual inventory shall be certified correct by the committee in charge thereof, attested by the representative of the auditor concerned and approved by the chief of agency in interest.”
observations.managementAction: As reiterated in this year's Annual Audit Report, the management is amenable to the recommendation provided by the Audit Team to hire or augment personnel to take charge of the physical inventory count and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE in the ensuing year.
observations.reason:
Lack of manpower since only one (1) personnel was tasked to conduct the physical count and prepare the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation:
The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUPs) for non-functional or our-of-order properties should be prepared and submitted to COA, and for the management to recommend for inspection so that the disposal of the same may be done in accordance with prescribed procedures under Sec. 79 of PD 1445 and the NGAS Manual and existing auditing rules and regulations.

The concerned Municipal Officers should submit to COA a written explanation for non-compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386, the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government.
observations.managementAction: As reiterated in this year's Annual Audit Report, the management is amenable to the recommendation provided by the Audit Team to hire or augment personnel to take charge of the physical inventory count and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to demand the collection of the P471,556.17 Loans Receivable-Others from its borrowers and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like.
observations.reason: Difficulty in collecting from debtors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to evaluate the collectability of the Loans Receivable-Others and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office its recommendation. If warranted, request for write-off of certain accounts in accordance with the COA guidelines.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office set-up the related impairment loss on long outstanding Loans Receivable – Others based on the data provided by the PESO.
observations.reason: The municipality is still exerting efforts to collect.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking reports and henceforth, observe the submission within the prescribed period of the Report of Collections and Deposits, Disbursement Vouchers, Liquidation Reports, Journal Entry Vouchers and general journal transactions to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in accounting department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:

To ensure efficiency and effectiveness of the solid waste management program and exert reasonable efforts expected of a public official for the implementation and maintenance of existing systems pursuant to established solid waste management plans and programs.

The conversion of the municipality’s new unutilized dumpsite into an Eco-Park with Residual Containment Area (RCA) or sanitary landfill facility pursuant to Section 37 of RA 9003.
observations.reason: Lack of funds to implement the project.
Not yet reverted because they are still contemplating to continue the project by administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer:

To explain and refrain the charging of expenditures to LDRRM Fund which are not related to disaster risk management activities; and,

To strictly adhere to the guidelines on the utilization of LDRRM Fund provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.
observations.reason: Reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality ensure the provision for a separate allocation of 1% of the IRA for the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) separate from the municipality’s allocation for LCPC under the social services sector as one of the one of the statutory and mandatory obligation prescribed under Republic Act 9344.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that all projects pertaining to 20% Development Fund in the AIP are included in the Annual Budget of the Municipality and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation/completion of projects from prior years that have unobligated allotments as of December 31, 2017.

Further, we recommend that the Local Development Committee should optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through:  

proper planning of project including timing of execution; 
coordinated action from LGU officials for better result and 
strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.

We recommend that the Local School Board carefully evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the specific programs/projects/activities set forth under Section 1 of R.A No. 5447, Sections 100 (c) and 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and the latest issued Guidelines on the use of the SEF under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. Otherwise, expenditures not falling within those allowed under the said laws and rules shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Improper charges to SEF made during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to ensure compliance to Section 262 of GAAM, Section 4 (2) of PD 1445, and Section 335 of RA 7190 by stopping the practice of accommodating payroll deductions for personal loans of its employees acquired from private banks and other lending institutions.
observations.reason: With MOA entered by LGU that they must abide.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should exert additional efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government within the prescribed period and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty imposition except if waived by concerned parties.
observations.reason: No proper monitoring

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, take immediate action to resolve its waste management transition from utilizing its old dump to opening a new and operational one that conforms with the requirements of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to prevent the accumulation of garbage in the barangay waste yards.
observations.reason: Restriction on budget

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that a copy of the report of inspection or its equivalent shall be submitted to the Head of the Auditing Unit within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered and, in the case of unserviceable and disposable property/assets, immediately after inspection thereof by management.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipality assign the inspection of the procured items to personnel not under the control or supervision of the supply/property officer of the Municipality who is entrusted to accept the items delivered.
observations.reason: Management still failed to submit the AIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should provide a written explanation on what caused such delays or if there were time extensions granted and to exert extra efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty deduction.
observations.reason: Delays were still observed in the implementation of projects funded by national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee thru the BAC Secretariat comply with the requirement on the submission of perfected contracts and/or purchase orders together with all supporting documents within five (5) days after approval to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to submit the contracts/ purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof within 5 days from approval thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management immediately use the amount deposited in the HYSA to pay off portion of the principal amount of the loan to economically manage its resources.
observations.reason: HYSA still exists in the accounts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that legal processes be availed of to recover the amount of P196, 585, 74 from the supplier JVC, which amount was erroneously charged against the funds of the municipality. Also, require the personnel responsible for the omission to submit written justification why he/she may not be held personally responsible therefore.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor:

1.9.1 Require the Municipal Accountant – Designate and Treasurer to
submit the remaining DVs and all other reports/documents
enumerated in item 1.6 as soon as possible;
1.9.2 Determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to
comply with the required submission dates of accounts and reports;
1.9.3 Hire a qualified Municipal Accountant or assign additional
personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate prompt
submission of accounts and reports; and
1.9.4 Ensure that the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Acting
Municipal Treasurer exert all necessary efforts to render the
agency’s accounts within the prescribed period.

Management’s comment:

2.11 Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

4.10 We have recommended that Management undertake the following actions:
4.10.1 Require concerned officials to submit the required liquidation
documents through the issuance of demand letters;
4.10.2 Upon submission of the required documents by concerned officials,
the Municipal Accountant – Designate should properly review the
documents and take up the necessary journal entries in the books of
accounts; and
4.10.3 If appropriate, necessitate the refund of unliquidated cash advances.

4.11 We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate
review the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider
the proposed accounting entry as follows to reflect the correct balances of
the accounts.

Advances for Payroll 31,000.00
Government Equity 31,000.00

4.12 We have further recommended that, henceforth, Management limit the
grant of cash advances only to specific legal purposes and the Municipal
Accountant – Designate to strictly implement the policy that no additional
cash advances granted to officials and employees in the event that they fail
to submit all the liquidations on time.

6.5 We have recommended that the Accounting Unit:
6.5.1 Prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers and follow the rules on
accounting for Receivables and Liabilities as prescribed in the
Manual on NGAS for LGUs; and
6.5.2 Identify items for reconciliation, adjustment or those for further
action pertaining to long outstanding receivables and liabilities.

7.12 We have recommended that Management submit: (1) the RIS and SSMI,
listing of recipients/beneficiaries properly signed acknowledging receipt of
items and quantity of items distributed, and the ICS for the JEVs related to
the issuance of supplies and materials in CY 2018; (2) the unsubmitted
disbursement vouchers noted; and (3) the supporting documents to the
related disbursement vouchers noted.

7.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate review
the journal entries made for the above transactions, and to consider the
proposed accounting entry as follows to reflect the correct balances of the
accounts.

ICT Equipment 74,468.09
Government Equity 74,468.09

7.14 We have further recommended that the:
7.14.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and semi-annual inventory taking, and prepare and submit
the RPCI to the COA Office for verification;
7.14.2 Management invite through letter a representative of the
Commission on Audit to witness the inventory taking;
7.14.3 The Accounting Office and the Property Unit maintain and update
Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Cards, respectively, for each
inventory item to promote effective monitoring and controls over
Inventory; and
7.14.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian reconcile
with the RPCI and verify variances for appropriate action and
subsequent adjustments as necessary.

8.7 We have recommended that the:
8.7.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an
actual and yearly inventory taking, and prepare and submit the
RPCPPE to COA Office for verification and reconciliation with the
complete Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC)
and the Property Cards maintained by the Accounting Office and
Property Unit, respectively;
8.7.2 Management invite through writing a representative from the
Commission on Audit to witness the inventory taking;
8.7.3 The Accounting Office and Property Unit maintain and update
PPELC, Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) and
Property Cards, respectively, for each inventory item to promote

8.7.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian reconcile

subsequent adjustments as necessary; and
8.7.5 The Municipal Accountant trace back those PPE noted to not have
been depreciated, compute for the supposed depreciation to date and
prepare adjusting as necessary.
