We recommended that Management submit the year-end Financial Statements, trial balances and other financial reports within sixty (60) days after the 31st of December each year.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement and submit the same to COA for audit purposes; and
Maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account for easy preparation of the monthly BRS.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof and to perform the same regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume 1, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Collecting Officers/Municipal Treasurer should strictly observe the above mentioned provision of DOF-BLGF Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) to protect the funds of the municipality. Henceforth, collections should be diligently turned-over to the Municipal Treasurer daily.

We recommended that management:
Comply strictly with the fidelity bond requirement by renewing the fidelity bond of the Municipal Treasurer and all the accountable officers.
Monitor the validity period of approved applications for bond to ensure that all active accountable officers are properly bonded in accordance with law.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submits all the documents or returns the unused cash to fully liquidate his cash advances at the end of the year to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that management (a) trace-back all available records pertaining to the cancelled project ; (b) demand the immediate refund of the remaining unrecouped advances from the contractor; and (c) exert all effort to recoup advance payments to prevent loss of government fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office record all purchases to its corresponding inventory account classification and to adjust the account for whatever consumption on the basis of Summary of Supplies and Material Issued (SSMI), duly supported with the original copy of Requisition Issue Slip (RIS), which should be prepared and submitted weekly or monthly to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management:
create/designate an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties belonging to the Municipality and prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with accounting records;
instruct the designated Property Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of Property, Plant and Equipment that contain the historical record of each item of PPE, from acquisition, custody, disposal and other information about PPE; and
require the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of each class of PPE

We recommended that management:
require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the implementing department (e.g. Engineering Office) to identify, determine and reconcile those projects that were already completed and close the same to the appropriate PPE account. Subsequently, prepare entries to transfer the PPE accounts amounting to P69,702,764.40  to the General Fund book to close the Government Equity account in the Trust Fund book.
require the Engineering Office to provide the Accounting Office with copies of a comprehensive report of completed projects and other necessary documents; and
based on the adjusted data, require the Municipal Accountant to compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute a disposal committee to initiate the proper disposal of the above unserviceable PPE pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agriculture Officer to confirm the existence of the biological assets and thereafter prepare an adjusting entry, if any, to derecognize the recorded balance of the biological assets.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are adjusted on timely basis.
Perform constant review of transactions to deter error and omissions in recording.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts or effect the necessary adjustments. Being reciprocal accounts, it should end up with the same balance.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to properly coordinate to have all expenditures obligated and recorded in the year they were incurred. Unpaid obligations/expenses incurred during the year should be recorded as Accounts Payable in order to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statement at year end.

We recommended that management purchase common-use supplies from DBM-PS as provided by the afore-quoted provisions. However, if it not available, then first secure Certification of Non-Availability of Stocks before proceeding to outside sources.

We recommended that Management:
explain why the procurement of common-use supplies were made piecemeal or in series although the same can be procured from PS-DBM or can be conducted in a single procurement activity thru public bidding.
strictly comply with provisions set forth in the R.A. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
refrain from resorting to alternative methods of procurement of good by splitting the purchase orders and/or contracts. Procure common-use supplies equivalent to quarterly requirements based on Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and any additional needs should be amendments in the APP.

We recommended that Management:
explain why the procurement of three (3) units of air-condition were made in piecemeal or in series although the same can be conducted in a single procurement activity thru public bidding.
strictly comply with provisions set forth in the R.A. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that all financial assistance given to barangays should be directly paid and under the name of the requesting barangay and should be supported by an AF 51 for proper accounting and auditing of the donations made.

We recommended that Management:
Control and evaluate the use of fuel and ensure that the usage are only for official purposes before approving the request for gasoline and oil.
Require the driver of each motor vehicle to prepare at the end of the month, a Monthly Report of Official Travels, summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month.

We recommended that Management:
Require the driver/employee concerned to subject the motor vehicles/heavy equipment to pre-repair inspection before requisition for repair and post-repair inspection by designated LGU inspector to ascertain whether the desired repairs have been actually carried out.
Prepare Waste Material Report after every repair/replacement and to turn-over to designated Property Officer all waste material even such materials are considered scrap items.
Require the designated property officer to maintain Equipment Ledger Cards indicating the history of repair for each motor vehicle/heavyequipment owned by the Municipality.
Require the Municipal Accountant to only process payments for repairs with complete supporting documents to ensure that government funds are for proper and valid claims.

We recommended that Management:
protect and conserve the municipality’s funds and resources by exercising prudence in utilization thereof.
Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-003 to avoid further disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately the remaining balance of unutilized PDAF balances to the National Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision.

We recommended that the agency submit the lacking supporting documents for the progress/final payments of the infrastructure projects in the Trust Funds to avoid suspension of the said disbursements.
Also, in the future, inorder to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit,  we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims of  progress billing/final payment unless  all the required supporting documents are present and attached to the disbursement vouchers; and
comply strictly to Sections 9.1.1.1 to 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated  June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular in the Project Implementation  of the Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities, for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular in the Project Reporting  of the Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Municipalities for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that management require the BAC to observe Sections 20.1 and 20.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and with all the applicable guidelines and regulations related to procurement in order to promote transparency and accountability.
Finally, we recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the processing of payment to contractor without the BAC conducting the pre-procurement conference for the procurement of infrastructure projects with an Approved Budget for the Contract of more than Five Million Pesos (P5,000,000.00).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
the BAC to post in PhilGEPS the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through public bidding to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement procedures.
the BAC to explain the failure to post in PhilGEPS the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed despite being a requirement of RA 9184.
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the printout copy of posting of the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS were included among the supporting documents for the payment of the claim for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through public bidding.

We recommended that the municipality remit the unutilized fund relating to LGSF – ADM amounting to P124,691.46 to the Bureau of Treasury. Also, we recommended the agency to not use any unutilized fund contrary to the provisions of any related guidelines of the fund entrusted to the agency and the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Municipality open a separate depository account with the Local Branch of the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) for the Supplemental Feeding and Senior Citizen Social Pension Programs as required in the Omnibus Guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 117 in utilization and implementation of the Local Government Support Fund – Financial Assistance for transparency and accountability.

We recommended management to explain the one year delay on the implementation of CBMS activities as approved in the agency’s activity design of implementation.
Also, we recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-69 dated May 23, 2016, also known as the Guidelines for the Implementation of Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that management:
Submit explanation on the reason of delay/non-implementation of the above unimplemented PPAs; and
Facilitate the immediate implementation of the programmed PPAs funded from the 20% Development Fund so that the desired services and benefits of these PPA be delivered to the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that the utilization of 20% Development Fund must be for capital expenditure and/or in the nature of investments; and
Strict adherence with the DILG and DBM joint Circular No. 2017-1 in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to be funded out of the 20% EDF to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities which are compliant with the guidelines.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to transfer the unutilized balance of LDRRMF to Trust Liability –DRRMF account under the Trust Fund account; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the unutilized fund to the LDRRMF bank accounts.

We recommended that the MDRRMO fully implements the planned programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the DRRM Funds would be maximized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.

We recommended that the municipality should disburse the GAD Fund in pursuance to its appropriation and utilize it in accordance with the DILG-endorsed GAD Plan and Budget. Moreover, the accountable officers responsible in the disposition of government funds shall be cautious in discharging their duties and responsibilities.
In addition, submit also an explanation why the said expenditures were disbursed even without the certification as to existence of appropriation by the Municipal Budget Officer and why should it not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the municipality utilize the GAD Budget in accordance with the DILG-endorsed GAD Plan and refrain from charging to the GAD Fund the aforementioned not GAD related activities in order to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommended that Management:
Submit explanation on the non-implementation and partial implementation of the above enumerated projects
Submit explanation on the reason of declaring the appropriations of the enumerated unimplemented and partial implemented PPAs as savings and available for augmentation.
Strictly comply with the afore-cited provisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that all disbursements made are aligned with the specific purpose for which they have been appropriated.
Require the focal person to submit explanation why the Day Care Service Annual Program Implementation and Year-End Review was held at Baguio City when it can be done within the office premises.
Require the focal person to submit explanation why the disbursement for Bambanti Festival was charged to LCPC Fund and why it should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of the persons liable as indicated in COA Order of Execution (COE) for the settlement of their liabilities
Enforce the full settlement of the above-listed disallowances by the persons liable; and
Ensure compliance with COA rules on settlement of accounts.

We recommended that the municipality should utilize the SEF Budget in accordance to the approved Local School Board Plan and to fully implement all its priority programs/projects stated. Adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to record as inventory all purchases of supplies and materials except those purchased through petty cash fund regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period in order to prepare an accurate and correct information in the financial statements; (b) Municpal Treasurer to (i) conduct physical count of inventories and submit the required RPCI to the Auditor’s Office not later that July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, as mandated under Section 124, of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I for LGUs; (ii) prepare SSMI supported by consolidated RIS and submit the same to OIC-Municipal Accountant for preparation of JEV to record appropriate expense accounts.

We  recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by documents to establish  the validity and propriety of claims in compliance to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to use appropriate account corresponding to every government transactions; (c) Municipal Treasurer draw cash advance and use Petty Cash Voucher for payment of cash assistance to proposed beneficiaries; and (d) MSWDO require the proponents to submit the required documents enumerated  in Section 5 of DWSD MC No. 04 Series of 2015 to support the evaluation and assessment made.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET receivables for the year; (ii) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that maybe  implemented by Management; and (iii) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the municipality; and (b) direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) observe the provision under Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accountable Officer to (i) return or produce immediately the missing funds amounting to ₱1,244,486.57; and (ii) submit a written explanation on why he failed to produce/account the missing funds; and (iii) file/institute the necessary administrative and/or criminal proceeding against the Accountable Officer pursuant to Articles 171 and 217 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) instruct the collectors to use/issue only the Accountable Forms under their accountability; (b) verify whether the official receipts included in the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) submitted by the collectors are part of their accountability; and (c) make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) strictly enforce the daily turn-over of collections by the collectors and likewise deposit intact all collections daily or in the next banking day; and (ii) designate a responsible Collecting Officer and ensure close supervision to avoid occurrence of risks of loss of public funds; (b) direct the Municipal Treasurer and the assigned Liquidating Officer to diligently verify the Accountable Officers’ RCDs and Statement of Accountable Forms and conduct physical count and inspection of Official Receipts under their custodies; and (c) designate an Accountable Officer for each type of transaction in the Treasury Department to avoid mishandling of government funds.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the MSWDO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly adhere to the fundamental principles in the disbursement of funds prescribed under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; (b) observe full compliance with the requirements consistent with the guidelines of the DSWD in the implementation of Cash for Work for Climate Change  Adaption and Mitigation Program; and (c) facilitate the immediate submission of lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor such as the cash for work vouchers of the respective claimants/beneficiaries, logbook certified by the assigned person-in-charge/Team Assigned Worker, Certificate of Indigency and Residency duly certified by the respective Punong Barangay and validated accomplishment reports pertaining to the program.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Administrator to (i) submit documents or any proof that will show where the 1,028 pieces of tarpaulin were used; and (ii) submit written explanation why the purpose and information to be printed in the tarpaulin were not indicated in the Purchase Request; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of information and supporting documents before certifying or signing box A of the Disbursement.
Management Comment:
The Local Chief Executive instructed all department to minimize the request and use of tarpaulins.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to (a) only include projects under the 20% Development Fund which are in conformity with the guidelines in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and (b) refrain from charging expenditures that are not aligned in the fund’s intended purpose.

We recommended that management revised its Local Revenue Code to generate more revenues in order to strengthen the cash position of the Municipality and to afford for more development projects for its constituents.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer informed the Audit Team that the Revision of the Local Revenue Code is on finalization stage for endorsement to the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to conduct examination of books, accounts and pertinent records of registered businesses operating within its jurisdiction to ensure that correct amount of business tax is collected; (b) BPLO (i) to be prudent in assessing the declared gross sales to compute for the correct amount of tax due; and (ii) require submission of sworn statement of gross receipts or sales and copy of Certified Income Tax Return as required in the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality; and (c) BPLO and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to cause recovery of the under collections of ₱77,715.00 arising from undeclared gross receipts/sales of the six (6) sampled business establishments.
Management Comment:
The Local Chief Executive instructed the Municipal Treasurer to assist in the endorsement of the Local Revenue Code and formulate strategies to intensify local taxes collections.

We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board: (a) ensure compliance with the limitations provided in Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160; RA No. 5447; DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 2017 and its implementing regulations.
Management Comment:
The Management will discuss the issues with the Local School Board to ensure compliance with the limitations provided in Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160; RA No. 5447; DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 2017 and its implementing regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the ICO-MENRO (a) continue to embark effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s Ecological Solid Waste Management Program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment; (b) intensify the information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with barangay officials; (c) accelerate efforts in the enforcement of “no segregation, no collection” and “no littering” policies; (d) take appropriate actions to expedite necessary restoration of the MRF of Barangay Alua, Mangga, Calaba, Pulo and San Roque; and (e) pursue the establishment of MRFs in Barangays Tabon and Sto. Cristo to avoid illegal dumping of assorted garbage along barangay roads, public market, drainage, canals and waterways.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the MDRRM Officer to (a) comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121, the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, and COA Circular 2012-002; (b) ensure that the standards set forth in the NDRRMC Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards, Volume 2 was complied with to be considered fully disaster-prepared municipality; and (c) refrain from charging expenditures which are not aligned with the fund’s intended purpose.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure/monitor the posting of signboards containing relevant information of the PPAs of the Municipality in either of the forms stated in Section 2.2.1 to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to procure all common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM.
Likewise, the Municipal Government should continuously submit yearly to the DBM, its Annual Procurement Plan for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment to assist the Procurement Service in strengthening its service in delivery and expanding its product list.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Local DRRM Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012–002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere with the provision of Section 5.5 of JMC 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA regarding submission of Accomplishment Report not later than end of January of the ensuing year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with provision of Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996,  requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection and Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries to validate if the same conform with the quality and quantity asked for.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the BAC Chairman to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
The Local Chief Executive instructed the BAC Chairman to comply with the necessary documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and record OBR for all procurements based on the Purchase Request and prior to issuance of PO. If the amount paid differs from that stated in the OBR, the difference should form part of the unobligated allotment and the amount of recorded obligations should be adjusted in the RAAOs accordingly.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance to Section 6 of Revenue Regulation No. 10-93 to avoid sanctions as stated in Section 272 of Republic Act No. 8424 otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to  cause the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher for taxes withheld but not yet remitted to the BIR as of December 31, 2018.
We recommended and management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the GSIS.
We recommended and management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the Pag-IBIG.
We recommended and management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer fully implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries of the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities.

We recommended and management agreed that that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notice of Suspensions, to prevent maturing into disallowances.

We recommend Management the following:
Immediately liquidate/settle all outstanding cash advances.
Withhold the salaries of the accountable officers and employees who failed to promptly liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
The municipal accountant must comply to COA Circular 97-002 and 2012-001 and issue a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.
Refrain granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled cash advances.

We recommend management the immediate submission of the liquidation report to the Office of the Chairman, COA, Quezon City and furnish to COA Audit Team the transmittal letter of the said liquidation report duly stamped/received by the said Office as proof of compliance of the required report.

We recommend the municipality and all concerned personnel to observe and comply the requirements and procedures as established in the GAM Volume I and COA Circular 2012-003. We recommend that strict adherence to the rules and procedures concerning the processing, releasing, and recording of cash advances be observed by the Treasurer and Accountant. Further, we recommend the municipality to submit an explanation on the above mentioned deficiencies/irregularities to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.

We recommend to management the creation of an inventory committee to conduct the actual physical count of all the municipality’s PPEs as well as assess its actual condition to establish reliability of reported amount. Further, require the committee/s to prepare the inventory report and submit to the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 124 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units.

It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due From Other Funds and Due To Other Funds and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to promptly prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the Cash in Bank account of the municipality and submit the same with the bank statements, paid checks and debit/credit memos.
It is further recommended that adjusting journal entries must be prepared on the month following the preparation of the BRS in order to correct the book balances and avoid the accumulation of substantial reconciling items.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer investigate and examine why negative balances pertaining to the accounts enumerated above occurred and effect the necessary adjustments or impose appropriate measures to correct them. Likewise, Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) has to be maintained as basis in recording in the GL.

We recommend that management strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports. Furthermore, we recommend the management to have an accountant in the most immediate time in order to efficiently and effectively record transactions, correct account balances and submit reports promptly to this office.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and submit subsidiary ledgers that show the complete details and breakdown of the account balances reflected in the financial statements. This also facilitates the financial audit review conducted by the Commission on Audit.

We recommend that management require the recipient barangays to liquidate the funds. Henceforth, observe COA Circular Nos.2012-001 and 2015-009 on the documentation and recording requirements on fund transfers.

We recommend that Management:
Intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, objectives, laws, regulations and the utilization of the GAD Budget.
Consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address issues concerning women’s human rights and economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommend the municipality to properly formulate the projects to be included in the appropriation under the 20% development fund and to ensure that the projects fall under the provisions enumerated in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1. It is to be noted that the projects must address the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommend the management to closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre-disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained.

We recommend the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents as noted above and to submit an explanation on the deficiencies enumerated on the remarks indicated. We recommend that the municipality observe and comply strictly with the documentary requirements as required by COA Circular 2012-001 and P.D. No. 1445 and submit them to this Office to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances of the said transactions.

We recommend that:
The Municipal Treasurer must strictly adhere to the above-mentioned policies and not to release/issue check without a duly approved disbursement voucher because doing so renders the transaction irregular in nature and without basis.
The Municipal Mayor must not approve the payment and sign on the check without the supporting documents necessary for its validity.

We recommend that the:
Municipal Accountant certify on the completeness of the supporting documents to the payrolls as enumerated in the above-mentioned Circular, including the verified/accepted accomplishment reports.
Municipal Treasurer prepare, issue and release check based on a duly approved DV for cash advance corresponding to the net amount of payrolls.

We recommend that management discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱5,000 take home pay per month for them to meet their needs and maintain self-respect and dignity as employees of the government, in pursuance of Section 48 of the 2018 GAA.
Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and regulate/control loan availment by not signing certifications for application of loans should an employee net pay fall below ₱5,000.00.

We recommend that the Municipality submit a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products procured fall within and conformed with PNDF, Volume I, current edition, pursuant to the above mentioned EO and AO otherwise the transaction will be suspended/disallowed in audit.
Henceforth, all procurement of drugs and medicines need to be accompanied with this certification.

We recommend that the:
Municipal driver/s comply with Section 361 (e) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and pertinent section of COA Circular No.75-6 in accomplishing the Driver’s Trip Ticket in order to properly monitor and control gasoline consumption.
Municipal Accountant certify on the completeness of the supporting documents to the purchase of fuel as enumerated in the above-mentioned regulations, particularly the Driver’s Trip Ticket.
Municipal Treasurer issue/release check only upon a duly approved DV with the certification from the Municipal Accountant of the completeness of its supporting documents.
We recommend that the management assign immediately to another personnel the liquidating or accounting function, since these activities being not compatible.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory
Committee who will conduct an actual inspection and listing of all road lots
owned by LGU San Isidro and require the said Committee to prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU.
2.10 We also recommended that:
A. The Municipal Engineer:
a. Assess immediately the local roads implemented in prior years and
submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for proper
adjustment on the value of road lots in the books;
b. Conduct an assessment every end of the year or whenever there is
any indication that a component of the local road may be impaired;
and
c. Submit a report to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO of any
impairment, removal and repairs on any of the road components of
the LGU.
B. The Municipal General Services Officer:
a. Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and its components and update the same based on the report of
the Municipal Engineer on any repairs or removal done.
b. Complete the reconciliation of his records with that of the Municipal
Accountant and the Inventory Committee.
C. The Municipal Accountant:
a. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network of the LGU;
b. Reconcile the book balance with the Local Road Inventory Report,
and;
c. Compute the depreciated replacement cost of each local road
projects implemented by the LGU based on the Appraisal Report of
the Municipal Engineer.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the journal
entries to record in the appropriate accounts the Project on the
Rehabilitation/Improvement of Village-Type Corn Dryer Shed and
Construction of Canal at Brgy. Crossing. Also, ensure that counter-
checking and review is maintained in the keeping of accounts to ensure that
errors of one can be detected and corrected by another before accounting
reports are finalized.

4.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) prepare the
appropriate journal entries to reverse the accounts payable of P159,546.04
from the books of the LGU, and; (2) record as payables of the LGU only if
the claims are duly supported with complete and approved necessary
documents on delivered goods and rendered services; (3) maintain a
subsidiary ledger for the payable account to identify the composition of the
account.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist: (1) provide technical
assistance to the six farmers associations; (2) evaluate the necessity of the
interventions on livestock raising; (3) monitor closely through site validation
and inspection; (4) render a monthly report to the Municipal Mayor and DA-RFO8 on these activities with appropriate recommendations on
violations of the recipients.

5.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Presidents of
the six Farmers Association to submit monthly to the LGU updates and
progress reports of their respective programs. Impose sanctions to the
Farmers Association concerned on violations of the MOA provisions.

6.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in behalf of Daja Daku
Integrated Farmers Association, make a formal representation to request
assistance from the Regional Corn Program Management Unit and the
Regional Agriculture Engineering Division of DARFO8 for the following:
a. Installation of the electrical transformer in order to have the Corn Mill
operational;
b. Training of members of the Association on the operation and
maintenance of the unit, and;
c. Conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation to ensure that the goals and
objectives of the program are achieved.

6.11 We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor: (1) require the President of
Daja Daku Integrated Farmers Association to sign a Memorandum of
Agreement with LGU San Isidro, Leyte to formalize the responsibilities of
both parties, including a provision to provide a secure, sheltered area to
protect the equipment from adverse conditions and utilize the equipment as
intended; (2) provide a proper garage for the Hauling Truck, and; (3) obtain
a comprehensive insurance for all the equipment.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Agriculturist
and the BAC start the procurement process of all programs funded by the
National Government Agencies immediately upon receipt of the funds and
implement the intended programs within the timeframe of the MOA to
achieve the objectives of the program on the desired period.

8.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, and members of the Local
Development Council, include only in the AIP and utilize the 20% DF, for
socio-economic and environmental development projects which are capital
in nature to avoid audit disallowances.

8.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement all identified
priority projects in the AIP during the year to maximize the utilization of
the 20% Development Fund.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRMO and
the concerned implementing offices, cause the implementation of the stated
unimplemented projects which can contribute to the strengthening of the
capacity of the LGU for disaster risk reduction management.

10.8 We recommend that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC), through the BAC Secretariat, post all Notices of Awards, Notices to
Proceed and Approved Contracts in the PhilGEPS and LGU website in
addition to the postings in at least three conspicuous places reserved for the
purpose.

11.6 We recommend that the GAD Focal Point and its members: (1) submit to
the Audit Team the GAD Plan and Budget within five working days from
receipt of the approved plan from the DILG, and; (2) prepare and submit to
the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days
from the end of January of the preceding year to avoid sanctions for non-
compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to intensify collection to recover government funds invested in the program and to institute appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

2.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and require to:
Liquidate immediately the outstanding cash advances by compelling the employees to submit liquidation papers and refund the unused cash advances, if any;
Monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; and
Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advance to employees who have unliquidated balances.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to take up the procurement of all items in the appropriate inventory accounts under the perpetual inventory method, and maintain an updated ledger cards to keep an eye on the inflows and outflows of inventories.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to constitute the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all PPE and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.

We recommended for the strict observance of limitations on the use of the Special Education Fund as provided for in Section 4 of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the utilization of SEF.

We recommended that the three (3) DRRM position be filled immediately to begin the institutionalization of the organization of LDDRMO for better operation and implementation of LDRRM.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned department heads to optimally utilize the LDRRMF in order to uphold the rights of the people to life and properties through preparing for disasters, mitigating its effect, post-disaster response and recovery measures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prudently monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipal Government.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive:
Require the GAD Focal Person and Committee to attend trainings to heighten their knowledge on gender audit and analysis to generate gender responsive GPB;
Recognize gender issues within the mandate of the LGU and incorporate in the GPB the causes of the gender issues; and
Assess the impact of GAD activities and policies in relation to gender equality and monitor the GPB implementation.

Supplementary Feeding Program (SFP) –
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MSWDO, Municipal Accountant, BAC and other concerned to conform with the implementing procedures and scheme provided by the DSWD and SIA in the execution of the supplemental feeding program to ensure full attainment of the program’s objective. Further, to submit the required documents for the disbursements to ascertain propriety and validity of the claims.

Social Pension for Indigent Senior Citizens –
We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the:
MSWDO to cause the immediate submission of the required documents as prescribed under the rules and procedures of the DSWD Guidelines to guarantee attainment of the primary purpose of the social protection program; and
Municipal Treasurer for the immediate liquidation of his cash advances and submit complete supporting documents of his liquidation.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld intact to lessen the liability and accountability of the LGU, and prevent the possibility of using the funds for other purposes.

We recommended that management either hire or  augment  personnel  for
the  Inventory  Committee  for  the  purpose  of  conducting  the   physical
inventory count, and prepare the necessary report  on  the results thereof
(RPCPPE).

We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify
the current and non-current portions of the Loans Payable-Domestic with  its
appropriate amount in the financial statements.

We recommended that management employ additional  workers  to  monitor
the projects of the Municipality, and  establish  a  system  for  monitoring
projects in order to formulate alternative  courses  of  actions  to  timely
address the issues/concerns that maybe encountered in the implementation  of
the projects.

We   recommended   to   the   management   to   secure    the    prior
approval/authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan  in  the  implementation  of
projects, programs, and activities which are described in the budget of  the
Municipality in generic terms, and to also, as much as possible,  thoroughly
plan and  propose  only  PPAs  for  implementation  that  are  specific  and
sufficiently detailed.

We  recommended  that  management  adequately  plan  and   study   the
feasibility of each project before proposing it to be  included  as  one  of
the projects to be funded by the 20% Development Fund, and to ensure  proper
coordination among concerned offices. We also recommended  either  employing
additional workforce for the Engineering Office to help  implement  priority
development projects, or to resort to  implement  projects  by  contract  in
case hiring of additional workers is not feasible.

We recommended that the management thoroughly plan and  ensure  proper
coordination of the projects and activities  to  be  implemented  under  the
LDRRMF. In addition, we also recommended the  preparation  of  a  monitoring
report in order to check the status of each project/activity, and  also  for
the management to be able to quickly and carefully plan the measures  to  be
done in case there are difficulties encountered  in  the  implementation  of
the said projects and activities.

We  recommended  that  management  1)   strictly   enforce   the   "No
Segregation, No Collection" Policy on wastes to be disposed;  2)  prioritize
the purchase of additional equipment for garbage  collection  and  operation
of RCA/SLF and; 3) expedite the processing of the necessary  procedures  and
provide adequate funding for the establishment and utilization of the SLF.

We recommended that management adopt the ATM  Payroll  System  in  the
payment of salaries and wages of its employees.

We  recommended  that  management  thoroughly  plan  each  PPA  before
proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by  the  GAD
Fund, as well as ensure that each proposed  PPA  should  be  implemented  on
time. We also recommended  to  direct  the  GAD  Focal  Person  to  strictly
monitor the status of implementation of each approved GAD PPA.

We recommended to  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  a
special account in the General Fund for the  20%  Development  Fund  and  to
prepare corresponding financial statements with complete subsidiary  records
to provide users of financial statements with vital information relative  to
its assets, liabilities, equity, and results of operations.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to trace all mortalities of dispersal animals from inception of the project until CY 2018 and to substantiate all claims of loss through death of said animals with complete documentary attachments upon filing of a request for relief from accountability for the proper disposition thereof pursuant to Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to the audit team.
Likewise, conduct regular inspection of stocks dispersed and physical inventory of existing animals in relation to the respective beneficiaries, in connection with any of such project.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to demand the collection of the P471,556.17 Loans Receivable-Others from its borrowers and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like.
Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like. Management should undertake reasonable efforts to collect this debts owing from the borrowers lest the intention of the program benefits will be defeated.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the revised withholding tax table under Republic Act 10963 or TRAIN Law effective January 1, 2018.
Recommendation: We recommended that the responsible employees abide by the guidelines set by the DBM on the payment of honoraria to those involved in procurement activities.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality source the payment of honoraria to its BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat from those expressly enumerated under DBM BC 2007-3, otherwise, the irregular payments thereof shall be disallowed in audit.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Head of Procuring Entity require the Bids and Awards Committee and other participants on the procurement of goods to strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 dated October 28, 2016 and other issuances related to procurement. Otherwise, any procurement not in accordance with the procurement law shall be disallowed in audit. Likewise, we recommended that the supporting documents attach to the claim be thoroughly checked before approval for payment is made.

Recommendation: We recommended that an annual transfer of excess collections over payments made for the honoraria and overtime pay of the members of the BAC should be made to be recorded as income in the General Fund.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) pertaining to all BuB and other NGA-funded projects of the Municipality for the years 2015 and above and should exert all reasonable efforts and diligence in procuring said contracts/ Memorandum of Agreements in a timely manner from the source agency.

Recommendation: We recommend that management direct Municipal Accountant that financial statements such as Balance Sheet, Statement of Income and Expenses and Cash Flows should be prepared and presented as part of the schedules of the General Fund Trial Balance. Compliance with Section 105 and 106 on provision of adequate information as to assets, liabilities and equity for each special account is enjoined.

Recommendations: We recommended the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation/completion of projects from prior and current years that have unobligated allotments.
Further, we recommended that the Local Development Council should optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through: (a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; (b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result and (c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.

Recommendations: We recommended that the municipality strictly utilize the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) only for disaster risk management activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 so as to ensure that the objectives set forth in the allocation of the LDRRMF are achieved thru proper usage thereof.
Moreover, the accountant is directed to transfer the above charges to the municipality’s general fund otherwise the same will be a subject of disallowance.

Recommendations: We recommended the following courses of action:
1. The Local School Board should ensure that expenditures charged against the SEF include only those among the priority list of expenses to be charged against SEF, thus guaranteeing the welfare of the school children and that SEF are being utilized for the purpose for which it was created; and
2. Stop the practice of utilizing SEF to expenditures of wages and other allowances of non-teaching personnel.

Recommendations: We recommended that management intensify its efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of preserving our natural resources by proper disposal of wastes.
We also recommended that management comply with all the applicable rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and the environment by utilizing the 2.9 hectares lot that the municipality purchased last 2016.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor implementation of awarded projects to ensure that these are completed as scheduled. Ensure that projects are inspected on their scheduled completion dates so that percentage of unfinished work is determined as basis in the computation of liquidated damages to be deducted in the final billing. And comply with the required documentary requirements in granting time extensions.
Moreover, we recommended that the BAC issue a resolution blacklisting the concerned contractor for willful or deliberate abandonment or non-performance of the project or contract by the contractor resulting to substantial breach thereof without lawful and/or just cause pursuant to GPPB Resolution No. 40-2017.

Recommendation: We recommended that coordination between Municipal and San Isidro Fire Station Officials shall be made to demand the release of 20% share in revenues arising from the enforcement of R.A. No. 9514.  Further, require the Fire Station to submit copies of its Monthly Report of Collections and Deposits to the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate the monitoring and accounting of the LGU’s 20% share from the Fire Code Revenues.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit team assigned to the agency within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001.

8. We recommended and Management  agreed  to  instruct  the  Municipal
        Accountant to facilitate the request for write-off  by  religiously
        adhering to the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed by
        the aforementioned circular in order to achieve  fair  presentation
        of the financial statements.


     9.  The  Management  committed   strict   adherence   to   the   audit
        recommendations.

2.15    We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the
        requirements and provisions of existing laws concerning  Accounting
        for Supplies and Materials and:


        a. Designate a Municipal General Services Officer to facilitate the
           maintenance of stock cards  for  supplies  to  account  for  the
           receipt and disposition of the same and prepare Requisition  and
           Issue Slip; Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and  submit
           the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording  purposes  to
           fairly  present  its  inventory  accounts   in   the   financial
           statement;

        b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant  to  prepare  supplies  ledger
           card per stock number;

        c. Review and revitalize the composition of the Inventory Committee
           and fuel them up to religiously conduct the required semi-annual
           physical count of unused/undistributed supplies  and  materials;
           and

        d. Supervise the proper implementation  of  the  inventory  process
           regularly to ensure its effectiveness.

    2.16    The Municipal Treasurer commented and confirmed that they do not
        conduct physical inventory count  but  RIS  are  issued  for  every
        office. The Management assured compliance in the ensuing year.

13. We recommended and Management  agreed  to  instruct  the  Municipal
        Accountant to:


          a. Submit to the resident COA auditor any settlements from  issued
             NSs/NDs/NCs;
          b. Stop  the  practice  of  recognizing  receivable  arising  from
             disallowances/charges  which  have   not   become   final   and
             executory; and
          c. Derecognize the Receivables – Disallowances/Charges Account  in
             the full amount of  P1,475,675.26  in  order  to  achieve  fair
             presentation of its financial statements.


    3.14    The Municipal Accountant commented that the composition  of  the
        total amount is traveling expenses  that  are  old  and  cannot  be
        traced. However, they  committed  strict  adherence  to  the  audit
        recommendations.

9. We recommended and Management  agreed  to  instruct  the  Municipal
        Accountant to maintain a subsidiary  ledger  of  each  account  and
        perform constant review and  appraisal  of  transactions  to  deter
        errors  and  omissions  and  to  reconcile  accounts   and   effect
        adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from Other Funds and
        Due to Other Funds in the Financial Statements.


    10.  The  Municipal  Accountant  commented  that  he  has  started  the
        reconciliation but there is a discrepancy which he cannot trace. He
        will disclose the amount in the Notes to the Financial  Statements.
        The  Management   committed   strict   adherence   to   the   audit
        recommendations.

5.14    We recommended and Management agreed to:


           a. Designate a Municipal General Services Officer;
           b. Instruct the designated Municipal  GSO  to  maintain  property
              cards and issue acknowledgement  receipt  for  equipment  upon
              issuance of the  same  in  addition  to  his  tasks  expressly
              recommended in the previous AOM for Supplies and Materials;
           c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to train one  of  her  staff
              who will be tasked to maintain the required PPE  Ledger  Card;
              and
           d. Reactivate the Inventory Committee and assure  its  commitment
              to religiously exhaust all available resources and conduct the
              required  annual  physical  count  of  plant,   property   and
              equipment duly documented through incorporating in the  Report
              on Physical Count on Property, Plant and Equipment.

    5.15     The  Management  committed  strict  adherence  with  the  audit
        recommendations.

6.10    We recommended and Management agreed to instruct  the  Municipal
        Accountant to present the current portion of its Loans  Payable  in
        the face of the LGU’s financial statements to provide users of  the
        financial statements’ fair presentation as  far  as  liquidity  and
        solvency is concerned.


    6.11     The  Management  committed  strict  adherence  with  the  audit
        recommendation.

8.11    We recommended and Management agreed to  require  the  Municipal
        Treasury Office to adhere to the existing regulation on  collection
        remittances and reporting and to deposit all unremitted collections
        to the depository bank and ensure prompt remittance of  the  amount
        collected in order to safeguard the funds against  risks  of  loss,
        theft and misappropriation and in  order  to  reflect  the  correct
        income and cash balances in the financial statements.


    8.12    The Municipal Treasurer commented that the issue  is  inevitable
        since he is the Liquidating Officer and there are times that he  is
        on travel. But they agreed to require the  Collection  Officers  to
        each have a cashbook so that the accountability will be transferred
        to each one of them and have an alternate  Liquidating  Officer  in
        the absence of the Municipal Treasurer.

9.6     We recommended and Management agreed to  direct  the  Accounting
        Office to make  a  journal  entry  to  restore  the  cash  for  the
        equivalent amount of stale checks and instruct the Treasury  Office
        to send written notices to the payees to confirm existence  of  the
        stale checks.


    9.7     The Municipal Accountant commented that some of  the  checks  in
        the list have already been reissued. They assured to give the COA a
        copy of the list of the reissued  checks.  The  Management  ensured
        compliance in the ensuing year.

We recommended to Management the

and Treasurer to verify whether all
necessary supporting documents are
attached to the disbursement vouchers
before processing the claims, strict

to the respective employee/ personnel
for compliance of the requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE and all agency officials concerned revisit and observe the above mentioned provision of Section 6 of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and Section 33 of  PD No. 1445 to avoid extravagant expenses, save for necessary capital obligations and improve the agency financial position.
observations.managementAction: The austerity measures in disbursing government are now observed by the officials concerned.
