observations.managementAction: Fidelity bonds of all accountable officers were renewed.
observations.reason: Renewed fidelity bonds were submitted by the accountable officers and were verified by the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectable for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording.

Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the debit to RPT/SET receivable and credit to Deferred RPT/SET income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectable and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET receivables.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant has the list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectable for the year from the Municipal Treasurer and Certification from Municipal Assessor.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer has prepared the list of taxpayers and secured certification from the Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit, as soon as possible, its Agency Action Plan & Status of Implementation for the AAR 2017.

It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: Municipality submitted the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI).
observations.reason: AAPSI was submitted on March 4, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre-disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained.
observations.managementAction: Management reviewed the programs to be undertaken under the LDRRM Fund to include only the ones appropriate for its maximum implementation.
observations.reason: Per review of the LDRRMF Fund Utilization report, the Management still included some non-disaster/calamity preparedness related programs in the LDRRMF. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Acting Municipal Accountant to adhere with the required time frame for the submission of the monthly trial balance and schedules. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted incomplete documents without following the required time frame for their submission.
observations.reason: The Management submitted trial balances for two quarters only instead of monthly and were without supporting schedules and records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce all collectors to deposit their collections daily and intact and to monitor the issuances of all accountable forms. We also recommend compliance of Section 30 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual and to adhere strictly to existing rules and regulations on cash management to avoid the occurrence of loss or shortage.
observations.managementAction: The revenue collection clerks were required to remit their collections daily. The Municipal Treasurer was required to monitor closely the remittance of the collections and deposited in the depository bank.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to closely monitor the remittance of the daily collections of the revenue collection clerks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials/employees concerned to strictly comply on the prompt submission of copies of contracts, P.O.s and other similar contracts of understanding to the Auditor within five (5) days from its perfection pursuant to Section 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted copy of Purchase Orders.
observations.reason: Purchase Orders were submitted with no supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create an inventory and disposal committee to act immediately in the proper identification, ascertain existence, location and disposal of these assets in order to fairly present the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We further recommend that municipal accountant restore back the PPE accounts dropped from the books and adjustment have to be made only after proper inventory and disposal of the properties.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Team was already created to clearly verify those assets listed in the damaged assessment report during the 7.2 magnitude earthquake and made an Inventory Report of Property, Plant & Equipment as of December 31, 2014.
observations.reason: The amounts of PPE dropped from the books were based on the Inventory Report of Property Plant & Equipment as of December 31, 2014. No report of PPE was submitted as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
1. 	Submit justifications/explanations why the Municipal Mayor was made the payee of the purchase of lot instead of the true  vendor.

2. 	Submit justification why the same amount was booked as Accounts Payable still with the Mayor as payee. 

3. 	Submit justifications/explanations why the dates in the submitted documents did not reconcile with the logical chronology of things.

4. 	Submit justifications/explanations why DBP CheckNo.  43830881 dated September 23, 2014 previously issued to the Municipal Mayor and encashed on September 24, 2014  was the same check allegedly issued/received by Uri Sallacay  per undated DV No. 100-15-01-68.

5. 	Submit all documents to support the purchase of the lot pursuant to Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and  other pertinent provisions to support the payment.
observations.managementAction: The management is still in the process of complying the requirements for the titling of these lands.
observations.reason: Requirements not yet submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
Must initiate the move by creating the inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties of the municipality.  The corresponding equipment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts.  In the absence of available records of acquisitions in the past years, they will have no other recourse but to take hold of the disbursement vouchers to obtain the necessary data to be recorded in the equipment/property ledger cards.

Require the general services officer or municipal treasurer to submit the request for inspection with complete supporting papers so that the auditors can take proper action on the request.
observations.managementAction: COA Technical personnel already inspected the damaged properties and were already dropped from the books basing from the inventory report made by the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: Management did not submit the inventory report for the damaged properties as well as the request to drop these properties from the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and  Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts.

Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.


Adopt and always observe austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already maximized the services of permanent employees by assigning additional functions not related to their actual term of reference.
observations.reason: The municipality recorded the wages of casual employees to Salaries and Wages-Casual account and maximized the services of permanent employees.

observations.recommendation:
Require the municipal accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV) to reclassify cash advances  for travel and special purpose/ time-bound undertakings  recorded under the Receivables- Due from Officers and Employees (123) account to the appropriate account, Advances to Officers and Employees (148) pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002 dated January 19, 2007.  On the other hand, the cash shortage of Benjamin Abad amounting to ₱491,497.84 should be reclassified to the account Due from Officers and Employees (123).  

Send demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitor the liquidation of these cash advances.

Exert more effort to collect these receivables such as withholding payments of claims of municipal officials/employees who have long overdue accountabilities with the LGU as in the case of audit disallowances.

Follow-up with the Office of the Ombudsman the status of the case filed against the defalcating municipal treasurer.

Issue bills or collection/confirmation letters  to  agencies/persons concerned  as  proofs  that the Municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means  to collect these receivables that have become dormant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has implemented collections of unliquidated cash advances thru salary deduction.
observations.reason: The audit team reviewed a sample of the DVs for claims of the concerned accountable officers and verified that deductions were effected. The Journal Entry Vouchers also reflected the said deductions as credits to PY Receivables-Disallowances/Charges.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommend that the Inventory Committee secure a copy of the list of LGU properties from the Accountant before conducting the physical count of PPE to ensure that all properties will be completely accounted for.
2. We also recommend that the Inventory Committee include in the Report of Physical Inventory all donated equipment, regardless of source, and to reconcile their report with Accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the understatement of the assets and liabilities accounts. Also, make sure that subsequent similar transactions are recorded in the proper account for fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
5. We recommend that the Local Development Council, through the Municipal Mayor, include as priority projects in the AIP only those which are capital in nature as enumerated in Item 3 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
6. Also, to ensure that all identified priority projects in the AIP are implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so as to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.
observations.reason: Still with ineligible expenditures due to wrong perceptions on the PPAs to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
7. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the MDRRMO refrain from utilizing the LDRRMF for projects which are not in the approved LDRRMIP. Any revisions thereon must bear the approval of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Council and the Sangguniang Bayan.
8. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer transfer annually the unexpended balance of the QRF and Mitigation Fund–MOOE to the Trust Fund books to ensure accuracy of the reported data and status of LDRRMF of the LGU.
9. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that utilization of the LDRRMF is based on the duly approved LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.
observations.reason: The project Safe Closure and Rehabilitation of Existing Dumpsite & Establishment of Municipal Eco-Park was not implemented since the property is owned by the Local Chief Executive hence, the funds will be transferred or re-aligned to other projects; while the implementation of the Reclamation for the Protection ... was undertaken by the DPWH; and the IEC Campaign and Earthquake Drill were supposed to be implemented last March 2018 but the designated MDRRMO was sickly, hence, it will be implemented this year.

observations.recommendation:
10. We recommend that the Chairman of the BAC strictly observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days from their execution/issuance to afford timely review and evaluation of the transaction before the execution/issuance thereof.
11. We also recommend that the BAC award contracts only to bidders whose documents are compliant with the eligibility requirements and that contracts be awarded only to reputable suppliers and contractors capable of performing an efficient delivery of goods and services.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
12. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Engineer, provide a garage for the Cold Storage Van to maintain its usability. Also, instruct the concerned Municipal Agriculturist and the BUB focal person to submit justification why the said vehicle was given priority in the procurement instead of the repair of the slaughter house and access road, upon which the use of the Van was dependent.
13. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to fast track the procurement of civil works on the repair of the slaughter house and its access road in order that the Cold Storage Van and the program itself will be utilized and implemented in order to improve income opportunities within the locality.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
14. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor deduct from the claims of the supplier the equivalent amount of liquidated damages for delayed delivery. Impose legal sanctions to the supplier in case of failure to do so.
15. Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer process payments on contracts only when delivery is complete and adequately documented.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
16. We recommend that the Market Administrator enforce the collection of unpaid rentals and leasehold right fees and not to allow tenants to occupy market stalls without a Contract of Lease.
17. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor impose penalties in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Local Ordinance No. 02-08 series of 2008 for failure to pay within the prescribed period.
18. We also recommend that the Municipal Market Awards Committee grant the use of stalls and excess stalls occupied by their delinquent tenants to another interested party to generate more income from market stalls operations.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.
observations.reason: Partially implemented. Delinquent tenants refuse to vacate their stalls promised to pay unpaid stall rentals.

observations.recommendation:
19. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure that funds previously released to the barangays are fully accounted and reported in the books when additional funds are released to the said recipient barangay by indicating in the disbursement vouchers that “funds previously transferred has been liquidated and accounted for in the books.”
20. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter to each concerned recipient Barangay Chairman for the submission of liquidation documents on each fund received from the LGU, including pictures of completed projects/programs and Inspection Reports of the Municipal Engineer or Program Coordinators.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
21. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor establish the Local Council for Protection of Children in the LGU from among its department heads, with himself as the Chairman.
22. We also recommend that the Local Council to prepare the documents for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LCC).
23. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that 1% of the IRA of the Municipality is appropriated for the implementation of LCPC-identified programs, projects and activities for child protection and welfare and prevention of juvenile delinquency.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.
observations.reason: LCPC was created but unable to prepare AIP and Annual Budget following due to leave of absence of the MSWDO, and the OIC is not aware on the requirements.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommend that the Local School Board incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development (ECCD).
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured medicines and supplies in the appropriate inventory accounts, while record the related inventory expense upon its issuance, issuances and consumption of said items could not be monitored and validated due to absence of subsidiary records and reports.
26. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office maintain ledger cards and Stock Cards respectively, for each type of inventory item, to monitor receipts and issuance and reconcile the respective balances monthly.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.
observations.reason: Both Offices maintained the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards respectively, reconciliation of their records still on-going.

observations.recommendation: 27. We recommend that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat strictly observe the requisites on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders and complete required supporting documents within five (5) working days from their execution/issuance, to afford timely review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
29.a.) In resorting to Shopping as the alternative mode of procurement, always consider the threshold of P50,000.00 prescribed in Annex H of the IRR.
30.b.) Procurement thru the alternative mode, in this case, the Shopping, must be supported with a BAC Resolution indicating the reason why they resort to the said alternative mode duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity.
31.c.) Always support procurement thru Shopping of supplies and equipment with PS Certificate of non-availability of stocks to prevent the transactions from being disallowed in audit.
32.d.) Request for Purchase of supplies, materials and equipment with amounts more than the prescribed threshold of P50,000.00 for Shopping, procurement shall be done thru Competitive Bidding.
33.e.) Invitation to Bid of procurement of supplies, materials and equipment more than the prescribed threshold of P50,000.00 must be posted in the PhilGEPS copy of which must be attached to the disbursement vouchers to serve as proof of postings. Likewise, the certification from the BAC Secretariat that said procurement opportunities were posted at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the procuring entity for seven (7) calendar days must also be attached.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.
observations.reason: There are procurements thru Shopping where the BAC Resolution were not supported with the said alternative mode and some procurements were not supported with PS Certificate of non-availability of stocks due to the long distance of the PS Depot location.

observations.recommendation:
34. We recommend that all collecting officers including the Municipal Treasurer be directed to strictly remit and deposit their collections on time, thereafter the subsequent intact deposit of such funds to the agency’s Authorized Government Depository Banks (AGDB). This must be carried out strictly to ensure that none of the funds of the government is lost or misused.
35. We recommend that the erring accountable officers be directed to carry out full turn-over of the unremitted collections and their consequent intact deposit by the Municipal Treasurer to the agency’s depository banks. Consequently, all collections shall be duly recorded both in the cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer and per the books of accounts for which adjustments under said records are to be made.
36. We recommend that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer concerning the preparation of RAAF regularly which shall be submitted to the Audit Team for audit together with all other accounts on monthly basis. In addition adequate records shall be maintained for all accountable forms that were requisitioned and released to all collectors which is to be presented during audits and whatever valid purposes.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.
observations.reason: Several corrective actions and processes were recommended and implemented; certain collections still in court or subject to reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: 36. We recommend for management to direct prompt and intact remittance of collections thereafter the reporting of said transactions thru RCDs and their corresponding intact deposit by the Municipal Treasurer. This must be carried out to ensure that none of the government funds is lost or misused. Any differences between records shall then be reconciled and corresponding adjustments effected.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 36. We recommend for management to direct prompt and intact remittance of collections thereafter the reporting of said transactions thru RCDs and their corresponding intact deposit by the Municipal Treasurer. Any difference between both records shall then be reconciled, the corresponding adjustments effected for the particular account to be accurately presented in the agency’s books of accounts and financial statements as of any given period.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 36. We recommend for management to direct the Municipal Treasurer concerning the preparation of RAAF regularly which shall be submitted to the Audit Team for audit together with all other accounts on monthly basis. In addition adequate records shall be maintained for all accountable forms that were requisitioned and released to all collectors which is to be presented during audits and whatever valid purposes.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 38.We recommend for management to direct the Municipal Treasurer concerning the preparation of RAAF regularly which shall be submitted to the Audit Team for audit together with all other accounts on monthly basis. In addition adequate records shall be maintained for all accountable forms that were requisitioned and released to all collectors which is to be presented during audits and whatever valid purposes. Said record like logbook, shall be under the charge of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. 
Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the accounts on the average of 0-15 days after the 10th day of the succeeding month for the DVs, and an average of 15 to 30 days for the bank recon and financial reports.
observations.reason: Some reports failed to be submitted on the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should admonish the Local Accountant and Budget Officer to be responsive to their duties and responsibilities. The maintenance and recording of appropriation and allotment received, obligation incurred and disbursed is a must to prevent incurrence of obligation and disbursements in excess of allotment received.

observations.recommendation:

Make a regular updating of the supplies ledger card/stock cards/Bin Cards; advise Accounting and the Supply Sections to conduct regular reconciliation of their records
Conduct physical count/inventory of said items every semester as prescribed in the National Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual

Prepare Subsidiary Ledgers as prescribed by Section 111 (1) of PD 1445.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance and/or Notice of Suspension as a result of post-audit.

observations.recommendation:

We recommend for the strict observance of limitations on the use of the Special Education Fund as provided for in Section 4 of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the utilization of SEF.

We recommend that the Special Education Fund be fully utilized in order to improve/rehabilitate existing school buildings and acquire the needed facilities and equipment for a conducive learning condition.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant ensure that only expenditures enumerated under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that LGU strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 5% of the revenue from the regular sources for disaster preparedness and mitigation to help achieve desirable protection and preservation of the lives and properties of the constituents of the municipality.

We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office. 

We recommend that management transfer the unutilized balance of P1,025,131.92 to comply with the prescribed rules and regulations. 

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant transfer the balance from Trust Fund into General Funds be made available for other social services.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents and rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exist and whose nature of business differs with the nature of the items to be procured; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement its project within the specified contract time, plus any time extension granted and if warranted, collect the necessary liquidated damages from the non-compliant contractors. Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without complete documentations and ensure the authenticity of the documents submitted by the bidders during bidding process. We also recommend that the BAC submit justifications why bidding an disbursements were done despite lack of funds for the project Concreting of Poblacion Norte to Happy Valley Farm to Market Road; why a legal consultant was hired without the proper procurement process; why consider bidders whose nature of business was different from the nature of the transaction; why liquidated damages were not collected despite delays in the completion of the projects; why there were delays in the procurement process; why there were positive variance in the completion of projects; and why COA was not informed of the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance. Likewise, we recommend that official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after such acceptance. 

These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, Notice of Disallowance and Notice of Charge as a result of evaluation of the documents and justifications to be submitted by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Lacking procurement documents already submitted
observations.reason: Inability to furnished copies of delivery documents to COA for procurement under alternative mode of procurement due to limited period of time (within 24 hours from acceptance) considering the number of documents to be reproduced, however, for procurement under public bidding, copies of delivery documents were provided to COA on time

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following:

1. Stop the granting of cash advance to official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made in compliance with the above-referred provisions of PD1445;

2. Regularly send confirmation/ demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures;

3. Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts.

In compliance with COA Circular 2012-004, furnish this Audit Team with the list of accountable officers with unliquidated and outstanding cash advances, together with copy of written notices duly acknowledged by the concerned accountable officers.  Submit also copies of disbursement vouchers (DVs) and related support documents of those with outstanding balances, especially long overdue accounts.

This LGU is reminded further of the consequences provided for in section 9.0 of the same COA Circular 2012-004, as follows:

“9.1 Failure of an accountable officer to liquidate his outstanding cash advance on or before January 31, 2013 shall constitute cause for the filing of malversation charge under Article 217, failure to liquidate cash advance under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of PD No. 1445.

9.2 The suspension of salaries of erring accountable shall be ordered by the Auditor concerned to the proper agency official through the head of the agency.

9.3 Appropriate administrative proceedings shall likewise be instituted.”

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following:

Approving officials should not allow or grant additional cash advances to employees if their previous cash advances/travel prepayments have not been liquidated.

Demand letters/confirmation letters should regularly be prepared by the accounting office as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officer’s record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures.

The accountant should enforce the immediate liquidation or settlement pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 ad Section 89 of PD 1445.

The Municipal Mayor shall likewise make a follow-up on all liquidation report for confidential and intelligence submitted to COA Central Office so that appropriate action could be done in case of deficiencies noted.

The management already stopped the granting of cash advances to those personnel with outstanding cash advances. Also, the requesting for write-off of dormant accounts is also being considered and the preparation of appropriate documents for the said request is still being made.

observations.recommendation:
The Head of the Agency should cause the issuance of demand letters, thru the initiative of the Municipal Accountant.  If after two (2) notices these accountable officers’ employees still fail to liquidate, the matter should be brought to the attention of the legal officer for proper disposition, copy furnished the Commission on Audit.

The Municipal Mayor, being primarily responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the municipality, and being the approving officer, should enforce the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

Failure of the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for withholding of their salaries and the institution of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.3 of the said circular and Section 128 of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to revert the “P16,697.18 CY 2011 Savings” in the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account to the General Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) pertaining to all BuB and other NGA-funded projects of the Municipality for CYs 2015, 2016 and 2017.

We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto, to fully eliminate the difference in the accounts Due from and Due to Other Funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for CY 2017 and for the years thereafter and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

We recommend that Management should stop the accommodation of private checks to comply with Section 67(3) of Presidential Decree 1445 and prevent the occurrence of dishonored checks. Further, we recommend the submission of any proof the aforecited private check has been honored and credited to the account of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a reclassification entry to correct the balances of Due to LGUs and Account Payable accounts in the amount of P45,228.79.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor to ensure that GAD programs/projects/activities are formulated based on the specifically identified gender equality issues and aimed at addressing the unequal status of women and men particularly on the protection and realization of women’s rights, economic empowerment, and participation in local governance and decision-making.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require an annual physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the local government as of December 31 of each year and designate an inventory committee of three (3) consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman and the general services officer and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned, as members.

Likewise, we recommend that:

The Audit Team or its representative shall be invited to witness the conduct of the annual inventory;

The RPCPPE shall be certified correct by the inventory committee and attested by the Audit Team or its representative; and

The General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balances of the property, plant and equipment per RPCPPE and the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto, to fully eliminate the difference in the accounts at the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that management:

1.	Stop the practice of assigning job order personnel to collect revenues;
2.	Utilize the existing bonded collectors to collect revenues under the strict   supervision of the Municipal Treasurer; and
3.	If warranted by funds, consider the regularization of the job order personnel so as they can perform collection functions.
observations.reason: Lack of Funds

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the concerned NGOs/POs to liquidate the long outstanding funds transferred to them; failure to liquidate the same should disqualify the said NGOs/POs from availing additional funds from the Municipality.

We recommended that the LGU GAD Focal Point System ought to strive to identify gender issues and formulate GAD programs/projects/activities that reflect the unequal status of women and men in order that the issues and concerns of women particularly on the protection and realization of their rights, economic empowerment, participation in local governance and decision-making would be effectively addressed.

Likewise, we recommended that the LGU GAD Focal Point System see to it that the Annual GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality is duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG Provincial Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer properly review the details of the payment to be made to the contractors/suppliers by considering any recoupment of advance payment and retention money before making any payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for CY 2015 and for the years thereafter and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.
