observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the municipal accountant to review and check the status of the listing/schedule of accounts payable to determine if there are valid claims against these obligated amounts, and if none, these should be reverted immediately to the general fund for subsequent budgeting.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is in the process of retrieving documents to validate the correct amount.
observations.reason: Some of the amount represent obligation for delivered materials to be paid thru the PDAF but was not continued due to the Court Order.  The municipality is waiting for appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that a reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts be made by the Municipal Accountant and to effect necessary correcting entries.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor and review the recording of transactions in the books of the agency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is on the process of reconciling the said account.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office is currently reconciling these reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUPs) for non-functional or our-of-order properties should be prepared and submitted to COA, and for the management to recommend for inspection so that the disposal of the same may be done in accordance with prescribed procedures under Sec. 79 of PD 1445 and the NGAS Manual and existing auditing rules and regulations. The concerned Municipal Officers should submit to COA a written explanation for non-compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386, the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government. The Municipal Officer should plan and prepare schedule in the conduct of physical inventory and the preparation of the corresponding RPCPPE for its timely submission on or before January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: Only two (2) motor vehicles were returned to the Treasury Office/Property custodian. IIRUP had been completed and already posted for Public Auction but reappraise all by COA is recommended because price is to high and no bidder signify interest on the items.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above observations, we recommend the following: Create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE. To ensure the success of the committee, all department heads are advised to instruct their members to cooperate in the conduct of physical count; The inventory committee shall prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation with PPE Ledger Cards and the Property Cards to be maintained by the Accounting and Property Offices, respectively; All fully depreciated PPE, if found unserviceable shall be classified to Other Assets; and Proper disposal of unserviceable property should be made.

Require the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee who will conduct physical inventory of all its Property, Plant and Equipment together with the General Services Office (MGSO)/Municipal Treasurer on a specific time table; Require the MGSO/Municipal Treasurer to prepare Property Cards on all counted items and on all subsequent acquisition/procurements for the year; Monitor submission of the Report on the Physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); Require the Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile recorded entries in the PPE account with the RPCPPE and property cards for an immediate adjustment in either books/records.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee who will conduct physical inventory of all its Property, Plant and Equipment together with the General Services Office (MGSO)/Municipal Treasurer on a specific time table; Require the MGSO/Municipal Treasurer to prepare Property Cards on all counted items and on all subsequent acquisition/procurements for the year; Monitor submission of the Report on the Physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); Require the Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile recorded entries in the PPE account with the RPCPPE and property cards for an immediate adjustment in either books/records.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE. To ensure the success of the committee, all department heads are advised to instruct their members to cooperate in the conduct of physical count; The inventory committee shall prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation with PPE Ledger Cards and the Property Cards to be maintained by the Accounting and Property Offices, respectively; All fully depreciated PPE, if found unserviceable shall be classified to Other Assets; and Proper disposal of unserviceable property should be made.

We recommended that management should exert additional efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government within the prescribed period and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty imposition except if waived by concerned parties.
observations.reason: No proper monitoring

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, take immediate action to resolve its waste management transition from utilizing its old dump to opening a new and operational one that conforms with the requirements of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to prevent the accumulation of garbage in the barangay waste yards.
observations.reason: Restriction on budget

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee thru the BAC Secretariat comply with the requirement on the submission of perfected contracts and/or purchase orders together with all supporting documents within five (5) days after approval to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to submit the contracts/ purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof within 5 days from approval thereof.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that a copy of the report of inspection or its equivalent shall be submitted to the Head of the Auditing Unit within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered and, in the case of unserviceable and disposable property/assets, immediately after inspection thereof by management.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipality assign the inspection of the procured items to personnel not under the control or supervision of the supply/property officer of the Municipality who is entrusted to accept the items delivered.
observations.reason: Management still failed to submit the AIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should provide a written explanation on what caused such delays or if there were time extensions granted and to exert extra efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty deduction.
observations.reason: Delays were still observed in the implementa-tion of projects funded by national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer properly review the details of the payment to be made to the contractors/suppliers by considering any recoupment of advance payment and retention money before making any payment.
observations.reason: Said amounts were not yet deducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management immediately use the amount deposited in the HYSA to pay off portion of the principal amount of the loan to economically manage its resources.
observations.reason: HYSA still exists in the accounts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that legal processes be availed of to recover the amount of P196, 585, 74 from the supplier JVC, which amount was erroneously charged against the funds of the municipality. Also, require the personnel responsible for the omission to submit written justification why he/she may not be held personally responsible therefore.
observations.reason: Management has not yet recovered the amount in question though a communica-tion has been sent in CY 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to make certain that all barangay shares from the Real Property and Community Tax Certificate collections are remitted to the LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160 without need of any further demand.
observations.managementAction: RPT remittances were still delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to immediately designate an employee who shall be responsible person to facilitate the registration and insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality. Apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.
observations.managementAction: The Agency did not designate a person who shall be responsible in facilitating the registration and insurance of all motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to exert all efforts necessary to prepare and
submit the financial statements, disbursement vouchers and liquidation
reports on cash advances immediately considering that these were
already long-overdue. Henceforth, submit all the financial reports on or
before the 10th day of the ensuing month for verification purposes.
Further, we recommended that Management submit justification for the
noted lapses. We also reiterate our previous recommendation for the
hiring of a qualified Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant in
as much as these are mandatory position under Section 470 and 474 Book
III, Title V of the Local Government Code of 1991.

Management Comment:

1.13 The Municipal Mayor informed the audit team that they are already looking
into the said matter and will submit the required documents the soonest
possible time.

2.10 We have recommended the following:
a) That the Municipal Mayor instructs the Budget officer and
Accountant to justify the non-maintenance of the required registries,
failure to duly accomplish the required obligation request as well as
the non-submission of SAAOB;
b) The Municipal Mayor instruct the Budget Officer to coordinate with
the Accountant in the preparation and submission of the SCBAA the
soonest possible time so as to allow the audit team to have ample
time to verify its validity;
c) The Municipal Mayor instructs the Budget Officer and different
department heads to strictly adhere to the budgetary and
disbursement process; and
d) Henceforth, familiarize themselves and ensure strict compliance with
the different budgetary requirements.

Management Comment:

2.11 The Municipal Mayor informed the audit team that they are already in the
process of preparing the said report as well as the needed reconciliation
statement. Management further assured the audit team that the required
reports shall be submitted the soonest possible time.

3.11 In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended
Management the following:
⮚ The respective personnel in charge of the noted deficiencies to submit
the required supporting documents; and
⮚ Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to verify
whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the
disbursement vouchers before processing the claims, otherwise the
claim should be returned to the respective employee/ personnel for
compliance of the requirements.

4.6 We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant maintain a subsidiary ledger/ or a
list of liabilities due and demandable. Also, the Municipal Treasurer see
to it that amount withheld be remitted on or before the prescribed
deadline in order to avoid the incurrence of penalties and surcharges and
the suspension of benefits of the Municipal Employees from the GSIS
and PhilHealth.
4.7 Also, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit a copy of the
MOA signed with GSIS. Management should revisit the said MOA and
determine whether overpayment was made to GSIS.

Management Comment:

4.8 The Municipal Accountant will reconcile their records with the records of the
respective government bodies such as GSIS, BIR, PHIC and HDMF

5.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified
personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under the provision of
R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of operations of the LGU.

Management Comment:

5.8 Management informed the audit team that they are already in the process of
hiring qualified personnel to serve as head of various departments. However,
some of the applicants still lack the needed experience and mandatory
qualifications.

6.7 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and
that the Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the
preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.

Management Comment:

6.8 Management informed the audit team that both of the required plans are
already being made by the LGU since said plans were also being required by
the DILG for the Seal of Good Local Governance.

7.6 We have recommended that the agency prepare and submit a copy of
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency for 2017 and henceforth,
an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is
made.

Management Comment:

7.7 Management vowed to submit a copy of the APP the soonest possible time.

8.5 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and
that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following concerned personnel:
● The Municipal Budget officer to submit supplemental investment
plan/ NADAI or additional appropriation ordinance as justification
for the utilization of CY 2017 Share from Tobacco Excise Tax; and
● The Municipal Treasurer thru the help of the Local Sanggunian to
open a special account solely for the receipt and utilization of the
Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Upon creation of which, the
Municipal Accountant should maintain special account under the
General Fund.

Management Comment:

8.6 Management vowed to submit the NADAI and together with the help of the
local sanggunian they will look into the possibility of opening a separate
account.

9.6 We have recommended that for the programs/ projects and activities
which are MOOE in nature which remained unimplemented/
unobligated, the LDC should identify other projects needed to be
implemented which are capital expenditures in nature in order to
properly re-align the previously noted MOOE projects.

Management Comment:

9.7 Management will convene with the respective head of departments and will
look further on the said matter.

10.8 We have recommended that management see to it during the Budget
planning phase, all programs, project and activities proposed to be
charged to the 20% Development Fund conform to Item 3.1 of the DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1. Henceforth,
Management should familiarize themselves with the provisions of DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the expenditure items
covered and prohibited to be paid out of the 20% Development Fund for
guidance.

Management Comment:

10.9 Management will convene with the respective head of departments and will
look further on the said matter.

11.4 We have recommended that the following actions be undertaken by
management:
a. The Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, thru the
issuance of an SB resolution, create a MDRRMO and provide the
appropriate PS and MOOE fund relative thereto;
b. In the event that hiring a full time LDRRMO could not be made, the
Municipal Mayor should see to it that the corresponding designation
to be issued entails the services of a permanent official;
c. The MDRRM Designate justify the absence of a duly approved
LDRRM Investment Plan;
d. The Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRM Designate, Budget Officer
and Municipal Accountant Designate to account the disbursements
funded by the 5% LDRRM Fund. Submission of accomplishment
report which includes all the necessary supporting documents to
support said claims should be prioritized;
e. Henceforth, the MDRRM Officer Designate ensure that all plans,
reports and liquidation documents be submitted on time so as not to
40
cause delay in post-audit activities; and
f. Concerned personnel familiarized themselves with the relevant rules
and regulations with regards to the planning, allocation, utilization
and generation of reports for the LDRRM Fund.

Management Comment:

11.5 Management will convene with the respective head of departments and will
look further on the said matter.

12.8 We have recommended that management submit the Sangguniang
Bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC and
Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2017, if any. Henceforth,
management should observe and abide with the rules and regulations
with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC
fund.

Management Comment:

12.9 The Municipal Mayor assured the audit team that he will ask the MSWDO
designate to prepare justification on the said observation. Henceforth,
management assured the audit team that said observation will be fully
address in CY 2017.

14.7 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
a) The Municipal Mayor creates a GFPS Execom, TWG and Secretariat
that will supervise, advocate and install a GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS) as well as the required Gender Responsive Monitoring and
Evaluation (M&E) System;
b) The Municipal Mayor with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan,
formulate and adapt a Local GAD Code;
c) The MSWDO being the leading authority in identifying gender related
issues and concerns, see to it that GAD Plan and Budget are included
in the Annual Budget of the LGU;
d) The Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO to work closely/
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Budget officer to
determine with certainty all GAD related activities as well as to be
able to generate a detailed and credible GAD Accomplishment
Report;
e) Henceforth, management should follow the prescribed format of GAD
Accomplishment Report and see to it that said plan and budget are
approved by the DILG and the PPDO; and
f) Also, the concerned personnel should familiarize/update themselves
with the recent promulgations regarding Gender and Development.

Management Comment:

14.8 The Municipal Mayor vowed that said observation will be fully tackled and
address in CY 2017 but for the time being he will instruct the MSWDO to
prepare justification on the above noted observations.

15.3 We have recommended that management require the persons
responsible for the audit suspensions and disallowances to settle their
account.

We recommended that Management:
Consider assigning additional personnel in the Accounting Office to solve the problem of lack of manpower to do the accounting functions;
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to set-up a workable system of receipt and prompt releasing/ submission of documents to ensure that these are submitted on a timely basis to facilitate the timely preparation of financial statements and other financial reports; and
Strictly comply with the above mentioned provisions.

We recommended that management:
Cause the immediate submission of the 59 unsubmitted disbursement vouchers for audit purposes and to avoid possible suspension or disallowance;
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts; and
Strictly comply with the above-mentioned provisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers to determine at any given time the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement and submit the same to COA for audit purposes; and
Maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account for easy preparation of the monthly BRS.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Cashbook for each collection handled by the Collecting Officers to facilitate verification of their cash accountability at any given time and for proper monitoring and recording purposes.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer comply strictly with the preparation and submission of the above enumerated reports to be able to monitor the transactions made by the MTO.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Locate all available documents regarding the abovementioned accounts in order to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers.
Exhaust all possible remedies to locate records or documents of the balances of long outstanding receivables as basis for requiring the concerned agencies/organizations to immediately cause the liquidation and settlement of their accounts, otherwise, request for the write-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately the remaining balance of unutilized PDAF balances to the National Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Create/designate an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties belonging to the Municipality and prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with accounting records;
Instruct the designated Property Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of Property, Plant and Equipment that contain the historical record of each item of PPE, from acquisition, custody, disposal and other information about PPE; and
Require the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of each class of PPE

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Assess the nature of the biological assets held by the Municipality. Thereafter, adopt an accounting policy that best suits the Municipality’s biological assets;
Perform physical count of the biological assets; and
Properly include in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosures as per PPSAS 27.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are adjusted on timely basis.
Perform constant review of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to transfer the unutilized balance of LDRRMF to Trust Liability –DRRMF account under the Trust Fund account; and
The Municipal Treasurer to deposit the unutilized fund to the LDRRMF bank accounts.

We recommended that the municipality fully implement the planned programs, activities and projects for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery so that the DRRM Funds would be maximized and the intended benefits would be fully achieved.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to strictly comply with COA Circular 2012-002 and the Minimum Standards on Disaster Preparedness of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC) to ensure completeness and propriety of claims charged to LDRRMF.

We recommended that management submit the required Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, as provided in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular  No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, in order to monitor compliance on accounting and reporting of the utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management stop charging the wages of the two job order employees to the GAD Fund and adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2014-01 dated March 18, 2014 for how the funds may be utilized.
We recommended that management prioritize the provision of a sanitary landfill for the LGU in compliance with the pertinent provisions of the Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that the Management should ensure that the SEF will only be disbursed exclusively for the specific purpose for which they have been established as provided in DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 and Section 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 or RA 7160.

We recommended that the management through the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office ensure that contracts and all its supporting documents including those required in auditorial review be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from its execution to facilitate its review and evaluation as required in COA Circulars No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims of  progress billing payment unless  all the required supporting documents are present attached to the disbursement vouchers; and
Comply strictly to Section 9.1.1.1 to 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular 
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended the Management to strictly adhere to COA Circular 
No. 98-002 dated June 9, 1998. The services of the Provincial Legal Officer or the Provincial Prosecutor should be requested in case the Municipality needs legal services consistent with Section 481 of RA 7160.

We recommended that management require the officials responsible in processing payrolls that disbursement vouchers be supported with complete documentary requirements in adherence to the above cited provisions to ensure the validity of the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop salary deductions of loans and other deductions that will reduce the net take home pay of the employees to less than P4,000.00.
Plan a scheme with the lender on the mode of payment of the employee’s monthly loan repayments other than deductions from payroll.
Strictly comply with the provisions of the GAA, regarding the allowed deductions from salaries and other benefits of government employee.

We reiterated our recommendation that management (a) create an inventory committee and conduct complete physical inventory of all properties of the municipality to determine their existence and physical conditions in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual; (b) ensure that the inventory committee created will conduct the physical count on a specific period for which the subject undertaking must be completed and the period of submission of the corresponding complete physical inventory report; (c) issue the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for property/equipment under the care of the end user and maintain property ledger cards as required under the regulation; and (d) exert extra efforts to locate old records of acquisition to provide basis in the preparation of subsidiary records, computation for depreciation or to document the request for dropping from records all obsolete, destroyed and unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management require compliance with the following courses of action: (a) enforce strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 in the     granting of cash advances and the subsequent liquidations thereof; (b) religiously monitor  liquidation  of  outstanding  cash advances, refrain from granting new/additional cash advances to those officers and employees with unliquidated accounts; (c) send demand letters to those officers and employees and/or if warranted effect the withholding of salary for  those who failed to settle   immediately their accounts as required by the regulations; and (d) initiate collection measure to recover the aging accounts from those liable persons who were already terminated from the government service or no longer connected with the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the officials   concerned to ensure that the programmed projects for the 20% Development Fund comply with Section 3.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to attain its optimal utilization and to fully achieve the socio economic development and environmental outcomes as expressly prescribed by the circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local DRRM Officer and the BAC Chairman to procure rice for programs/activities of the Municipality solely from the NFA unless a waiver was issued by the latter in compliance with EO 51 dated December 22, 1998.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the (1) Municipal Accountant to (a) ensure   that  all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated  under Section 4.2 of DSWD  MC  No. 02 s. 2014 to establish  the validity and propriety of claims; and  (b)  ensure that the proper account is use in recording the payment of AICS; and (2) MSWDO to  prepare and maintain a file of each client/beneficiary’s case evaluation  to facilitate the validation of the propriety of the said transaction.

We recommended and management agreed that all agency officials concerned revisit and observe the personal services limitation provided in Section 325 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the preparation of the succeeding annual budget of the agency.

We recommended that the  Local Chief Executive cause the immediate submission of the required documents and reports pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of PD No. 1445.
We also recommended the withholding of salary of persons responsible for the continued failure to submit the transaction documents/Disbursement Vouchers until the submission of all the required accounts/documents for audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to cause the submission of the required pre- evaluation/inspection report and other requirements pursuant to Section 6.08 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 to prove the validity of transactions.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with provision of Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996,  requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection and Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries to validate if the same conform with the quality and quantity asked for.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procurement Monitoring  Report  in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing  Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Local DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended again,  that Members of the Local School Board: (a) ensure compliance with the limitations provided in Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160; RA No. 5447; DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 2017 and its implementing regulations; and (b) for the purpose of hiring teachers chargeable against the SEF, the LSB in the municipality shall utilize the list found in the Registry of Qualified Applicants (RQA) as provided in Section 4.1.1.1 of DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) exert effort to expedite the establishment of a Sanitary Landfill; (b) establish functional MRF; (c) strictly enforce ordinances pertaining to the solid waste management and other relevant policies; and (d) strictly enforce the  no-littering ordinance and encourage active participation of barangay officials  to support the solid waste management program of the government pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003 the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LGU GAD Focal Person and Municipal Accountant to submit an Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year using the format in Annex B   pursuant to paragraph  V of COA Circular No. 2012–004 and Section 10 of the Joint Circular No. 2012-1.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer fully implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries of the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities.

We recommended that the LCE and all agency officials concerned revisit and observe the above mentioned provision of Section 6 of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and Section 33 of  PD No. 1445 to avoid  extravagant expenses, save for  necessary capital  obligations and  improve the agency financial position.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notice of Suspensions, to prevent maturing into disallowances.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all his collections with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) daily or on the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

We recommend that management demand immediate liquidation/settlement of cash advances from concerned LGU personnel and compliance to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Further, impose withholding of salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation/settlement of outstanding cash advances.

We recommend that management immediately settle/liquidate cash advances of Intelligence/Confidential Fund pursuant to DBM-DILG-DND-GCG-COA Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 and provide COA the proof of transmittal of liquidation documents sent to ICFAU, COA, Quezon City.
Submit explanation on the delayed/late and non-compliance of the above-mentioned provisions regarding the release /liquidation of Intelligence/Confidential Fund.

We recommend immediate liquidation/settlement of outstanding cash advances for Confidential/Intelligence Fund. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on allowing the grant of cash advances despite non-liquidation of previous cash advances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant fast-tract the identification/verification of payable account to establish correct amount and to present correct balances of accounts payable in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the classification/identification of payable account to establish correct balances in the financial statements.  Comply COA Circular No. 2004-008 and COA Circular No. 94-103.

We recommend Management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply to RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.

We recommend management to closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre-disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained.

We recommend the management to submit copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports on procured items to the Auditor’s Office within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to comply with the provision of Paragraph 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and for the Auditor to conduct timely inspection of items delivered.

We recommend management, through the coordination of the Municipal Treasurer, to renew the fidelity bond of all accountable officers of the LGU pursuant to Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and comply the requirements as provided in Section 4.10 & 7.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned employees to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports pursuant to Section 100 & 107 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit, as soon as possible, its Agency Action Plan & Status of Implementation for the AAR 2017.
It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2017.

We recommend that management require the Acting Municipal Accountant to adhere with the required time frame for the submission of the monthly trial balance and schedules. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommend that the Inventory Committee secure a copy of
the list of LGU properties from the Accountant before conducting the physical count of PPE
to ensure that all properties will be completely accounted for.

We also recommend that the Inventory Committee include in the Report of
Physical Inventory of PPE all donated equipment, regardless of source, and to
reconcile their report with Accounting records.

Further, we recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer and
the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective Property Cards and PPE
Ledger Cards and reconcile the same monthly to afford a reliable and accurate
PPE balances in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate
adjusting journal entries to correct the understatement of the assets and liabilities
accounts. Also, make sure that subsequent similar transactions are recorded in
the proper account for fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommend that the Local Development Council, through the
Municipal Mayor, include as priority projects in the AIP only those which are
capital in nature as enumerated in Item 3 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1.

Also, to ensure that all identified priority projects in the AIP are
implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development
Fund so as to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the MDRRMO refrain from
utilizing the LDRRMF for projects which are not in the approved LDRRMIP. Any
revisions thereon must bear the approval of the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Council and the Sangguniang Bayan. Any disbursements
of LDRRMF for purposes other than those clearly prescribed in the LDRRMFIP
may result in audit disallowances.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer transfer annually the unexpended balance of the QRF and Mitigation
Fund– MOOE to the Trust Fund books to ensure accuracy of the reported data
and status of LDRRMF of the LGU.

Further, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that
utilization of the LDRRMF is based on the duly approved LDRRMFIP.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) submit justification for awarding the contracts to ineligible
suppliers and contractors.

We also recommend that the BAC award contracts only to bidders whose
documents are compliant with the eligibility requirements and those determined to
be the lowest calculated responsive bids to ensure that contracts will be awarded to
reputable supplier and contractor capable of performing an efficient delivery of
goods and services.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Engineer, provide a garage for the Cold Storage Van to maintain its usability. Also,
instruct the concerned Municipal Agriculturist and the BUB focal person to
submit justification why the said vehicle was given priority in the procurement
instead of the repair of the slaughter house and access road, upon which the use of
the Van was dependent.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and
Awards Committee to fast track the procurement of civil works on the repair of the
slaughter house and its access road in order that the Cold Storage Van and the
program itself will be utilized and implemented in order to improve income
opportunities within the locality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor deduct from the claims of the
supplier the equivalent amount of liquidated damages for delayed delivery. Impose
legal sanctions to the supplier in case of failure to do so.

Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer process payments on contracts only when delivery is complete and
adequately documented.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Market Administrator
to enforce the collection of unpaid rentals and leasehold right occupancy fees and
not to allow tenants to occupy market stalls without a Contract of Lease.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor impose penalties in
accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Local Ordinance No. 02-08 series
of 2008 for failure to pay within the prescribed period.

We also recommend that the Municipal Market Awards Committee grant the
use of excess stalls occupied by delinquent tenants to another interested party to
generate more income from market stalls operations.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure that funds previously
transferred to the barangays are fully accounted and reported in the books when
additional funds are released to the said barangay by indicating in the
disbursement vouchers that “funds previously transferred has been liquidated and
accounted for in the books.”

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter to
each concerned recipient Barangay Chairman for the submission of liquidation
documents on each fund received from the LGU, including pictures of completed
projects/programs and Inspection Reports of the Municipal Engineer or Program
Coordinators.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor establish the Local Council for
the Protection of Children in the LGU from among its department heads, with
himself as the Chairman.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the created LCPC to
prepare the following documents for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and
Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according
to needs for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan
for Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC).

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that 1% of
the IRA of the Municipality is appropriated for the implementation of LCPC-
identified programs, projects and activities for child protection and welfare and
prevention of juvenile delinquency.

We recommend that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its
budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.

Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommend
that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts
and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to
the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and
reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month.

Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are
reminded of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of P.D. 1445 in relation to Art. 218 of
the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure
to render accounts in a period of two months.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should admonish the Local
Accountant and Budget Officer to be responsive to their duties and responsibilities.
The maintenance and recording of appropriation and allotment received, obligation
incurred and disbursed is a must to prevent incurrence of obligations and
disbursements in excess of allotment received.

We recommend that Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustment for the stale
checks to reflect the correct balance of the cash accounts;

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit timely the bank
reconciliation statements with the bank statement and original copies of paid
checks as prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-
011; and

c. Submit the list of deposits in transit and other items for adjustment by the
bank, indicated in the BRS, and the negotiated and cancelled checks pursuant
to the pertinent paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular 96-011

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of
the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the
books;

b. Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the reason why
these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action
thereon; and

c. If warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the
guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel concerned to:

a) Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) regularly
and submit to the Municipal Accountant as basis in preparing the Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) to booked up the issuance of supplies and materials
on stock.

b) Make a regular updating of the supplies ledger card/stock cards/Bin
Cards; advise Accounting and the Supply Sections to conduct regular
reconciliation of their records.

c) Conduct physical count/inventory of said items every semester as
prescribed in the National Government Accounting System (NGAS)
Manual.

d) Prepare Subsidiary Ledgers as prescribed by Section 111 (1) of P.D. 1445.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Disallowance and/or Notice of Suspension as a result of post-audit.

We recommend that the LSB develop a more comprehensive strategic plan
and budget priorities based on logical framework of program to ensure that all
components and activities were performed in line with the program design and that
it is achieving its objectives.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the provision
of Section 6 of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the
preparation and submission of the quarterly and annual fund utilization reports.

We recommend for the strict observance of the limitations on the use of the
Special Education Fund as provided for in Section 4 of DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint
Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the utilization of SEF.

We recommend that the Special Education Fund be fully utilized in order to
improve/rehabilitate existing school buildings and acquire the needed facilities and
equipment for a conducive learning condition.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Safeguard the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the
purpose/projects enumerated under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1.

b) Ensure that the projects identified proposed or funded under the 20%DF-
GF are feasible, without impediments to its implementation, and
implementable within the period for which it was proposed.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal
Accountant ensure that only expenditures enumerated under the DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend strict observance to the provision of Section 5.1.5of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 requiring submission of the report on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month to the COA auditor of the LGU.

We recommend that LGU strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 5% of the revenue
from the regular sources for disaster preparedness and mitigation to help achieve
desirable protection and preservation of the lives and properties of the constituents
of the municipality.

We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-
CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 be filled up
immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.

We recommend that management transfer the unutilized balance of
P1,025,131.92 to comply with the prescribed rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the account from
Trust Fund into General Fund and strictly observe the accounting guidelines.

We recommend that management observe the accounting and reporting
guidelines, set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 by following the prescribed
format in disclosing the amount allocated for MOOE and Capital Outlay.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer and the MPDC and LDRRM Officer to review the LDRRMFIP and AIP.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their efforts in reconciling their respective
records to ascertain the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts reflected in the
financial statements.

We recommend strict compliance with Section 114 and 120 of the NGAS
Manual for proper maintenance of Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and
Real Property Ledger Cards by the Accounting Unit and Stock Cards and Property
Cards by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a) The concerned Municipal officials to work out and to see to it that Titles of
the purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the
Municipal Government of San Isidro to establish absolute ownership
thereof.

b) The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to coordinate with each other to
ensure correct recording of the parcels of land in the books of accounts,
property records and related reports.

We recommend strict compliance with Sections 51 and 114 of the Manual on
the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units

We recommend strict compliance with the accounting and reporting
guidelines for local road network construction and maintenance system prescribed
by COA Circular No. 2015-008

We recommended  management  to  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
prepare and submit on time the monthly  bank  reconciliation  statements  on
all bank accounts maintained by the Municipality.

In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

   a. Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all
      the properties of the Municipality  and  prepare  the  Report  on  the
      Physical Count of Property, Plant  and  Equipment  (RPCPPE)  with  the
      corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment;


   b. Reconcile the result thereof  with  available  accounting  records  to
      determine unrecorded properties; and


   c. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation  which  shall  be  our
      basis for appropriate opinion and further action.

We recommend management to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a
thorough evaluation/reconciliation of the journal entries made to  determine
the causes of the discrepancy. Likewise, effect  the  necessary  adjustments
to clear the  reciprocal  accounts  of  possible  erroneous  entries  and/or
omissions made.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to  cause
the return of  the  unutilized  balances  to  various  source  agencies  and
constantly monitor and review  the  composition  of  the  account  to  avoid
dormancy of the balances.

We recommended  management  to  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
immediately transfer the said amount  to  reflect  correct  balance  of  the
affected accounts.

We recommended management to direct the LDRRMO to prepare  and  submit
the  Local  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  Management   Fund   Investment   Plan
(LDRRMFIP) and the Municipal Accountant  to  submit  timely  the  Report  on
Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund.

In order to secure the ECC for eventual construction of  the  sanitary
landfill, we recommended the following:


        1. Direct the  concerned  personnel  to  facilitate  the  immediate
           execution  of  the  Deeds  of  Donation  and  MOA  between   the
           Municipality and the private owners to prevent  any  dilemma  or
           conflict of interest that may arise over utilization of affected
           land area; and


        2.   Direct   the    Engineering    Office    to    expedite    the
           completion/finalization of the site development  plan  with  due
           consideration to the applicable basic minimum requirements under
           prevailing  rules  and  regulations  to  be  complied  with  and
           submitted to the proper authorities of the EMB.

We recommended the following:

   1. Direct the LCPC Focal Point Person to ensure  strict  compliance  with
      its Plan and Budget to the optimum level in  utilizing  the  available
      resources set aside for the  desired  achievement  of  the  identified
      needs for the protection of children as determined by the Council; and

   2. Direct the LCPC to prepare a complete accomplishment report to include
      adequate  and  reliable  information  as  to  the  status  of   actual
      performances as against the plans.

We recommended the following:

     1. Provide all the information deemed necessary in the  evaluation  of
        the projects to be presented in  details  upon  submission  of  the
        report in order to  establish  reliability  of  the  accomplishment
        embodied therein; and


     2. Include those which are to  be  implemented  by  the  Municipality,
        whether infrastructure projects or other form  of  PPAs  so  as  to
        facilitate prompt monitoring and validation thereof.

We recommended the following:

   1. Allocate fund for the construction of additional water tap  stand  for
      the water distribution for the target beneficiaries;

   2. Consider setting aside financial resources to provide adequate shelter
      of the water pump necessary for protection against  faulty  electrical
      wiring/short circuits;

   3. Enforce collection of the liquidated damages from the contractor; and

   4. Justify why the Municipality allowed the request for extension  almost
      30 days beyond the original date of completion or  the  expiration  of
      the contract.

1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to expedite  the
   collection of taxes due and collectible by sending demand letters to
   delinquent taxpayers giving priority to delinquent taxpayers  having
   large balances;


     2. Create a committee  which  is  responsible  in  the  conduct  of  an
        information dissemination drive regarding the importance  of  paying
        taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment thereof; and


     3. Avail of the remedies provided for by law to enforce the strict  and
        prompt collection of delinquent real property taxes.

We recommended the following:


     1. Direct the HRMO to formulate an employee manual  that  will  define
        the job description, rights and responsibilities, among others,  of
        all the employees within the Municipality.


     2. Issue a uniform and clear-cut policy on the  hiring  of  Job  Order
        workers to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto.

     3. Reduce/limit the hiring of job order  workers  and  fill-up  vacant
        positions and evaluate  and  determine  the  appropriate  personnel
        complement of each office.

We recommended management  to  reconstitute  the  municipality’s  GFPS
Executive Committee and TWG to conform to the minimum requirements in  order
to accelerate gender mainstreaming within the Municipality.

We recommended management to establish  a  Monitoring  and  Evaluation
(M&E) Team to evaluate the outcomes of the GAD-responsive PPAs.

We recommended management to advise the GFPS to  submit  the  required
reports to the concerned agencies within the prescribed period.

We recommend management to direct the Municipal Accountant to  evaluate
and analyze the subsidiary ledgers of accounts with  negative  balances  and
effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to  arrive  at  the
accurate balances.

We recommended the following:

     1. Direct the responsible  officers  to  strictly  comply  within  the
        prescribed dates of submission of the mandatory reports.


     2. Require the responsible official/s to explain in writing as to  the
        cause of delay in the preparation and submission of  the  financial
        reports.

We recommended that Management should stop the accommodation of private checks to comply with Section 67(3) of Presidential Decree 1445 and prevent the occurrence of dishonored checks. Further, we recommend the submission of any proof the aforecited private check has been honored and credited to the account of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Update the balances in the ledger to reflect whether there has been unrecorded disbursements pertaining to each individual water system projects and if there is none.
2. To either return the unutilized balance of P1,487,627.00 to the source agency or deposit the amount to the National Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto, to fully eliminate the difference in the accounts Due from and Due to Other Funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to evaluate the collectability of the Loans Receivable-Others and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office its recommendation. If warranted, request for write-off of certain accounts in accordance with the COA guidelines.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office set-up the related impairment loss on long outstanding Loans Receivable – Others based on the data provided by the PESO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) pertaining to all BuB and other NGA-funded projects of the Municipality for CYs 2015, 2016 and 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a reclassification entry to correct the balances of Due to LGUs and Account Payable accounts in the amount of P45,228.79.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer:
1) To explain and refrain the charging of expenditures to LDRRM Fund which are not related to disaster risk management activities.
2) To strictly adhere to the guidelines on the utilization of LDRRM Fund provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that all projects pertaining to 20% Development Fund in the AIP are included in the Annual Budget of the Municipality and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation/completion of projects from prior years that have unobligated allotments as of December 31, 2017.

Further, we recommended that the Local Development Committee should optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through: (a) proper planning of project including timing of execution; (b) coordinated action from LGU officials for better result and (c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project.

We recommended that the Local School Board carefully evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the specific programs/projects/activities set forth under Section 1 of R.A No. 5447, Sections 100 (c) and 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and the latest issued Guidelines on the use of the SEF under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. Otherwise, expenditures not falling within those allowed under the said laws and rules shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipality ensure the provision for a separate allocation of 1% of the IRA for the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) separate from the municipality’s allocation for LCPC under the social services sector as one of the one of the statutory and mandatory obligation prescribed under Republic Act 9344.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to ensure compliance to Section 262 of GAAM, Section 4 (2) of PD 1445, and Section 335 of RA 7190 by stopping the practice of accommodating payroll deductions for personal loans of its employees acquired from private banks and other lending institutions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that GAD programs/projects/activities are formulated based on the specifically identified gender equality issues and aimed at addressing the unequal status of women and men particularly on the protection and realization of women’s rights, economic empowerment, and participation in local governance and decision-making.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to revert the “P16,697.18 CY 2011 Savings” in the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account to the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for CY 2017 and for the years thereafter and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking reports and henceforth, observe the submission within the prescribed period of the Report of Collections and Deposits, Disbursement Vouchers, Liquidation Reports, Journal Entry Vouchers and general journal transactions to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO to demand the collection of the P471,556.17 Loans Receivable-Others from its borrowers and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the individual borrowers complete with the necessary details like address, date of grant, reference, amount and the like.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require an annual physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the local government as of December 31 of each year and designate an inventory committee of three (3) consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman and the general services officer and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned, as members.

Likewise, we recommend that:
1. The Audit Team or its representative shall be invited to witness the conduct of the annual inventory.
2. The RPCPPE shall be certified correct by the inventory committee and attested by the Audit Team or its representative.
3. The General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balances of the property, plant and equipment per RPCPPE and the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto, to fully eliminate the difference in the accounts at the year-end financial statements.

7. We recommended and  Management  agreed  to  require  the  Municipal
        Treasury Office to adhere to the existing regulation on  collection
        remittances and reporting in order to safeguard the  funds  against
        risks of loss, theft and misappropriation and in order  to  reflect
        the correct income and cash balances in the financial statements.


     8. Management committed strict adherence to the audit recommendations.

9. We recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  direct  the  Municipal
        Accountant to:


        a. identify all prior years’  fund  transfers  to  the  Trust  Fund
           accounts of the Municipality and verify if duly recorded in  the
           books.  If the former  Municipal  Accountant-Designate  had  not
           turned over documents  and  records  to  the  current  Municipal
           Accountant-Designate, the latter should at least verify all fund
           transfers from the date of his assumption up to CY 2017;


        b. trace the disbursements from  the  fund  transfers  through  the
           Report of Checks Issued and Cash in Bank-Cash  book  and  secure
           the corresponding Disbursement Vouchers;


        c.  check  the  accounting  entries  in  the   journals   for   the
           disbursements made and prepare necessary adjusting  entries  for
           identified errors;


        d. secure the Liquidation  Report  or  Statement  of  Receipts  and
           Disbursements (SORD) duly received  by  the  Source  Agency(SA).
           Otherwise, send confirmation letters  to  the  SA  to  determine
           whether or not liquidations had already been submitted;


        e. submit  liquidations  to  the  SA  if  it  was  determined  that
           liquidations had not been made; and

        f. transfer the asset accounts from the Trust Fund to  the  General
           Fund books.

11. We recommended and Management  agreed  to  instruct  the  Municipal
        Accountant – Designate to make the necessary adjusting  entries  to
        report the correct balance  of  the  Trust  Liability  account  and
        eliminate/remove the Government Equity account in  the  Trust  Fund
        books.   Further,  follow  strictly  the  recording  and  reporting
        guidelines for LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002  dated
        September 12, 2012.

        For the current  year’s  erroneous  entry,  we  suggest  the  below
        correcting entry:


            Dr. Government Equity P 875,543.68
            Cr. Trust Liability                    P 875,543.68

        As to prior years’ recording of the unexpended balance,  Management
        agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to  gather  the
        original journal entry vouchers and the cash  receipts  journal  to
        check the actual entries made  and  make  the  necessary  adjusting
        entries to correct the errors in the TF books.

6. We recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  direct  the  Municipal
        Accountant to:


             a. observe proper usage of accounts for every transaction  and
                event;


        b. stop the  practice  of  transferring  amount  from  Advances  to
           Officers and Employees to Due from Officers and Employees  which
           are assessed to be uncollectible accounts because liquidation is
           a requirement for cash advances; and

        c. prepare accounting entries to  correct  the  improper  adjusting
           entries made. Below  is  the  Audit  Team’s  proposed  Adjusting
           Entry:

           Advances to Officers and Employees      P868,299.03
           Due from Officers and Employees                    P868,299.03

11. We recommended and management agreed to  instruct  their  Inventory
        Committee to conduct annual physical count of all properties  owned
        by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to
        COA not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover,  they  agreed
        to direct the  Treasury  and  Accounting  Offices  to  prepare  and
        maintain Property Cards and PPE  Ledgers  Cards,  respectively  and
        ensure  that  reconciliation  of  their  records  is  done   before
        preparation of the annual financial statements.

We recommended that Management:  

a] Maximize utilization of these heavy equipment particularly for road graveling of various barangays of San Isidro, Isabela;
 b] 	Assign each truck a driver/custodian from among the employees to monitor the activities of each truck and to handle the maintenance of it in order to avoid further damage brought by continuous exposure to wear and tear; and

c] 	Issue an Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: Two (2) heavy equipment were used for rehabilitation of roads while the other heavy equipment were either unserviceable or not used by the LGU.
Liquidations were made for the cash advances granted for CY 2017
observations.reason:
No liquidations were made from the previously granted cash advances.

Reiterated under Finding No. 8

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

       a] 	adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 on the specifications for the signboards, tarpaulins to be posted where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy governing public interest;

      b] 	furnish the Audit Team with a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details as stated in the Circular; and

       c] 	refrain from incurring expenses in violation of the afore-cited Circular as this shall be disallowed in audit and subject to administrative disciplinary action provided under penalty clause of the same COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already adhered with the recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries of the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities.

We also recommended that in order to avoid the recurrence of similar violations, the Local Chief Executive issue a written directive/ memorandum to all agency officials concerned to strictly observe the rules, regulations and laws (particularly the procurement law) governing the receipt and utilization of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The management had been gradually settling the COA Order of Execution. Amount refunded was P468,972.40 as of October 27, 2017.
observations.reason: The amount refunded by concerned officials did not cover  the full amount of disallowance.

observations.managementAction: Some measures to address waste disposal is currently being undertaken.
observations.reason: There was no available lot found by the Municipality to be used for the construction of MRF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Budget Officer and the ICO - Municipal Accountant to:

discontinue payment of membership fees/dues of leagues/federation of appointive officials; and 

require the refund of the same.
observations.managementAction: The management had called the attention of responsible employees and commits to discontinue the practice.
observations.reason: The amount of ₱6,000.00 was not yet refunded by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU appoint a GAD\Focal Person as required to facilitate the implementation and monitoring of GAD activities as planned, in conformity with the provisions of the Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of the DILG, DBM and the NCRFW so that GAD objectives designed to settle gender issues and concerns within the locality be fully attained.
observations.managementAction: The management had appointed a GAD Focal person.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and its supporting documents be submitted daily to Accounting Unit so that the General Ledgers and subsidiary ledgers will be updated to maintain the relevance and value of the financial information.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has prepared the RCI and submitted to the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert collection efforts by sending demand letters to concerned NGOs/POs, for possible collection. It is also recommended that management submit to the Audit Team, the corresponding schedule or list of NGOs/POs which are beneficiaries of municipal funds, for evaluation. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the bank statements, list of all bank and book reconciling items, paid and cancelled checks in order to facilitate verification of the submitted BRS, to validate the accuracy of the recorded Cash in Bank balances. In addition, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant explain the variance between the Cash in Bank balances per Trial Balances and per submitted BRS. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, observe Paragraph 3.5 of COA Circular No.96-011 dated October 2, 1996, to render reliability on the reported Cash in Bank balances per submitted financial statements.

Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant be more cautious in recording transactions in order to properly reflect the account balance in the financial statements, pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate liquidation of cash advances through demand letters signed by him or authorized representative.  Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code states in unequivocal terms that the failure of a public officer to have duly forthcoming any public funds or property with which he is chargeable upon demand by any duly authorized officer shall be prima facie evidence that he has put such missing funds or property to personal use.

We also recommend that the concerned officials adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002 especially the withholding of salaries of those who refuse to settle their accountabilities.

employees.
