observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections at least once a week and intact and stop the practice of keeping huge maximum amount of cash in his possession.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasury to submit the Daily Cash Position Report.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the errors in the affected accounts. Subsequent similar transaction should be evaluated so that the proper account and amounts are used in recording transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured medicines and supplies in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase to recognize the asset in the books and record the related expense upon its issuance for fair presentation of the inventory and financial statements.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure that receipts, issuances and consumption are monitored and validated and maintain subsidiary records and reports.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured swine and livestock in the Biological Asset account upon purchase to recognize the assets in the books and record a reduction in the liabilities accounts only upon dispersal or disposal for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office maintain subsidiary records respectively for each type of livestock to monitor receipts and disposal and reconcile the balances monthly.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the transaction in the appropriate books of accounts, particularly in the General Journal, the unrecorded transaction on the procurement of various materials and equipment for pedicab fabrication using the appropriate accounts provided in the Local Government Chart of Accounts under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) to ensure fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a focal person and the designated heads of the concerned offices to obtain the MOA and other necessary documents to guide the LGU in its project implementation. Also, ensure that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the CY 2016 20% development fund to account all disbursements and balances of the fund and provide information as to balance at any given time during the year.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that every financial assistance granted by national agencies be covered with a Memorandum of Agreement. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant undertake the following: (a) demand immediate liquidation of the transferred funds and submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to source agencies to facilitate release of succeeding funds; (b) adjust the books of the municipality by recognizing a receivable from the recipient barangay using “Due from LGU” account and set-up a reciprocal account “Due to LGU” in the books of the concerned recipient barangays; and (c) ensure immediate submission of Liquidation Reports and other necessary documents to source agencies to facilitate succeeding releases.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant undertake the following: (a) issue demand immediate liquidation of the transferred funds from the concerned recipient barangays together with pictures of completed projects/programs and Inspection Reports of the Municipal Engineer or Program Coordinators; (b) adjust the books of the municipality by recognizing a receivable from the recipient barangay using “Due from LGU” account and set-up a reciprocal account “Due to LGU” in the books of the concerned recipient barangays; and (c) ensure full accounting and reporting of the funds and support documentation.

We recommend that the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Planning & Development Officer, include as priority projects in the AIP those which are capital in nature and ensure that identified priority projects in the AIP are implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that the MDRRMO prepare annually the LDRRMFIP in the prescribed format to support the projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and indicate in a separate caption the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended balance of previous years.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board, through its Chairman, utilize the SEF mainly for the improvement of school facilities and other authorized activities compliant with the purpose of the SEF. Also, stop the payment of honoraria and allowances which has no legal basis and ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documents to prove its validity and propriety.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board, through the MENR Officer, follow-up the approval from DENR of the new dump site location and immediately take action on any requirements to expedite its acquisition. Also, initiate the procurement of additional equipment for collection of waste materials and the rehabilitation of the Materials Recovery Facility and sustain the effective implementation of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommend that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat strictly observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after execution/issuance of contracts/purchase orders to afford timely review and evaluation of the transaction before it is consummated.

We recommend the following courses of actions: a) Grant cash advances only when previous ones have been settled and duly accounted for; b) Send demand letters to all accountable officers requiring urgent liquidation or settlement of outstanding cash advances; c) Instruct Municipal Treasurer to withhold salaries and other claims in case of failure to liquidate; d) Instruct Municipal Accountant to stop practice of crediting Due from Officers & Employees in absence of liquidation reports; e) Instruct Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each accountable officer & employee; f) Payments for expenses shall be made thru checks as mandated and procurements shall be made thru public bidding as a general rule.

We recommend the following: a) Create an inventory committee who will conduct a thorough physical count of PPE including their classification to the appropriate accounts; b) Cause the immediate submission of inventory report on PPE to the Audit Team for audit, verification and validation; c) Require the Accounting Office and the Supply Office to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records; d) Instruct the Municipal GSO or Municipal Treasurer to always issue Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) issued to end-users to pinpoint accountability.

We recommend the following: a) Immediately submit the subject missing DVs in CY 2015 together with complete supporting documents; b) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to comply the requisites in the Notice of Suspensions issued within the prescribed period to prevent maturity into a disallowance; c) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to institute control in releasing processed DVs to concerned payees or authorized persons only and ensure paid DVs were intact as listed in the Report of Checks Issued; d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to always transmit complete submission of accounts to facilitate thorough audit and verification.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the requisites on the hiring of a legal consultant and shall specifically undertake the following: a) Secure written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and written concurrence of the Commission on Audit before the hiring of legal consultant; b) The LGU may opt for hiring of a Municipal Legal Officer, in which case the necessity shall first be assessed.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184 particularly in resorting to alternative mode of procurement; when resorting to Shopping, always consider the threshold of P50,000.00 and support procurement with BAC Resolution indicating reason for alternative mode; request for purchase postings in PhilGEPS for amounts above threshold and attach copy of postings to disbursement vouchers; and support procurements thru Shopping with Certificate of non-availability of stocks to prevent disallowed transactions.

We recommend that management specifically instruct the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the following: a) Impose penalty and apply the civil remedies provided in the pertinent section of the Municipal Tax Code to enforce collections particularly from delinquent tenants of wet and dry market stalls and business taxpayers; b) Conduct monthly examination of the books of accounts of the business taxpayers and to ascertain, assess and collect the true and correct amount of tax due; and c) Promptly assess and collect the delinquent amounts.

We recommend management to strictly adhere to the stated provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 in controlling the use of government vehicles, to establish transparency, validity and propriety of expenditures, and undertake the following: a) require the concerned official to have a serially numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket duly approved and properly filled-up; b) the Supply Officer or authorized representative shall witness the reading of the beginning balance of gasoline before loading; c) at the end of the trip the driver shall indicate liters used and balance; d) at the end of the month the Supply Officer shall submit a Monthly Summary of Fuel Consumption for each government vehicle.

We recommend that all collecting officers remit and deposit their collections on time and thereafter the intact deposit recorded in the cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer and the books of accounts; direct the erring accountable officers to carry out full turn-over of unremitted collections and their consequent intact deposit; require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare reconciliation of both cashbook and accounts and submit RAAF regularly; ensure periodic reconciliation and submission to the Audit Team; and maintain adequate subsidiary records for accountable forms.

We recommend that the AAR Municipal Accountant submit the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers and supporting documents on some entries in the General Ledgers, prepare the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and rectify the other deficiencies noted. Henceforth, submit annually a complete General and Subsidiary Ledgers, all necessary schedules and the supporting documents for all entries in the General Journal and refrain from posting entry directly to the General Ledgers because all entries should be based from the General Journal and the Special Journals with corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers and supporting documents and strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: To ask assistance from COA or attend seminar/ workshop on PPSAS specially on the Operating Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and rectify the other deficiencies noted.
observations.reason: The accountant finds it hard to Reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that AAR management submit the lacking supporting documents and rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with suppliers especially those where pecuniary interest exist and whose nature of business differs with the nature of the procurement items to be procured; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; implement procurement under proper procedures and collect necessary liquidated damages from non-compliant contractors; ensure COA is furnished copies of delivery documents within 24 hours from acceptance; and that the BAC submit justifications why bidding and disbursements were done despite lack of funds for certain projects and why other irregularities occurred.
observations.managementAction: To enquire technical specification for the proposed project because of the distance from the LGU to Catarman. But those deliveries which were procured through public bidding were procured to be able to submit responsive quotation/ proposal.
observations.reason: Delay on the Procurement process due to unavailability of prospective eligible suppliers/ contractors/ contractors were sent to be able to submit responsive quotation/ proposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that AAR management submit the Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement of the different projects as well as the Project Briefs and Local Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP) for the livelihood projects. Likewise, submit the lacking supporting documents and justifications as well as rectify the other deficiencies noted. Further, we recommend that projects be implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management efficiently utilize the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF and rectify the deficiencies noted. Likewise, prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and the disclosure for LDRRMF in the prescribed format, transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of CY 2016 Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account 'Trust Liability-DRRM' in the Trust Fund books and submit the lacking supporting documents.

Likewise, rectify the deficiencies noted, submit the lacking supporting documents and refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exist.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate a 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan; justify the procurement of plastic trash containers despite not included in the AIP and without appropriation and the reasonableness of the procured quantity and amount. Further, submit the distribution list or Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment for the plastic trash containers. Henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of Section 2 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Section 6.1 of COA Circular 2012-003.

We recommend that the AAR remind officials and employees to issue Accountable Form 51 for all their payments. Likewise, require the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess amount of P2,000.00 per day for the registration paid to private institution.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements and inclusion of appropriate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive cause the implementation of the audit recommendations towards improved financial operations and governance. observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:

We recommended that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following: Approving officials should not allow or grant additional cash advances to employees if their previous cash advances/travel prepayments have not been liquidated. Demand letters/confirmation letters should regularly be prepared by the accounting office as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officer’s record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures. The accountant should enforce the immediate liquidation or settlement pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445. The Municipal Mayor shall likewise make a follow-up on all liquidation report for confidential and intelligence submitted to COA Central Office so that appropriate action could be done in case of deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: Inadequate monitoring of cash advance.

observations.recommendation: The Head of the Agency should cause the issuance of demand letters, thru the initiative of the Municipal Accountant. If after two (2) notices these accountable officers employees still fail to liquidate, the matter should be brought to the attention of the legal officer for proper disposition, copy furnished the Commission on Audit. The Municipal Mayor, being primarily responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to the municipality, and being the approving officer, should enforce the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445. Failure of the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for withholding of their salaries and the institution of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.3 of the said circular and Section 128 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Head of Agency had made demands letters on these cash advance thus lowering this amount to P1,893,936.85 from P3,566,763.41. After this report we will be sending another set of demand letters to the concerned personnel. The Municipal Mayor shall enforce the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

Submit also copies of disbursement vouchers (DVs) and related supporting documents of those with outstanding balances, especially long overdue accounts.

We have recommended that management stop the practice of utilizing 20% Development Fund to other purposes for which the fund was created. We further recommended to also prepare their AIP in accordance with the priority projects stated in the aforementioned Joint Memorandum Circular. With this, social, economic and environmental development would be achieved by the Municipality.

We recommended the Management to be prudent in the disbursement of the LDRRMF and shall limit its expenditures to those prescribed in the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 and DILG Circular 2012-73 in order to faithfully carry out the objectives of the fund, for the immediate protection of the environment and the inhabitants in times of calamity. We further recommended the Management to allocate a separate budget of LDRRMC for the expenditures that does not fall within the aforementioned guidelines.
We have recommended the management to stop granting the honoraria to these personnel because it has no basis and to strictly comply with pertinent laws, rules and regulations relative thereto.

Hasten the implementation of developmental projects as embodied in the Municipal Annual Investment Plan within the time frame to accelerate socio-economic growth and environmental development in the Municipality. Direct the Municipal Planning Development Council or the Municipal Project Engineer to exercise their monitoring function to immediately identify and address any problem(s) that tend to hamper / slow down development.

We recommend that the MPDO in close coordination with the Barangay Officials concerned and the Municipal Agriculturist to develop strategies in the monitoring of funds released for livelihood projects and its corresponding implementation to ensure attainment of the objective of the program thus benefitting majority of the targeted recipients. The following has to be considered: a. The preparation of legal documents required in the implementation of the programs; b. Capacity of the barangay officials and people’s organization to implement the project especially when it requires technical expertise; c. Related trainings required of the programs and the counterpart materials needed by the recipient; d. The preparation of Monitoring Report and signed master list of recipients.

We have recommended that management stop the practice of utilizing 20% Development Fund to other purposes for which the fund was created and to prepare their AIP in accordance with the priority projects stated in the aforementioned Joint Memorandum Circular. Also recommended reconciliation and verification of accounts converted to e-NGAS and review of accounts payable composition to determine valid claims and ensure recorded payables are supported with required documents.
Fast track the settlement of accounts disallowed and to assist the persons liable for the settlement. Adhere to Section 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to set up a workable system of receipt and prompt releasing/submission of documents to ensure that these are submitted on a timely basis to the accounting office for recording and subsequently forwarded to COA for review.

Require the Municipal Accountant to observe the timely submission of LGUs financial reports in accordance with the provisions of the above-mentioned laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: None.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management take appropriate action in recovering the missing documents from the former Municipal Mayor and submit immediately to this Office for post-audit.  Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of paid disbursement vouchers including their supporting documents to the Auditor pursuant to the afore-cited provisions of P.D. 1445.  Also, we remind management that non-submission of these documents is sanctioned under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: None.  A Notice of Suspension requiring the submission of these 26 disbursement vouchers was issued to management.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:

Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and

Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.
observations.managementAction: None.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 15.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate remittance the corresponding RPT shares to the province and barangays; and 

Updating of the subsidiary records to facilitate the determination of the account balances due to province and the 13 barangays.
observations.managementAction: None.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 14.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that management observe strict compliance with the provisions of Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and Section 361 (g) of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: This practice still exist in 2016 but promised to stop such practice this coming year.
observations.reason: Promised to comply with guidelines this year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that all agency officials concerned revisit and observe the abovementioned provisions of Section 325 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the preparation of the succeeding annual budget of the agency.
observations.managementAction: The management had acknowledged prior years’ lapses but failed to address the issue properly. Reiterated in Finding No.5.
observations.reason: The management promised to strictly comply with Section 325 of the Local Government Code of 1991 this coming year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC and other concerned Unit/Department Heads to immediately effect the regular submission of the required reports stated under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. Further, continued failure on the part of the MPDC to comply with the requirement should be meted with penalty provided under Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management had acknowledged the noted lapses/delays and ensures that needed reports will be submitted promptly, this year 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that management strictly observe the procurement procedures prescribed under Republic Act No. 9184. 

Use the facilities of the PhilGEPS to post all procurement opportunities as this will provide advantage in term of wider dissemination of information in favor of the municipality. Accordingly, the required posting of the same in the bulletin board and other conspicuous places of the municipality should be observed. This will facilitate a more competitive bidding and transparency in the procurement activities in favor of the government.
observations.managementAction: The management had ordered the Bids and Awards Committee to update the present procurement procedures of the agency thus complying with the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRRs) of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.reason: The Management promised to try to comply with the provisions this coming year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our earlier recommendation that Management instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection and Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The management had ordered concerned officials to comply with the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within the required period this coming year 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) exert effort to expedite the establishment and completion of the Sanitary Landfill; (b) establish functional MRF and (c) strictly enforce implementation of the ordinances pertaining to solid waste management other relevant policies and intensify information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection” policy and “no-littering” policy.
observations.managementAction: The management promised to comply with RA No 9003, this coming 2017.

observations.recommendation:
1. 	Initiate the identification of the donor as well as the pertinent documents to support the recording of  every donated asset by executing a deed of donation duly signed by the donor and donee .Reflect  thereon the type of asset being donated, the related costs, the intent for the use of the said donation and  furnish the acting GSO/Treasure’s Office and Municipal Accountant copies thereof for recording purposes.

2.	Both the treasurer and accounting office shall record all donated assets in the property and accounting records respectively based on the deeds of donations being furnished by the LCE office. These offices should coordinate to ensure compliance with regulations on donated assets.

3.	For donations without value, the Municipal Mayor may create a committee to appraise the value of the donated asset for proper recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: The management could not determine the donor of some of these equipment since it was given to the municipality with only the delivery receipt as supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall not be released and shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for the other priority programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Transferred the amount to Due to Other Funds.
observations.reason: Management is still questioning the bank if the fund was actually PDAF or not.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of  Section 4 of EO 298 dated March 23, 2004.  

 It is further recommended that the municipal accountant should review carefully the travel claims to avoid unnecessary/excessive claims of LGU personnel.
observations.managementAction: Excessive claims were still claimed and were issued Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: Management was not able to strictly enforce the provisions of AO 298.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management allocate funds for insurance with the GSIS General Insurance Fund of all the existing and future insurable assets of the municipality pursuant to Section 489 of GAAM, Volume I to secure the properties for whatever loss due to fire, earthquake, storm or other casualty.
observations.reason: The Management commented that since most of their buildings specifically their municipal buildings are still in construction due to the earthquake, they are hesitant to insure the temporary buildings used by the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectable for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording.

Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the debit to RPT/SET receivable and credit to Deferred RPT/SET income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectable and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant used the target income of the Municipal Assessor’s Office as basis in the recording of receivables.
observations.reason: The municipal mayor instructed the municipal treasurer and accountant to provide accurate data for the collection of taxes.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and  Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts.

Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.

Adopt and always observe austerity measures provided by Administrative Order No. 103 in the disbursements of government funds.
observations.reason: Lack of resources to hire regular agency personnel.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concern elected officials to demand the issuance of an Official Receipt of the Republic of the Philippines for every payment of League assistance, contributions or any other kind  of disbursement related thereto.

Strict observance of the Section 515 and 510 especially pertaining to the keeping of league fund and disbursement as provided.
observations.managementAction: The LCE stated that they will be discussing this particular finding during the leagues’ meetings.
observations.reason: Leagues do not provide government official receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the municipal accountant to review and check the status of the listing/schedule of accounts payable to determine if there are valid claims against these obligated amounts, and if none, these should be reverted immediately to the general fund for subsequent budgeting.
observations.reason: The amount represent obligation for delivered materials to be paid thru the PDAF but was not continued due to the Court Order. The Municipality is waiting for appropriation.
According to the municipal mayor, there is no other way of increasing market collections due to the geographic location of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned payees to refund the amount received as Calamity Assistance and 14th Month Pay totaling P2,640,947.00 for the said payments have no legal basis. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance. Henceforth, strictly observe pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and RA 6758 to avoid disallowance of disbursements.
observations.reason: According to management, they have filed an appeal but the audit team was not furnished with a copy.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned regular officials and employees, and the job-order/contractual personnel be required to refund the amount received for additional year-end benefits which are bereft of legal basis. Henceforth, stop the grant of such benefits in the absence of any legal or statutory basis.

In view of the above
observations, we recommend the
following:
Create an inventory committee
to conduct physical count of
PPE. To ensure the success of
the committee, all department
heads are advised to instruct
their members to cooperate in
the conduct of physical count;
The inventory committee shall
prepare the Physical Inventory
Reports to be submitted to the
Property Officer for
verification and reconciliation
with PPE Ledger Cards and the
Property Cards to be maintained
by the Accounting and Property
Offices, respectively;
All fully depreciated PPE, if
found unserviceable shall be
classified to Other Assets; and
Proper disposal of
unserviceable property should
be made.

Require the Municipal Mayor to
create an Inventory Committee
who will conduct physical
inventory of all its Property,
Plant and Equipment together
with the General Services
Office (MGSO)/Municipal
Treasurer on a specific time
table;
Require the MGSO/Municipal
Treasurer to prepare Property
Cards on all counted items and
on all subsequent
acquisition/procurements for
the year;
Monitor submission of the
Report on the Physical count
of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE);
Require the Municipal
Accountant to periodically
reconcile recorded entries in
the PPE account with the
RPCPPE and property cards for
an immediate adjustment in

The MGSO/Municipal Treasurer

Reiterated in Part II of this
report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the Perpetual Inventory System in recording purchases of supplies and materials for stock and their eventual issuance.
observations.reason: Perpetual inventory method of accounting was still not strictly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee thru the BAC Secretariat comply with the requirement on the submission of perfected contracts and/or purchase orders together with all supporting documents within five (5) days after approval to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to submit the contracts/ purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof within 5 days from approval thereof.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that a copy of the report of inspection or its equivalent shall be submitted to the Head of the Auditing Unit within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered and, in the case of unserviceable and disposable property/assets, immediately after inspection thereof by management.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipality assign the inspection of the procured items to personnel not under the control or supervision of the supply/property officer of the Municipality who is entrusted to accept the items delivered.
observations.reason: Management still failed to submit the AIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should provide a written explanation on what caused such delays or if there were time extensions granted and to exert extra efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty deduction.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in Part II of this Report) | Delays were still observed in the implementa-tion of projects funded by national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer properly review the details of the payment to be made to the contractors/suppliers by considering any recoupment of advance payment and retention money before making any payment.
observations.reason: Said amounts were not yet deducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for CY 2015 and for the years thereafter and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.
observations.reason: Management still failed to submit the required reports.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the BAC, through its Secretariat, post or cause to be posted the APP, the PMR and the List of Observers on the On-Line Monitoring Evaluation System (OMES) of the GPPB. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipality strive to create and maintain its own website so as to fulfill the transparency requirement of the procurement law.
observations.reason: The BAC still failed to post the APP, PMR and List of Observers in the OMES of the GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for the supplies and materials procured for construction projects. Thus, it should be properly recorded to their appropriate inventory accounts as provided for under the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: Perpetual inventory method of accounting was still not strictly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management immediately use the amount deposited in the HYSA to pay off portion of the principal amount of the loan to economically manage its resources.
observations.reason: HYSA still exists in the accounts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that legal processes be availed of to recover the amount of P196, 585, 74 from the supplier JVC, which amount was erroneously charged against the funds of the municipality. Also, require the personnel responsible for the omission to submit written justification why he/she may not be held personally responsible therefore.
observations.reason: Management has not yet recovered the amount in question though a communica-tion has been sent in CY 2014.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management   agreed to:                      

a. Instruct the Municipal       Accountant to issue demand      letters to the officials and    employees with unliquidated cash|advances otherwise withhold     their salaries including those  of the job order personnel as   provided for under Item 5.9 of  COA Circular No. 97-002;

For job orders with unliquidated|cash advances, cause the refund |through outright payroll        deduction;

Require the Municipal Accountant|to issue a certification that   previous cash advances have been|liquidated and accounted for in |the books before granting       additional cash advances;       

Stop the granting of additional |cash advances in CY 2016 to     those with unliquidated cash    advances as of December 31,     2015.
observations.reason: Reiterated Finding    No.3

observations.reason: Reiterated Finding    No.5

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management  create an Inspection/Inventory  Committee  to  conduct  the  physical count of the PPE owned |by the municipality and submit  the Report on the Physical Count of|the Property, Plant and         Equipment (RPCPPE) to the       Auditor pursuant to Section 124 |of the Manual on the NGAS for   LGUs, Volume 1 in order for us  to determine the validity and     existence of the PPEs owned by  the Municipality. observations.reason: Not        Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to exert all efforts necessary to prepare and submit the financial statements, disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports on cash advances immediately considering that these were already long-overdue. Henceforth, submit all the financial reports on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for verification purposes. Further, we recommended that Management submit justification for the noted lapses. We also reiterate our previous recommendation for the hiring of a qualified Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant in as much as these are mandatory position under Section 470 and 474 of Book III, Title V of the Local Government Code of 1991.

In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended the following:
The respective personnel in charge of the noted deficiencies to submit the required supporting documents; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing the claims, otherwise the claim should be returned to the respective employee/personnel for compliance of the requirements.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, limit the amount of cash advances granted to the Municipal Treasurer. Also, the Municipal Accountant is advised to strictly implement the no additional cash advances policy in the event that the Municipal Treasurer fails to submit all the liquidations on time.

In view of the above noted observation, we have recommended that management:
Justify the resort to reimbursements basis which resulted in the disregarding of proper accounting and budgetary procedures as well as the non-observance of procurement laws, rules and regulations; and
Henceforth, limit the use of reimbursement as a method of payment for procurement of property, plant and equipment unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under the provision of R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of operations of the LGU.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and that the Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.

We have recommended that the agency prepare and submit a copy of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency for 2016 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following concerned personnel:
The Municipal Budget officer to submit supplemental investment plan/ NADAI or additional appropriation ordinance as justification for the utilization of CY 20176 Share from Tobacco Excise Tax;
The Municipal Treasurer thru the help of the Local Sanggunian to open a special account solely for the receipt and utilization of the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Upon creation of which, the Municipal Accountant should maintain special account under the General Fund; and
The responsible personnel submit accomplishment report together with the copy of the disbursement vouchers for review and evaluation

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Sangguniang Bayan should pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate depository account for the 20% Development Fund. Upon creation of which, the Municipal Accountant should maintain two (2) special accounts under the General Fund.

We have recommended that management create a separate depository account for the proceeds of the 20% Development Fund. For the programs/projects and activities which are MOOE in nature that remained unimplemented/unobligated, the LDC should identify other projects needed to be implemented which are capital expenditures in nature in order to properly re-align the previously noted MOOE projects.

We have recommended that management submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC and Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2016, if any. Henceforth, management should observe and abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.
We have recommended that management require the persons responsible for the audit suspensions and disallowances to settle their account.

We recommended that Management:
a] Maximize utilization of these heavy equipment particularly for road graveling of various barangays of San Isidro, Isabela;
b] 	Assign each truck a driver/custodian from among the employees to monitor the activities of each truck and to handle the maintenance of it in order to avoid further damage brought by continuous exposure to wear and tear; and
c] 	Issue an Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to establish accountability.

We recommended that the Management:
a] Follow up and make appropriate representation with the National Solid Waste Management Commission for the expeditious approval of the municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to ensure compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9003; and
b] 	Undertake best practices in adhering to and implementing the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment of VAW Desk in every barangay to address concerns of violence against women.
We recommended that Management remit immediately to the National Treasury the unexpended balances of PDAF pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management:
a]  Assign additional personnel to the accounting office to solve the lack of manpower to do the accounting function;
b]   Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to set up a workable system of receipt and prompt releasing/submission of documents to ensure that these are submitted on a timely basis to the accounting office for recording and subsequently forwarded to COA for review; and
c]  Require the Municipal Accountant to observe the timely submission of LGUs financial reports in accordance with the provisions of the above-mentioned laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct regular reconciliation of the cash book and SL/GL balances to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions and the balance of Cash in Local Treasury account as of a given period.
We recommended that:
a] 	the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly Bank 	reconciliation Statement of all its depository bank accounts, take up 	reconciling items, and effect adjustment in the GL to ensure the correct 	balance of the Cash in Bank accounts;
b]	the Municipal Treasurer post the balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account in the cashbook and coordinate with the depository bank to ensure for the prompt delivery of the monthly bank statements with supporting documents to facilitate the timely  reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts against the GL balances; and
c]	the Municipal Accountant exert more time and effort to trace, verify and reconcile regularly the book balances against the bank records including reconciliation with the cashbook maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all the accounts appearing in the financial statements to determine at a given time the details and status of these accounts.  Apply the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create/designate an inventory committee to continue the conduct of annual physical inventory of all properties belonging to the municipality and the necessary inventory report be prepared and submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation with the accounting records.  The same report be furnished to the Auditor for verification.

We recommended that Management take appropriate action to have all lands belonging to the Municipality be covered by TCT duly issued by the Land Registration Authority to substantiate its claim for ownership.    Further, require the production of two missing title and be turned-over to the Municipal Treasurer for proper custody and safekeeping in the absence of property custodian.

We recommended that Management continue the investigation, pinpoint responsibility for the loss of these assets, if any, and hold the concerned person/s accountable for said loss.  Moreover, file an appropriate information for malversation of public properties to those who perpetrated this transaction, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant effect the immediate remittance of the corresponding RPT shares to the Province and Barangays and update the subsidiary records to facilitate the determination of the unreconciled balances.  Effect adjustment, if necessary.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:
a] 	Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
b] Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended that Management:
a] adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 on the specifications for the signboards, tarpaulins to be posted where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy governing public interest;
b] furnish the Audit Team with a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details as stated in the Circular; and
c] refrain from incurring expenses in violation of the afore-cited Circular as this shall be disallowed in audit and subject to administrative disciplinary action provided under penalty clause of the same COA Circular.
We recommended that the municipal officials concerned in the procurement strictly comply with the requirements of the aforementioned COA Circulars.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a] 		require the persons liable to fully settle the NS and ND; and
b] 	ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the suspensions and disallowances for which they are liable, otherwise, the officials who issued their clearances shall be jointly liable for the unsettled disallowances of the retired employees.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that management (a) create an inventory committee and conduct complete physical inventory of all properties of the municipality to determine their existence and physical conditions in accordance with abovementioned rules and regulations; (b) exert extra efforts to locate old records of acquisition to provide basis in the preparation of subsidiary records, computation for depreciation or request for the dropping from records all obsolete, destroyed and unserviceable properties.

Management gave assurance that measures will be undertaken to correct the deficiencies noted regarding the assets owned by the municipality.

We recommended  the following courses of action:
Enforce strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and the subsequent liquidations thereof.
Monitor  liquidation  of  outstanding  cash advances, refrain from  granting new/additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated or a proper accounting thereof has been made.
Send demand letters to those officers and employees and/or if warranted effect the withholding of salary for those who failed to settle   immediately their accounts as required by the regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries of the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities.

We also recommended that in order to avoid the recurrence of similar violations, the Local Chief Executive issue a written directive/memorandum to all agency officials concerned to strictly observe the rules, regulations and laws (particularly the procurement law) governing the receipt and utilization of government funds.

Management gave the assurance that the concerned officials will try to reduce the amount of their respective accountabilities and that compliance with the other audit recommendations regarding the matter will be made.

We recommended that the LGU officials concerned ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to developmental priority projects to achieve socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for purposes expressly prescribed by law.

4.10  The Local Chief Executive directed the municipal employees concerned to implement the audit recommendation to rectify the deficiency noted.

5.5  We recommended that management observe the limitation and comply with Section 325 of RA No. 7160. Likewise, the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Budget Officer were advised to ensure that there will be no filling – up of vacant position and no hiring/promotion will be effected until the required 45/55 PS limitation is achieve.

6.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) exert effort to expedite the  establishment of a Sanitary Landfill; (b) establish functional MRF; (c) implement and enforce ordinances pertaining to the solid waste management and other relevant policies; and (d) pass a no-littering ordinance and encourage active participation of barangay officials  to support the solid waste management program of the government  pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003 the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and all other concerned officials to strictly adhere the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 on the submission of a list of all on-going PPAs and to be implemented during the year and monthly monitoring reports to enable the Audit Team to conduct prompt validation of the said PPAs and timely submission of Quarterly Report to the COA Regional Director.

9.5 We recommended that management observe strict compliance with the provisions  of Section 2 and Section 4 of COA Circular  No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and Section 361  (g) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual  (GAAM ) Vol. I.

9.6  Management agreed with the observations noted by the audit team and promised to implement the recommended courses of actions regarding the deficiencies noted.

10.5 We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation  that Management instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with provision of Section 1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and  Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996,  requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection and Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries.

11.5  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Budget Officer and the ICO - Municipal Accountant to discontinue payment of membership fees/dues of leagues/federation of appointive officials and to require the refund of the same.

11.6  The Municipal Budget Officer and the ICO Municipal Accountant gave the assurance that immediate compliance with the audit recommendation will be effected.

12.3 We recommended that the LGU appoint a GAD\Focal Person as required to facilitate the implementation and monitoring of GAD activities as planned, in conformity with the provisions of the Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of the DILG, DBM and the NCRFW so that GAD objectives designed to settle gender issues and concerns within the locality be fully attained.

13.4  We recommended that management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to (a) submit the documents that would prove compliance with RA No. 9184, for our evaluation; and (b) conduct procurement processes in accordance with the provisions of RIRR of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184.   Likewise, the ICO Municipal Accountant is advised to (a) review completeness of supporting documents of all disbursements; and (b) refrain action on claims that did not meet the requirements of RIRR of RA No. 9184, Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, COA Circulars Nos. 2012-001 and 2013-003.  The Municipal Treasurer is also advised to issue checks in the name of the registered business name only.

14.2 We recommended the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notice of Suspensions, otherwise, these will mature into disallowances.

14.3 Management officials concerned assured the audit team that they will try to reduce the unsettled amount disallowance.

We recommend that Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and the complete supporting documents be prepared  daily and regularly submitted  to Accounting Unit to facilitate recording/reconciliation and preparation of financial reports  to maintain the relevance and value of the financial information in a given period of time.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace/verify the unreconciled/difference of balances of  the two accounts and take up/effect  the necessary adjustments in the books to present the accurate/reconciled  balances  in the financial statements.

We recommend that management closely follow-up/monitor from the Inventory Committee the conduct of the physical count of all properties as well as the  preparation  and submission of the  RPCI and RPCPPE  to COA as required under Section 124 of the NGAS Manual. Likewise, we recommend reconciliation of balances from both  the Accounting and Treasure’s office to  present the correct/accurate amount in the financial statements.
Comply Section 32 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to prevent possible misuse or loss of government funds	.

We recommend management to submit pertinent documents in the grant/release of funds to the concerned NGOs/PO’s and the corresponding report of receipt and utilization of all  funds supported with complete documentations.
Comply the provisions embodied in COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 to safeguard government funds  granted  to NGOs/POs .

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to establish existence of the breeding stocks by conducting physical inventory taking. Require the Municipal Agriculture Office  (MAO)  to  maintain individual record of items  to match/reconcile  with the report of the inventory committee and the LGUs books.  Likewise, the Accounting Unit  maintain ledger cards for  Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks as provided under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the bank statements and other required documents to facilitate verification of the submitted BRS .  Comply Paragraph 3.5 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and effect reconciliation in both books and bank balances and present it fairly in the financial statements.  Further, the Municipal Accountant also review the reports prepared by his subordinate to ensure accuracy of records.

We recommend that management  submit explanations on their  failure to implement  the appropriated solid waste management program, projects and activities  for 2016 and comply RA 9003 in the actual  implementation of future  lined-up projects.

We recommend that management issue demand letters to all concerned officials  and employees for the immediate settlement/liquidation of their outstanding cash advances  otherwise, we recommend that management withhold or suspend salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management require the BAC Secretariat  to post all the Notices of Awards and Notices to Proceed together with the approved contracts for all procurements undertaken by the LGU in the PhilGEPS and in the agency website if any, also in any conspicuous place in premises of the municipality within the prescribed period in compliance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the RIRR of R.A. 9184.
Further, printed documents evidencing such posting be attached as one of the supporting documents attached to the disbursement voucher.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections at least
once a week and intact and stop the practice of keeping huge amount of cash in his
possession.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to submit to her the Daily Cash Position Report in order to monitor the
status of cash of the municipality, both in the bank and in the custody of the
Treasurer, and detect whether continued large amount of collections are withheld
in the custody of said official.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate
adjusting journal entries to correct the errors in recording payments made on the
procurement of infrastructure projects and reflect the accurate balances of the
affected accounts. Subsequent similar transaction should be evaluated so that the
proper account and amounts are used in recording transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured medicines
and supplies in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase to recognize in
the books the asset and record the related expense upon its issuance for fair
presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General
Services Office maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards respectively, for
each type of inventory item, to monitor receipts and issuance and reconcile the
respective balances monthly.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured swine and
livestock in the Biological Asset account upon purchase to recognize the assets in
the books and record a reduction in the liabilities accounts only upon dispersal or
disposal for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General
Services Office maintain Stocks Ledger Card and Property Cards respectively, for
each type of livestock to serve as subsidiary records in monitoring receipts and
disposal and reconcile the balances monthly.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record in the Trust Fund
books of accounts, particularly in the General Journal, the unrecorded transaction
on the procurement of various materials and equipment for pedicab fabrication
using the appropriate accounts provided for in the Local Government Chart of
Accounts under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) to
ensure fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary
Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account all disbursements and balances
of the fund and provide information as to available balance at any given time
during the year.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a focal person and the
designated heads of the concerned office to obtain from the funding agencies the
MOA and other necessary documents to guide the LGU in its project
implementation. Require the concerned department heads to fast-track the
implementation of programs and projects funded by the various NGAs in
accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the
Liquidation Reports and other documents to the source agencies to facilitate
succeeding release of funds for the projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that every financial
assistance granted to barangays are covered with a Memorandum of Agreement to
define their responsibilities on the use of the fund including the submission of Fund
Utilization Reports.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant specifically undertake
the following:
a) Demand immediate liquidation of the transferred funds from the
concerned recipient barangays, including pictures of completed projects
/programs and Inspection Reports of the Municipal Engineer or
Program Coordinators;
b) Adjust the books of the municipality by recognizing a receivable from
the recipient barangay using “Due from LGU” account and set-up a
reciprocal account “Due to LGU” in the books of the concerned recipient
barangays;
c) Require the submission of all documentary requirements prescribed in
the guidelines before processing subsequent fund transfers to the
different barangays.

We recommend that the Municipal Development Council, through the
Municipal Mayor as Chairman, and the Municipal Planning & Development
Officer, include as priority projects in the AIP those which are capital in nature
and authorized to be funded from the 20% Development Fund.

Also, to ensure that all identified priority projects in the AIP are
implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development
Fund and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal DRRMO prepare annually the
LDRRMFIP in the prescribed format to support the amounts and the activities
indicated in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU with details of the
programs/projects/activities to be funded from the current year 70% mitigation
fund. Also, indicate in a separate caption the list of projects and activities to be
funded from the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board, through its Chairman,
utilize the SEF mainly for the improvement of school facilities and other authorized
activities compliant with the purpose of the SEF. Also, stop the payment of
honoraria and allowances which has no legal basis and ensure that all
disbursements are supported with complete documents to prove its validity and
propriety.

We recommend to the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through
the MENR Officer, follow-up the approval from DENR of the new dump site
location and immediately take action on any requirements to expedite its
acquisition. Also, initiate the procurement of additional equipment for collection of
waste materials and the rehabilitation of Materials Recovery Facility and sustain
the effective implementation of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the provision of
funds for the immediate acquisition of the lot and equipment necessary for the
delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste to attain the
objectives of the LGU’s Ten-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommend that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat
strictly observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February
12, 2009, on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the
Auditor within five (5) days after execution/issuance thereof, to afford timely
review and evaluation of the contract before the transaction is consummated.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents for some entries in the General Journal, the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers and supporting documents on some entries in the General Ledgers, prepare the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and rectify the other deficiencies noted. Henceforth, submit annually a complete General and Subsidiary Ledgers, all necessary schedules and the supporting documents for all entries in the General Journal and refrain from posting entry directly to the General Ledgers because all entries should be based from the General Journal and the Special Journals with corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers and supporting documents and strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements..

We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents and rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exist and whose nature of business differs with the nature of the items to be procured; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement its projects within the specified contract time, plus any time extension granted and if warranted, collect the necessary liquidated damages from the non-compliant contractors. Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without complete documentations and ensure the authenticity of the documents submitted by the bidders during bidding process.

We also recommend that the BAC submit justifications why bidding and disbursements were done despite lack of funds for the project Concreting of Poblacion Norte to Happy Valley Farm to Market Road; why a legal consultant was hired without the proper procurement process; why consider bidders whose nature of business was different from the nature of the transaction; why liquidated damages were not collected despite delays in the completion of the projects; why there were delays in the procurement process; why there were positive variance in the completion of projects; and why COA was not informed of the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance.

Likewise, we recommend that official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after such acceptance.

These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, Notice of Disallowance and Notice of Charge as a result of evaluation of the documents and justifications to be submitted by the municipality.

We recommend that management submit the required Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement of the different projects as well as the Project Briefs and Local Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP) for the livelihood projects. Likewise, submit the lacking supporting documents and justifications as well as rectify the other deficiencies noted. Further, we recommend that projects be implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries.

We recommend that management efficiently utilize the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF and rectify the deficiencies noted. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant make adjusting entries for the purchased equipment, prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and the disclosure for LDRRMF in the prescribed format, transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of CY 2016 Quick Response Fund amounting to P1,382,835.00 and the DRRMF-MOOE totaling P1,161,882.75 to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books and submit the lacking supporting documents. Further, we recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) submit the required report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC). Henceforth, strictly observe the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF.

We recommend that management fully implement the identified programs, projects and activities in the budget for SEF for the benefit of the students which were the purpose of RA 5447. Likewise, we recommend that management rectify the deficiencies noted, submit the lacking supporting documents and refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exist. Henceforth, strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 100(a) of RA 7160 and Section 1 of RA 5447.

We recommend that management formulate a 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan; justify the procurement of the plastic trash containers despite not included in the AIP and without appropriation and the reasonableness of the procured quantity and amount. Further, submit the distribution list or Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment for the plastic trash containers. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommend that management refrain from conducting its planning workshop or other related activities outside the premises of the municipality. Henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of Section 2 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Section 6.1 of COA Circular 2012-003. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommend that the officials and employees demand from the leagues of elected officials that Accountable Form 51 be issued for all their payments. Likewise, we recommend that management rectify the deficiencies noted and require the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess amount of P2,000.00 per day for the registration paid to private institution.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and other accountable/concerned officers to immediately submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents for audit. Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with the pertinent provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of audit of the documents submitted after the issuance of this memorandum.

Recommendation:


        We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should comply with the
   documentary requirements provided under Sec. 3 of COA Circular No.  2012-
   001 to ensure the propriety and validity of the transaction and to record
   fund transfers to barangay under the account code 1-03-03-030 – Due  from
   Local Government Units to ensure proper monitoring of fund releases.


        We  further  recommended  to  enforce  liquidation  of   the   fund
   transferred to this barangay to ensure that this  was  utilized  for  the
   intended projects.

Recommendations:


        We  recommended  to  the  Local  Development  Council  (LDC)  that
   priority should be given in the implementation of development  projects,
   programs and activities identified  or  included  in  Annual  Investment
   Program.

We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:

      • Enforce collection of the liquidated damages from the  supplier  if
        no document to prove the extensions of delivery dates


      • Submit written justification/explanation on the following:
        a. deficiencies noted on the  documents  related  to  extension  of
           deliveries;
        b. why the Municipality continuously granted extensions?
        c. despite the BAC resolution’s recommendation on the imposition of
           liquidated damages, why were there no effort exerted to  collect
           such?

Recommendation:


        We recommended that Management  exert/enforce  efforts  to  collect
   these collectibles so that  whatever  collection  can  be  used  for  the
   operation of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should  determine
   the collectivity of these accounts so that proper  adjustments  could  be
   made in order not to overstate the receivable balances as at year-end.

Recommendations:
We recommend that management:
Stop the practice of assigning job order personnel to collect revenues;
Utilize the existing bonded collectors to collect revenues under the strict   supervision of the Municipal Treasurer; and
If warranted by funds, consider the regularization of the job order personnel so as they can perform collection functions.
Management Comment:
In his letter-reply, the Municipal Treasurer stated that one of the job order personnel in their department was already recommended for regularization and that they will try their best to implement the recommendations.

Recommendations:
We recommend that a special account be maintained exclusively for the 20% Development Fund for proper monitoring of projects implemented under this fund.
In the meantime, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant furnish the Audit Team with a summary of all transactions pertaining to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund (current and continuing appropriations) in lieu of the subsidiary ledgers detailing the utilization of the same.
Management Comment:
Management acknowledged that they have not yet provided a special account for the 20% Development Fund and committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should exert additional efforts in implementing projects funded by the National Government within the prescribed period and to strictly comply with the provisions stated in their Memorandum of Agreement because failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract would entail a penalty imposition except if waived by concerned parties.
Management Comment:
In the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant acknowledged that indeed there were delays in the implementation of the programs and committed to be more efficient in implementing the projects and to avoid further delays.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require:
The Municipal Accountant to record in the books the cost of the hogs and goat procured totaling P416,000.00 and to maintain Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) for all the livestock procured;
The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain livestock cards for all livestock procured and to submit livestock inventory report to the Audit Team every July 31 and January 31 of each year; and
The Offices of the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer/Municipal Treasurer to reconcile both accounting and property records, respectively before submission to COA for validation.
Management Comment:
Management during the exit conference committed to implement the recommendation.

Recommendation:
