We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Vice-Mayor’s League of the Philippines to issue government-sanctioned receipt such as the Accountable Form No. 51, duly printed by recognized government printers, before releasing the financial aid due to the League.
Management Comment:
Management during the exit conference committed to implement the recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the concerned NGOs/POs to liquidate the long outstanding funds transferred to them; failure to liquidate the same should disqualify the said NGOs/POs from availing additional funds from the Municipality.
Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant said that she was trying her best to follow up the in-charge for the liquidation of funds received by the concerned NGOs/POs and promised to undertook earnest efforts in order that said funds will be liquidated

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local School Board carefully evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the specific PPAs set forth under Section 1 of R.A No. 5447 and Sections 100 (c) 272 of R.A. No. 7160. Otherwise, expenditures not falling within those allowed under the said laws shall be disallowed in audit.
Management Comment:
Management during the exit conference committed to implement the recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return immediately the P500,000.00 Disbursement Acceleration Program fund it received from the Office of Senator Francis “Chiz” Escudero to the Bureau of the Treasury and furnish, accordingly, the Audit Team a copy of the pertinent deposit slip.
Management Comments:
Management during the exit conference committed to implement the audit recommendation. They also commented that said funds were never utilized by the Municipality because they have doubts as to its constitutionality.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, take immediate action to resolve its waste management transition from utilizing its old dump to opening a new and operational one that conforms with the requirements of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to prevent the accumulation of garbage in the barangay waste yards.
Management Comment:
In the exit conference, management commented that what they actually have is a Materials Recovery Facility and not an open dump site and all garbage are arranged.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Materials Recovery Facility of the Municipality upon inspection of the Audit Team did not seem like such as upon seeing it, the same looked like an open dump as can be seen in the pictures due to the volume of undisposed garbage found therein.
Management is reminded that unless Republic Act No. 9003 is amended, the Municipality has to abide with its provisions including the establishment of a sanitary landfill. Hence, the same must be included in its priority projects.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant properly observe the provision of depreciation by allocating those that have to be depreciated and recording the same in the proper depreciation accounts.
Management Comment:
During the exit conference, management committed to implement the audit recommendation.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Property Officer to initiate the conduct of the annual physical count of all property, plant and equipment and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the noted variance in the movements of the PPE balances as presented in the financial statements, particularly in the Cash Flow Statement and Notes to Financial Statements.
Management Comment:
In the exit conference, management committed to implement the audit recommendations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the responsible official(s) of the Municipality to submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) pertaining to all BuB and other NGA-funded projects of the Municipality for the FY 2015 and 2016.
Management Comment:
During the exit conference, management committed to implement the audit recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the Bongo-type rescue vehicles under the account “Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment” instead of recognizing the same under the “Motor Vehicles” account.
Management Comment:
Management, during the exit conference, committed to implement the audit recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking reports and henceforth, observe the submission within the prescribed period of the Report of Collections and Deposits, Disbursement Vouchers, Liquidation Reports, Journal Entry Vouchers and general journal transactions to the Audit Team.
Management comment:
In the exit conference, management committed to implement the audit recommendation.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the LGU GAD Focal Point System ought to strive to identify gender issues and formulate GAD programs/projects/activities that reflect the unequal status of women and men in order that the issues and concerns of women particularly on the protection and realization of their rights, economic empowerment, participation in local governance and decision-making would be effectively addressed.
Likewise, we recommend that the LGU GAD Focal Point System see to it that the Annual GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality is duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG Provincial Office.
Management Comment:
During the exit conference, management committed to implement the audit recommendations.

1.6     We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee to
    conduct complete physical inventory of  all  properties  owned  by  the
    Municipality and prepare a report thereon in  accordance  with  Section
    124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I to determine  the  existence  and
    accuracy of the PPE owned by the LGU. Moreover,  direct  the  Municipal
    Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and issue Acknowledgement  Receipt
    for Equipment, and the Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  PPE  Ledger
    Cards as required in Section 114 of the same Manual.

2.5         We  recommended  that  Management  require  the  Municipal
    Treasurer to record the  financial  transactions  of  the  Municipality
    daily pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual  for  LGUs,
    Volume  II.  Likewise,  direct  her  to  update  and  balance  all  the
    transactions and reconcile the same with the cash on hand  as  provided
    in Section 63 (D) of LTOM. Moreover, direct the Liquidating Officer  to
    maintain Liquidating Officer’s Cashbook for  each  fund  and  shall  be
    updated and balanced daily as provided for in Section 63 (B)  of  LTOM.
    Besides, compel  all  the  Revenue  Collectors  to  maintain  Teller’s/
    Collector’s to record all collections received and all  remittances  to
    the designated Liquidating Officer in accordance with Section 63 (C) of
    LTOM.

3.10  We recommended the Local Chief Executive to  ensure  the  proper
    granting and utilization of all cash advances as provided in Section  8
    of COA Circular No. 97-002. Besides, require the  accountable  officers
    concerned  to  immediately  liquidate  and/  or  settle  their  overdue
    outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, such failure / or settle the same
    shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding  of  their  salaries
    and the instruction of other sanctions. Moreover, direct the  Municipal
    Accountant to regularly update the  Monthly  and  Quarterly  Monitoring
    Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances Granted  to  Accountable  Officers
    including cash advances for salaries, wages and other specific  purpose
    supported with liquidation report promptly  for  immediate  review  and
    submit the same on or before 5th day of  the  following  month.  Before
    processing  additional  cash  advances,  require  her   to   attach   a
    certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated  and
    accounted for in the books.

4.7   We  recommended  that  Management  require  the  Municipal
    Accountant to adhere strictly the provisions of Sections 4(e) and 5  of
    Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section 31 of  the  same
    Manual,  Volume  II.  Likewise,  direct  him  to  prepare  and   submit
    immediately the  Journal  Entry  Vouchers  (JEVs),  Check  Disbursement
    Journal (CkDJ), Cash  Disbursement  Journal  (CDJ)  and  Cash  Receipts
    Journal (CRJ).


            4.8 Otherwise, failure on the part of  the  officials  concerned
    to  submit  the  documents   and   reports   mentioned   herein   shall
    automatically cause the suspension of payment of their  salaries  until
    they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.

5.7   We recommended the Municipal Local School Board  should  utilize
    SEF within the approved  budget  and  adhere  strictly  the  prioritize
    programs, projects and activities (PPAs)  pursuant  to  R.A  No.  5447,
    Section 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No.  01  series  of  1998  of
    DECS, DBM and DILG.  Stop using the SEF to PPAs not within the list  of
    priority expenses as provided  in  the  latter’s  provisions.  Further,
    direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal
    Treasurer to  stop  disbursing  funds  without  approved  appropriation
    pursuant to Section 4 (1) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

6.4  We recommend the management to strictly adhere RA  9003  to
    adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management
    programs. Further, direct the barangay officials  to  strictly  enforce
    the MRF in  every  barangay  to  adhere  proper  sorting,  segregation,
    composting, and recycling of solid waste.

7.4  We recommended that management:

     1. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to  strictly
        adhere to the provisions of  DILG-DBM  Joint  Circular  No.  2011-1
        dated April 13, 2011;


     2. Ensure that 20% Development Fund be fully utilized to  achieve  the
        optimum desirable socio-economic and environmental development.

8.9        We recommended that management:

          a. Prepare LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually in the
             prescribed format required under COA Circular No. 2012-002;



































          b. Direct the concerned officer to submit Report on Sources and
             Utilization of DRRMF, prepared and certified correct by the
             Municipal Accountant as provided in the prescribed format, on
             or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through
             the LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA Auditor of the municipality;

9.6   We recommend that Management implement and utilize the LCPC Fund
    to its programs, projects and activities (PPAs). Direct the members  of
    the LCPC to prepare Local  Development  Plan  for  Children  and  Local
    Investment Plan for Children, and maximize the allocated LCPC  Fund  to
    identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No 2012-120 dated July 4,  2012
    to ensure full strengthening.

10.4  We recommended that management strictly adhere Section  7,  Rule
    II of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and prepare PPMP and consolidated
    APP. Require the BAC to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated
    PPMP and APP yearly for us to validate and ensure that  all  procurement
    undertaken by the LGU were based  on  the  approved  annual  procurement
    plan.

11.6        We recommended  that  management  require  the  GAD  Focal
      Person to prepare the Annual GPB using  the  prescribed  form  and  in
      coordination with the Municipal  Budget  Officer,  submit  it  to  the
      Provincial Planning and Development Office then to the  DILG  Regional
      Office  for  review  and  approval.  Further,  develops  GAD  PAPs  to
      strengthen the Municipality’s gender-responsive governance and  ensure
      that GAD Fund is utilized in align with  the  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  Joint
      Circular No. 2013-01.

12.9  We recommended that the LGU establish and maintain GAD  database
    and conduct gender analysis  of  their  plans,  programs  and  projects
    through the administration of the HGDG to serve as  basis  in  the  its
    gender-responsive programs, projects and activities in accordance  with
    PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to exert all efforts necessary to prepare and submit the financial statements, disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports on cash advances immediately considering that these were already long-overdue. Henceforth, submit all the financial reports on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for verification purposes. Further, we have recommended that Management submit justification for the noted lapses. We also reiterated our previous recommendation for the hiring of a qualified Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant in as much as these are mandatory position under Section 470 and 474 of Book III, Title V of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted some of its accounts
observations.reason:
Reiterated in the Management Letter

Same situation was noted for CY 2016

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation. Likewise, exert effort in hiring a qualified Municipal Accountant in order for the Municipal Budget Officer to assume his previous position.
observations.managementAction: In December 2016, the Municipal Budget Officer was no longer the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management, through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO), prepare the LDRRMP or LDRRMFIP annually.  The said plan shall present the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management furnished COA copy of Annual Investment Plan for CY 2016 wherein LDRRMFIP was incorporated and presented in lump sum.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:

Designate GAD Focal person who can perform the duties and responsibilities inherent to his/her designation and be required to ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget is appropriately evaluated by the NCRFW to guarantee that the Plans, Programs and Activities (PPAs) to be undertaken are part of the GAD priority agenda of the government and that activities are sufficiently funded; and

Intensify proper training and seminar to the GAD Focal person relative to GAD concepts, principles and policies as well as GAD planning and budgeting concepts to enhance and broaden their knowledge in planning and proper execution of GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Designation of GAD Focal person will be discussed further with the LCE.

The GAD Plan and Budget is based from the priority GAD related programs of different frontline service providers including the secondary schools where the concerned persons were informed verbally to submit necessary documents for the release of funds
. 
There were PPAs that exceeded the allocated budget like the Women’s month celebration, where attendees were beyond the targeted number of participants thus expenses increased.

The GAD Plan and Budget was already submitted to DILG Isabela and the latter likewise submitted to DILG Regional Office for review and approval. However, no feedback was received by the municipality.

The management has sent the focal person to seminars and trainings to be equipped with GAD concepts, principles and policies in GAD planning and budgeting however, schedules of trainings were in conflict with other tasks designated to the assigned focal person.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer must ascertain that the identified programs/projects under the 20% EDF are those mentioned under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and Section 287 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2016 budget included appropriation for LCPC and repairs and maintenance of heavy equipment.  The allocated amount for these purposes were not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer adopt measures to facilitate the timely release of social pension to the beneficiaries.  To hasten the implementation, we further recommended that payment of social pension be made thru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) Cash Card.  In doing so, MOA will be entered into with the Land Bank of the Philippines as conduit for the payment of social pension to beneficiaries to expedite the production of ATM Cards as one of the guaranty for the timely release of the social pension.
observations.managementAction:
The liquidation report was submitted to concerned offices and the unspent/ unutilized fund was returned to DSWD FO2 in 2016.

The Social Pension Focal person was able to speak with DSWD FO2 Social Pension focal person and other Social pension staff regarding the CY 2017 payment, wherein a partnership with Veterans Bank will be entered and payment will be sent thru Philippine Post Office who will deliver door-to-door to the recipients.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor and require all his collectors to remit or turn-over to him their collections within the period prescribed under the afore-cited regulations and be deposited daily or intact to avoid accumulation of cash under his custody or under the custody of his deputized collectors;  

Require the Municipal Accountant to account all deposits made to SEF and be deducted from the accumulated balance of the Cash in Vault account;  and 

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records at the end of each month to avoid incurrence of accumulated and unreconciled balances.
observations.managementAction: The huge balance were adjusted based on the report of cash examination covering the period November 10, 2011 to November 4, 2015.  Remaining balance pertain to unreconciled accounts prior to November 10, 2011.
The amount was reduced to ₱21,726,874.61.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 8.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the members of the Sangguniang Bayan submit certified photocopies of attendance sheet and logbook during regular and special sessions conducted as basis in determining the payments of their claims for RATA.
observations.managementAction: SB Members are now aware of their attendance in session hence, required documents to substantiate their claim for RATA were duly verified prior to the preparation of payroll for RATA.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all the accounts appearing in the financial statements to determine at a given time the details and status of each.
observations.managementAction: Initially, the Municipal Accountant prepared SL of various accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require immediate settlement of the audit disallowances and suspensions, and strict compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of its funds to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions in the future.
observations.managementAction: Partial recovery of ₱51,900.00 was made.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 18.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

instruct the Administrative Officer and the Human Resource Management Officer to conduct deeper re-assessment of the factors that contributed to the unsatisfactory performance of the accounting unit, taking into consideration the personnel competence, work attitude and sense of responsibility towards the fulfilment of work commitments of the officials/employees concerned; and

require the  ICO Accountant to-

submit year-end financial statement for all funds within the due dates as required under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 02; and

make personal re-assessment and evaluation on the performance of the accounting unit and accordingly introduce necessary corrective measures that would address the problems causing the non-preparation and submission of the required reports. 

withhold the salary of persons responsible until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to the filing of an administrative case against them for their unjustified and continued failure to abide by law and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management had acknowledged the noted lapses/delays and ensures that needed reports be submitted promptly.

observations.recommendation: We therefore reiterated our recommendation that the LCE cause the immediate submission of the required documents to avoid disallowance of unsubmitted vouchers pursuant to Section 39 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: All documents have been forwarded to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The ICO Accountant submitted the transaction documents up to August 2016 as of this writing

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that management create an inventory committee and conduct complete physical inventory of all properties of the municipality to determine their existence and physical conditions in accordance with abovementioned rules and regulations. Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the physical count. 
Issue a written order to designated members of the inventory committee indicating therein the specific period for which the subject undertaking must be completed and the period of submission of the corresponding complete physical inventory report. Accordingly, due to the repeated lapses of the officials concerned, sanctions for   continued failure of the committee to come up with the required reports should also be expressly provided in the said order.

Exert extra efforts to locate old records of acquisition to provide basis in the preparation of subsidiary records, computation for depreciation or for dropping from records all obsolete, destroyed and unserviceable properties.

Considering that the subject deficiencies had been repeatedly discussed in prior years’ report,   the recommended courses of actions and corrective measures offered should be given utmost consideration by the Management.
observations.managementAction: Management has not yet created Municipal Inventory Committee
observations.reason: Promised to create Municipal Inventory Committee this coming year.

observations.recommendation: We therefore reiterated that the Local Chief Executive issue a written directive to the OIC Accountant to facilitate the updating of records and accounting reports up to the current year transactions. Timely preparation and submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements must be observed in order to easily determine whether the cash in bank accounts are accurately presented in the general/subsidiary ledgers at any given time
observations.managementAction: Monthly bank reconciliation and bank statements were regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer implement the lawful order of this Commission on the withholding of salaries of the officials named liable in the COA Order of Execution (COE) in settlement of their respective accountabilities.

Further, to avoid the recurrence of similar violations, the Local Chief Executive to issue a written directive/memorandum to all agency officials concerned to strictly observe the rules, regulations and laws (particularly the procurement law) governing the receipt and utilization of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The management recognizes said order and plan to deal with it accordingly by implementing settlement gradually and promised to partially settle this coming year.
observations.reason: The Agency personnel concerned promised  that they will make partial or installment payment of their dues this coming year.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Immediately instruct the LGU officials concerned to formulate guidelines/criteria that will be used in the selection of commercial space occupants;

Facilitate the awarding of occupancy rights to qualified stall applicants/entrepreneurs in order to meet the target revenue and generate income for the municipality;

To cause the collection from the contractor the amount equivalent to liquidated damages for non-compliance with the terms of completion of the project stipulated under Article No. 2 of the Contract Agreement.

We also reiterate our earlier recommendations which remained unimplemented by the agency, to wit:

The OIC Accountant to effect recording of transactions and submission of complete disbursement vouchers and financial reports within the reglamentary period in order to facilitate timely review and issuance, if applicable, of corrective measures and/or recommendations.

The LCE to cause the immediate submission of the required documents and reports.  Withhold the salary of persons responsible until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to the filing of an administrative case against them for their unjustified and continued failure to abide by law and regulations. Henceforth, observe the timelines of reportorial requirements of the Commission set forth in pertinent regulations.
observations.managementAction: Occupancy rights of market stalls are awarded to qualified applicants and resources from operations is being enjoyed by the municipality, starting October 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notices of Suspensions, otherwise, these will mature into disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The management recognizes said findings. Proper requests/appeals with compliance to needed requirements, were filed to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Unsubmitted disbursement vouchers for CY 2015 were already forwarded to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce all collectors to deposit their collections daily and intact and to monitor the issuances of all accountable forms. We also recommend compliance of Section 30 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual and to adhere strictly to existing rules and regulations on cash management to avoid the occurrence of loss or shortage.
observations.managementAction: The revenue collection clerks were required to remit their collections daily. The Mun. Treasurer was required to monitor closely the remittance of the collections and deposited in the depository bank.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to closely monitor the remittance of the daily collections of the revenue collection clerks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances for travel and to adhere to provisions of COA Circular 96-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant was required to closely monitor the grant of cash advances to officers and employees.
observations.reason: There were some cash advances not totally liquidated but the outstanding cash advances were already liquidated almost half from the outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate liquidation of cash advances through demand letters signed by him or authorized representative.  Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code states in unequivocal terms that the failure of a public officer to have duly forthcoming any public funds or property with which he is chargeable upon demand by any duly authorized officer shall be prima facie evidence that he has put such missing funds or property to personal use.

We also recommend that the concerned officials adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002 especially the withholding of salaries of those who refuse to settle their accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Not all were liquidated because other employees and officers were separated from office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create an inventory and disposal committee to act immediately in the proper identification, ascertain existence, location and disposal of these assets in order to fairly present the accounts in the Financial Statements.

	We further recommend that municipal accountant restore back the PPE accounts dropped from the books and adjustment have to be made only after proper inventory and disposal of the properties.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Team was already created to clearly verify those assets listed in the damaged assessment report during the 7.2 magnitude earthquake.
observations.reason: The amount  of PPE dropped from the books were based on the Inventory  Report of Property Plant & Equipment as of December 31, 2014.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Submit justifications/explanations in the delay of project implementation and completion.

Submit justifications/explanations on the discrepancies noted on the dates of documents which will not reconcile to the other type of documents submitted when traced to the actual flow of the transaction.

Submit all pertinent documents pursuant to RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Faithfully adheres to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement, RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
Explanation were already given why there was delay on the implementation because of the lack of stock area for the delivered goods.

The modes used in the procurement was emergency in order to cater the needs of the constituents affected by the 7.2 magnitude earthquake

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management faithfully adheres to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Circular 2011-1 in the appropriation of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The management already classify during budget hearing we will adhere in the DILG-DBM joint circular 2011-1
Other charges where already charge to general fund in order to adhere to the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular as to the usage of the 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the continuing projects of the 20% development fund.  Further, projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials.
observations.managementAction: The funds was an advance budget for the procurement of the heavy equipment. The Municipality cannot outright appropriate the heavy equipment in one year only because other project may be hamper.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Submit justification for providing appropriation for Intelligence Funds.

Submit justification for charging the aforementioned expenses to the Intelligence Fund appropriation to prevent disallowance.

To strictly follow the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in the release, utilization and liquidation of Intelligence Funds.
observations.managementAction: The appropriation was intended to Confidential fund and not intelligence fund as part of the peace and order budget. The budget officer failed to change the term from intelligence fund to confidential fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant cease from processing and the Local Chief Executive cease from approving claims for honoraria of BAC, TWG and the BAC Secretariat unless supported by the required documents pursuant to Sec. 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant was required to make sure the any claims from honorarium of BAC members should be supported by complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned employees to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant and Mun. Treasurer were required to coordinate so that all the respective vouchers, official receipt and financial report will be submitted on time.
observations.reason: The Municipal treasurer office failed to indorsed in the accounting office the documents for recording. The Mun. Treasurer office failed to submit paid vouchers for the respective period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan & Status of Implementation for the AAR 2014.

It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was advised to prepare the agency action plan in order to implement the comments and recommendation of the audit.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the Agency Action plan on the respective period required.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management:

Intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, objectives, laws, regulations and the utilization of the GAD Budget.

Consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address issues concerning women’s human rights and economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

1.	Submit justification/explanation for failure to conduct physical inventory taking of all PPE accounts and prepare /submit the inventory report  to COA.

2.	 Property custodian/Municipal Treasurer should prepare individual Property Card/ARE for every property issued and renew the records every 3 years thereafter pursuant to Section 42 of the NGAS manual, to easily pinpoint responsibility and accountability.

3.	Conduct physical inventory taking of all PPE accounts and submit the inventory report to COA.

4.	Maintain Property Ledger Cards of all PPE accounts to facilitate identification, monitoring and validation of items/units reflected in the inventory report.

a.)	Submit justification/explanation for  non-settlement of cash advances despite repeated inclusion in the  annual audit reports.

b.)	After due notice from concerned personnel, unliquidated cash advances shall be deducted from  whatever amounts due from the concerned Officers and Employees.

c.)	Direct all concerned officials responsible in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 particularly in the prompt liquidation of cash advances. 

d.)	Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advances are liquidated. The Municipal Accountant shall ensure  the compliance of the  COA Circular No.97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant enforced the liquidation of these cash advances in 2015 and said that most of these cash advances were liquidated in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant enforced liquidation and called attention to concerned employees.

observations.recommendation:
1.	Submit justifications/explanations why the Municipal Mayor was made the payee of the purchase of lot instead of the true  vendor.

2.	Submit justification why the same amount was booked as Accounts Payable still with the Mayor as payee. 

3.	Submit justifications/explanations why the dates in the submitted documents did not reconcile with the logical chronology of things.

4.	Submit justifications/explanations why DBP CheckNo.  43830881 dated September 23, 2014 previously issued to the Municipal Mayor and encashed on September 24, 2014  was the same check allegedly issued/received by Uri Sallacay  per undated DV No. 100-15-01-68.

5.	Submit all documents to support the purchase of the lot pursuant to Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and  other pertinent provisions to support the payment.
observations.managementAction: The management is in the process of complying the requirements for the titling of these lands. They have also submitted the justifications required in the recommendation but were found deficient by the COA Auditor.
observations.reason: Late compliance of the requirements for titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to keep and maintain a check register where all checks drawn for the day are immediately recorded in chronological order  and where the rightful claimants are required to acknowledge receipt of the check thereon, pursuant to Section 42 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, as a tool of management in detecting possible irregularities and avoiding possible claim disputes.

We recommend that management and concerned officials immediately  comply with the provisions specified under Section 4, of P.D 1445 and Section 168(c) and 362(b-c) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I, and that all claims relative to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles be properly supported with complete documents to substantiate its validity, propriety and legality

1.	We recommend that the Municipality use competitive bidding in the procurement of various supplies, materials and equipment to avail  low prices of supplies that is  most advantageous to the government.

2.	Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to consolidate the items needed on or about the same time into one purchase request to eliminate /avoid splitting of request/orders and avail best offers/terms if procured in bulk orders.

1.	Management refrain/stop the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis, except petty purchases to be paid out of the Petty Cash Fund. 

2.	Management may choose  any of the alternative methods of procurement provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184, if competitive  bidding is not feasible.

1.	Acquire and provide Biometric Machine to LGU personnel to register actual and prescribed office or work hours, as well as to safeguard the signature of the supervisor who will attest the correctness of the DTR of each employee. 

2.	Require submission of Daily Time Record (DTR) based on the biometric machine entries/records to support in computing the salaries and wages reflected in the payroll transactions of the municipality.

3.	In the meantime, carefully monitor and scrutinize log book entries to arrive at the correct man-hours/attendance actually rendered by LGU personnel to avoid overpayment and waste of government funds.

4.	Require all employees to use the bundy clock/biometric machine to record attendance.

5.	Require all employees to prepare daily time record based on bundy card/biometric print out signed by the immediate supervisor and attached same to the payroll.

Require the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards to be the basis of inventory reports and accounting records.

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical count of its properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment on or before the due date.

Require the GSO-Designate to reconcile her Property Cards with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, likewise with the General Ledger every end of the year.

Require GSO-Designate to renew Acknowledge Receipts of Equipment every three years from its issuance.
observations.managementAction: Inventory count was conducted and the report was submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel complement.

observations.recommendation:
Must initiate the move by creating the inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties of the municipality.  The corresponding equipment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts.  In the absence of available records of acquisitions in the past years, they will have no other recourse but to take hold of the disbursement vouchers to obtain the necessary data to be recorded in the equipment/property ledger cards.

Require the general services officer or municipal treasurer to submit the request for inspection with complete supporting papers so that the auditors can take proper action on the request.
observations.managementAction: Damaged properties were inspected by the COA Technical personnel however request to drop these properties from the books was not made.
observations.reason: Management did not submit the inventory report for the damaged properties as well as the request to drop these properties from the books.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections intact daily or not later than the next working day to the authorized depository bank pursuant to Section 32, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.  Moreover, advise the Municipal Treasurer to record collections first before its deposit to avoid negative balances of the Cash in Vault account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials/employees concerned to strictly comply on the prompt submission of copies of contracts, P.O.s and other similar contracts of understanding to the Auditor within five (5) days from its perfection pursuant to Section 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001.
observations.reason: Purchase Orders were submitted with no supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Exercise fully the taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to 150 of R. A. 7160 in order to earn more revenue necessary to augment the funding for the operation of the municipality.  Otherwise, implement austerity measures to reduce operating expenses and utilize internal revenue fund for developmental projects that would be beneficial to the constituents.
observations.reason: Due to the size and capacity of the municipality and its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management comply with the 1% appropriation requirement for the senior citizens and differently-abled persons during the preparation of its budget in order to ensure appropriate funding for programs, projects and activities concerning this specific group.

observations.managementAction: The municipality has appropriated 5% for GAD but the PPAs were not in consonance with the objectives of GAD.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to enforce settlement/liquidation from the concerned accountable officials and employees of their outstanding unliquidated cash advances as provided in the existing COA regulation.  Otherwise, appropriate action will be instituted by this Office.

Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe COA Circular 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances in the future similar transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant enforced the liquidation of these cash advances in 2015 and said that most of these cash advances were liquidated in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
Stop the practice of granting cash advance for Payroll Fund very much earlier than the pay day to avoid exposing LGU payroll funds to misuse, borrowings, and risk of loss.  The Payroll cash advance maybe prepared earlier, but the check should be dated not earlier than three (3) days before payday.

Require the Municipal Accountant to control the grant of cash advances for payroll fund in such date not earlier than 2 days from pay day to strengthen control on cash disbursements on payroll fund and to avoid the payroll fund from risk of  misuse, borrowings or loss.

Enjoin all accountable officers (Disbursing Officer and Special Disbursing Officers) to liquidate their cash advances on time as required by law, rules and regulations.

Require the accountable officers to record on time all transactions incurred by them in their cashbooks and in the Report of Cash Disbursements to facilitate verification of accountability at any given time.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why the check for the Payroll fund were issued in much advance date contrary to existing auditing regulations exposing such fund to misuse, borrowings and loss.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to record his deposits on the actual date the collections are deposited to reflect an accurate record of his accountability as provided under Section 48 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Accounting
and Auditing Manual, Volume III that transactions must be promptly recorded in accordance with the state accounting standards. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to provide the Municipal Treasurer the Bank Reconciliation Statement monthly and copies of the Journal Vouchers for appropriate recording in the Cashbook all the valid reconciling items. 

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Cashbook balances of Account 101 and 111  with the General Ledger every end of the month especially every end of the year to ensure correctness and reliability of these accounts in the financial statements presentation.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Disbursing Officer to prepare and submit the required Report of Check Issued and Report of Cash Disbursement in compliance to the existing regulation in order to render complete recording of the financial transactions and a reliable presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

Require the Municipal Accountant to demand the required reports from the concern Accountable Officers to facilitate in the preparation of a complete, accurate  and reliable financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The accounting unit and the Treasurer’s Office has prepared the said reports but was not submitted to the COA Auditor on time.
observations.reason: Management was not aware about the deadline for the submission of the documents.

observations.recommendation:
Stop the practice of deducting and remitting   loan payments of private banks and/or cooperative  except those allowed by existing COA regulation. 

Terminate existing Memorandum Agreements that are contrary to existing COA regulations.
observations.managementAction: They have limited the number of private banks and cooperative they transact with.
observations.reason: Employees continue to loan from these banks and cooperatives.

observations.recommendation:
Require the municipal accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger for each Construction project of the LGU and monitor their completion so that they can be closed and transferred to the appropriate asset account.

Require the municipal accountant to close the completed construction projects and/or acquired equipment of the Trust Fund  to the Government Equity  and effect their transfer to the General Fund books for LGU use. Upon submission of the full liquidation to the Grantor, the liability accounts  (Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs, Due to NGOs/POs) shall be debited and closed).

Require the municipal accountant to close at the end of the year, cost of completed public infrastructures funded out of regular income to Government Equity account.  The assets should then be transferred from the agency books to the Registry of Public Infrastructures through memorandum entries, and the value of Roads, Highways, Bridges and other public infrastructures be dropped from the Property, Plant and Equipment and Government Equity accounts of the municipality as provided for in Section 50 of the Manual of the New Government Accounting System Volume I. 

Create a task force to identify the lots owned by the LGU, review if the same are titled in the name of the LGU and  to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots.

Update/adjust the Land (201) account balance in the books of accounts based on the results of the identification of municipal-owned lots and its titling.

Require the municipal accountant to transfer fully depreciated, unserviceable and obsolete  properties to the Other Assets (290) account, pursuant to Section 143 of the NGAS Manual Volume III;

Require the Inventory Committee  to complete the conduct of the physical count of all the properties recorded in the books and those not yet recorded but actually found in the LGU offices/premises and to prepare the corresponding inventory reports thereof; and reconcile them with the accounting records to come up with the correct account balances in the financial statement. 

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain stock, property and ledger cards, respectively to properly account the receipt and disposition of property and supplies.

Require the Municipal Accountant,  Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to prepare subsidiary ledgers pertaining to the MDGF loan liability accounts based on the Statement of Annual Amortization to facilitate the reconciliation of the loan account with the records of the grantor of the loan and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the corrects balances of Loans Payable and other related accounts in the financial statements.

Advise the municipal accountant to strengthen the monitoring and review of loan transactions to ensure proper and correct recording of financial  transactions in the books of accounts.

See to it that the Accountant is furnished by the local chief executive with all the documents that involves financial matters so she could appropriately take action.

observations.reason: Collection made during end-of year were deposited in the ensuing year  however, the accounting unit recognized  in advance the amount as deposited.

observations.managementAction: Dead livestock included in the books were dropped duly supported with the relief of accountability from the COA.
