observations.recommendation:
Require the municipal accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV) to reclassify cash advances  for travel and special purpose/ time-bound undertakings  recorded under the Receivables- Due from Officers and Employees (123) account to the appropriate account, Advances to Officers and Employees (148) pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002 dated January 19, 2007.  On the other hand, the cash shortage of Benjamin Abad amounting to P491,497.84 should be reclassified to the account Due from Officers and Employees (123).  

Send demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitor the liquidation of these cash advances.

Exert more effort to collect these receivables such as withholding payments of claims of municipal officials/employees who have long overdue accountabilities with the LGU as in the case of audit disallowances.

Follow-up with the Office of the Ombudsman the status of the case filed against the defalcating municipal treasurer.

Issue  bills  or  collection/confirmation letters  to  agencies/persons concerned  as  proofs  that the Municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means  to collect these receivables that have become dormant.
observations.reason: To be fully implemented in the ensuing year

observations.recommendation: Please require the refund of the extra cash gift received from concerned officials and employees.
observations.reason: Deduction done only at the time of retirement /separation of the employee from the active service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere on the provisions of keeping accounts of the agency and shall specifically undertake the following:
i require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to have a regular monthly reconciliation of the balances of undeposited collections of the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund to bring the Cash – Local Treasury balances accurate and reliable and fairly presented in the financial statements as of a given period.
ii. require/instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile balances in their respective records at least quarterly as mandated by the above-stated Circular to have an accurate and reliable balance of the Advances for Payroll Fund Account in the financial statements as of a given period.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to have a regular monthly reconciliation of the balances of undeposited collections of the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund to bring the Cash – Local Treasury balances accurate and reliable and fairly presented in the financial statements as of a given period.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile balances in their respective records at least quarterly as mandated by the above-stated Circular to have an accurate and reliable balance of the Advances for Payroll Fund Account in the financial statements as of a given period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly implement and adhere to the pertinent governing rules and regulations in the granting of cash advances and shall specifically undertake the following courses of actions:
1. Instruct the accountable officers requiring urgent liquidation or settlement of outstanding cash advances and require the Accountant to stop the practice of crediting the account Due from Officers & Employees in the absence of a proper liquidation reports together with the required supporting documentation and that reports which are supported with complete supporting documentation are all considered for liquidation before the claiming of salaries and other cash claims due them.
2. Send demand letters to all concerned accountable officers and employees who were granted cash advances, hence, accuracy of the reported unliquidated balance of P1,325,711.69 as of December 31, 2015 cannot be ascertained. The management will require the concerned accountable officer to settle their unliquidated advances or face withholding of salaries and other cash claims due them.
3. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure liquidation of the liquidated reports which are supported with copy of the said liquidation reports together with the required supporting documentation.
4. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of cash advances to facilitate audit and verification on the accuracy of the reported balance of unliquidated cash advances.
5. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of the crediting the account Due from Officers & Employees in the absence of a proper liquidation reports together with the required supporting documentation.
6. Payments for expenses shall be made thru checks as mandated in the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No, 97-002.
7. Procurements shall be made thru public bidding as a general rule as mandated by RA 9184, procurements thru reimbursement and payment thru cash advances shall be discouraged except on very rare cases.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure liquidation of the liquidated reports which are supported with copy of the said liquidation reports together with the required supporting documentation.
The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of cash advances to facilitate audit and verification on the accuracy of the reported balance of unliquidated cash advances.
Payments for expenses shall be made thru checks as mandated in the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No, 97-002.
Procurements shall be made thru public bidding as a general rule as mandated by RA 9184, procurements thru reimbursement and payment thru cash advances shall be discouraged except on very rare cases.
The Municipal Treasurer to require the concerned Accountable Officers to liquidate unliquidated advances and to ensure proper recording and submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere to the above-cited governing rules and regulations in the recording and conduct of physical inventory of property, plant and equipment and shall specifically undertake the following:
a) Create an inventory committee who will conduct a thorough physical count of PPE including their classification to the appropriate accounts and the assignments of the correct unit value to afford reconciliation of both records maintained in the Accounting Section and the Supply Office, the segregation of those that are no longer serviceable by class or type.
b) Cause the immediate submission of inventory report on the physical count of PPE to the concerned Audit Team for audit, verification and validation;
c) Require the Accounting and the Supply Office to regularly update and reconcile their records pertaining to PPE and the Supply Office are required to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and to reconcile records once the physical inventory of PPE is completed.
d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions on when to take up in the books the recorded costs to asset account implemented projects and to observe proper recording in the books of accounts.
e) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjusting entries on the overstatement and understatement of the above-stated accounts and to observe proper classification of accounts in the recording of transactions.
f) Instruct the Municipal GSO to always issue Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to the respective end users of purchased equipment to pinpoint accountability and responsibility in case of loss or damage to property.
observations.managementAction:
a) Create an inventory committee who will conduct a thorough physical count of PPE including their classification to the appropriate accounts and the assignments of the correct unit value to afford reconciliation of both records maintained in the Accounting Section and the Supply Office, the segregation of those that are no longer serviceable by class or type.
b) Cause the immediate submission of inventory report on the physical count of PPE to the concerned Audit Team for audit, verification and validation;
c) Require the Accounting and the Supply Office to regularly update and reconcile their records pertaining to PPE and the Supply Office are required to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and to reconcile records once the physical inventory of PPE is completed.
d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions on when to take up in the books the recorded costs to asset account implemented projects and to observe proper recording in the books of accounts.
e) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjusting entries on the overstatement and understatement of the above-stated accounts and to observe proper classification of accounts in the recording of transactions.
f) Instruct the Municipal GSO to always issue Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to the respective end users of purchased equipment to pinpoint accountability and responsibility in case of loss or damage to property.
observations.reason: Thorough conduct of Physical inventory of PPE is currently on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere to the above-cited laws, rules and regulations and must take a serious steps in addressing this recurring observation on missing DVs and specifically undertake the following:
a) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to immediately submit the subject missing 138 DVs in CY 2015 together with the complete supporting documents to avoid being suspended or disallowed in audit and to prevent the concerned personnel from facing the other penalties concerning non-rendition of accounts.
b) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to immediately comply the requisites in the Notice of Suspensions issued on missing DVs for CY 2014 and 2013 to prevent the suspensions from maturing into a disallowance.
c) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to institute control in the releasing of processed DVs to concerned payees or to authorized persons only for payment of LGUs obligations and ensure that paid disbursement vouchers were intact as listed in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) upon transmittal to the office of the Accountant; and
d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to always transmit complete submission of accounts to facilitate thorough audit and verification of the agency’s financial transactions.
observations.managementAction:
a) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to immediately submit the subject missing 138 DVs in CY 2015 together with the complete supporting documents to avoid being suspended or disallowed in audit and to prevent the concerned personnel from facing the other penalties concerning non-rendition of accounts.
b) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to immediately comply the requisites in the Notice of Suspensions issued on missing DVs for CY 2014 and 2013 to prevent the suspensions from maturing into a disallowance.
c) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to institute control in the releasing of processed DVs to concerned payees or to authorized persons only for payment of LGUs obligations and ensure that paid disbursement vouchers were intact as listed in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) upon transmittal to the office of the Accountant; and
d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to always transmit complete submission of accounts to facilitate thorough audit and verification of the agency’s financial transactions.
observations.reason: There are still unsubmitted DVs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of the above stated laws, rules and regulations and must specifically undertake the following corrective measures:
1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents were complete before processing the following claims:
a) payment of monthly RATA were supported with DTRs for appointive officials for easy determination of absences, likewise for the elective officials, adopted work schedule, attendance sheet, copy of the minutes of the sessions conducted in addition to the certification that the amount claimed was really spent in the discharge of regular functions and that he/she does not use any government vehicle;
b) payment of honoraria of Day Care workers were duly supported with Daily Time Records (DTRs) and validated accomplishment report to prove that services were really rendered;
c) payment of subsistence and laundry allowance to public health workers were supported with DTRs for easy reference in determining leave of absences and official travels undertaken.
2. Stop the grant of the following:
i. travelling allowances to concerned public health workers since not included in the Magna Carta and absence of legal basis, instead, grant travelling expenses in accordance with Executive order no. 248.
ii. gasoline allowances to field collectors in the absence of legal basis, instead allow the utilization of gasoline for government vehicles only, in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
3. Refrain from designating Job Order personnel as collectors since not allowed under existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents were complete before processing the following claims:
a) payment of monthly RATA were supported with DTRs for appointive officials for easy determination of absences, likewise for the elective officials, adopted work schedule, attendance sheet, copy of the minutes of the sessions conducted in addition to the certification that the amount claimed was really spent in the discharge of regular functions and that he/she does not use any government vehicle;
b) payment of honoraria of Day Care workers were duly supported with Daily Time Records (DTRs) and validated accomplishment report to prove that services were really rendered;
c) payment of subsistence and laundry allowance to public health workers were supported with DTRs for easy reference in determining leave of absences and official travels undertaken.
2. Stop the grant of the following:
i. travelling allowances to concerned public health workers since not included in the Magna Carta and absence of legal basis, instead, grant travelling expenses in accordance with Executive order no. 248.
ii. gasoline allowances to field collectors in the absence of legal basis, instead allow the utilization of gasoline for government vehicles only, in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
3. Refrain from designating Job Order personnel as collectors since not allowed under existing laws, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for strict adherence and specifically undertake the following:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002 to the COA Audit Team of the LGU.
b) In observance of transparency in disbursement transactions, instruct the concerned personnel of the LGU to always support with copy of the LDRRMFIP all releases and uses of LDRRMF.
c) Refrain from realigning funds intended for the implementation of programs/projects/activities listed in the CY 2015 LDRRMFIP and from utilizing Local Disaster Risks Reduction Fund for the procurement of equipment and procurement of consultancy services which are not included as among the PPAs to be funded.
d) Cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE from CY 2011 to 2015 in the total amount of P3,829,989.10 to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books to ensure proper utilization of the fund for its intended purpose.
e) The LDRRMO to always support with copy of the LDRRMFIP all releases and uses of LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002 to the COA Audit Team of the LGU.
b) In observance of transparency in disbursement transactions, instruct the concerned personnel of the LGU to always support with copy of the LDRRMFIP all releases and uses of LDRRMF.
c) Refrain from realigning funds intended for the implementation of programs/projects/activities listed in the CY 2015 LDRRMFIP and from utilizing Local Disaster Risks Reduction Fund for the procurement of equipment and procurement of consultancy services which are not included as among the PPAs to be funded.
d) Cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE from CY 2011 to 2015 in the total amount of P3,829,989.10 to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books to ensure proper utilization of the fund for its intended purpose.
e) The LDRRMO to always support with copy of the LDRRMFIP all releases and uses of LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for immediate actions:
1. Cause the immediate creation/establishment of the GAD Focal Point System to accelerate gender mainstreaming and formulation/enhancement and implementation of the Local GAD Code or Ordinances. The LGU shall sign appropriate issuances to institutionalize the establishment, reconstitution and/or strengthening of the GFPS.
2. Prepare annual GAD Plan and have it reviewed and duly approved by the DILG Regional Office and incorporate in the Annual Budget with equivalent cost of 5% of its total amount.
3. Prepare and submit the annual GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
1. Cause the immediate creation/establishment of the GAD Focal Point System to accelerate gender mainstreaming and formulation/enhancement and implementation of the Local GAD Code or Ordinances. The LGU shall sign appropriate issuances to institutionalize the establishment, reconstitution and/or strengthening of the GFPS.
2. Prepare annual GAD Plan and have it reviewed and duly approved by the DILG Regional Office and incorporate in the Annual Budget with equivalent cost of 5% of its total amount.
3. Prepare and submit the annual GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of the above-cited rules and specifically undertake the following:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to be serious in complying the audit recommendations on complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month since, this has been a finding in the CY 2013 & 2014 AR.
b) Submit other needed reports required by the audit team as representative of the Commission on Audit, to facilitate on time audit and verification on the propriety and validity of the consummated transactions as well as the accuracy and reliability of the accounts recorded and presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to be serious in complying the audit recommendations on complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month.
Submit other needed reports required by the audit team as representative of the Commission on Audit, to facilitate on time audit and verification on the propriety and validity of the consummated transactions as well as the accuracy and reliability of the accounts recorded and presented in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere to the requisites on the hiring of a legal consultant and shall specifically undertake the following:
a) Secure written conformity and concurrence of the Commission on Audit before the hiring of the legal consultant.
b) The LGU may opt the hiring of a Municipal Legal Officer, in which case the necessity of said course of action shall first be assessed.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still in the process of securing the written conformity and concurrence of the Commission on Audit before the hiring of the legal consultant and will fast track the approval of its hiring.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management shall adhere to the following corrective measures:
a) In resorting to Shopping as the alternative mode of procurement, always consider the threshold of P50,000.00 prescribed in Annex H of the IRR.
b) Procurement thru the alternative mode, in this case Shopping, must be supported with a BAC Resolution indicating the reason why they resort to the said alternative mode duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity.
c) Always support procurement of commonly used supplies and equipment with PS Certificate of Non-Availability to prevent the transactions from being disallowed in audit.
d) Request for Purchase of supplies, materials and equipment with amounts more than the prescribed threshold of P50,000.00 for procurement shall be done thru Competitive Bidding.
e) Invitation to Bid of procurement of supplies, materials and equipment more than the prescribed threshold of P50,000.00 must be posted in the PhilGEPS and copy of the posting must be attached to the disbursement vouchers to serve as proof of postings including the certification by the BAC Secretariat that said procurement opportunities were posted at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the procuring entity for seven (7) calendar days must also be attached.
observations.managementAction:
The newly designated BAC Chairman to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of RA 9184 in all shopping undertakings.
The LGU was required to procure commonly used supplies from DBM due to the long distance from LGU San Isidro to Tacloban and according to them it entails additional cost because of the travelling expenses incurred.
The LGU will ensure compliance and will support all shopping procurements with BAC Resolutions and supporting documents and will ensure procurements exceeding threshold shall go to public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and shall specifically instruct the designated BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to always submit perfected contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) days from the date of perfection/execution in order to afford timely auditorial review and technical evaluation of the contract before the transaction is consummated.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat was instructed to submit the required supporting documents together with the perfected contracts within the reglementary period of five (5) days from date of perfection/execution.
observations.reason: There were delayed submissions and some required supporting documents still not attached.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere with the required payment procedures and shall specifically consider the following:
Stop the practice of reimbursement and strict adherence to the procurement law is hereby enjoined to promote transparency in all LGUs transactions and to afford assurance on the reasonableness of the prices being paid for the existence of the procured goods and services.
Payment shall be directly made to suppliers except for small value items that could be paid out of petty cash fund.
Ensure strict compliance and proper documentation and justification for any reimbursements.
observations.managementAction:
The Office will ensure strict adherence to procurement law and stop the practice of reimbursement and direct payments to payees for procurements.
Small value items may be paid out of petty cash fund only.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management shall undertake the following:
1. Cause the immediate full implementation of the identified priority projects contained in the CY 2015 AIP that partakes the nature of investment and expenditures.
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account all disbursements and balances of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council will ensure optimum utilization and will cause the immediate full implementation of the identified priority projects in the CY 2015 AIP. The Municipal Accountant will maintain the Subsidiary Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account all disbursements and balances of the fund.
observations.reason: Appropriations for AIP in CY 2016 were not fully implemented; amendments to ineligible programs to conform with rules are planned.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant were instructed to impose penalties and to conduct examination of delinquent taxpayers. Efforts to collect delinquent market stall rentals and business taxes were undertaken; some penalties were imposed on market real property taxpayers. The MTO and Municipal Treasurer were required to submit DVs intact every 2nd day of the following month to facilitate collection and monitoring.
observations.reason: Penalty was imposed only to some market taxpayers; collection and full examination of delinquent accounts still ongoing.

observations.managementAction:
The concerned Administrative official to have serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets and to have it duly approved and properly filled-up as to plate number of the vehicle, and the details of the balances of gasoline, beginning, additions and ending and to determine the number of liters of the gasoline to be loaded based on the distance in kilometers on the supposed trip or scope of work to be done on any undertaking to be accomplished.
The Supply Officer or his/her authorized representative together with the inspection committee shall witness the reading of the beginning balance of the gasoline contained in the tank before loading the determined number of liters per approved withdrawal of gasoline duly signed as inspected.
At the end of the trip or undertaking the concerned Driver shall indicate the number of liters of gasoline used and the balance in liters remaining in the tank which would serve as the beginning balance on the next trip or undertaking.
At the end of the month the Supply Officer shall submit a Monthly Summary of Fuel Consumption for each government vehicle owned by the LGU.
observations.reason: Not all trip tickets with reading of the beginning and ending balance were available; monthly summary of fuel consumption not consistently prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
All collecting officers including the Municipal Treasurer be directed to strictly remit and deposit their collections on time, thereafter the reporting of said transactions thru RCDs and their consequent recording in the cashbooks and in the accounting records.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to practice the required deposit intact as well as on time of all collections under his possession and that only revenues received during the final week of each month shall comprise the undeposited moneys of said period.
Instruct management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to practice prompt and intact remittance thereafter the reporting of transactions thru RCDs and their consequent recording in the cashbooks as well as accounting records. For undeposited balances, immediate reconciliation and regular submission of RAAF and supporting documents to the Audit Team shall be enforced.
For procurement and disbursements from PDAF ensure complete supporting documents before payments are made.
Record and reconcile all collections and ensure accounting entries are complete and consistent.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was directed to remit and deposit collections intact and on time. Collections were to be recorded in the cashbooks and accounting records and adjustments made. Demand to submit related records and RCDs and reconcile differences. Some collectors have already remitted their collections and records were adjusted; some deceased treasurer issues impacted earlier records.
observations.reason: Some unrecorded collections were later remitted by the concerned collector; reconciliation and submission of full supporting documents remain outstanding for certain periods.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommend that management strictly direct the Municipal Treasurer to practice the required deposit intact as well as on time of all collections under his possession; collections should be deposited intact and not be held by accountable officers. Also enforce submission of cashbooks and monthly reconciliations and submission of RAAF and other supporting records to the Audit Team for review.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was instructed to deposit collections intact and on time and to submit required records; the Municipal Treasurer has been unable to fully comply due to death of the then Municipal Treasurer and transitional issues, but the Office has taken steps to adjust records and deposit collections.
observations.reason: Some collections were later deposited; however, full compliance and reconciliation remain to be completed and are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Liquidation Reports and supporting documents for the cash advances totaling P1,151,159.44 which were recorded as liquidated and the supporting documents on the entries for the reduction of Cash in Bank account totaling P19,652,397.46 and the expenses charged to Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees accounts totaling P2,023,616.98 and P214,014.44, respectively. Henceforth, all entries in the General Journal should have supporting documents to establish the propriety and validity of the transactions. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance as a result of the post-audit of transactions upon submission of the required documents.
observations.reason: Some documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive establish a Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) with a DRRMO and three (3) staffs as required in RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, and direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF, correct the deficiencies and submit the lacking supporting documents. Likewise, transfer the unutilized LDRRMF for CY 2015 to the trust fund books.
observations.reason: The management did not submit the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to promptly submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements including bank statements, paid checks and original copies of the credit/debit memos.
We recommend that the management maintains its accounts only on government financial institutions (GFIs) and strictly comply with DOF Circular Nos. 001-2015 and 003-2015.
observations.reason: was able to close the PNB account on June 1, 2017 but still maintain the PVB account because of amend ment issued by DOF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements and inclusion of appropriate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) was still not included in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management create an inventory committee, require the conduct of annual physical count and render a report thereon. Likewise, an inventory and inspection report of unserviceable property (IIRUP) should also be prepared prior to its disposal. Meantime, require the Accountant to transfer all unserviceable properties to other assets account.
observations.reason: Some documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the guidelines on the utilization of the 20% development fund of the Internal Revenue Allotment as provided under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of notices of suspension and/or disallowance resulting from the post-audit of transactions.
observations.reason: There were projects which were still unimplemented as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive cause the implementation of the audit recommendations towards improved financial operations and governance. Henceforth, require all concerned to strictly observe existing laws, accounting and auditing rules and regulations to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the Municipality.
observations.reason: There were still unimplemented recommendations prior to CY 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountable officers concerned immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances. This is without prejudice to the filing of the appropriate criminal and administrative cases against them as provided for in Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated cash advances. This is without prejudice to the filing of appropriate cases.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the personnel concerned payees to refund the allowance/honoraria received without legal basis. Henceforth, strictly observe COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 30, 2013, Items 5.0 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and Section 101 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Notified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned personnel and officers to strictly comply with the provisions of E.O. 298 and NBC 486, and refund the amount received in excess of the authorized travel rates and registration fees. Henceforth, stop the practice of issuing travel authority for attendance to conventions of organizations/leagues which the personnel is not affiliated in order to preclude unnecessary travel expenses. Likewise, strictly observe the proper classification of accounts as provided in the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason: Concerned were notified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU officials concerned be required to submit the documentary requirements for payment of honoraria and overtime services specified in Items 5.7 and 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: Concerned filed an appeal at the Director Regional then to Commission Proper; subsequent appeal filed at the Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the preparation of the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (MRSMI) for Drugs and Medicines. We further recommend to the Accountant to draw a JEV to restore the amount credited under the drugs and Medicines Inventory account.
observations.reason: There was no submission of the Physical Count of Inventories.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management should implement
policies and control
procedures in the granting and
liquidation of cash advances
through the following:
1.  Stop the  granting of cash
advance to official or
employee unless the previous
cash advance given to him/her
is first settled or a proper
accounting thereof is made in
compliance with the
above-referred provisions of
PD1445;

2. Regularly send
confirmation/demand letters as
basis in the reconciliation of
accounting and accountable
officers record and as basis
in imposing disciplinary
measures;

3. Strictly comply with COA
Circular No. 2012-004 for long
outstanding accounts.

In compliance with COA
Circular 2012-004, furnish
this Audit Team with the list
of accountable officers with
unliquidated and outstanding
cash advances, together with
copy of written notices duly
acknowledged by the concerned
accountable officers.  Submit
also copies of disbursement
vouchers (DVs) and related
support documents of those
with outstanding balances,
especially long overdue
accounts.

This LGU is reminded further
of the consequences provided
for in section 9.0 of the same
COA Circular 2012-004, as
follows:

“9.1 Failure of an accountable
officer to liquidate his
outstanding cash advance on or
before January 31, 2013 shall
constitute cause for the
filing of malversation charge
under Article 217, failure to
liquidate cash advance under
Article 218, both of the
Revised Penal Code or criminal
prosecution under Section 128
of PD No. 1445.

9.2  The suspension of
salaries of erring accountable
officer shall be ordered by the
Auditor concerned to the
proper agency official through
the head of the agency.

9.3 Appropriate administrative
proceedings shall likewise be
instituted.”
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant will
start to demand those who have
outstanding balance.

observations.recommendation:
Fast track the settlement of
accounts disallowed within six
(6) months from receipt of the
Notice of Disallowance (ND) by
the persons liable in
accordance with Section 10.4
of said circular.

Adhere to Section 7.1 and 7.2
of COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction:
The Management will continue
to deduct the corresponding
amount to concerned
employees.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that a
reconciliation of the
reciprocal accounts be made by
the Municipal Accountant and
to effect necessary correcting
entries.

We further recommended that
the Municipal Accountant
should closely monitor and
review the recording of
transactions in the books of
the agency.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant is
on the process of reconciling
the said account.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above
observations, we have
recommended the following:
• Create an inventory
committee to conduct physical
count of PPE. To ensure the
success of the committee, all
department heads are advised
to instruct their members to
cooperate in the conduct of
physical count;
• The inventory committee
shall prepare the Physical
Inventory Reports to be
submitted to the Property
Officer for verification and
reconciliation with PPE Ledger
Cards and the Property Cards
to be maintained by the
Accounting and Property
Offices, respectively;
• Proper disposal of
unserviceable property should
be made.

The following provisions of
Sec. 490 of Government
Accounting and Auditing Manual
shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies,
materials and equipment. —
Physical stock-taking is an
indispensable procedure for
checking the integrity of
property custodianship. In all
cases, the physical
inventory-taking which is
required semi-annually or
annually should be regarded
with importance.

For effective control of
government property, the COA
prescribes the following
regulations: a. Chiefs of agencies
are required to take a physical
inventory of all the equipment
and supplies belonging to
their respective offices at
least once a year, unless
otherwise determined by the
COA Chairman in specific
cases. Such inventory shall be
made as of December 31 on
General Form 41 (A) (Appendix
12), and submitted to the
Auditor not later than January
31 of each year, unless
extended by the Chairman,
Commission on Audit, upon
prior request of the chief of
agency concerned. When the
exigencies of the service
permit, the taking of
inventory should be in the
charge of a committee of two
or more employees designated
by the chief of agency,
including the property officer
or custodian, depending on the
extent of property
accountability in a particular
agency, to be witnessed by a
representative of the Agency
Auditor. Xxx”

b. The required
annual/semi-annual inventory
shall be certified correct by
the committee in charge
thereof, attested by the
representative of the auditor
concerned and approved by the
chief of agency in interest.”

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Accounting Unit to
coordinate with the Municipal
Social Welfare and Development
Office (MSWD) in booking-up
the Receivables Account and
maintenance of the individual
subsidiary ledger.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation on the items listed under Power Supply Systems to properly reflect their net book values in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto, to fully eliminate the difference in the accounts.
We recommended that the disbursements of funds intended for specific projects particularly those under the Trust Funds shall be based strictly on the project’s Work and Financial Plan.

It is recommended that the BAC scrutinize properly the experience required of from participating bidders on infrastructure projects of the Municipality so as to award the contract only to qualified bidders. It should have at least secured a confirmation from the LGU itself, more so that the Municipality of Dujali is part of Davao del Norte and just approximately 50 kilometers from San Isidro, Davao del Norte.

It is recommended that management secures from the contractor an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the procuring entity and submit a copy of the same to the Audit Team or deduct theentire amount from the first billing of the contractor, if any.
We recommended that the 10% retention money not previously deducted be withheld from the next progress billing of the contractor.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant require a valid receipt from the contractor; otherwise, the payment made shall be disallowed in audit.

It is recommended that Management deduct the uncollected tax of P143,112.19 from the succeeding progress billings of the contractors. Otherwise, the amount of tax not withheld including penalties shall be charged to the persons responsible for the withholding and remittance of the tax as provided for under Section 251 of R.A. No. 8424, otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act of 1997.

We recommended that the transfer of funds to other LGUs be accounted for in accordance with the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 94-013 wherein a Memorandum of Agreement is entered into by the parties involved and the amount transferred is accounted for as receivable subject to liquidation. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the amount of P296,000.00 transferred to the Provincial Government of Davao del Norte under the account “Due from Local Government Units (1-03-03-030)” and require the liquidation of the same from the latter.

We recommended that the Property Officer ensure that the date of receipt of the supplier of the Purchase Order is clearly indicated in the PO to serve as basis in the computation of liquidated damages in case the supplier fails to deliver the ordered items. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Property Officer and the Inspector properly accomplish the Acceptance and Inspection Report by serially numbering the same, filling up the date of preparation and indicating the dates of acceptance and inspection.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee clearly stipulate in the Notice of Award the posting of the required Performance Security within ten (10) days after receipt of the winning bidder of the NOA and the corresponding penalties in case of failure to do the same. 

Likewise, we recommended that the BAC ensure that the date of receipt by the winning bidder of the Notice of Award is clearly indicated in the NOA to serve as basis in the determination of the winning bidder’s compliance to the Performance Security posting.

We recommended that the Municipality submit at the beginning of the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities including those non-infrastructure PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year.

Likewise, it is recommended that the implementing offices of the Municipality inform its Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

Furthermore, it is recommended that the Municipality submit the Quarterly Report on Publicized Projects/Programs/Activities on or before the 10th day of the succeeding quarter to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still failed to submit at the beginning of the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities including those non-infrastructure PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year.

 The imple-menting offices of the LGU still failed to inform the SA and the ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infra project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

observations.reason: Some items of Property, Plant and Equipment were still not provided with depreciation as mentioned in Part II of this Report.

We recommend that the LGU direct the  Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Assessor to hasten the processing of the transferring of the land titles to the five lots under the name of the LGU, after which, require the appraisal of the lots for recording in the financial statement. 

Likewise, establish an archival system of filing documents evidencing the municipality’s proof of ownership of properties and such other documents that are of value to the municipality, to ensure the safety and protection of all government property.

Moreover, if necessary provide funds for titling and processing land titles for the five (5) parcels of land to safeguard the government property from possible squatters or intruders.
